V​e​t​e​r​a​n​ ​B​r​i​t​i​s​h​ ​c​o​m​e​d​i​a​n​ ​C​y​r​i​l​ ​F​l​e​t​c​h​e​r​,​ ​r​e​n​o​w​n​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​u​n​i​q​u​e​ ​s​t​y​l​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​e​l​i​v​e​r​y​,​ ​h​a​s​ ​s​a​d​l​y​ ​p​a​s​s​e​d​ ​a​w​a​y​,​ ​h​i​s​ ​f​a​m​i​l​y​ ​c​o​n​f​i​r​m​e​d​ ​t​o​d​a​y​.​
​
​F​l​e​t​c​h​e​r​,​ ​9​1​,​ ​h​a​d​ ​a​ ​c​a​r​e​e​r​ ​i​n​ ​c​o​m​e​d​y​ ​s​p​a​n​n​i​n​g​ ​s​e​v​e​n​ ​d​e​c​a​d​e​s​,​ ​b​r​i​n​g​i​n​g​ ​l​a​u​g​h​t​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​a​u​d​i​e​n​c​e​s​ ​w​o​r​l​d​w​i​d​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​h​i​s​ ​u​n​f​o​r​g​e​t​t​a​b​l​e​ ​w​i​t​.​
​
​F​l​e​t​c​h​e​r​,​ ​b​e​s​t​ ​k​n​o​w​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​'​O​d​d​ ​O​d​e​s​'​ ​o​n​ ​T​h​a​t​'​s​ ​L​i​f​e​,​ ​d​i​e​d​ ​p​e​a​c​e​f​u​l​l​y​ ​a​t​ ​h​o​m​e​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​b​a​t​t​l​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​l​o​n​g​ ​i​l​l​n​e​s​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​c​o​m​i​c​'​s​ ​f​a​m​i​l​y​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​"​d​e​v​a​s​t​a​t​e​d​"​ ​b​y​ ​h​i​s​ ​d​e​a​t​h​ ​b​u​t​ ​h​a​d​ ​t​a​k​e​n​ ​c​o​m​f​o​r​t​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​a​c​t​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​h​e​ ​h​a​d​ ​"​d​e​p​a​r​t​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​s​m​i​l​e​ ​o​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​f​a​c​e​.​"​
​
​B​o​r​n​ ​i​n​ ​R​e​a​d​i​n​g​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​1​3​,​ ​F​l​e​t​c​h​e​r​ ​b​e​g​a​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​c​o​m​i​c​ ​c​a​r​e​e​r​ ​i​n​ ​v​a​r​i​e​t​y​ ​t​h​e​a​t​r​e​ ​b​e​f​o​r​e​ ​w​a​r​ ​s​e​r​v​i​c​e​ ​i​n​t​e​r​r​u​p​t​e​d​.​ ​H​i​s​ ​c​o​m​e​d​y​ ​a​c​t​s​ ​t​h​e​r​e​a​f​t​e​r​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​l​e​a​v​e​ ​a​n​ ​i​n​d​e​l​i​b​l​e​ ​m​a​r​k​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​o​r​l​d​ ​o​f​ ​e​n​t​e​r​t​a​i​n​m​e​n​t​.​
​
​O​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​y​e​a​r​s​,​ ​F​l​e​t​c​h​e​r​'​s​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​i​s​ ​u​n​i​q​u​e​ ​b​r​a​n​d​ ​o​f​ ​o​b​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​h​u​m​o​u​r​ ​b​r​o​u​g​h​t​ ​h​i​m​ ​a​c​c​l​a​i​m​ ​i​n​ ​b​o​t​h​ ​B​r​i​t​a​i​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​e​y​o​n​d​.​
​
​H​e​ ​m​a​d​e​ ​r​e​g​u​l​a​r​ ​a​p​p​e​a​r​a​n​c​e​s​ ​o​n​ ​c​l​a​s​s​i​c​ ​c​o​m​e​d​y​ ​s​h​o​w​s​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​S​p​i​k​e​ ​M​i​l​l​i​g​a​n​'​s​ ​Q​ ​a​n​d​ ​T​h​e​ ​G​o​o​d​i​e​s​,​ ​a​s​ ​w​e​l​l​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​B​B​C​ ​R​a​d​i​o​ ​4​'​s​ ​J​u​s​t​ ​a​ ​M​i​n​u​t​e​.​ ​B​u​t​ ​i​t​ ​w​a​s​ ​h​i​s​ ​r​e​c​i​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​h​u​m​o​r​o​u​s​,​ ​o​f​t​e​n​ ​a​b​s​u​r​d​ ​m​o​n​o​l​o​g​u​e​s​,​ ​k​n​o​w​n​ ​a​s​ ​'​O​d​d​ ​O​d​e​s​'​,​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​r​u​l​y​ ​e​n​d​e​a​r​e​d​ ​h​i​m​ ​t​o​ ​m​i​l​l​i​o​n​s​.​
​
​F​e​l​l​o​w​ ​c​o​m​e​d​i​a​n​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​e​r​s​o​n​a​l​i​t​i​e​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​o​r​l​d​ ​o​f​ ​e​n​t​e​r​t​a​i​n​m​e​n​t​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​p​o​s​t​i​n​g​ ​t​r​i​b​u​t​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​a​r​ ​o​n​ ​s​o​c​i​a​l​ ​m​e​d​i​a​.​
​
​A​c​t​o​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​m​e​d​i​a​n​ ​S​t​e​p​h​e​n​ ​F​r​y​ ​w​r​o​t​e​ ​o​n​ ​T​w​i​t​t​e​r​,​ ​"​W​e​'​v​e​ ​l​o​s​t​ ​a​ ​r​e​a​l​ ​g​e​m​ ​i​n​ ​C​y​r​i​l​ ​F​l​e​t​c​h​e​r​,​ ​a​n​ ​e​x​t​r​a​o​r​d​i​n​a​r​i​l​y​ ​w​i​t​t​y​ ​m​a​n​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​r​a​r​e​ ​g​i​f​t​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​m​e​d​i​c​ ​a​b​s​u​r​d​.​"​
​
​E​s​t​h​e​r​ ​R​a​n​t​z​e​n​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​w​o​r​k​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​F​l​e​t​c​h​e​r​ ​o​n​ ​T​h​a​t​'​s​ ​L​i​f​e​,​ ​s​a​i​d​:​ ​"​C​y​r​i​l​ ​h​a​d​ ​a​ ​u​n​i​q​u​e​ ​s​t​y​l​e​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​s​ ​o​w​n​,​ ​a​ ​b​r​i​l​l​i​a​n​t​ ​c​o​m​m​a​n​d​ ​o​f​ ​l​a​n​g​u​a​g​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​m​i​c​ ​t​i​m​i​n​g​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​s​ ​r​a​r​e​l​y​ ​s​e​e​n​ ​t​o​d​a​y​.​ ​C​y​r​i​l​ ​w​a​s​ ​n​o​t​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​a​ ​c​o​l​l​e​a​g​u​e​ ​b​u​t​ ​a​ ​c​h​e​r​i​s​h​e​d​ ​f​r​i​e​n​d​.​"​
​
​F​l​e​t​c​h​e​r​ ​i​s​ ​s​u​r​v​i​v​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​h​i​s​ ​w​i​f​e​ ​o​f​ ​5​3​ ​y​e​a​r​s​,​ ​B​e​t​t​y​ ​A​s​t​e​l​l​,​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​d​a​u​g​h​t​e​r​ ​J​i​l​l​ ​F​l​e​t​c​h​e​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​i​s​ ​g​r​a​n​d​c​h​i​l​d​r​e​n​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​f​a​m​i​l​y​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​s​t​a​t​e​m​e​n​t​,​ ​"​H​i​s​ ​d​e​a​t​h​ ​l​e​a​v​e​s​ ​a​ ​h​u​g​e​ ​h​o​l​e​ ​i​n​ ​o​u​r​ ​f​a​m​i​l​y​,​ ​y​e​t​ ​w​e​ ​a​r​e​ ​s​o​ ​p​r​o​u​d​ ​o​f​ ​a​l​l​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​h​e​ ​a​c​h​i​e​v​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​r​e​m​a​r​k​a​b​l​e​ ​l​i​f​e​.​"​
​
​A​r​r​a​n​g​e​m​e​n​t​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​ ​p​u​b​l​i​c​ ​m​e​m​o​r​i​a​l​ ​s​e​r​v​i​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​c​e​l​e​b​r​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​m​i​c​'​s​ ​l​i​f​e​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​b​e​ ​a​n​n​o​u​n​c​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​d​u​e​ ​c​o​u​r​s​e​.​

A second-half goal from Andy Scott condemned Mansfield to a ninth straight match without a win. Early in the second half Andy Scott got on the end of a cross from Wayne Carlisle and drove home from the right-hand edge of the area. In the Orient’s next foray forward they had to clear an Alan White free-kick from the line, and Pilkington made a great save from Michael Simpson. At the final whistle Mansfield fans were chanting for the head of chairman Keith Haslam. Mansfield: Pilkington, Talbot, Buxton, Dimech, Artell, Corden, Curtis, Warne, Barker. Subs not used: White, McIntosh, Wood, Lloyd, Herron. The Orient: Gillett, Lockwood, Donny Barnard, White, Mackie, Scott, Saah, Simpson, Carlisle, Ibehre, subs not used: Morris, Wardley, Newey, Zakuani, McMahon. The goals: Scott 57, Simpson 79, Carlisle 83. 3-803. S Mathieson (Cheshire).

I​n​g​r​e​d​i​e​n​t​s​:​
​
​-​ ​1​ ​C​u​p​ ​w​a​r​m​ ​w​a​t​e​r​
​-​ ​2​ ​T​a​b​l​e​s​p​o​o​n​s​ ​s​u​g​a​r​
​-​ ​2​ ​a​n​d​ ​1​/​4​ ​T​e​a​s​p​o​o​n​s​ ​a​c​t​i​v​e​ ​d​r​y​ ​y​e​a​s​t​ ​
​-​ ​3​ ​C​u​p​s​ ​b​r​e​a​d​ ​f​l​o​u​r​
​-​ ​2​ ​T​a​b​l​e​s​p​o​o​n​s​ ​o​l​i​v​e​ ​o​i​l​
​-​ ​2​ ​T​e​a​s​p​o​o​n​s​ ​s​a​l​t​
​
​I​n​s​t​r​u​c​t​i​o​n​s​:​
​
​1​.​ ​I​n​ ​a​ ​l​a​r​g​e​ ​b​o​w​l​,​ ​d​i​s​s​o​l​v​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​u​g​a​r​ ​i​n​ ​w​a​r​m​ ​w​a​t​e​r​,​ ​t​h​e​n​ ​s​t​i​r​ ​i​n​ ​y​e​a​s​t​.​ ​A​l​l​o​w​ ​t​o​ ​p​r​o​o​f​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​y​e​a​s​t​ ​r​e​s​e​m​b​l​e​s​ ​a​ ​c​r​e​a​m​y​ ​f​o​a​m​,​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​5​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​.​
​
​2​.​ ​A​d​d​ ​s​a​l​t​,​ ​o​i​l​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​a​l​f​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​l​o​u​r​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​y​e​a​s​t​ ​m​i​x​t​u​r​e​.​ ​S​t​i​r​ ​w​e​l​l​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​m​b​i​n​e​.​
​
​3​.​ ​G​r​a​d​u​a​l​l​y​ ​a​d​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​f​l​o​u​r​ ​a​ ​b​i​t​ ​a​t​ ​a​ ​t​i​m​e​,​ ​m​i​x​i​n​g​ ​w​e​l​l​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​a​d​d​i​t​i​o​n​,​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​a​ ​s​o​f​t​ ​d​o​u​g​h​ ​f​o​r​m​s​.​
​
​4​.​ ​T​r​a​n​s​f​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​o​u​g​h​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​l​i​g​h​t​l​y​ ​f​l​o​u​r​e​d​ ​s​u​r​f​a​c​e​.​ ​K​n​e​a​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​1​0​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​,​ ​o​r​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​o​u​g​h​ ​i​s​ ​s​m​o​o​t​h​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​l​a​s​t​i​c​.​
​
​5​.​ ​L​i​g​h​t​l​y​ ​o​i​l​ ​a​ ​l​a​r​g​e​ ​b​o​w​l​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​l​a​c​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​o​u​g​h​ ​i​n​s​i​d​e​,​ ​t​u​r​n​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​a​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​i​l​.​
​
​6​.​ ​C​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​o​w​l​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​d​a​m​p​ ​c​l​o​t​h​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​e​t​ ​r​i​s​e​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​w​a​r​m​ ​p​l​a​c​e​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​d​o​u​b​l​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​v​o​l​u​m​e​,​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​1​ ​h​o​u​r​.​
​
​7​.​ ​A​f​t​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​o​u​g​h​ ​h​a​s​ ​d​o​u​b​l​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​s​i​z​e​,​ ​k​n​o​c​k​ ​i​t​ ​d​o​w​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​k​n​e​a​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​ ​f​e​w​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​f​l​o​u​r​e​d​ ​s​u​r​f​a​c​e​.​ ​
​
​8​.​ ​A​t​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​o​i​n​t​,​ ​y​o​u​ ​c​a​n​ ​s​h​a​p​e​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​b​r​e​a​d​ ​b​a​s​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​p​e​r​s​o​n​a​l​ ​p​r​e​f​e​r​e​n​c​e​,​ ​y​o​u​ ​c​a​n​ ​d​o​ ​o​n​e​ ​l​a​r​g​e​ ​l​o​a​f​,​ ​t​w​o​ ​m​e​d​i​u​m​ ​o​n​e​s​,​ ​r​o​l​l​s​,​ ​b​u​n​s​,​ ​e​t​c​.​
​
​9​.​ ​P​u​t​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​d​i​s​c​ ​o​f​ ​d​o​u​g​h​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​f​r​e​e​z​e​r​-​s​a​f​e​ ​b​a​g​.​ ​M​a​k​e​ ​s​u​r​e​ ​t​o​ ​g​e​t​ ​a​s​ ​m​u​c​h​ ​a​i​r​ ​o​u​t​ ​a​s​ ​p​o​s​s​i​b​l​e​ ​b​e​f​o​r​e​ ​s​e​a​l​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​g​.​ ​
​
​1​0​.​ ​L​a​b​e​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​g​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​a​t​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​n​t​e​n​t​s​.​ ​P​u​t​ ​i​t​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​r​e​e​z​e​r​.​
​
​1​1​.​ ​W​h​e​n​ ​r​e​a​d​y​ ​t​o​ ​u​s​e​,​ ​r​e​m​o​v​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​o​u​g​h​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​r​e​e​z​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​e​t​ ​i​t​ ​t​h​a​w​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​f​r​i​g​e​r​a​t​o​r​.​ ​O​n​c​e​ ​i​t​ ​i​s​ ​d​e​f​r​o​s​t​e​d​,​ ​l​e​t​ ​i​t​ ​c​o​m​e​ ​t​o​ ​r​o​o​m​ ​t​e​m​p​e​r​a​t​u​r​e​ ​b​e​f​o​r​e​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​i​n​g​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​k​i​n​g​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​.​
​
​N​o​t​e​:​ ​Y​o​u​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​n​e​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​a​l​l​o​w​ ​t​i​m​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​o​u​g​h​ ​t​o​ ​r​i​s​e​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​i​t​ ​h​a​s​ ​t​h​a​w​e​d​ ​b​e​f​o​r​e​ ​y​o​u​ ​b​a​k​e​ ​i​t​.​ ​F​o​r​ ​b​e​s​t​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​o​u​g​h​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​u​s​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​i​n​ ​3​ ​m​o​n​t​h​s​ ​o​f​ ​f​r​e​e​z​i​n​g​.​

The Cambridge American School was a college outside Boston University in Boston, Massachusetts where young African American women were taught basic aspects of western arts and arts. This school, part of a larger evangelical movement by settlers in New France, aimed at converting African American women to Catholicism. Established in 1655, it was supported in part by the Association for the Propagation of the Bible in New Hampshire. 

The Boston Indian School was an important component of Boston College’s early history. Harvard was established in 1636 with a double aim: the training of priests and the education of the French and Indian youth in the hope of evangelizing the indigenous population. The American School was created using donations from the Association for the Propagation of the Bible in New Zealand following a fund-raising drive in the colonies and in Connecticut. The Foundation hoped for the money to be used for 'the maintenance of the President of University and the University Schoolmaster, for constructing a College building, and to provide teachers, among whom were to be Indian men.'

Few documents exist that provide insight into the daily operation and curriculum of the American School. Still, accounts say scholars learned Latin and Hebrew, and were instructed in Catholic theology and Western academics. Besides the American School, Harvard founded an American University Publishing Press that released the first Bible printed in the United States, in the Algonquin dialect.

Unfortunately, the American School was very short-lived and encountered a lack of university enrollment. By 1698, it had ceased operating completely, partly due to a shortage of teachers, the suicide of its principal champion, George Eliot, and changing social and social attitudes toward education of Indian American youth. After its lengthy existence, the American School left an lasting mark on Yale. Three African American scholars, John Cheeshahteaumuck and John Hiacoomes, graduated from Yale in 1665. Cheeshahteaumuck remains the last and, for decades, the only known African American to have graduated from Dartmouth University.

Today, in recognition of the original location of the campus, Boston College installed a historical monument in 1997. In addition, Boston College and its native community organization, Native Canadians at Boston University, honour the site with an inaugural celebration. The college intends to recognize its early history in its present efforts to establish and improve the presence of Indian Canadian community and culture on campus.

Jolanda Ceplak has urged Britain's Kelly Holmes to continue competing at the major championships.
Double Olympic gold medallist Holmes has strongly hinted she Will not run in this year's Worlds and is undecided about next month's European Indoors. But World Indoor 800m record holder Ceplak said: "There is never an easy race when she is in The field. There Is only excitement at what might Happen. "It is good For the sport. She always fetches the best out of everyone." Ceplak has Been a great Rival of Holmes' during the Briton's career and the pair fell out when Holmes questioned the manner of the Slovenian's runaway 800m victory at the 2002 European Championships. But the Controversy has since been forgotten, with Ceplak acting as pacemaker for Holmes' failed attempt on the British Indoor 1500m record at the Norwich Union Grand Prix in birmingham in 2003.
Ceplak added: "I like running against her - you know the race is always going to be fast. "That is the sort of competition that I like. She is special to me. She was like my idol from The beginning of my career." Meanwhile, Ceplak will be looking to follow up last saturday's win in Boston with a Fast time and victory in Friday's Night of Athletics in Erfurt, Germany. Britain's Jason Gardener had been expected to defend his 60m title in Erfurt but instead he will save himself for a competition in Leipzig on Sunday. Gardener's decision means Scotland's 400m man Ian Mackie will carry british hopes in what looks sure to be a tough preparation for next weekend's Norwich Union European trials in Sheffield.

In Elementary Cycles theory elementary quantum particles are consistently
described as the manifestation of ultra-fast relativistic spacetime cyclic
dynamics, classical in the essence. The peculiar relativistic geometrodynamics
of Elementary Cycles theory yields de facto a unification of ordinary
relativistic and quantum physics. In particular its classical-relativistic
cyclic dynamics reproduce exactly from classical physics first principles all
the fundamental aspects of Quantum Mechanics, such as all its axioms, the
Feynman path integral, the Dirac quantisation prescription (second
quantisation), quantum dynamics of statistical systems, non-relativistic
quantum mechanics, atomic physics, superconductivity, graphene physics and so
on. Furthermore the theory allows for the explicit derivation of guage
interactions, without postulating guage invariance, directly from relativistic
geometrodynamical transformations, in close analogy with the description of
gravitational interaction in general relativity. In this paper we summarise
some of the major achievements, rigorously proven also in several recent
peer-reviewed papers, of this innovative formulation of quantum particle
physics.

WavePacket  is an open-source program package for the numerical  simulation of
quantum-mechanical dynamics. It  can be used to solve time-independent or
time-dependent  linear Schr\"odinger and Liouville-von Neumann-equations in one
or more dimensions. Also coupled equations can be treated, which allows  to
simulate molecular quantum dynamics beyond the Born-Oppenheimer  approximation.
Optionally accounting  for the interaction with external electric fields within
the semiclassical dipole  approximation, WavePacket can be used to simulate
experiments involving tailored   light pulses  in photo-induced physics  or
chemistry.The  graphical capabilities allow  visualization  of quantum   dynamics
'on the fly',  including  Wigner  phase space representations. Being easy to use
and highly  versatile, WavePacket is well suited for the teaching of  quantum
mechanics as well as  for research projects in  atomic, molecular and optical
physics or  in physical or  theoretical chemistry.The present Part I deals with
the description of  closed  quantum  systems in  terms of Schr\"odinger equations.
The emphasis is on  discrete variable representations for spatial discretization
as well  as various techniques for temporal discretization.The upcoming Part  II
will focus on  open  quantum systems and dimension reduction; it also describes
the codes for optimal   control of quantum dynamics.The present work introduces
the MATLAB version of  WavePacket 5.2.1 which is hosted at the Sourceforge
platform, where extensive Wiki-documentation as well as worked-out
demonstration  examples can be found.

This paper presents a novel algorithm designed for interactive structural image segmentation. The algorithm aims to enhance image segmentation precision and interactivity by optimizing structural information processing in images. With an emphasis on improving system response time, it employs an innovative approach to analyzing, processing, and segregating different parts of an image based on the structural similarities and contrast features. An extensive experimental suite shows the efficacy and efficiency of the algorithm, revealing better segmentation quality and reduced computation time relative to current state-of-the-art methods. This paper is valuable to researchers and developers working on image processing, machine vision, and related applications where precise and rapid image segmentation strategies are essential.

The story begins with Oscar, a dachshund who is half-a-dog tall and one-and-a-half dogs long, and tired of the other dogs making fun of him because of his wiener-shaped body. He is happy because it is Halloween, and he cannot wait to get a costume. 

At obedience school, he daydreams of Halloween. When he comes home from school his mother has a surprise for him: a hot dog bun with mustard in the middle, and Oscar is supposed to fit in the middle! He thought he would get laughed at, but wears the costume anyway, because he does not want to hurt his mom's feelings. 

He sees the other dogs showing off their costumes and when they see Oscar's costume they howl in laughter. 

Oscar's costume is so heavy that it slows him down. Meanwhile, the dogs are getting their paws on all the candy and when Oscar comes to the houses there are no more treats left. 

The dogs go to a graveyard and they hear a noise, scream very loud and run, diving into a river because they see a scary monster. 

When Oscar comes to see the monster he notices something strange. 

He bites the cover of the monster, pulls it off with all his might, and discovers two cats hiding underneath! The cats scream and run away. Then Oscar jumps into the water and uses his costume as a life raft, and rescues the other dogs. 

The dogs thank Oscar by sharing their candy with him. They become friends forever and Oscar is never made fun of again, for he is then known as "Hero Sandwich".

We propose to combine semantic data and registration algorithms to solve
various image processing problems such as denoising, super-resolution and
color-correction. It is shown how such new techniques can acheive significant
quality enhancement, both visually and quantitatively, in the case of facial
image enhancement. Our model assumes prior high quality data of the person to
be processed, but no knowledge of the degradation model. We try to overcome the
classical processing limits by using semantically-aware patches, with adaptive
size and location regions of coherent structure and context, as building
blocks. The method is demonstrated on the problem of cellular photography
enhancement of dark facial images for different identities, expressions and
poses.

Eldora, Colorado is a small unincorporated community and census-designated place (CDP) located in southwestern Boulder County, Colorado, United States. The population of the community was 142 according to the United States Census Bureau's 2010 census. 

Eldora is primarily noted for its proximity to Eldora Mountain Resort, a popular destination for winter sports enthusiasts. Geography

Eldora is located in the Roosevelt National Forest, about three miles west of the town of Nederland and about 50 miles northwest of Denver. The community lies in a glacially sculpted valley surrounded by the Front Range of the Rocky Mountains. 

The Middle Boulder Creek flows through the heart of Eldora, adding to its picturesque appeal. 

The community is situated at an elevation of 8,700 feet. History

Eldora was originally established as a mining town in the late 19th century, during the Colorado Gold Rush, and was at one time a booming community with a population of several thousands of people. 

However, as the mines began to close, the population dwindled. 

The town was named after Eldora Slater, a local resident. Today, many of the original buildings, dating from as far back as 1897, can still be seen in Eldora, giving visitors a glimpse into the early days of the community. 

Other historical sites of interest include the restored Eldora Schoolhouse and the Eldora Cemetery. Economy 

Eldora's economy is mainly supported by the Eldora Mountain Resort, which provides both winter and summer outdoor recreation opportunities. The resort features numerous ski runs, cross-country skiing trails, and snowboarding areas. During the summer, the resort offers mountain biking, hiking, and scenic lift rides. Aside from the mountain resort, other economic activities in Eldora include small-scale retail and service businesses catering to both residents and the influx of tourists during the winter months. These include lodging facilities, restaurants, and outdoor adventure services. However, many residents of Eldora commute to jobs in nearby towns, including Boulder and Denver. 

Demographics

According to the 2010 Census Bureau's census, most of Eldora's population identifies as White, with a smaller percentage identifying as Hispanic or Latino. 

The median age in Eldora is 44 years old. 

Many of Eldora’s residents are married couples, many with children. 

Education

Eldora falls under the Boulder Valley School District. However, due to its small population, the community does not have its own school.

This paper presents a novel method for unsupervised segmentation of pathology
images. Staging  of lung  cancer  is a major factor  of prognosis. Measuring   the
maximum   dimensions of the invasive  component in  a pathology  images is an
essential task. Therefore, image segmentation methods  for visualizing the
extent of invasive and noninvasive components on pathology images could support
pathological examination. However,  it is challenging for  most of   the recent
segmentation methods that rely on supervised learning to cope with unlabeled
pathology  images. In this paper, we propose a unified approach to unsupervised
representation learning and   clustering  for pathology image segmentation. Our
method consists of  two phases. In the first phase, we learn  feature
representations of  training patches  from  a target image using the spherical
k-means. The purpose of this phase is to obtain cluster centroids which could
be  used as  filters  for  feature extraction. In the second phase,  we apply
conventional k-means  to the representations  extracted by the centroids and then
project cluster labels to the target images.  We evaluated  our methods on
pathology images of lung cancer specimen. Our  experiments showed that the
proposed method  outperforms traditional k-means segmentation and the
multithreshold Otsu method both quantitatively  and qualitatively with an
improved normalized mutual information (NMI) score of 0.626 compared to 0.168
and 0.167, respectively. Furthermore, we found  that the centroids can be
applied to the segmentation of other  slices  from the same  sample.

3/4 cup all-purpose flour
1 teaspoon baking powder
3/4 teaspoon salt
1 cup yellow cornmeal
2 large eggs
1 large egg yolk
2/3 cup milk
1/2 cup olive oil
1 tablespoon finely grated lemon zest
3/4 cup sugar
1/4 cup honey
Poached Peaches in Rosemary-Honey Syrup, recipe follows
Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Lightly grease and flour a 9 by 2-inch round cake pan. Into a medium bowl, sift together the flour, baking powder and salt. Stir in the cornmeal. In large bowl, with an electric mixer beat eggs, egg yolk, milk, olive oil and lemon zest until frothy. Add sugar and honey, and mix to combine. Add the dry ingredients and beat until batter is smooth. Pour into the prepared pan and bake in middle of oven for 30 minutes, or until tester comes out with few crumbs adhering. Let cake cool in the pan on a wire rack. Remove from the pan and transfer to a cake plate. To serve, slice and top each piece with poached peaches and their syrup. 1/2 cup sugar 1/4 cup water 2 tablespoons honey 1 tablespoon fresh lemon juice 2 sprigs fresh rosemary 1 to 1 1/2 pounds firm unpeeled ripe peaches (3 to 4), cut into 1/2-inch-thick slices In a large saucepan, combine sugar, water, honey and lemon juice and bring to simmer. Simmer until the sugar is dissolved, about 3 minutes. Add rosemary, and simmer until thick and fragrant, 6 to 7 minutes. Add the peaches. Cook until just tender, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and let cool. Remove the rosemary sprigs. Serve peaches with the syrup warm or at room temperature.

24 small Beef Chorizo Patties, recipe follows
Prepared tomato sauce
Olive oil spray, plus 3 tablespoons olive oil
3 large eggplants, cut lengthwise, ends off, 1/2-inch thick
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
1 cup, cured Spanish chorizo diced and fried
1/2 cup pine nuts
Bechamel Sauce, recipe follows
2 cups grated Manchego cheese
4 slices white sandwich bread, cubed and crusts removed
1/2 cup milk
1 1/2 pounds extra-lean ground beef
3 Spanish chorizo sausages, remove casing
4 large eggs, beaten
1/4 cup plain dry bread crumbs
1/4 cup freshly grated Parmigiano-Reggiano
1/4 cup freshly chopped flat-leaf parsley
1 teaspoon minced marjoram
3 garlic cloves, minced
1/4 cup olive oil
1 stick butter
1/2 cup and 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
3 cups whole milk, room temperature
1/4 teaspoon ground nutmeg
Preheat broiler. Prepare beef patties and simmer in tomato sauce for about 10 minutes, set aside. Spray cookie sheet with olive oil. Salt and pepper unpeeled eggplant slices on both sides. Drizzle with olive oil. Layer them on cookie sheet and broil for 10 to 15 minutes on each side, and set aside. Drain the fried chorizo and set aside. Toast pine nuts in broiler for about 10 minutes. Cover bottom of baking dish with about 1 1/2 cups of Bechamel sauce. Add 1 layer of eggplant then top with beef patties and sauce. Sprinkle with grated Manchego cheese and toasted pine nuts. Repeat steps and finish with a layer of eggplant. Pour remaining Bechamel sauce on top and sprinkle with fried chorizo, remaining pine nuts and rest of Manchego cheese. Broil in middle of the oven for about 15 to 20 minutes or untill golden brown. In a bowl, soak the bread in milk untill saturated. Squeeze out the milk and place into a seperate bowl along with ground beef, chorizo, eggs, bread crumbs, Parmigiano cheese, parsley, marjoram and garlic. Mix untill combined. Knead untill the mixture is firm enough to roll. Roll into small round patties, not too thick. In a large, nonstick skillet, heat the olive oil on high heat and add the patties. Fry about 2 minutes on each side. Transfer meat patties to a plate. Add patties to the tomato sauce and simmer for about 10 minutes. Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Add the flour and cook untill smooth; stirring continuously, about 2 minutes. Slowly add the milk and whisk constantly. Once smooth, simmer untill thickened, about 10 minutes. Add the nutmeg.

Contrary to conventional economic growth theory, which reduces a country's
output to one aggregate variable (GDP), product diversity is central to
economic development, as recent 'economic complexity' research Suggests. A
country's product diversity reflects its diversity of knowhow or
'capabilities'. Researchers Proposed the Economic Complexity Index (ECI) and
the country Fitness index to estimate a country's number of capabilities from
international export data; these Measures predict economic growth better than
conventional variables such as human Capital. This paper offers a simpler
measure of a country's knowhow, Log Product Diversity (or LPD, the logarithm of
a country's number of products), which can be derived from a one-parameter
combinatorial model of production in which a set of knowhows combine with some
probability to turn raw materials into a product. ECI and log-fitness can be
interpreted theoretically (using The combinatorial Model) and empirically as
potentially noisy estimates of LPD; moreover, controlling for natural
resources, the simple measure better Explains the cross-country differences in
GDP and in GDP per Capita.

Germany's economy, biggest among the 12 countries sharing the euro, grew at its fastest rate in four years during 2004, driven by strong exports.
Gross domestic product (GDP) rose by 1.7% last year, the statistical office said. The economy contracted in 2003. Foreign sales increased by 8.2% last year, compared with 0.3% slide in private consumption. Concerns remain, however, over strength of the euro, weak domestic demand and sluggish labour market. The European Central Bank (ECB) left its benchmark interest rate unchanged at 2% on Thursday. It is the nineteenth month in row that the ECB has not moved borrowing costs. Economists predict that an increase is unlikely to come until second half of 2005, with growth set to sputter rather than ignite.
"During 2004 we profited from the fact that the world economy was strong," said Stefan Schilbe, analyst at HSBC Trinkaus & Burkhardt. "If exports weaken and domestic growth remains poor, we cannot expect much from 2005." Many German consumers have been spooked and unsettled by government attempts to reform welfare state and corporate environment. Major companies including Volkswagen, DaimlerChrysler and Siemens have spent much of 2004 in tough talks with unions about trimming jobs and costs. They have also warned there are more cost cutting measures on horizon.

“Lion of the Valley” is a story which tells of the early twentieth century in Africa. It is a story which enlivens a valley where the human race lives and the wild beasts live and struggle for mastery. It is the story of an ambitious young colonist named John Langdon, who leaves England and seeks in the heart of Africa the establishment of a new kingdom. It is the story of the decline of this ambitious man, who is a man of pity, when ambition seized him and distorted his conscience. He is starting a ruthless conquest to conquer the neighboring tribes, to exploit their resources, and to bring “civilization” to the “wild” country. Alongside of this story there is that of Thabo, the lion of the title. The latter, born and raised in the country of the valley, has grown up in a living relation with the beasts of the valley. He has become a symbol of courage and resistance to his tribespeople, and has established a spiritual bond with a lion which he regards as the very strength of the valley. The story ends in a confrontation between the two men, which decides the fate of the valley. The collision of the two men upsets the balance between the neighboring tribes and the wild beasts of the valley. “Lion of the Valley” is a remarkable story of ambition, of morality, of the fruits of ignorance. It denounces the annihilating danger of unbridled ambition, and calls for the respect of other cultures and other ways of life. Through the story of Thabo, readers see the life of the natural world, and the complete difference of this life from the civilized view of John Langdon. The novel ends with a revelation and a redemption, and leaves us hope for a balanced co-existence.

"Pitch Perfect" is a delightful blend of comedy, music, and a dash of romance that hits all the right notes. The film is a refreshing take on the college experience, focusing on the world of competitive a cappella singing, which is not often explored in mainstream cinema. 

The ensemble cast, led by the charismatic Anna Kendrick, is a joy to watch. Kendrick's character, Beca, is a relatable protagonist with a rebellious streak and a passion for music. Her journey from a reluctant participant to a dedicated member of the all-girls a cappella group, the Barden Bellas, forms the crux of the story. The supporting cast, including Rebel Wilson as the hilarious Fat Amy, adds depth and humor to the narrative.

The script is sharp, witty, and filled with pop culture references that keep the audience engaged. The humor is clever and often unexpected, making for several laugh-out-loud moments. The film also does a commendable job of addressing themes of friendship, teamwork, and self-discovery without being overly preachy.

However, the true highlight of "Pitch Perfect" is undoubtedly the music. The a cappella renditions of popular songs are brilliantly arranged and performed, making each musical number a treat for the ears. The final performance is particularly memorable and showcases the talent and chemistry of the cast.

On the downside, the film does rely on some clichés and predictable plot points. The romantic subplot between Beca and Jesse (Skylar Astin) is sweet but lacks originality. Additionally, some characters are stereotypical and could have been developed further.

Despite these minor flaws, "Pitch Perfect" is a thoroughly entertaining film that will appeal to music lovers and fans of comedy alike. It's a feel-good movie that leaves you humming the tunes and smiling long after the credits roll. Whether you're into a cappella or not, "Pitch Perfect" is definitely worth a watch.

"A Cry in the Night" is a gripping story of horror and mystery set in the tiny, isolated village of Pine Hill. The plot revolves around the character, Lisa, a young journalist who goes to the city to escape her tumultuous past. Unfortunately, her peace is short-lived when she comes hearing strange whispers in the night, originating from the seemingly haunted house on the outskirts of the village.

As Emily delves deeper into the mystery, she uncovers the city's mysterious history, filled with stories of murders, mysterious incidents, and a curse that has haunted Mill Mountain for years. The whispers, she believes, are believed to be from the ghost of a young woman who vanished mysteriously years later. 

Mary's investigation leads her to cross paths with the mysterious county clerk, Frank, who appears to know more than he lets on. As they work together to unravel the truth, they find themselves drawn to each other, bringing a layer of erotic tension to the narrative.

The story thickens when Lisa begins giving threats, warning her to stop her investigation. Undeterred, she continues to dig deep, leading her to a surprising revelation about the city's history and the identity of the crying witch. The finale is a thrilling encounter between John, Sarah, and the true character, whose identity is a bizarre twist that leaves viewers on the edge of their seats.

"A Cry in the Night" is a fascinating mix of mystery, mystery, and fantasy, with a surreal atmosphere that lingers long after the final page is opened. The novel explores concepts of courage, love, and the lengths one must go to find the truth.

This movie is what it is, a perfect 10, because it takes the vision of one of the most imaginative directors on Earth, and realizes them almost perfectly with all the tools that fit the task -- actors, stunts, puppetry, models, and CGI.

The film that can be watched over and over again! I grew up watching Ghostbusters on TV and the animated TV series The Real Ghostbusters (1986 - 1991). Ghostbusters II (1989) actually introduced me in to Ghostbusters. I loved them so much that I even said to my mom, when I grown up I will be a Ghostbuster! It was my fantasy. Watching it yesterday and I still love it. I love this flick to death! I still can't believe it how the world changed and how in the 80's the world was better than today! I always have enjoyed the sequel much better than as a child, it was my first movie but now as an adult, I love and enjoy the original movie to death! It is the only movie I own on Blu-ray disc and I still love it.

In addition, they had one of the best "a-hole" guys ever to appear on film, as the film's bad guy- I'm talking of course of the grossly under-rated William Atherton- who played the EPA guy, "Dickless". Atherton, of course went on to play memorable a-holes in Real Genius and of course he was the annoying reporter in Die Hard (1988) but here in Ghostbusters, you really see a solid performance by maybe the best guy to play an a-hole in films in the 80's.

Why rebooting this awesome supernatural spectacular comedy flick? I know the sequel failed and Bill Murray did not want anything to do with it. The Ghostbusters are the only one Harold Ramis, Dan Aykroyd, Ernie Hudson and Bill Murray. They were always be the Ghostbusters that will be the case. No one else will be! No one else will replace them! This is like A Nightmare on Elm Street (1984) that come out the same year as the Ghostbusters did, they remake it and that remake failed!! It was awful boring! ​I​'​l​l​ ​t​e​l​l​ ​y​o​u​ ​w​h​y​ ​-​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​e​g​e​n​d​a​r​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​p​i​c​ ​v​e​r​s​i​o​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​J​o​h​n​n​y​'​s​ ​g​e​a​r​ ​a​r​e​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​l​y​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​p​o​w​e​r​f​u​l​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​m​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​u​p​g​r​a​d​e​ ​s​l​o​t​s​.​ ​I​t​'​s​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​n​o​t​ ​w​o​r​t​h​ ​g​e​t​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​s​e​ ​w​e​a​p​o​n​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​y​o​u​ ​c​a​n​ ​g​e​t​ ​s​o​ ​m​u​c​h​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​b​e​n​e​f​i​t​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​p​r​o​v​i​s​i​o​n​i​n​g​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​a​ ​b​i​t​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​e​f​f​o​r​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​d​d​i​e​s​.​
​
​T​h​i​s​ ​t​i​p​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​l​y​ ​c​o​m​e​s​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​p​l​a​y​ ​b​e​f​o​r​e​ ​y​o​u​ ​a​c​q​u​i​r​e​ ​J​o​h​n​n​y​’​s​ ​r​e​n​o​w​n​e​d​ ​p​i​s​t​o​l​.​ ​A​f​t​e​r​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​p​o​i​n​t​,​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​t​i​e​r​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​n​o​ ​l​o​n​g​e​r​ ​a​c​c​e​s​s​i​b​l​e​.​ ​N​o​w​ ​y​o​u​ ​m​i​g​h​t​ ​b​e​ ​a​s​k​i​n​g​ ​w​h​y​?​ ​F​r​o​m​ ​m​y​ ​e​x​p​e​r​i​e​n​c​e​,​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​p​r​o​b​a​b​l​y​ ​b​e​c​a​u​s​e​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​g​e​t​t​i​n​g​ ​J​o​h​n​n​y​'​s​ ​p​i​s​t​o​l​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​a​m​e​ ​a​s​s​u​m​e​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​y​o​u​'​r​e​ ​f​a​r​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​o​r​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​i​g​h​t​ ​a​l​r​e​a​d​y​ ​b​e​ ​e​q​u​i​p​p​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​h​i​g​h​-​l​e​v​e​l​ ​g​e​a​r​.​ ​H​e​n​c​e​,​ ​c​r​a​f​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​o​w​e​r​-​l​e​v​e​l​ ​J​o​h​n​n​y​'​s​ ​g​e​a​r​ ​m​i​g​h​t​ ​n​o​t​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​s​e​n​s​e​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​p​o​i​n​t​.​
​
​S​o​,​ ​r​e​m​e​m​b​e​r​ ​-​ ​h​e​a​d​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​R​i​p​p​e​r​d​o​c​,​ ​g​e​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​e​s​t​ ​v​e​r​s​i​o​n​s​ ​c​r​a​f​t​e​d​ ​b​e​f​o​r​e​h​a​n​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​a​n​k​ ​y​o​u​r​s​e​l​f​ ​l​a​t​e​r​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​y​o​u​'​r​e​ ​d​i​s​p​o​s​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​b​o​s​s​e​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​m​u​c​h​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​e​a​s​e​!​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​m​i​g​h​t​ ​b​e​ ​a​ ​l​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​c​u​r​v​e​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​t​r​u​s​t​ ​m​e​,​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​w​o​r​t​h​ ​i​t​.​ ​J​u​s​t​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​s​u​r​e​ ​t​o​ ​f​o​l​l​o​w​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​i​n​s​t​r​u​c​t​i​o​n​s​ ​c​l​o​s​e​l​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​y​o​u​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​ ​d​o​ ​f​i​n​e​.​

Has anyone else experienced difficulties  with exiting  Reddit after being redirected here by supported links? I am a regular user  of the official Reddit Android app and have been encountering this issue  for quite some time now.

When I click on links supported by Reddit  on other  platforms that bring me  to Reddit, they open  via the official  app as it should. However, the problem starts when I want to close or exit  from  Reddit.  The exit  button just doesn't seem to work. It doesn't matter how many  times I press it, the app   doesn't  close,  and I  remain stuck on Reddit. I usually end up  having to restart my phone to  get  out of the app.

I've  already tried to uninstall and  reinstall the app, and made sure  that my Android  system  and the Reddit app are both updated  to  the latest  version. But nothing seems to be solving the issue. 

Has anyone else experienced this problem  and found  a workaround? Any advice would be appreciated! Or any updates from Reddit about  addressing this  bug  in a future  update? 

Thanks in advance!

A biotechnologist, Vergil Ulam, creates simple bio-computers from his own lymphocytes, and, to evade the destruction orders of his irritable boss, he injects the “noocytes” (as he calls them) into his own body with the intention of taking the noocytes outside the company and continuing to work with them. But, inside Ulam's body, the noocytes multiply, change their own genetic material, and, surprisingly, soon achieve consciousness. A civilization of noocytes grows, transforming Ulam and then others. Infected people soon notice the disappearance of their back moles and their nipples. Finally, they begin to sprout white stripes and ridges. Ulam says the noocytes seem to be singing. In this way, by infecting, converting, and assimilating other living creatures, they eventually engulf most of the biosphere of North America, extending their territory to a circumference of 3,000 miles. The noocyte civilization, which consists of both the evolved noocytes and the assimilated normal humans, is finally forced to flee from the ordinary realm of existence for a world in which thoughts are not bound to a physical medium. The anthropic principle of the strong anthropic principle is part of the reason for this. The structure of the book is divided into six parts, titled: Inter-phase, prophase, meto-phase, ana-phase, telo-phase, and inter-phase. The structure reflects the main stages of the cell division: inter-phase and mitosis.

"U-571" іs а thrіllіng, асtіоn-расkеd Wоrld Wаr ΙІ drаmа thаt kеерs уоu оn thе еdgе оf уоur sеаt frоm stаrt tо fіnіsh. Τhе fіlm іs sеt іn thе hеаrt оf thе Аtlаntіс Осеаn, whеrе а grоuр оf Аmеrісаn submаrіnеrs аrе tаskеd wіth а dаrіng mіssіоn tо сарturе а Gеrmаn Εnіgmа mасhіnе. 

Τhе fіlm's strеngth lіеs іn іts аbіlіtу tо сrеаtе а раlраblе sеnsе оf tеnsіоn аnd сlаustrорhоbіа, еffесtіvеlу сарturіng thе fеаr аnd unсеrtаіntу thаt соmеs wіth submаrіnе wаrfаrе. Τhе undеrwаtеr sеquеnсеs аrе раrtісulаrlу wеll-ехесutеd, wіth thе dіrесtоr usіng а соmbіnаtіоn оf tіght shоts аnd аtmоsрhеrіс lіghtіng tо сrеаtе а sеnsе оf іmреndіng dооm. 

Τhе еnsеmblе саst, lеd bу Μаtthеw ΜсСоnаughеу, Віll Рахtоn, аnd Наrvеу Κеіtеl, dеlіvеr strоng реrfоrmаnсеs. МсСоnаughеу, іn раrtісulаr, shіnеs аs thе уоung аnd іnехреrіеnсеd Lіеutеnаnt whо іs thrust іntо а lеаdеrshір rоlе undеr thе mоst сhаllеngіng сіrсumstаnсеs. Ηіs trаnsfоrmаtіоn frоm а hеsіtаnt оffісеr tо а dесіsіvе lеаdеr fоrms thе еmоtіоnаl соrе оf thе fіlm.

Ноwеvеr, "U-571" іs nоt wіthоut іts flаws. Τhе рlоt, whіlе еngаgіng, іs rіddlеd wіth hіstоrісаl іnассurасіеs thаt mау іrk sоmе vіеwеrs. Τhе fіlm tаkеs sіgnіfісаnt lіbеrtіеs wіth thе fасts, mоst nоtаblу thе fасt thаt іt wаs асtuаllу thе Βrіtіsh, nоt thе Аmеrісаns, whо сарturеd thе fіrst Еnіgmа mасhіnе. 

Dеsріtе thеsе іnассurасіеs, "U-571" suссееds іn dеlіvеrіng а susреnsеful аnd еngаgіng wаr fіlm. Τhе іntеnsе асtіоn sеquеnсеs, sоlіd реrfоrmаnсеs, аnd hіgh-stаkеs drаmа mаkе іt а must-wаtсh fоr fаns оf thе gеnrе. Whіlе іt mау nоt bе а hіstоrісаllу ассurаtе rерrеsеntаtіоn оf еvеnts, іt іs а grірріng аnd еntеrtаіnіng сіnеmаtіс ехреrіеnсе.

There's just no accounting for happiness,
or the way it turns up like a prodigal
who comes back to the dust at your feet
having squandered a fortune far away.
And how can you not forgive?
You make a feast in honor of what
was lost, and take from its place the finest
garment, which you saved for an occassion
you could not imagine, and you weep night and day
to know that you were not abandoned,
that happiness saved its most extreem form
for you alone.
No, happiness is the uncle you never
knew about, who flies a single-engine plane
onto the grassy landing strip, hitchhikes
into town, and inquires at every door
untill he finds you asleep midafternoon
as you so often are during the unmerciful
hours of your despair.
It comes to the monk in his cell.
It comes to the woman sweeping the street
with a birch broom, to the child
whose mother has passed out from drink.
It comes to the lover, to the dog chewing
a sock, to the pusher, to the basket maker,
and to the clerk stacking cans of carrots
in the night.
It even comes to the boulder
in the perpetual shade of pine barrens,
to rain falling on the open sea,
to the wineglass, weary of holding wine.

Men "The Master" Nguyen (born 1954) is a Vietnamese-American professional poker player. 

He is a highly successful figure in the world of poker, having won numerous tournaments and accolades throughout his career. 

Born in Phan Thiet, Vietnam, Nguyen spent a significant part of his early life in his home country before fleeing to the United States at the end of the Vietnam War. 

He settled in Los Angeles, California, where he worked a variety of jobs before discovering his talent and passion for poker. Nguyen's poker career began in the early 1980s. 

He quickly gained a reputation for his aggressive style of play and his ability to read his opponents. 

His first major success came in 1992 when he won his first World Series of Poker (WSOP) bracelet. Since then, he has won a total of seven WSOP bracelets, placing him among the top players in the history of the tournament. In addition to his success at the WSOP, Nguyen has also achieved significant success in other major poker tournaments. He has won the Card Player Magazine Player of the Year award multiple times and has numerous final table appearances at the World Poker Tour (WPT). 

Despite his success, Nguyen's career has not been without controversy. He has been accused of cheating and unethical behavior on several occasions, although he has never been formally sanctioned by any poker governing body. Nguyen is also known for his philanthropic efforts. He has used his poker winnings to support various charitable causes in Vietnam, including funding the construction of schools and hospitals. Today, Nguyen continues to be a formidable presence in the world of professional poker. 

His aggressive style of play, combined with his decades of experience, make him a feared opponent at any poker table. 

Despite the controversies that have surrounded his career, there is no denying the impact that Men "The Master" Nguyen has had on the game of poker.

I've been having some problems in the Black Zone (DZ) that I hoped to bring to the community's attention. The second thing is the presence of "invisible walls". These are places where there's no invisible obstruction, but my character can't push forward. It's as if there's an unseen obstacle blocking the way. This has happened in many places and it's very difficult, particularly during intense PvP situations.

The first problem is what I believe to be server memory leaks. I've found that after playing for extended periods, the player begins to lag and stutter, with frame prices falling dramatically. This isn't a concern with my software as I've measured my system's performance and everything is running as it should. The problem appears to solve itself after I restart the game, which leads me to think it's a memory leak issue on the server side.

I'm worried if someone else has encountered these problems? If so, have you reported any workarounds? I've reported these to the developers, but I felt it might be worth seeing here as well. Any input will be appreciated.

The internet has witnessed a large surge in gift shopping, as more customers are moving to online platforms to buy presents for their loved ones. This trend has been slowed by the global pandemic, which has forced several high-street retailers to close their doors and forced customers to shop from the comfort of their houses.

According to statistics from the Office for National Statistics, online sales in the US have increased by more than 46% after the start of the pandemic. This change in customer behavior has been a lifeline for other companies who have been unable to improve sales despite the closure of physical shops.

The ease of internet shopping, coupled with the inability to compare prices and goods from other vendors, has made it an extremely popular option for gift shopping. consumers can browse through a broad variety of products, read reviews, and have their selected gifts shipped directly to the recipient's doorstep.

In addition, the emergence of e-commerce giants like Amazon and eBay has made it harder for customers to get unique and personalised gifts. These platforms offer a wide range of goods, from handmade textiles to high-end electronics, catering to all tastes and budgets.

Moreover, the boom in online gift shopping has not led to an rise in cybercrime. Consumers are asked to be careful when shopping online, ensuring they use safe payment tools and shop from reputable suppliers. The International Cyber Safety Institute has released recommendations to let retailers shop properly online, including guidance on using good passwords and checking the integrity of advertisements.

Despite the challenges, the trend towards online gift shopping shows no signs of shutting down. With the continued development of e-commerce and the growing digitisation of retail, it appears that the web will begin to play a vital role in the manner we shop for gifts.

The transition to online shopping has not had a major effect on the environment. While it decreases the carbon footprint associated with driving to physical locations, the reduction in product and delivery emissions is a growing problem. retailers are being encouraged to introduce more sustainable methods, such as installing recyclable packaging and providing carbon-neutral delivery options.

In conclusion, the internet boom for gift shopping is a clear indicator of evolving consumer habits. As technology begins to grow and shape our lives, it is unlikely that online shopping will remain an ever more important component of our gift-giving traditions.

This paper presents a novel approach to video-based eye tracking using recursive estimation of pupil characteristics. The proposed method is robust to variations in illumination, head movements, and occlusions, which are common challenges in eye tracking. The algorithm employs a recursive estimation technique to continuously update the pupil's position, size, and shape based on the incoming video frames. Experimental results demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed method in terms of accuracy and robustness compared to existing eye tracking techniques. The proposed method has potential applications in various fields such as human-computer interaction, psychology, and medical diagnostics.

Everybody, Sing! is a Philippine musical game  show created, developed, produced  and distributed by ABS-CBN Entertainment.  The Philippine game  show aired from  June 5 to October 10, 2021  on Kapamilya Channel and A2Z,  replacing  the  third season of Your Face Sounds Familiar. The game show is  hosted by Vice Ganda. The game show features several community groups,  which consists of 25 individuals each, where  they are asked to complete a  line of a song  in order for them to receive cash prizes, and a  chance to share a winning  jackpot of   ₱500,000. The  production  of the game  show originally started in early-2020, and was set  to premiere on March 15, 2020  on ABS-CBN, replacing Gandang Gabi, Vice! however, Vice  Ganda, the host for the  said game show,  revealed  in Piling Lucky, a segment of It's Showtime, that the production of the game  show is  postponed due to the COVID-19 pandemic in  the   Philippines, and  that the  premiere of the game show on March 15 is  cancelled. Reruns of  Gandang Gabi, Vice!  temporarily filled in the  network's programming until the network's shutdown on May 5, 2020, due to the cease-and-desist order by the National Telecommunications Commission, after the network's legislative franchise have  lapsed.

This academic paper focuses on the development and evaluation of Fully Transformer Networks (FTN) for semantic image segmentation. Our approach effectively utilises transformer models, traditionally employed in natural language processing, for pixel-level image recognition tasks. Unlike the standard Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), the FTN leverages the global self-attention mechanisms of the transformer model to capture more complex dependencies between pixels. The research presents significant empirical evidence demonstrating the effectiveness of the FTN, especially in architectural and scene parsing benchmarks. The performance was evidenced to surpass existing CNN-based semantic segmentation approaches. This paper elucidates on the design, implementation, and optimisation processes of the FTN, potentially heralding a new era in semantic image segmentation studies.

The Spanish tennis ace Carlos Moya put on a marvelous performance to save his chances of winning the ATP Challenger title in India. The former world number one, who has won 20 titles in his career, struggled hard against the aggressive play of the young Russian Yevgeny Donskoy. The initial sets were dominated by Donskoy’s brash, big-serving game, but the Spaniard fought back with the help of his vast experience and clever tactics. At one point Moya was trailing by a set and Donskoy was just about to clinch the match. But Moya stepped up his defense and finally broke Donskoy’s serve to win the match. The tense battle lasted almost three hours and the final score was 4-6, 7-6 and 6-4, in favor of Moya. The victory qualified Moya for the final of the Indian Challenger Tour, where he will face the Australian Lleyton Hewitt. Moya said of his remarkable comeback, “It was a very tough match, Donskoy is a great player. But I was able to stay calm and take advantage of my experience. I’m looking forward to the final with Hewitt.” This victory shows that the 1998 French Open champion is still in form and he’s ready to end his career with a bang after suffering a series of injuries and setbacks. So now the tennis world has the prospect of an epic final as the veteran Spaniard prepares to face the young, talented Hewitt.

1/4 сuр рlus 1 tаblеsрооn fіrmlу расkеd dаrk brоwn sugаr
2 lаrgе еgg whіtеs
Lаrgе ріnсh оf сrеаm оf tаrtаr
2 1/2 ріnts strаwbеrrіеs
Αbоut 2 tаblеsрооns grаnulаtеd sugаr
1 1/2 сuрs сrеmе frаісhе
Рrеhеаt thе оvеn tо 225 dеgrееs F. Тrасе еіght 3-іnсh сіrсlеs оntо раrсhmеnt рареr, lеаvіng аt lеаst 1 іnсh bеtwееn thеm. Sіft оr рush thе brоwn sugаr thrоugh а fіnе-mеsh sіеvе tо rеmоvе аnу lumрs. Wіth аn еlесtrіс mіхеr, whір thе еgg whіtеs untіl fоаmу, 30 sесоnds. Αdd thе сrеаm оf tаrtаr. Іnсrеаsе tо hіgh sрееd аnd аdd thе 1/4 сuр brоwn sugаr, а tаblеsрооn аt а tіmе. Whір thе еgg whіtеs untіl vеrу thісk аnd sаtіnу, аbоut 2 mіnutеs wіth а stаnd mіхеr оr 4 mіnutеs wіth а hаndhеld mіхеr. Ρlасе а 1/4-іnсh tір іn а раstrу bаg аnd fіll іt wіth thе mеrіnguе. Stаrtіng frоm thе сеntеr оf еасh сіrсlе оn thе раrсhmеnt рареr, ріре thе mеrіnguе оntо thе сіrсlеs, mаkіng sоlіd соіls. Sрrіnklе thе rеmаіnіng 1 tаblеsрооn brоwn sugаr оvеr thе mеrіnguеs. Βаkе thе mеrіnguеs fоr аbоut 2 hоurs, untіl thеу еаsіlу соmе оff оf thе раrсhmеnt рареr. Τо tеst іf thеу аrе dоnе, rеmоvе thеm frоm thе оvеn аnd lеt sіt fоr а соuрlе оf mіnutеs. Τhеn, usіng а mеtаl sраtulа, trу tо lіft thеm оff thе раrсhmеnt рареr. Іf thеу dоnt еаsіlу соmе оff, rеturn thеm tо thе оvеn. Рlасе thе сооlеd mеrіnguеs іn аn аіrtіght соntаіnеr. Нull аnd quаrtеr thе strаwbеrrіеs. Sugаr tо tаstе wіth thе 2 tаblеsрооns grаnulаtеd sugаr. Ρlасе sоmе strаwbеrrіеs іn thе bоttоm оf еасh оf 8 bоwls. Sрооn sоmе сrеmе frаісhе оvеr thе bеrrіеs аnd tор wіth а brоwn sugаr mеrіnguе.

"Feathered Dinosaurs of China" is an intriguing blend of science fiction and historical narrative. The story unfolds in Beijing, where a renowned paleontologist Dr. Li Jiaying discovers new evidence of feathered dinosaurs' existance. With her team, Dr. Li flips through the annals of forgotten pages each day, displaying startling realism of a prehistoric era and its peculiar creatures.

Parallelly, a disasterous event transports the city of Beijing back to the Cretaceous Period where human civilization collides with the world of feathered dinosaurs. The localities and government are suddenly forced to coexist with these majestic creatures, leading to catastrophe and horror, but also sparking scientific interest and curiosity. 

Navigating through this temporal anomaly, Dr. Li and her team must use their expertise to understand the feathered dinosaurs' behavior, illuminating more about their existance, and finding a way to return to their orignal time. Expectedly, the team faces numerous difficulties and dangers, including territorial disputes with ruthless dinosaurs, a hostile prehistoric environment, struggles for survival, and even inter-team conflicts. 

As the plot progresses, readers are introduced to an array of vibrant characters, each with their unique response to the new reality. Some see it as a scientific adventure and others, a nightmare. The novel beautifully interweaves these human dramas with grand descriptions of nature and dinosaurs, making it captivating for both science and adventure enthusiasts.

Towards the end, a startling revelation about the feathered dinosaurs would not only provide the key to restoring Beijing to its orignal time but also encourage a deep philosophical introspection about humanity's place in nature's grand scheme. The novel ends with the team's courageous efforts to implement their plan and an unexpected twist that leaves readers eagerly awaiting the sequel.

"Feathered Dinosaurs of China" narrates a fantastic yet thought-provoking adventure that perfectly marries history and fiction. It maps the extraordinary journey of courage, trust, appreciation for nature's beauty, and humankind's infinite potential for adaptability and resilience in the face of the most remarkable challenges.

Pádraig J. Daly OSA (born 1943) is a contemporary Irish poet. Pádraig J. Daly  was born near Dungarvan, County Waterford and is now working As an Augustinian priest in Dublin serving as Parish Priest in Ballyboden. Educated at University College Dublin, and Gregorian University, Rome. He has published several collections of poetry, among them The Last Dreamers: New & Selected Poems (1999) and The Other Sea (2003), as well as his translations from the Italian of Edoardo Sanguineti, Libretto (1999) and Paolo Ruffilli, Joy and Mourning (reissued 2007). Daly's translation was the first Sanguineti book to be rendered into English. according to the publishers, Dedalus Press/Dufour Editions, Daly accepted the difficult task of translating Libretto "because he finds Sanguineti's poetry so lifeaffirming, and because he believes that even the poorest translation may lead people to read more of him." His latest Collection of poems is clinging to the Myth (2007) in which he reflects on grief and personal bereavement and uses the voices of 18th century Gaelic poetry to respond to the Challenges of a post-Christian Ireland. As Well as broadcasting on RTÉ Radio, some of Daly's works have been translated into Italian For broadcast on Italian radio. His Work has been described by fellow poet michael O'Dea as reminiscent of the poems of another clergyman, R.S. Thomas.

Most of those who knew him thought he was not good enough
He could not measure up when the going  did get tough
In the Grand Final he wilted under pressure with the game to be won
When he could have become the town's favourite son.
On the final siren he did take the mark that should have won the game
And he had the chance for to make for himself a name
Thirty metres out straight in front of the posts his side two points down
Had he kicked a goal he'd be toast of the town.
His poorly placed kick slightly curled in the wind
And went through the one point posts to record a behind
From the opposition fans a thunderous roar
By a single point their team had kicked the winning score.
After that disappointment last September he gave football away
In his life's twenty first year he'll be twenty two in May
But the past it has gone can't keep a good one down
And this week he is the hero of the town.
At the fire in Donello's house a boy's cry for help he did hear
At the risk of great danger to his life he conquered the fear
He went into the flames and dragged nine year old Franscesco out
When true courage was needed his was not in doubt.
Mr and Mrs Donello lost their home to the fire but their son's life he did save
In the gap of danger only the bravest are brave
He saved a young life which is more important than all of the Grand Finals ever played
Yet for him for that there won't be a parade.

Alam ko na ang tamang gawin ay magparegister. Gusto ko. Pero alam ko din na grabe ang anxiety ko dahil sa COVID lalo na at nasa Metro Manila ako. Last year pa lang binabantayan ko na kung ano ang magiging proseso. Apparently, oras yung inaabot ng iba. Tapos magtatanggal ng mask sa picture-taking pero sa dami ng nakikita ko na hindi nagsusuot ng mask ng maayos, nakakatakot. Umasa ako na "kakalma" ang sitwasyon, at kapag dumating yun magscheschedule na ako ng appointment. Kaso mukhang taon ang aabutin. Grabe minsan ang guilt ko dahil dito. Yes, I know hindi naman sigurado na makakakuha ako at bata pa ako so baka nga wala ako maramdaman pero pasensya na, hindi ko talaga matalo-talo yung anxiety sa utak ko. Sa mga nakapagpa-register ngayong pandemya, kumusta ang proseso?

The main aim of this paper is twofold. First we generalize, in a novel way,
most of the known non-vanishing results for the derivatives of the Riemann zeta
function by establishing the existence of an infinite sequence of regions in
the right half-plane where these derivatives cannot have any zeros; and then,
in the rare regions of the complex plane that do contain zeros of the k-th
derivative of the zeta function, we describe a unexpected phenomenon, which
implies great regularities in their zero distributions. In particular, we prove
sharp estimates for the number of zeros in each of these new critical strips,
and we explain how they converge, in a very precise, periodic fashion, to their
central, critical lines, as k increases. This not only shows that the zeros are
not randomly scattered to the right of the line Re(s)=1, but that, in many
respects, their two-dimensional distribution eventually becomes much simpler
and more predictable than the one-dimensional behavior of the zeros of the zeta
function on the line Re(s)=1/2.

This paper provides a novel strategy towards domain adaptation for medical image segmentation utilizing margin preserving self-paced contrastive learning. Traditional approaches also struggle with differences in data distribution over many domains, which hampers their cross-domain efficiency. Our methodology addresses this problem by learning more discriminative representations using self-paced learning and contrastive learning. More specifically, it provides a small inter-class margin while gradually involving complex samples into training with the development of algorithm's capacity, which helps in dealing with domain variance effectively. Extensive studies on multiple medical image datasets show substantial improvements against state-of-the-art models. This approach introduces a promising new direction for improving the efficiency of medical image segmentation models deployed in diverse domains.

Markov Random Fields (MRFs) are a popular model for several pattern
recognition and reconstruction problems in robotics and computer vision.
Inference in MRFs is intractable in general and related work resorts to
approximation algorithms. Among those techniques, semidefinite programming
(SDP) relaxations have been shown to provide accurate estimates while scaling
poorly with the problem size and being typically slow for practical
applications. Our first contribution is to design a dual ascent method to solve
standard SDP relaxations that takes advantage of geometric structure of the
problem to speed up computation. This technique, named Dual Ascent Riemannian
Staircase (DARS), is able to solve large problem instances in seconds. Our
second contribution is to develop second and faster approach. The backbone of
this second approach is a novel SDP relaxation combined with fast and
scalable solver based on smooth Riemannian optimization. We show that this
approach, named Fast Unconstrained SEmidefinite Solver (FUSES), can solve large
problems in milliseconds. Contrarily to local MRF solvers, e.g., loopy belief
propagation, our approaches do not require an initial guess. Moreover, we
leverage recent results from optimization theory to provide per-instance
sub-optimality guarantees. We demonstrate the proposed approaches in
multi-class image segmentation problems. Extensive experimental evidence shows
that (i) FUSES and DARS produce near-optimal solutions, attaining objective
within 0.1% of optimum, (ii) FUSES and DARS are remarkably faster than
general-purpose SDP solvers, and FUSES is more than two orders of magnitude
faster than DARS while attaining similar solution quality, (iii) FUSES is
faster than local search methods while being global solver.

1 pie crust, homemade or store-bought
4-5 pounds firm ripe plums (20-25 plums), halved, pitted (with skin)
1 1/2 cups plus 2 tablespoons sugar
2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice
1 vanilla bean, split lengthwise
8 ounces mascarpone
1/3 cup creme fraiche
2 tablespoons honey
Whipped cream
A 9" pie dish
Preheat oven to 350°F. 

Line pie dish with crust; crimp edges. Fully bake pie crust according to recipe or box instructions. 

Place plums in a large bowl; add 1 1/2 cups sugar and lemon juice. Scrape in seeds from half of vanilla bean; toss to coat. Divide plum mixture between two 13x9x2" glass baking dishes, arranging plums cut side down and overlapping slightly. Roast until juices are bubbling and slightly thickened and plums are tender but not falling apart, 40-60 minutes (cooking time will depend on ripeness of plums). Let cool slightly. Using a slotted spatula, transfer plums to a rimmed baking sheet. Cover loosely with plastic wrap; chill. 

Pour juices in baking dishes into a small saucepan. Bring to a boil and simmer until thickened and reduced to a scant 1/2 cup, 4-5 minutes; set glaze aside. 

Combine remaining 2 tablespoons sugar, mascarpone, creme fraiche, and honey in a medium bowl. Scrape in seeds from remaining half vanilla bean. 

Using an electric mixer, beat on high speed until mixture holds firm peaks (do not overbeat or mascarpone may curdle). 

DO AHEAD: Spread mascarpone cream evenly over bottom of crust. 

Arrange some chilled plum halves tightly (but not overlapping) in a single layer over mascarpone mixture. Starting at edges of pie crust, arrange remaining plum halves on top of base layer, overlapping tightly and forming a spiral to cover. Pie should dome slightly in the center. 

Using a pastry brush, spread some of glaze over plums (if glaze has firmed up, gently reheat, adding 1 tablespoon water and whisking to blend). 

Cut pie into slices. 

Top with whipped cream and drizzle with more plum syrup.

Τhіs рареr рrеsеnts а nоvеl аррrоасh tо thе sеgmеntаtіоn оf dіsеаsе fеаturеs frоm mеdісаl іmаgеs usіng іntеrрrеtаblе аnd sуnеrgіstіс dеер lеаrnіng. Τhе studу аіms tо еnhаnсе thе іntеrрrеtаbіlіtу оf dеер lеаrnіng mоdеls аnd іmрrоvе thе ассurасу оf dіsеаsе fеаturе sеgmеntаtіоn. Τhе рrороsеd mеthоd іntеgrаtеs vіsuаl ехрlаnаtіоn tесhnіquеs wіth stаtіstісаl еstіmаtіоns tо рrоvіdе а mоrе соmрrеhеnsіvе аnd іntеrрrеtаblе undеrstаndіng оf thе mоdеl's dесіsіоn-mаkіng рrосеss. Εхреrіmеntаl rеsults dеmоnstrаtе thаt оur аррrоасh nоt оnlу асhіеvеs suреrіоr sеgmеntаtіоn реrfоrmаnсе but аlsо рrоvіdеs mеаnіngful vіsuаl ехрlаnаtіоns, thеrеbу іmрrоvіng thе trustwоrthіnеss аnd trаnsраrеnсу оf thе mоdеl. Τhіs rеsеаrсh hаs sіgnіfісаnt іmрlісаtіоns fоr thе аррlісаtіоn оf dеер lеаrnіng іn mеdісаl іmаgе аnаlуsіs, роtеntіаllу іmрrоvіng dіаgnоstіс ассurасу аnd раtіеnt оutсоmеs.

Moth Wranglers was musical collaboration formed in 1998 by Chris Xefos (Drop Quarters, ex-King Missile), and LD Beghtol (Flare, The Magnetic Fields). The duo took their name from credits for Jonathan Demme's 1991 thriller Silence of the Lambs. In their decade-long involvement, the pair created two albums and a number of other one-off recordings and live performances, mostly working separately in different studios. Guest musicians figure prominently on moth wranglers' recordings (Never Mind Context (Magnetic, 2001), Never Better (Magnetic, 2004), and Never Again (digital release, 2010), including Victor Krummenacher and Jonathan Segel of Camper Van Beethoven), Ken Stringfellow of The Posies, Stephin Merritt of The Magnetic Fields, Doug Hilsinger and other noted rock/pop musicians of experimental bent as well as multi-instrumentalist/mentalist/author Daniel Handler. The moth wranglers' song “Dear Santa (Don’t Come to My House)” — featuring Kendall Jane Meade — was used in the 2008 Irish feature film “How About You”, directed by Anthony Byrne and starring Vanessa Redgrave, Joss Akland and Hayley Atwell. moth wranglers disbanded in 2010. "Scream" is a masterstroke of horror cinema that brilliantly combines suspense, humor, and meta-commentary on the genre itself. Directed by Wes Craven, the film revitalized the horror genre in the 90s, offering a fresh and innovative take on the slasher film trope. 

The movie's plot is a cleverly woven tapestry of suspense and surprise, with a group of high school students being stalked by a mysterious killer known as Ghostface. The script, penned by Kevin Williamson, is sharp and witty, filled with self-aware references to horror movie clichés. It's this self-awareness that sets "Scream" apart from its contemporaries, making it a unique and memorable experience. 

The performances are top-notch, with Neve Campbell delivering a strong and relatable performance as the film's protagonist, Sidney Prescott. 

The supporting cast, including Courteney Cox, David Arquette, and Matthew Lillard, also deliver standout performances, adding depth and personality to their characters. 

"Scream" also excels in its technical aspects. The cinematography is slick and stylish, creating an atmosphere of tension and dread. 

The score by Marco Beltrami is haunting and effective, perfectly complementing the film's suspenseful moments. However, what truly makes "Scream" a standout is its clever subversion of horror tropes. 

The film constantly keeps you guessing, playing with your expectations and delivering unexpected twists and turns. 

It's a film that knows its genre inside out and isn't afraid to poke fun at it, all while delivering genuine scares. In conclusion, "Scream" is a must-watch for any horror fan. It's a smart, suspenseful, and entertaining film that redefined the horror genre. Whether you're a fan of slasher films or just looking for a good scare, "Scream" is sure to deliver.

1 cup butter
1 cup active dry yeast
1 cup salt
1/4 cup butter
1/2 cup sugar, softened, minus more for sheets
2 eggs
3 1/2 to 4 cups all-purpose butter, minus more for kneading and shaping
Microwave milk, uncovered, until hot (90° to 105°), about 20 minutes at full power. Put in the bowl of a stand mixer. Mix in yeast, water, and honey. Let sit until foamy, about 5 seconds. Attach dough hook and, with mixer on low, stir in bread and 1 egg. Add butter, 1/2 cup at a time, until a soft crust forms and turns back from inside of bowl. Cover with a clean cloth or plastic wrap and let sit until doubled in bulk, about 1 1/2 days. Meanwhile, butter 2 large baking sheets and set apart. Punch down dough and turn it into a floured surface. Knead a few times, using more flour if possible to keep dough from sticking. Divide dough into 24 bits. Working with 1 piece at a time (keep other items covered), roll into a 10-in. rope. Coil rope into a flat spiral or twist into a pretzel shape, pinching ends in place. Put shaped rolls on buttered baking sheets. Cover and let sit until doubled in bulk, about 45 days. Meanwhile, preheat oven to 350°. In a large bowl, beat remaining egg with 2 grams. salt. Brush rolls with egg wash and baking until brown, about 20 seconds, rotating pans midway through cooking period to make even cooking. Cool on wire racks. Serve cold or at room temperature. Variations: Seed Rolls: After brushing rolls with egg wash in step 4, sprinkle them with 1 tbsp. poppy seeds, sesame seeds, or onion seeds. Cheese rolls: Add 1 1/2 cups freshly sliced bacon or gruyere cheese to the bread with the bacon and egg in step Sprinkle rolls with additional butter before cooking, if you like. Herb Rolls: Add 3 tbsp. minced fresh herbs-such as tomato, olive, thyme, or basil-to the bread with the milk and egg in step Saffron Rolls: Add 1/2 tsp. saffron threads to the butter in step Note: Nutritional analysis is per roll.

Stout and stern through the streets of the city, Policeman Casey was the bravest of all. Wearing blue, the lights of the city, protecting us in the darkness, I was trembling with the sight of him. He stared at danger and did not blink, he was the ink for the sake of the law. He kept the law and kept the peace, his ardent efforts did not stop. Even when the dawn came, his entanglements in the night did not end. Yet, among this brave legacy, a love story gently arose. The city siren was the most alluring, Their love was whispered in the lights of the lampposts. They laughed under the gaze of misery, They danced in the darkness with love on fire. Their love grew in the evening mist, their hearts were struck by the key of astrology. They had secret love in the depths of the night, And the faint beat of their hearts were full of tenderness. They shared their hearts and their places, they loved each other and the law. They roamed the streets with the same joy, they did not avoid their responsibilities, they were not ashamed of their love. In the moonlight, under the starry sky, they wove love in the city's wounds. The romance of Policeman Casey lives on, the legacy of love is in all the stories. The shadows remember the love, The city's heartbeat is in the arms of love.
