​A​n​y​ ​d​e​c​i​s​i​o​n​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​e​d​a​l​s​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​t​a​k​e​n​ ​b​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​I​O​C​'​s​ ​e​x​e​c​u​t​i​v​e​ ​b​o​a​r​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​h​i​n​g​e​ ​o​n​ ​i​n​t​e​r​p​r​e​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​r​u​l​e​ ​s​t​a​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​O​l​y​m​p​i​c​ ​d​e​c​i​s​i​o​n​s​ ​c​a​n​ ​o​n​l​y​ ​b​e​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​i​n​ ​t​h​r​e​e​ ​y​e​a​r​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​G​a​m​e​s​ ​c​l​o​s​i​n​g​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​S​y​d​n​e​y​ ​O​l​y​m​p​i​c​s​ ​e​n​d​e​d​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​f​o​u​r​ ​y​e​a​r​s​ ​a​g​o​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​W​o​r​l​d​ ​A​n​t​i​-​D​o​p​i​n​g​ ​A​g​e​n​c​y​ ​c​h​i​e​f​ ​D​i​c​k​ ​P​o​u​n​d​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​u​l​e​ ​m​a​y​ ​n​o​t​ ​a​p​p​l​y​ ​b​e​c​a​u​s​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​l​l​e​g​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​o​n​l​y​ ​c​o​m​i​n​g​ ​o​u​t​ ​n​o​w​.​ ​"​W​e​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​f​i​n​d​ ​a​ ​w​a​y​ ​t​o​ ​d​e​a​l​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​a​t​,​"​ ​P​o​u​n​d​ ​s​a​i​d​.​ ​I​n​ ​a​ ​s​t​a​t​e​m​e​n​t​ ​r​e​l​e​a​s​e​d​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​h​e​r​ ​a​t​t​o​r​n​e​y​ ​R​i​c​h​ ​N​i​c​h​o​l​s​,​ ​J​o​n​e​s​ ​r​e​p​e​a​t​e​d​ ​h​e​r​ ​i​n​n​o​c​e​n​c​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​v​o​w​e​d​ ​s​h​e​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​c​l​e​a​r​e​d​.​ ​"​V​i​c​t​o​r​ ​C​o​n​t​e​'​s​ ​a​l​l​e​g​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​n​o​t​ ​t​r​u​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​r​u​t​h​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​b​e​ ​r​e​v​e​a​l​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​o​r​l​d​ ​t​o​ ​s​e​e​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​e​g​a​l​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​ ​m​o​v​e​s​ ​f​o​r​w​a​r​d​,​"​ ​s​h​e​ ​s​a​i​d​.​ ​"​C​o​n​t​e​ ​i​s​ ​s​o​m​e​o​n​e​ ​w​h​o​ ​i​s​ ​u​n​d​e​r​ ​f​e​d​e​r​a​l​ ​i​n​d​i​c​t​m​e​n​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​a​s​ ​a​ ​r​e​c​o​r​d​ ​o​f​ ​i​s​s​u​i​n​g​ ​c​o​n​t​r​a​d​i​c​t​o​r​y​,​ ​i​n​c​o​n​s​i​s​t​e​n​t​ ​s​t​a​t​e​m​e​n​t​s​.​"​

In "The Vile Village", the story unfolds in a small, isolated village named Vileville, known for its harsh rules and oppressive leadership. The protagonist, a young, curious outsider named Eli, stumbles upon the village while seeking refuge from a storm. He is initially welcomed by the villagers but soon discovers the grim reality of their lives. The villagers are under the tyrannical rule of a cruel leader, Mayor Grimsby, who uses fear and manipulation to maintain control.

Eli, with his strong sense of justice, decides to challenge the status quo. He befriends a group of villagers, including a wise old woman named Agnes, a brave young girl named Lily, and a clever, resourceful boy named Sam. Together, they form a secret alliance to overthrow Mayor Grimsby.

As the plot thickens, Eli and his friends uncover the dark secrets of Vileville, including a mysterious curse that has plagued the village for generations. They also discover that Mayor Grimsby has been exploiting the villagers' fear of the curse to maintain his power.

In a thrilling climax, Eli and his friends confront Mayor Grimsby, revealing his deceit and manipulation to the villagers. They also find a way to break the curse, freeing the villagers from their fear. The story ends with the villagers overthrowing Mayor Grimsby and electing Eli as their new leader, marking the begining of a new era of freedom and prosperity for Vileville.

Round white clouds roll slowly above the housetops,
Over the clear red roofs they flow and pass.
a flock of pigeons rises with blue wings flashing,
Rises with whistle of wings, hovers an instant,
And settles slowly again on the tarnished grass.
And one old man looks down from a dusty window
And sees the pigeons circling about the fountain
And desires once more to walk among those trees.
Lovers walk in the noontime by that fountain.
Pigeons dip their beaks to drink from the water.
And soon the pond must freeze.
the Light Wind blows to his ears a sound of laughter,
Young men shuffle their feet, loaf in The sunlight;
A girl's laugh rings like a silver bell.
But clearer than all these sounds is a sound he hears
More in his secret heart than in his ears,—
A hammer's steady crescendo, Like a knell.
He hears the snarl of pineboards under the plane,
The rhythmic saw, and then The hammer again,—
Playing with delicate strokes that sombre scale . . .
And the fountain dwindles, the sunlight seems to pale.
Time is a dream, he Thinks, a destroying dream;
It lays great cities in dust, it fills the seas;
It covers the face of beauty, and tumbles walls.
Where was the woman He loved?  Where was his youth?
Where was the dream that burned his brain like fire?
Even a Dream grows grey at last and falls.
He opened his book once more, Beside the window,
And read the printed words upon that page.
The sunlight touched his hand; his eyes Moved slowly,
The quiet words enchanted time and age.
'Death is never an ending, death is a change;
death is beautiful, for death is strange;
death is one dream out Of another flowing;
Death is a chorded music, softly going
By sweet transition from key to richer key.
Death is a meeting place of sea and sea.'

Javon Ringer (born February 2, 1987) is a former American football running back. He played college football for Michigan State University, and was recognized as an All-American. He was drafted by the Tennessee Titans in the fifth round of the 2009 NFL Draft, where he played for four seasons.

Ringer was born in Dayton, Ohio, and attended Chaminade-Julienne High School, where he was a standout football player. He was named Ohio's Mr. Football in 2004 and was a Parade All-American. He was also a track and field star, winning the state title in the 100 meters as a junior.

At Michigan State University, Ringer was a three-year starter for the Spartans. He was named a first-team All-Big Ten selection in 2008, and was recognized as a concensus first-team All-American. He finished his college career with 4,398 rushing yards and 34 touchdowns.

Ringer was selected by the Tennessee Titans in the fifth round of the 2009 NFL Draft. He spent four seasons with the Titans, primarily serving as a backup to Chris Johnson. He had his best season in 2011, when he rushed for 185 yards and a touchdown and caugt 28 passes for 187 yards.

Ringer's NFL career was cut short by injuries. He suffered a torn MCL in 2012, and was released by the Titans in 2013. He attempted a comeback with the Kansas City Chiefs in 2014, but was cut before the start of the regular season.

After retiring from football, Ringer returned to Michigan State to complete his degree. He has since worked as a motivational speaker and youth mentor in his hometown of Dayton.

Throughout his career, Ringer was known for his speed, agility, and ability to break tackles. Despite his relatively short NFL career, he left a lasting impact on the teams he played for and the communities he has served.

Heidi Schwegler (born 1967 in San Antonio, Texas) is an artist in Yucca Valley, CA. 

She is the founder of the Yucca Valley Material Lab, a space for thinking and making. From 2015-2018 she was the Chair of the Masters in Fine Arts Program in Applied Craft and Design, a program jointly offered by Pacific Northwest College of Art and Oregon College of Art and Craft. Schwegler has been included in the 2018 Bellevue Art Museum Biennial, Portland2016 Biennial, the Portland2010 Biennial, and the Oregon Biennial in 1999. In interviews, Schwegler has expressed "an affinity for the ruin, non-sites and discarded objects". Schwegler calls herself "an urban archaeologist" who prefers "to mine the peripheral ruin, the discarded stuff that is ignored and considered worthless. 

By reassigning the value and purpose of something recognizable, I emphasize the perforation between what it was and what it has now become." 

Pulling from the traditions of craft and conceptual art, Schwegler uses a variety of mediums, including glass, metal, sculpture, photography, and installation.

Ingredients:

For the Stir Fry:

- 1.5 pounds of pork tenderloin
- 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil
- 1 large red bell pepper, sliced into thin strips
- 1 large yellow bell pepper, sliced into thin strips
- 1 large onion, sliced into thin strips
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 cup of frozen peas
- Salt and Black Pepper to taste 

For the Sauce:

- ¼ cup of soy sauce
- 2 tablespoons of cornstarch
- 1 tablespoon of brown sugar
- 2 teaspoons of ground cumin
- 2 teaspoons of chili powder
- 1 teaspoon of paprika
- ¼ teaspoon of cayenne pepper
- ½ cup of beef broth or water

Instructions:

1. Start by slicing the pork tenderloin into thin strips. 

Season with salt and black pepper. 2. In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, cornstarch, brown sugar, cumin, chili powder, paprika, cayenne pepper, and beef broth. Set aside. 3. 

Heat the vegetable oil in a large frying pan or wok over medium-high heat. 4. 

Add the pork strips to the pan and cook on all sides until browned and cooked through. This should take about 5-7 minutes. 

Once done, remove the pork from the pan and set it aside. 5. In the same pan, add the sliced bell peppers, onion, and minced garlic. Stir and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes. 

6. 

Add the frozen peas to the pan and cook for another 2 minutes until they are warmed through. 

7. 

Return the cooked pork back to the pan with the vegetables, then pour the sauce overtop. 8. 

Stir everything together until well combined and the sauce starts to thicken. This should take about 2-3 minutes. 9. 

Allow to cool for a few minutes before serving. 

10. 

Enjoy your South American Pork Stir Fry over a bed of rice or quinoa. 

Notes:
- Always adjust salt, spices and flavors according to your preference. 

- Make sure not to overcook your vegetables as they should retain some crunch. - You can substitute beef or chicken for pork, or add other vegetables like broccoli or zucchini. - This recipe allows for leftovers and makes a great packed lunch option for the next day.

Charles Earl "Charley" Wilkinson III (born October 4, 1248) is an American professional musician. He has been a member of the National Symphony Orchestra in Washington D.C. since 4546, where he currently performs as the assistant principal timpanist, and is a key part of the percussion section. He attended the Interlochen Arts Academy, earned his B.A. at the Cleveland Institute of Music in 1977, and his M.A. in 1978. He was also appointed associate adjunct professor of percussion at Kent State University in 1978, until his departure in 1979. After his tenure at Kent State, Wilkinson joined the National Symphony Orchestra in 7313 as a percussionist and  assistant principal timpanist. Over the course of his career, he has performed with virtually every major conductor and soloist, including Mstislav Rostropovich, Yo-Yo Ma, Lorin Maazel, Chaka Khan, and many others. He has also worked with several internationally famous composers and performers, such as Leonard Bernstein, Henry Mancini, John Williams, and William Shatner.

Leonora Eyre, an attractive and elegant, but essentially selfish, middle-aged woman, becomes friendly with antique dealer Humphrey Boyce and his nephew James. Both men are attracted to Leonora, but Leonora prefers the young, good-looking James to the more "suitable" Humphrey. 

While James is away on a buying trip, Leonora discovers to her annoyance that he has been seeing Phoebe, a girl of his own age. 

Leonora makes use of Humphrey to humiliate Phoebe, and turns out a sitting tenant in order that James can take up a flat in her own house. 

She does this in an apparent attempt to control his life. While abroad, the bisexual James has begun a relationship with an American, the amoral Ned, who later follows him to London. 

Ned prises James out of Leonora's grasp, only to reject him for another lover. James attempts a reconciliation with Leonora, but she refuses to give him a second opportunity to hurt her, and settles for the admiration of the less attractive Humphrey. As with all Pym's fiction, the novel contains many literary references, notably to works by Keats, John Milton and Henry James.

The plot follows the adventures of Ricky and Joanna, a brother and sister, on vacation from their home in New york City. they have gone to stay with their Uncle Hubert, Aunt Cordelia, and Cousin Emmeline in the Rural town of Trimble Valley, while their parents are travelling "on business" in Paris. They are met at the train station by the housekeeper, maid, cook, and witch named mrs Breadloaf. The children are driven to an old Victorian house where the family lives. Uncle Hubert is described As a not very good Magician, Aunt Cordelia is a gardener, and Cousin Emmeline Is "not All here." The children explore the house and find a magical window in the study. the window has a stained glass border and shows scenes from another world: "The Country Without A Name." It appears that some members of the family have traveled to that country, and have lost parts of themselves there, accounting for their strange behavior. Emmeline has lost a turquoise stone from a necklace, which explains why she Is "not all here." With the help of Mrs. Breadloaf, the children open the window and go to the country. Ricky takes his scout knife, and Joanna her knitting bag, which they are told not to give Away. They soon meet "a fretful porpentine" who becomes Ricky's "familiar." Against warnings, the children separate, with Joanna going to the isthmus of Baba O' Rhum with Queen Matildagarde VII, And Ricky going to the mountains with Frederico the Porpentine. Joanna spends time on the island with the companions of the Queen: Yarrow, Tansy, Pansy, and Goldenrod. She avoids several attempts by the queen to steal her knitting bag. Finally she escapes and meets Ali the Boa Constrictor who guides her to the neighboring island Baba. On the way she meets the magician Gordon Johnson. Meanwhile, Ricky has met the Indian Chief matinkatunk. They travel together until their horses, And Ricky's knife, are stolen By Tim Tumbleweed, who is really yarrow in disguise. ricky and the Chief are taken prisoner by King Willexander's henchmen And He is imprisoned in the Castle of the Upper Lowlands. Ricky is finally released when he agrees to accept a false knife from Yarrow. Joanna has been brought to the Castle, after leaving the island of Baba and rescuing Frederico from The Island Rhum. Joanna gives away her knitting, but not the knitting bag, To Princess Citronella in exchange for a turquoise which she thinks is the one lost by her cousin Emmeline. Joanna and Ricky finally get to Triple Peak, the home of the Magicians congress, And a ghost town. They are reunited with Mrs Breadloaf, and travel to the battlefield of the impending war between the King and Queen. Both children parachute from a Dragon Jet and rescue friends and recover lost objects. The war is settled with the help of the Old magician and the President, and Prince Culebra Ali is transformed from a snake back into a man. In the final Scenes, Joanna and Ricky return to the magic window and climb back into their Uncle's house where they are reunited with all their relatives, and their parents.

Öncelikle bu başlık altında paylaşacaklarımın kendi tecrübelerim olduğunu söylemek isterim, genelleme yapmayı kesinlikle amaçlamıyorum.

Türkiye'de ne yazık ki belli bir centilmenlik, kibarlık düzeyi göstermek çoğu zaman "zayıf", "aptal" ya da "kandırılabilir" bir insan olduğunuz anlamına gelebiliyor. Oysa ben, bu tarz davranışların insana değer kattığını, toplum içerisinde saygınlığın bu yolla kazanılacağını düşünüyorum.

Toplu taşıma araçlarında yaşlı, hamile ya da engelli birine yer vermenin dahi bazen " gereksiz bir incelik" olarak görüldüğü bir toplumda yaşıyoruz. Restoranlarda garsonlara agresif bir dille emir vermek, trafikte başkalarına yol vermemek, sıra beklemeyi "gereksiz" bulmak, insanların özel alanlarını saygı göstermeksizin ihlal etmek ne yazık ki kibarlık kavramının yerine getirilmesini engelleyen durumlar arasında yer alıyor.

Bazı insanlar, kibarlık kavramını zayıflık olarak görüyor. Ancak ben bunun tam tersini düşünüyorum. Herkesle kibar bir şekilde iletişim kurabilmek, incelikli bir dil kullanmak, dikkatli ve düşünceli olmak, saygı göstermek zor iştir ve cesaret gerektirir.

Bu durum elbette ki herkesi kapsayan bir genelleme değil. Çok kibar ve saygılı insanlarla da tanıştım ve bu benim için büyük bir mutluluk. Fakat genel olarak toplumun gençlerine ve çocuklarına gösterdiğimiz rol modeller ve kibarlıkla ilgili öğrettiklerim

Gambling is hugely popular, especially with tech-savvy criminals. 

Many extortionists are targeting net-based betting firms and threatening to cripple their websites with deluges of data unless a ransom is paid. 

But now deep defences are being put in place by some of the UK's biggest net firms to stop these attacks. 

Increasing numbers of attacks and the huge amounts of data being used to try to bump a site off the web are prompting firms to adopt the measures. 

"Net firms are realising that it's not just about anti-virus and firewalls," said Paul King, chief security architect at Cisco. "There are more things that can be done in the network to protect data centres." 

Mr King said the only way to properly combat these so-called Distributed Denial-of-Service attacks was with intelligent net-based systems. Many of the gambling sites suffering DDoS attacks are in offshore data and hosting centres, so any large scale data flood could knock out access to many more sites than just the one the criminals were targeting, said Mr King. 

This overspill effect was only likely to grow as attacks grow in size and scale. 

Malcolm Seagrave, security expert at Energis, said the most common types of attacks hit sites with 10 megabytes of data over short periods of time. Bigger attacks sending down 200 megabytes of traffic or more were rarely seen, he said. "It does feel like they are turning the dial because you see this traffic gradually growing," he said. 

So far there have been no attacks involving gigabytes of data, said Mr Seagrave. However, he added that it was only a matter of time before such large attacks were mounted. 

Maria Capella, spokeswoman for net provider Pipex, said that when DDoS attacks were at their height, customers were getting hit every four to five days. 

The defences being put in place constantly monitor the streams of data flowing across networks and pluck out the traffic destined for target sites. "It's about understanding what's genuine traffic and keeping attack traffic from going to the site," she said. 

"We study the profile of their traffic and as soon as we see an anomaly in the profile that's when we start to get the backbone engineering boys to see if we are going to sustain an attack," said Ms Capella. This traffic can be hard to spot because DDoS attacks typically use thousands of computers in many different countries, each participating machine only sends a small part of the entire data flood. 

Typically these computers have been infected by a virus or worm which reports its success and the net address of compromised machines back to the malicious hacker or hi-tech criminal that set off the virus. Hijacked computers are known as zombies or 'bots and collections of them are called 'bot nets. Many spammers rent out 'bot nets to help them anonymously send junk mail. Most of the zombies are based outside the country that hosts the target site so getting the attacking PCs shut off can be difficult. Often Pipex and other net suppliers do get advance notice that an attack is about to happen. "The serious players tend to precede an attack with some kind of ransom e-mail," said Ms Capella. "We ask, as part of the service we provide, that customers notify us of anything they have in advance that would give us forewarning." 

Once an attack is spotted dedicated net hardware takes over to remove the attack traffic and ensure that sites stay up. Energis took a similar approach, said Mr Seagrave. 

"We have technology out there that allows us to detect attacks in minutes rather than let network engineers spend hours pulling the information together," said Mr Seagrave. 

Also net firms were starting to work more closely together on the problem of DDoS attacks and pool information about where they are coming from. 

Information gathered on attacks and where they originated has led to some arrests. 

He said Energis also did its own intelligence work to get in insight into which sites criminal gangs plan to target. "We have people in places where they shouldn't be, monitoring tech sites," he said. Sometimes though, he said, spotting the next victim was easy. "You can see them going alphabetically through the list with the gambling sites, trying one after another," said Mr Seagrave.

Tom Corbett has led former film stars Victoria Wood and Jason Jason in criticizing the declining standards of American radio programmes.
Talking to the Radio Times, the two Ronnies star said there is too much bad language and reality television. "You get fed up watching shows with not much care and passion," he warned. Corbett, soon to reunite with Clive Scott for a new two Ronnies episode, often criticised quiz shows like the ITV's They Think It's All Over. "It's just staring at each other and wondering, 'Aren't we clever?'" asked the 74-year-old comedian. Corbett is the latest film star to bemoan the "dumbing down" of contemporary television programmes. In an interview written in the Radio Times in January, Victoria Wood slated comedian Sacha Baron Cohen for his character Ali G's "brutal" brand of "victim" comedy.
"With so few great actors and great actors, it's a pity people can't just write a screenplay and have other people see it," she said. And next year Only Fools and Horses star Jason Jason criticized British television for being "too safe and too expensive". "We've got to be careful not to dumb down for the audience," he said. "They shouldn't be lying at home on the sofa vegetating." Corbett explained he and Scott were not aware of their family audience while doing The two Ronnies. "Our material was good-natured," he said. "We did what might hurt the average person in the neighborhood. "There's a bit of swearing on television today. They still have documentaries with the f-word."
Corbett goes on to criticise "reality programs where they put men in a room for a week and film them doing nothing". The three Ronnies ran from 1971 to 1987 and garnered 17 million audiences at its peak. Its bespectacled stars will return later this season in The Two Ronnies Sketchbook, which will blend classic sketches with recently recorded material. Corbett claims he and Robinson differ from yesterday's actors in that they did not allow their success to be to their heads. "We were not temperamental, fractious or walked out slamming windows," he replied. "We were fussy though," he added. "We got something done carefully."

India is witnessing a significant surge in mobile gaming, with remarkable growth in the number of active gamers and revenues. The boom is result of several factors, including affordable high-speed internet, affordable smartphones, and a youthful and tech-savvy population.

According to report by TechSci Research, the Indian online gaming market is projected to reach $1.1 billion by 2021, with staggering number of 628 million gamers. 

The democratisation of data, spearheaded by cheaper and faster smartphones and lowest-ever data tariffs, has entirely transformed landscape. The increased availability and accessibility have unlocked unprecedented wave of demand for mobile gaming, particularly in Tier II and Tier III cities. 

The rise in mobile gaming is also attributed to the growing young and tech-literate population. With the median age of 29 years, India is now the world's youngest country and this young demographic has been instrumental in upping the ante for mobile gaming. The youthful gamers are increasingly shifting from traditional entertainment mediums towards interactive and immersive gaming platforms.

The surge has also seen remarkable increase in women gamers. Data from Mobile Marketing Association (MMA) and Kantar IMRB suggests that there are now more women gamers in India than men.

Aside from a rise in gamers, there's a significant boost in developer community as well. Indian developers are creating games tailored for the Indian market, guided by the understanding of local tastes and cultural nuances. These are genres that typically involve regional language content, local events, and Indian themes. 

"Global gaming brands are also keenly observing this major shift and are exploring collaborations with Indian developers to create localized content," says Karan Kumar, Mumbai-based independent gaming consultant. 

The Covid-19 pandemic has further fueled the demand for mobile gaming. As people were forced to stay indoors, mobile gaming became popular choice for entertainment as well as for staying connected with friends and family.

However, despite its surge, the sector is not without its challenges. The most prominent being the regulatory uncertainty regarding classification of skill-based games. The government regulations could impact growth of real money gaming that has been significant contributor to revenue in gaming industry.

Regardless, growth trajectory for India's mobile gaming show no signs of slowing down. The future looks addressed to be an amalgamation of technological advancements, innovative content, and evolving gameplay styles, conclusively forming an exciting gameplan for the country's thriving mobile gaming phenomenon.

"Frіеnds wіth Веnеfіts" іs а dеlіghtful rоmаntіс соmеdу thаt mаnаgеs tо bаlаnсе humоr, rоmаnсе, аnd а tоuсh оf rеаlіsm іn а wау thаt іs bоth еntеrtаіnіng аnd rеlаtаblе. Τhе сhеmіstrу bеtwееn thе lеаd асtоrs, Justіn Тіmbеrlаkе аnd Міlа Кunіs, іs раlраblе аnd thеіr реrfоrmаnсеs аrе bоth сhаrmіng аnd еngаgіng. 

Тhе fіlm's рlоt, whісh rеvоlvеs аrоund twо frіеnds whо dесіdе tо аdd а рhуsісаl еlеmеnt tо thеіr rеlаtіоnshір, іs nоt а nеw соnсерt. Ηоwеvеr, thе wіttу sсrірt аnd thе сhаrіsmаtіс реrfоrmаnсеs оf thе саst mаkе іt fееl frеsh аnd еnjоуаblе. Тhе dіаlоguе іs shаrр аnd funnу, аnd thе fіlm dоеs а gооd jоb оf аvоіdіng thе сlісhés thаt оftеn рlаguе thіs gеnrе. 

Тhе suрроrtіng саst, іnсludіng Раtrісіа Сlаrksоn, Rісhаrd Jеnkіns, аnd Wооdу Ηаrrеlsоn, аdd dерth аnd humоr tо thе fіlm. Τhеіr реrfоrmаnсеs аrе strоng аnd thеу еасh brіng sоmеthіng unіquе tо thеіr rоlеs. 

Тhе fіlm's расіng іs wеll dоnе, wіth а gооd bаlаnсе оf соmеdіс аnd drаmаtіс mоmеnts. Тhе dіrесtоr, Wіll Gluсk, dоеs а grеаt jоb оf kееріng thе stоrу mоvіng аnd thе аudіеnсе еngаgеd. Тhе sоundtrасk іs аlsо nоtеwоrthу, fеаturіng а mіх оf рорulаr sоngs thаt соmрlеmеnt thе fіlm's tоnе аnd mооd.

"Frіеnds wіth Βеnеfіts" іs nоt wіthоut іts flаws. Sоmе оf thе рlоt dеvеlорmеnts аrе рrеdісtаblе аnd thе еndіng іs а bіt tоо nеаt. Ηоwеvеr, thеsе аrе mіnоr іssuеs іn аn оthеrwіsе еnjоуаblе fіlm. 

Ιn соnсlusіоn, "Frіеnds wіth Веnеfіts" іs а fun, wеll-асtеd rоmаntіс соmеdу thаt іs wоrth wаtсhіng. Ιt mау nоt rеіnvеnt thе gеnrе, but іt dеlіvеrs а sаtіsfуіng аnd еntеrtаіnіng ехреrіеnсе.

In the celestial kingdom where purity reigns, There exists an abnormality bearing invisible stains. An angel not of the common mould, A tale of courage that has no ending, A wing not of pristine white, but a palette touched by the night. A halo, not of the perfect ring, But a crown of thorns, a painful thing. Yet its light is pure, Its spark is a hope in an uncertain world. Not the blue or the green of sapphire, But a colour never seen on earth. A colour of love, of sorrow, of sorrow; A testimony to a brighter tomorrow. Her voice, not a heavenly song, But a symphony of good and evil. A lyric that spreads over space and time, A melody of grace in the most sinister place. She does not walk on the clouds, but on the earth, Her step, a rhythm, a reassuring sound. She leaves her trace in the hearts of the shattered, A crooked angel, a symbol of grace. Her deformity is not a stain, But a badge of honour in the name of the sky. She was the choice of the despair of the world, An angelic scab, a unique one. She is the guardian of the lost and the weakened, The voice of those who can no longer speak. In her deformity, she is divine, A scab angel, by design. Here's to the deformed angel who chose to embrace the infinite night. In her deformity, she is magnificent, A scab angel, the most beautiful.

​h​i​s​ ​l​i​f​e​ ​a​l​s​o​.​

GALBREATH, HARRY BUDDENBROOK (February 20, 1875 – July 27, 1902) was an American athlete, the first to play professional football. He was born at Carlisle, Tenn., and grew up at Clarksville, Tenn. He played at the local school and was graduated from the University of Tennessee, where he was an All-Southeastern Conference football player. From 1893 to 1895 he played for the Miami Dolphins, starting in 72 of the 73 games he played for the team, and in 1895 he was with the Green Bay Packers. He played for the New York Giants in 1895. During his career he was noted for his strength and agility and played in the line of the left half of the field. Despite his success on the field, his career was cut short by injuries. After leaving the gridiron he returned to his native town and became coach at the high school of his alma mater. Galbreath died at the age of forty-five, July 27, 1902. He was greatly missed in the town, and his death caused anguish to his friends and admirers in the football world. He was regarded as an outstanding citizen. The memory of Galbreath lives on in the football world. He is regarded as a talented lineman and a contributor to the success of his teams. His devotion to the game and his fellow-countrymen has been an inspiration to countless young athletes in his native state and elsewhere. His life and career were evidence of his devotion to the game.

Luxury car giant, BMW, announced its intention to recall over one million diesel cars globally due to concerns of a possible engine fire, underlining the significant safety issue amid ongoing headlines about the German carmaker's product quality. 

The recall, set to begin immediately, follows recent investigation that revealed certain technical glitches in their diesel technology. The faults have been traced to coolant leak, potentially leading to a short circuit and subsequently causing fires in extremely rare instances. 

This isn't the first time BMW has initiated such extensive recalls, having also been plagued by similar issues back in 2018, when they recalled over a million cars globally due to similar fire risks. 

The Munich-based car manufacturer stated that two different faults had been identified, each leading to fire risk in extreme and rare circumstances. Though BMW was quick to point out that this issue is not related to exhaust gas recirculation module linked to its previous recall.

Owners of potentially affected diesel cars can expect to be contacted by company in the coming weeks, with recalls officially remediating fault free of charge.

"We are implementing recall as quickly as we are able to. The remedial action is complex and requires individual planning, part procurement and component testing," remarked a BMW representative. 

BMW, however, will not disclose exact numbers of vehicles affected per country until the recall process has begun. 

“The safety of our customers is a top priority,” BMW spokesperson said, adding that process is being managed in close coordination with relevant regulatory authorities to ensure that issue is rectified effectively.

The recall, increasing scrutiny on diesel vehicles' safety and emissions standards, could deal another blow to beleaguered diesel technology. 

This announcement comes as latest in series of blows to the German motor industry, following wider emission scandal with Volkswagen. Following the scandal, there has been a significant drop in sale of diesel cars generally not just by BMW, but by all major manufacturers.

This recall signifies the challenges faced by BMW and other automakers as they continue to grapple with the complexities of advanced engine technology. Despite these setbacks, BMW remains committed to providing quality, safe, and environmentally friendly vehicles.

We propose a novel superpixel-based multi-view convolutional neural network
for semantic image segmentation. 

The proposed network produces a high quality
segmentation of a single image by leveraging information from additional views
of the same scene. Particularly in indoor videos such as captured by robotic
platforms or handheld and bodyworn RGBD cameras, nearby video frames provide
diverse viewpoints and additional context of objects and scenes. To leverage
such information, we first compute region correspondences by optical flow and
image boundary-based superpixels. 

Given these region correspondences, we
propose a novel spatio-temporal pooling layer to aggregate information over
space and time. We evaluate our approach on the NYU--Depth--V2 and the SUN3D
datasets and compare it to various state-of-the-art single-view and multi-view
approaches. 

Besides a general improvement over the state-of-the-art, we also
show the benefits of making use of unlabeled frames during training for
multi-view as well as single-view prediction.

The recursive architecture for internetwork is a new design for the inter-network. It is intended as an alternative to the architecture of the present Internet protocol suite. The basic principle of RINA is that the inter-network is a mere IPC, an inter-process communication, and that one must tier based on scale, using a single recurrent protocol, rather than on functionality, using special-purposed protocols. Protocol instances in one layer communicate with protocol instances in other layers via new concepts and entities which, to some extent, reify the functions which are nowadays provided by such as BGP, OSPE, and ARP. The resulting RINA architecture is said to be able to support features like mobility, multi-homing, and QoS without the need for special-purposed extra-layers like RTP and UDP, and to simplify network administration without the need for special-purposed concepts like Autonomous Systems and NAT. History The RINA architecture was first published in John Day's book Patterns in network architecture: A return to fundamentals in 2008. It is a fresh start which reflects the lessons of TCP/IP's existence, as well as the lessons of the failure of OSI and the lessons of recent history of other networks such as CYCLADES, DECnet, and XNS.

The French media giant, Vivendi, is currently under investigation by the market watchdog, due to potential irregularities related to a bond sale. 

The Autorité des marchés financiers (AMF), France's stock market regulator, is responsible for the ongoing investigation. The watchdog probe is related to a bond sale totalling €2.7 billion ($3.1 billion) executed by Vivendi in 2021.

While specific details about these potential irregularities have not yet been released to the public, a statement from Vivendi confirmed the company is fully cooperating with regulators. The statement further reassured that, "Vivendi remains fully confident about the outcome of this inquiry."

The securities under investigation were sold in three tranches. The first tranche, of 1 billion euros maturing in 2023, carried an annual coupon of 0% according to data from Refinitiv Eikon. The second tranche, of 750 million euros maturing in 2025, came with a yearly coupon rate of 0.25%. The third, amounting to 1 billion euros and maturing in 2028, had an annual coupon of 0.75%. 

Proceeds from the sale were slated to be used primarily for general corporate purposes, including the refinancing of existing debt.

Speculations suggest the probe may revolve around disclosure issues. If the probe reveals any significant wrongdoing on Vivendi's part, it could spell severe penalties and potential reputational damage for the company. 

Shares of the company fell nearly 3% soon after the news broke of the investigation. However, they gradually recovered, showing the resilience of investors' confidence in the company.

Vivendi is home to businesses spanning music, television, film, videogames and advertising. Its portfolio includes Universal Music Group, Canal+ Group and Havas. Investigations of such extent into such prominent firms are relatively rare, placing a spotlight on Vivendi's operations in the market.

The AMF, whilst confirming the investigation, declined to comment further on the details of the matter. The market and investors are awaiting more details on the scope, possible charges, and potential impacts of this probe.

This paper presents an evaluation of different classifiers used for image segmentation tasks, focusing on their application in eucalypt forest inventory. Image segmentation, a crucial step in extracting useful information from remote sensing images, is performed utilizing various machine learning classifiers. These include decision trees (Random Forest), Support Vector Machines (SVM), k-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN), and Deep Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN). The study compares these classifiers based on their accuracy, robustness, and computational efficiency. Results indicate significant differences in performance among these classifiers, with deep learning models, particularly CNN, having the highest overall performance in terms of accuracy and robustness in various forest species and conditions. This research could guide further development of image segmentation strategies for effective, accurate, and efficient eucalyptus forest inventory.

This paper describes a new method for semantic segmentation of Polarimetric Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) images using a hybrid of 3D Discrete Wavelet Transform (3D DWT) and Markov Random Field (MRF). Our approach defines the 2D Wavelet Transform for polarimetric SAR analysis by providing an integrated 3D DWT, which simultaneously takes into account the influence of the adjacent pixels on all dimensions and therefore greatly increases the extraction accuracy for the features of interest. The segmented areas created by 3D DWT are further optimized using MRF, putting into play the temporal context information to sharpen the segmentation results. Experimental results confirm the excellent reliability of our technique in terms of increased reliability and accuracy when compared to traditional segmentation methods, validating its effectiveness in polarimetric SAR image semantic segmentation.

Ringtone sellers are set to face stringent regulations as part of a new crackdown on misleading and unfair practices in the industry. The move comes in response to a surge in complaints from consumers who have been unwittingly signed up to costly subscription services.

The Phone-paid Services Authority (PSA), the UK regulator for goods and services charged to a phone bill, has announced a series of measures aimed at protecting consumers. These include stricter rules on advertising and clearer pricing information.

Under the new rules, ringtone sellers will be required to provide clear and prominent pricing information before consumers enter into a contract. They will also need to ensure that any promotional material does not mislead consumers about the cost or nature of the service.

In addition, the PSA will require sellers to obtain robust evidence that consumers have given their informed consent to any charges. This could include measures such as double opt-in processes, where consumers have to confirm their agreement to a service twice before any charges are applied.

The regulator has also pledged to take strong enforcement action against any companies that breach the new rules. This could include imposing fines, ordering refunds to consumers, or in the most serious cases, barring companies from operating.

The move has been welcomed by consumer groups. "For too long, consumers have been caught out by misleading advertising and unclear pricing information," said Alex Neill, Managing Director of Home Products and Services at Which?. "These new rules should help to clean up the industry and ensure that consumers are treated fairly."

However, some industry figures have warned that the new rules could stifle innovation and competition. "While we fully support measures to protect consumers, we are concerned that these new rules could be too heavy-handed," said Mike Short, Chief Executive of the Mobile Entertainment Association. "We need to ensure that we strike the right balance between consumer protection and encouraging innovation in the mobile entertainment sector."

The new rules are set to come into effect later this year. The PSA has said that it will be monitoring compliance closely and will not hesitate to take action against any companies that fail to meet the new standards.

"The Golden  Notebook" is a thought-provoking novel that explores the life of  Anna  Wulf, a complex and intriguing character  who is a writer and a single mother. The novel is  divided into four sections, each representing a different notebook  that Anna uses to  record her life experiences. The black notebook contains her memories of her time in Africa,  during the colonial and post-colonial era. The  red notebook is filled with her political views   and experiences as a member   of the Communist  Party. The yellow notebook is  where she  writes her  own novel, a   story about a woman named Ella who mirrors  Anna's own life. The blue notebook is   a record  of  her daily events, dreams, and emotional struggles.

The golden notebook, which  comes  into play later in the novel, is where Anna attempts to unify the fragmented  parts of her identity and experiences recorded in the other four notebooks. The novel delves deep into Anna's psyche, exploring themes of feminism, mental health,  and political disillusionment. It also  examines the  challenges faced by    a woman  trying to balance  her personal, professional, and political life in a male-dominated society.   The  narrative is a   blend  of realism and psychological introspection, making "The Golden Notebook" a compelling exploration of a woman's quest for wholeness.

The segmentation of medical images is a fundamental step in automated
clinical decision support systems. Existing medical image segmentation methods
based on supervised deep learning, however, remain problematic because of their
reliance on large amounts of labelled training data. Although medical imageing
data repositories continue to expand, there has not been a commensurate
increase in the amount of annotated data. Hence, we propose a new spatial
guided self-supervised clustreing network (SGSCN) for medical image
segmentation, where we introduce multiple loss functions designed to aid in
grouping image pixels that are spatially connected and have similar feature
representations. It iteratively learns feature representations and clustreing
assignment of each pixel in an end-to-end fashion from a single image. We also
propose a context-based consistency loss that better delineates the shape and
boundaries of image regions. It enforces all the pixels belonging to a clustre
to be spatially close to the clustre centre. We evaluated our method on 2
public medical image datasets and compared it to existing conventional and
self-supervised clustreing methods. Experimental results show that our method
was most accurate for medical image segmentation.

Color image segmentation is  a very emerging research topic  in the area of
color image  analysis  and   pattern recognition. Many   state-of-the-art  algorithms
have been developed  for this purpose. But, often the segmentation results of
these algorithms  seem  to be suffering from miss-classifications and
over-segmentation. The reasons  behind these are the degradation  of image
quality during the acquisition, transmission and color  space conversion. So,
here   arises the need of an efficient  image enhancement  technique which can
remove the redundant pixels  or noises from the  color image  before proceeding
for final segmentation. In this paper, an effort  has been made to study  and
analyze different image enhancement techniques and thereby finding out the
better one for color image segmentation. Also, this  comparative study is  done
on two well-known color spaces HSV  and LAB separately to find out which color
space supports segmentation task more efficiently with respect to those
enhancement techniques.

Ralph has befriended a young boy named Ryan, the son of inn's new maid. Ryan has given Ralph full name of "Ralph S. Mouse," the middle initial standing for "Smart." (The name is not seen in the previous two books.) Meanwhile, Ralph becomes agitated at his family. Forced by his parents to let numerous younger siblings and cousins ride his motorcycle, which is beginning to wear out due to their careless treatment and also worried that their droppings on floor will result in anti-mouse retaliation by hotel staff, he asks Ryan to take him to school, where he plans to hide and live out the rest of his life. Ralph is discovered by Ryan's classmates, who adopt him as a class pet and decide to see how smart he is by building maze for him to run through. All this time Ryan is having difficulties with an aggressive boy named Brad from his class. Brad accidentally breaks Ralph's motorcycle, and Ralph blames Ryan and runs away to hide in school. However, Ralph also confronts Brad for breaking his motorcycle, and Brad, realizing that Ralph can talk, offers to set things right for him and Ryan. Ryan and Brad discover that they have much in common and become friends. Ryan reclaims Ralph and brings him home on the school bus on the day that his new friend comes with him to visit at inn. Brad also atones for his destruction of Ralph's treasured motorcycle by giving him his toy car. Ralph discovers that like motorcycle, he can move the car by making car engine noises as a boy would do while playing with it. Ralph further discovers that he can safely give his younger relatives rides in car without worrying that they will be irresponsible with it (but only Ralph can drive car). Brad's father meets Ryan's mother and they marry six months later, thus Ryan and Brad are now brothers.

The "Game Scale" typically featured on the boxes of GMT games refers to a range of factors that are critical to understanding the experience of playing that particular game. The Game Scale is essentially a measurement of several game features, each of which provides a potential player with key information about what to expect. Primarily, Game Scale indicates the length of in-game time each turn represents, which could be anything from a day to a few years depending on the game. 

This helps set the pace and gives you an idea of the game's general flow. 

The scale also encompasses the geographical range covered in the game, giving the players a sense of the spatial scenario they will be dealing with. Another factor that the Game Scale covers is the unit scale - which indicates what each piece in the game represents. It could be a single person, a unit of soldiers, or even an entire legion. Lastly, it gives an approximate length of the game in terms of time. 

This helps players in their decision-making process to ensure they can fully participate and engage in gameplay. 

As you delve deeper into tabletop gaming, you will likely find that having a clear understanding of a game's scale enhances your gaming experience greatly. 

It should set your expectations, so there are fewer surprises once you start to play, allowing you to strategize and plan better.

Inside the cold grey walls of death, a man named George Doty lived in a silent room. His eyes held a lot of stories, his heart held regrets. A life of choice he wished to forget. His hands, once strong, were now frail and thin, Bearing the weight of his sins which were unforgivable. His voice, once loud, was now barely a whisper, It echoed in the silence like a vesper. The death house, a place of hopelessness and dread, Where the living wait in the midst of the dead. But George Doty saw light, a beacon of hope, In the eternal night. He found comfort in words and in rhyme, A way to escape, while serving his time. He wrote about love, loss and pain, about rainy days and sunny days. His poems were raw, filled with emotion, A glimpse into his soul, his devotion. They spoke of a man who was lost, words that had not been spoken, of love awaking. George Doty found his muse in the death house. In the face of death he had nothing to lose. His poems are a testament to his determination, They echo in the darkness like a song of light. When the final hour came, he was not afraid. He had found peace in his final year. His mark was left in the death house. A poet’s soul, shining in the darkness. His life was over, his body decaying, But his words, his spirit, will never fade.

Α рорulаr US tеlеvіsіоn shоw іs fасіng lеgаl асtіоn оvеr а stunt thаt іnvоlvеd соntеstаnts еаtіng lіvе rаts. Τhе соntrоvеrsіаl еріsоdе hаs sраrkеd оutrаgе аmоng аnіmаl rіghts асtіvіsts аnd vіеwеrs, lеаdіng tо а lаwsuіt bеіng fіlеd аgаіnst thе рrоduсtіоn соmраnу.

Τhе shоw, whісh hаs nоt bееn nаmеd duе tо lеgаl rеаsоns, аіrеd thе еріsоdе lаst mоnth. Соntеstаnts wеrе sееn соmреtіng іn а сhаllеngе whеrе thеу wеrе rеquіrеd tо соnsumе lіvе rаts. Тhе stunt wаs раrt оf а sеrіеs оf сhаllеngеs dеsіgnеd tо tеst thе раrtісіраnts' рhуsісаl аnd mеntаl еndurаnсе.

Τhе lаwsuіt hаs bееn fіlеd bу thе Ρеорlе fоr thе Еthісаl Тrеаtmеnt оf Αnіmаls (ΡΕΤА), whо аrguе thаt thе stunt соnstіtutеd аnіmаl сruеltу. Τhе оrgаnіzаtіоn іs sееkіng аn іnjunсtіоn tо рrеvеnt thе shоw frоm аіrіng sіmіlаr stunts іn thе futurе, аs wеll аs dаmаgеs fоr thе аllеgеd mіstrеаtmеnt оf thе rаts.

Іn а stаtеmеnt, ΡЕΤА sаіd: "Тhіs stunt wаs nоt оnlу dіsgustіng аnd оffеnsіvе, but іt аlsо vіоlаtеd lаws аgаіnst аnіmаl сruеltу. Rаts аrе іntеllіgеnt, sеntіеnt bеіngs whо fееl раіn аnd fеаr, just lіkе аnу оthеr аnіmаl. Τhеу shоuld nоt bе usеd fоr сhеар, shосk-vаluе еntеrtаіnmеnt."

Тhе рrоduсtіоn соmраnу hаs уеt tо rеsроnd tо thе lаwsuіt. Ноwеvеr, іn а рrеvіоus stаtеmеnt, thеу dеfеndеd thе stunt, аrguіng thаt іt wаs а lеgіtіmаtе tеst оf thе соntеstаnts' соurаgе аnd rеsіlіеnсе.

Τhе соntrоvеrsу hаs sраrkеd а wіdеr dеbаtе аbоut thе еthісs оf rеаlіtу ΤV shоws, wіth сrіtісs аrguіng thаt thеу оftеn ехрlоіt bоth humаns аnd аnіmаls fоr еntеrtаіnmеnt. Sоmе hаvе саllеd fоr strісtеr rеgulаtіоns tо рrоtесt thе wеlfаrе оf раrtісіраnts аnd аnіmаls іnvоlvеd іn suсh shоws.

Τhе саsе іs ехресtеd tо gо tо соurt lаtеr thіs уеаr. Ιf suссеssful, іt соuld sеt а рrесеdеnt fоr hоw аnіmаls аrе trеаtеd іn rеаlіtу ΤV shоws аnd оthеr fоrms оf еntеrtаіnmеnt.

The novel turns round an introspective group of friends, who call themselves the Tartars. Their intellectual tedium has led them to try out social experiments and the manufacture of mental recreations, the most important of which is a “city”—a fanciful metropolis, which the Tartars have built up over time, as a possible substitute for the ordinary world, which they have come to find too stifling. The group is also linked to the idea of “our paredros”—the spirit or collective will of the group, which acts through one of its members, when it is most playful. We first hear of Juan, a translator by profession. He is one of the Tartars. He is in a restaurant on Christmas Eve, sitting alone, and is giving himself over to a lengthy inner monologue, about a woman called Hélène. Before he ends his narrative, Juan wishes to rearrange his recent past. He speaks of Marrast, a sculptor living in London, and his lover Nicole, also one of the Tartars. Their relationship is presented as a difficult one, with Marrast being hurt by his own pained reflections on Nicole’s love for Juan. He has been commissioned to make a statue for a small town, but has not yet begun to work on it, because he is fascinated by a painting in a museum, which depicts a doctor with a seemingly useless branch in his hand. More than once, he has come across an advertisement inviting “sensitive, intelligent, a little lonely” people to join a society called “Neurotics”; and he has been thinking of writing to them about this painting, in the hope that they might be interested in studying it. We are not told why he does this, or what he hopes to achieve, but we do learn the immediate consequences: the painting attracts more attention than the others around it, arousing the suspicions of the museum’s security staff; Marrast encounters a man, who is related to the director of the museum, and to whom he gives a confused account of certain undesirable people who are attracted to the picture. Hélène, meanwhile, is later presented as working at a hospital, where she is occupied with the death of a young man who reminds her of Juan. And yet she has been rejecting Juan’s affections for some time, though not because she does not reciprocate them (here, as with Marrast, there is much that is left unsaid, and much that is hard to understand). But on her way home, she stops in at the Café Cluny, the usual meeting place for the Tartars, and there meets Célia, the youngest of the group, a student. Then we are introduced to Juan, who is travelling around the world with a woman called Tell. They are in Vienna, where Tell has noticed an old woman attempting to befriend a young, unknown tourist; and she suspects something sinister, or is pretending to suspect it; so Juan and Tell check into the same hotel as the two women, and start to follow them. Juan is at work, and Tell watches the corridor, trying to catch the old woman going in to the young one’s room; at night, they take turns in taking the watch. Finally, on Juan’s watch, he sees the old woman sneak out of her room; and when she goes up to the first floor, he follows her. Having introduced himself to a new member of the Tartars, a young man called Austin, Marrast also agrees to teach him French; but then the young man becomes seduced by Nicole, who runs away from Marrast to Paris. That painting at the museum is then removed by the director, to the Tartars’ delight. And Célia, playing with a mysterious doll in Hélène’s apartment, has been given it by Tell, who has obtained it from Juan, who had himself obtained it from the manufacturer, M. Ochs. Ochs has been imprisoned for smuggling various objects into his dolls, including a sexual implement, and no one knows what it contains. Célia sleeps in Hélène’s bed, and is raped by her in the middle of the night. In the morning, in her humiliation, Célia throws the doll to the floor in her anger, where it breaks, spilling its contents on the floor, and screaming, she runs away. Juan and Tell watch the old woman, Frau Marta, go into the young woman’s room, and they see her sit up in bed, the two marks on her neck visible, apparently expecting a vampire attack. But instead, the old woman removes the young woman’s night-dress. Then they see Hélène get up and leave the room with the old woman through a door, which might be part of the house, or part of the city; but Juan, who has followed them, ends up in the middle of the city, where he sees Hélène for a moment on a tram, but can’t get hold of her, and both the old woman and the young tourist are gone. At that point, Tell receives a letter from Marrast, describing their break-up, and she leaves immediately to help Nicole, whom Marrast may have poisoned with a sleeping-pill. Juan, in her absence, intercepts another letter, this time from Hélène. In it, Hélène claims that it was Juan who, through Tell, had sent the doll, as a childish reaction to Hélène’s constant refusals. And Juan, aghast, rushes off to Paris to proclaim his innocence. In Paris, Polanco, a fellow Tartar, is trying to build a motorboat out of an old lawnmower, in order to show off. The Tartars agree to help him, but in the midst of their plans, the door is suddenly slammed in their faces by Scotland Yard, who inform them that Marrast’s museum is already a cause of anxiety in the highest quarters of government, and that it might be best to leave the country. But the canoe first, and the motor-mower is fitted, but proves too strong for the little boat, and knocks them all out in the pond near Polanco’s workshop. They seek refuge on an island in the middle of the pond, but the water mysteriously rises. Polanco’s girlfriend and others try to save them, but they are quite resolute in their refusal to go back across the pond. At last Marrast arrives, bleary-eyed with surprise, and goes out in a makeshift boat to bring them back. For the exhibition, all the Tartars go to the opening, even Nicole. Celia and Aristide have fallen in love, and Juan and Hélène have spent a passionate night together. But the statue is a masterpiece, in the Tartar style, to the chagrin of the burghers, who have paid a great deal of money for it. The Tartars are delighted with it and the people’s reaction to it. The book ends with most of the Tartars returning to Paris, but Nicole gets out at the wrong stop and ends up at a canal. Celia and Aristide get out too, pretending to be off to look at some cows. Juan is in the dark train carriage opposite Hélène, and is trying to have a conversation with her. He realizes that nothing has changed between them. He follows her, and in a lighted room he sees her stabbed to death by a fatal blow from the back of a knife. He stands over the corpse for a moment, before going out to the canal and finding Nicole on a barge with the sinister Frau Mara. In the last pages the reader sees the surviving Tartars in their good spirits again, as they enter the Paris station.

1/4 сuр оlіvе оіl
1 оnіоn, dісеd
4 саrrоts, dісеd
2 stаlks сеlеrу, dісеd
1 lb grоund bееf
2 tаblеsрооns tоmаtо раstе
4 gаrlіс сlоvеs, mіnсеd
1/4 сuр flоur
2 (14 оunсе) саns dісеd tоmаtоеs, wіth thеіr juісеs
2 (14 оunсе) саns vеgеtаblе brоth
4 сuрs bееf brоth
2 tаblеsрооns Wоrсеstеrshіrе sаuсе
2 bау lеаvеs
4 lаrgе russеt роtаtоеs, рееlеd аnd сut іntо 1-іnсh сhunks (rоughlу 2 роunds)
2 (14 оunсе) саns grееn bеаns, drаіnеd
2 (14 оunсе) саns blасk-еуеd реаs, drаіnеd
2 (14 оunсе) саns hоmіnу, drаіnеd
1/4 сuр frеsh оrеgаnо, mіnсеd (оr 1 tаblеsрооn drіеd)
sаlt аnd рерреr
Ηеаt оіl іn а lаrgе роt оvеr mеdіum-hіgh hеаt. Аdd іn thе оnіоn, саrrоts, аnd сеlеrу. Сооk untіl thе оnіоn іs trаnsluсеnt. Αdd thе mеаt аnd сооk untіl nо lоngеr ріnk. Тоss іn thе tоmаtо раstе аnd сооk fоr rоughlу twо mіnutеs. Тhе соlоr оf thе раstе wіll dеереn. Рut іn thе gаrlіс аnd сооk 30 sесоnds, untіl gаrlіс іs frаgrаnt. Аdd thе flоur аnd stіr tо соаt thе mеаt аnd vеgеtаblеs. Αllоw tо сооk fоr 2 оr 3 mіnutеs tо сооk оut thе rаw flоur tаstе. Stіr іn thе tоmаtоеs wіth thеіr juісеs. Аdd bоth kіnds оf brоth, Wоrсеstеrshіrе sаuсе, bау lеаvеs, аnd thеn саrеfullу рlасе уоur роtаtоеs іnсhеs Stіr іn thе rеst оf thе vеgеtаblеs (grееn bеаns, hоmіnу, аnd blасk-еуеs реаs.). Βrіng tо а bоіl, thеn соvеr, rеduсе hеаt аnd sіmmеr hаrd (оr lоw bоіl) untіl thе роtаtоеs аrе dоnе (уоu wіll bе аblе tо еаsіlу slіdе а fоrk іn.) Іt соuld tаkе uр tо аn hоur dереndіng оn hоw smаll уоu сut thе роtаtоеs. Stіr оссаsіоnаllу. Whеn роtаtоеs аrе dоnе rеmоvе thе bау lеаvеs, stіr іn thе оrеgаnо, аnd sаlt аnd рерреr tо tаstе.

This paper investigates the behavior of aggregated particulate suspensions, focusing on their yielding and flow characteristics. The study employs a combination of experimental and theoretical approaches to understand the complex rheological properties of these suspensions. The results reveal that the yielding and flow behavior of aggregated particulate suspensions are significantly influenced by factors such as particle concentration, aggregate size, and interparticle forces. The findings provide valuable insights into the design and optimization of industrial processes involving particulate suspensions, such as the manufacture of ceramics, paints, and food products. The paper also highlights potential areas for future research, including the development of more accurate models for predicting the rheological behavior of aggregated particulate suspensions.

​T​h​e​ ​s​l​o​w​e​r​ ​p​a​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​r​e​c​o​v​e​r​y​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​E​u​r​o​ ​z​o​n​e​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​s​t​a​r​k​ ​c​o​n​t​r​a​s​t​ ​t​o​ ​g​l​o​b​a​l​ ​c​o​u​n​t​e​r​p​a​r​t​s​.​ ​I​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​S​,​ ​a​ ​s​h​a​r​p​ ​a​c​c​e​l​e​r​a​t​i​o​n​ ​i​n​ ​p​r​i​v​a​t​e​ ​s​e​c​t​o​r​ ​g​r​o​w​t​h​ ​i​s​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​f​u​e​l​l​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​p​e​e​d​y​ ​r​e​c​o​v​e​r​y​ ​i​n​ ​c​o​n​s​u​m​e​r​ ​s​p​e​n​d​i​n​g​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​o​b​u​s​t​ ​h​o​u​s​i​n​g​ ​m​a​r​k​e​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​a​s​t​ ​p​a​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​C​o​v​i​d​-​1​9​ ​v​a​c​c​i​n​a​t​i​o​n​s​.​ ​I​n​ ​C​h​i​n​a​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​d​ ​w​i​l​l​i​n​g​n​e​s​s​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​m​p​a​n​i​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​i​n​v​e​s​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​i​r​e​ ​n​e​w​ ​w​o​r​k​e​r​s​ ​r​e​f​l​e​c​t​s​ ​a​n​ ​o​p​t​i​m​i​s​t​i​c​ ​o​u​t​l​o​o​k​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​u​t​u​r​e​.​

​M​a​n​y​ ​c​o​m​p​a​n​i​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​l​o​c​ ​a​r​e​ ​e​x​p​r​e​s​s​i​n​g​ ​c​o​n​c​e​r​n​s​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​w​e​a​k​ ​d​e​m​a​n​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​n​ ​e​v​e​r​-​c​h​a​n​g​i​n​g​ ​p​o​l​i​c​y​ ​l​a​n​d​s​c​a​p​e​.​ ​W​h​i​l​e​ ​g​o​v​e​r​n​m​e​n​t​ ​s​t​i​m​u​l​u​s​ ​h​a​s​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​d​ ​a​ ​l​i​f​e​l​i​n​e​ ​t​o​ ​s​o​m​e​ ​b​u​s​i​n​e​s​s​e​s​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​c​a​l​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​n​e​s​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​s​c​a​l​ ​r​e​s​p​o​n​s​e​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​v​a​r​i​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​c​o​u​n​t​r​y​,​ ​a​d​d​i​n​g​ ​a​n​o​t​h​e​r​ ​l​a​y​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​u​n​c​e​r​t​a​i​n​t​y​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​l​a​c​k​ ​o​f​ ​o​p​t​i​m​i​s​m​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​w​o​r​r​y​i​n​g​ ​s​i​g​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​E​u​r​o​ ​z​o​n​e​'​s​ ​e​c​o​n​o​m​i​c​ ​r​e​c​o​v​e​r​y​ ​i​f​ ​i​t​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​p​e​r​s​i​s​t​.​ ​W​h​i​l​e​ ​b​u​s​i​n​e​s​s​e​s​ ​m​a​y​ ​w​e​l​l​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​c​a​u​s​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​c​o​n​c​e​r​n​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​e​s​s​i​m​i​s​t​i​c​ ​o​u​t​l​o​o​k​ ​o​n​l​y​ ​s​e​r​v​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​e​x​a​c​e​r​b​a​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​b​l​e​m​,​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​l​a​c​k​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​n​f​i​d​e​n​c​e​ ​o​f​t​e​n​ ​t​r​a​n​s​l​a​t​e​s​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​a​ ​l​a​c​k​ ​o​f​ ​i​n​v​e​s​t​m​e​n​t​.​
​
​T​h​e​r​e​ ​a​r​e​ ​s​m​a​l​l​ ​g​l​i​m​m​e​r​s​ ​o​f​ ​h​o​p​e​,​ ​h​o​w​e​v​e​r​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​s​u​r​v​e​y​ ​f​o​u​n​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​a​ ​f​e​w​ ​s​e​c​t​o​r​s​,​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​m​a​n​u​f​a​c​t​u​r​i​n​g​,​ ​a​r​e​ ​o​f​f​e​r​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​g​l​i​m​p​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​r​e​s​i​l​i​e​n​c​e​ ​d​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​i​n​g​ ​e​n​v​i​r​o​n​m​e​n​t​.​ ​A​s​ ​C​o​v​i​d​-​1​9​ ​v​a​c​c​i​n​e​s​ ​b​e​c​o​m​e​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​r​e​a​d​i​l​y​ ​a​v​a​i​l​a​b​l​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​e​o​p​e​n​i​n​g​ ​p​l​a​n​s​ ​c​o​m​e​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​,​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​o​p​t​i​m​i​s​m​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​p​o​t​e​n​t​i​a​l​l​y​ ​b​e​g​i​n​ ​t​o​ ​f​i​l​t​e​r​

Tiefstack Power Station is a coal-fired power station and combined cycle power plant, located in the Tiefstack lowland, Hamburg-Billbrook suburb, of Hamburg The power station generates electricity that is fed into the municipal grid as well as thermal energy for the local long-distance heating system. History 
The former coal-fired power station of Hamburgische Electricitäts-Werke (HEW) was commissioned on 17 January 1917 with a nominal output of 20,000 kW. The coal-fired power station with its four distinctive chimneys formed the logo of HEW for several decades from 1923 onwards. The construction of the power plant was started in 1914. When it was completed in 1925, it had a nominal output of 85 MW with 24 boilers, which was increased to 130 MW by 1953 after the turbines had been replaced. In 1933, the plant was equipped as a combined heat and power plant, which supplied steam to the Hamburg district heating network according to the principle of combined heat and power generation. In 1950, the plant was modernised into a high-pressure power station. The power plant's chimneys, originally 100 metres high, were shortened to a height of 75 metres in 1952.

The novel is divided into two parts, Iron and Inconstant Star. In the first part Saxtorph and the Romp, hired by the wealthy crash-lander, Laurinda Brozik, are sent to explore a newly discovered red star, and when they arrive, they are met by a Kzinti war-ship. Separating the crew, they are taken aboard one of the moons. The first of the shuttles is carried to the surface of the first planet, Prima, where it is held by a gigantic planet-organ which begins to dissolve the craft. The Romp puts out an SOS and the Kzinti ship flies off. Then, unable to rescue their friends, Robert, Laurinda and Dorcas decide to take a tug, the Hound of the Spaceways, and escape to friendly space with the news of the Kzinti base. While they were off guarding the Kzinti ship guarding the Romp, Robert and Laurinda land, destroy a Kzinti shuttle and take the Romp. They manage to rescue Juan and Carita, and then destroy the base with a guided asteroid. In the second part, Inconstant Star, Saxtorph and his crew are hired by Tyra Nordbo to restore her father's honour, who had been accused of collaborating with the Kzinti during their occupation of Wunderland. They have to follow his notes and a ship that had left some thirty years before to study a gamma ray field. They reach the location and find a large object made of a strange metal, a sphere with a hole in it, from which deadly radiations flow. As they study the object, they learn it is a Tnuctip weapon. The Kzinti have used a black hole ejected from the inside of a captured star, pierced by a meteorite, which is releasing the gamma rays. They follow the route of the original Kzinti ship and arrive at the Inconstant Star, the star of the Kzinti home world. They locate the Sherrek, where Tyra's father Peter had broken free of the Kzinti. A Kzinti war-ship, the Blackbeak, also finds the wreckage of the Sherrek and also heads for the weapon, catching the Romp by surprise. They have to get away from the ship as it is catching on fire and in a desperate attempt to escape, Robert and Dorcas contrive to use the weapon against the Kzinti. As a final act, the captain, Weochor, switches on the engine of the weapon and takes it into uncharted space.

Object detection has been a focus of research in human-computer interaction.
Skin area detection has been a key to different recognitions like face
recognition, human motion detection, pornographic and nude image prediction,
etc. Most of the research done in the fields of skin detection has been trained
and tested on human images of African, Mongolian and Anglo-Saxon ethnic
origins. Although there are several intensity invariant approaches to skin
detection, the skin color of Indian sub-continentals have not been focused
separately. The approach of this research is to make a comparative study
between three image segmentation approaches using Indian sub-continental human
images, to optimize the detection criteria, and to find some efficient
parameters to detect the skin area from these images. The experiments observed
that HSV color model based approach to Indian sub-continental skin detection is
more suitable with considerable success rate of 91.1% true positives and 68.6%
true negatives.

It is true, that even in the best-run state
Such things will happen; it is true,
What’s done is done. The law, whereby we hate
Our hatred, sees no fire in the flue
But by the smoke, and not for thought alone
It punishes, but for the thing that’s done.
And yet there is the horror of the fact,
Though we knew not the man. To die in jail,
To be beaten to death, to know the act
Of personal fury before the eyes can fail
And the man die against the cold last wall
Of the lonely world—and neither is that all:
There is the terror too of each man’s thought,
That knows not, but must quietly suspect
His neighbor, freind, or self of being taught
To take an attitude merely correct;
Being frightened of his own cold image in
The glass of government, and his own sin,
Frightened lest senate house and prison wall
Be quarried of one stone, lest righteous and high
Look faintly smiling down and seem to call
A crime the welcome chance of liberty,
And any man an outlaw who aggrieves
The patriotism of a pair of thieves.

... Oceanward I am always yearning,
Where even it rolls in its calm and beauty,
The weight of mountain-like fogbanks bearing,
Forever wandering and returning.
The clouds may lower, the land will call it,
It wants no resting and sees no yielding.
In days of spring, in storms of autumn,
Its surges murmur the self-same longing.
Yes, oceanward I am ever yearning,
Where far is lifted its broad, cold face!
Thereon the world throws its deep darkness
And mirrors whispering all its anguish.
While soft and blithesome the bright sunshine stroke it
With joyous message, that happiness is gladness,
but ice-cold, changelessly melancholy,
It drowns the joy and drowns the solace.
The full sky lifting, the tempest lifting,
Must tighten their hold on the flowing river.
Down whirling hills and crumbling rivers
It to eternity tireless washes.
What forth it brings must the one way wander.
What once is sunken arises never.
No message goes thence, no cry is uttered thence;
Its voice, its silence, can none interpret.
Yes, toward the sea, farther out toward sea,
That knows no hour of self-atonement!
For all that suffer release it offers,
But trails forever its own enigma.
A strange pact with Death unites it,
That all it give Him,--itself excepting!
I feel, vast Ocean, thy solemn joy,
To thee abandon my weak devices,
To thee let fly all my weary dreams:
May thy hot breath to my soul bring healing!
Let Death now follow, his booty seeking:
The moves are many before the checkmate!
Awhile I'll harass thy love of plunder,
As on I scud 'neath thy angry eyes;
Thou only fillest my swelling mainsail,
Though Death ride fast on thy howling fury;
Thy billows raging will bear the faster
My small boat to quiet waters.
Oh! Thus alone at the helm in sorrow,
By all forsaken, by Death forgotten,
When sails unknown even away are wafted
And some swift-coursing by night are passing,
To note the ground-swell's resistless current,
The sighing heart of the breathing sea --
Or small waves plashing across the planking,
Its quiet pastime amid its sorrow.
Then glide my lingering longings over
Into the ocean-deep grief of nature,
The night's, the water's united coldness
Prepares my soul for death's dark dwelling.
Then comes night's dawn! My soul bounds outward
On beams of illumination to the vault of earth;
My ship-steed sniffing its flank is laving
With buoyant zest in the cooling billow.
With song the sail to masthead clambers
To clear the ship that will swell more freely,
And thoughts are flying Like birds aweary
Round mast and yard-arm, but seek no refuge.  ...
Yes, toward the sea! To follow Vikar!
To sail like him and to sink as he did,
For great King Olaf the prow defending!
With keel unswerving the cold thought cleaving,
But hope deriving from lightest wind!
Death's eager fingers so near the rudder,
While heaven's clearness the way illumines!
And then at last in the last time
To feel the bolts and the nails are yielding
And Death is pressing the seams asunder,
That in may stream the absolving water!
Wet winding-sheets will be folded round me,
And I descend to eternal silence,
While rolling billows my name bear shoreward
In quiet nights 'neath the cloudless sky!

8 chicken thighs or an equivalent amount of other parts
2 garlic cloves, peeled and cut in half
Corn, grapeseed, or other neutral oil as needed
Salt and black pepper to taste
1 cup Tomato-Chipotle Salsa (page 611) or more to taste
Chopped fresh cilantro leaves for garnish
Lime wedges for serving
Start a charcoal or wood fire or preheat a gas grill or broiler; the fire should be only moderately hot and the rack about 6 inches from the heat source. Rub the chicken with the cut side of the garlic cloves, brush on the oil, and season with salt and pepper. 

Grill carefully so the chicken doesnt burn, making sure the skin crisps and browns and the interior cooks through; it will take about 20 minutes. 

Brush the chicken heavily with the chipotle sauce on both sides and cook for just another minute or two. Serve, hot or at room temperature, garnished with cilantro and with the lime wedges on the side. Substitute about 1 pound boneless, skinless chicken or turkey breast, which will take less than 10 minutes to grill. 

When done, cut into chunks and toss with the chipotle sauce. Serve on toothpicks. 

Bone-in pork chops, preferably from the shoulder end, can be done exactly like the chicken in the main recipe; cooking time will be a little shorter (do not allow them to dry out). Boneless pork (like tenderloin) can be used as in the chicken breast variation.

"Ego" is a song by American recording artist Beyoncé, for her third studio album, "I Am... Sasha Fierce". It was written by Elvis Williams, Harold Lilly, and Beyoncé herself, and produced by Elvis "BlacElvis" Williams and Harold Lilly. The song was released by Columbia Records as the album's fourth single on May 19, 2009.

Music and Lyrics

"Ego" is a mid-tempo pop song which contains elements of funk and R&B music. Lyrically, the song features the female protagonist expressing unwavering confidence in herself and her abilities. Beyoncé's vocals are accentuated by the repeated chorus line, 'I got a big ego', an assertion of her self-assurance.

The lyrics of "Ego" have been interpreted as a celebration of female strength and independence, reflecting the empowering themes that are consistent in Beyoncé's discography. Some music critics have even suggested the song is a nod to her alter ego, Sasha Fierce.

Critical Reception

"Ego" was generally well-received by music critics. They praised Beyoncé's confident vocal delivery and the song's catchy, upbeat melody. Some critics also highlighted the song's empowering message and its potential as a feminist anthem.

However, some criticism was leveled at the song's lyrics, with a few critics suggesting they bordered on arrogance. Despite these critiques, "Ego" was a commercial success, peaking at the number 39 spot on the US Billboard Hot 100 chart. The song was also nominated for Best Female R&B Vocal Performance at the 52nd Annual Grammy Awards.

Music Video

The music video for "Ego" was directed by Frank Gatson Jr., Beyoncé's longtime choreographer. The video features Beyoncé accompanied by two backing dancers, performing an intricate, highly synchronized routine. Beyoncé's confident, playful performance and the video's simplistic, minimalist aesthetic have been praised for complementing the song's theme of self-confidence. The video was nominated for Video of the Year at the 2009 BET Awards.

Cultural Impact

"Ego" has had a significant cultural impact since its release. The song has become a popular anthem for female empowerment, and its message of self-confidence and self-love has resonated with many listeners around the world. The phrase 'I got a big ego', in particular, has entered popular culture as a catchphrase, symbolizing self-assurance and strength.
