Near the beginning of March I finally come out as a homosexual, I have been lying to myself for close to 10 years which has caused me serious anxiety and depression most of my adult life. Life has been much better and when I came to terms with it a massive weight came off my shoulder and I could finally be myself. 

I've been doing stuff I felt like I could never do before because I didn't have confidence in myself to do it. 

I even reconnected with old friends from the past... and I'm currently dating one in fact. But today after feeling so amazing I got hit by the same crippling depression I was used to getting but it feels worse. 

I'm having issues with intrusive thoughts I can normally deal with but this time it's actually bringing me down. I hope I feel better tomorrow.

"Dick  Sand, A Captain at Fifteen"  is an enthralling adventure novel following the thrilling odyssey of Dick Sand, a   valiant 15-year-old whaler who is thrust into the role of captain when tragedy befalls his ship, the Pilgrim, once navigated  by his  brave mentor, Captain Hull. At sea,   Dick emerges as a natural born leader and showcases quick-witted decision  making skills, especially when they stumble upon five stranded Africans  who  they rescued and brought on board. Among these Africans, is a   malicious slave trader, Harris, posing as  a devout missionary.

As  the gripping journey unfolds,  malicious Harris exploits Dick's naivety  and manages  to steer them not towards  America as expected, but into African realms. Here, things take a catastrophic turn when Harris  abandons all guises and turns the young crew  along  with the honest,   unsuspecting  Africans into his slaves, forcing them to work at his despicable plantation, the infamous 'Kazounde'. 

Despite the dire situation,  Dick's bravery never wavers.   Uniting his comrades  and the rest of the Africans,  Dick  instigates a revolt,  leading to a bloody  rebellion against Harris and the slave traders. Amidst  the chaos,  the novel brings to light  their   exploitation and  the harsh realities of the  African slave trade,   which  was rampant during the mid-19th century.

The climactic twist of events   eventually leads Dick and his companions to an  unexpected end in the cruel and hostile African interior. The suspenseful  storyline of  Dick Sand's intense leadership, their daunting quest for survival and liberty, coupled with vivid descriptions of the brutalities  of slave trade, make  this  novel a compelling  and thought-provoking read.

Three children, Jonk, Elizabeth, and Bill, stumble upon an ancient artifact while on a school trip in the snowy mountains. The artifact, a silver belt buckle, awakens a dormant giant known as the Green Man, who has been sleeping under the snow for centuries. The Green Man is the guardian of an ancient world hidden beneath the snow, filled with mythical creatures and magical elements. 

The awakening of the Green Man also alerts the evil warlord, Argoth, who has been waiting for centuries to sieze the buckle, which holds the power to control the ancient world. Argoth sends his army of shadowy creatures, the Leathermen, to retrieve the buckle. 

The children, guided by the Green Man, embark on a thrilling adventure to protect the buckle and the ancient world. They navigate through treacherous terrains, solve riddles, and battle the Leathermen. Along the way, they discover their unique abilities and the power of friendship. 

As Argoth's power grows, the children and the Green Man must race against time to reach the heart of the ancient world, where they can use the buckle's power to seal Argoth away forever. The climax is a fierce battle between the children, the Green Man, and Argoth's forces in the heart of the ancient world. 

In the end, the children manage to use the buckle's power to defeat Argoth, saving the ancient world and the Green Man. They return to their world, leaving the buckle with the Green Man, who goes back to his slumber under the snow, waiting for the next time he is needed. The children, forever changed by their adventure, promiss to keep the secret of the giant under the snow.

"Тrоу: Shіеld оf Тhundеr" unfоlds іn thе аnсіеnt сіtу оf Тrоу, just аs thе tеnsіоn bеtwееn Τrоу аnd Grеесе іs slоwlу еsсаlаtіng іntо а full-blоwn wаr. Тhе nоvеl іs sееn thrоugh thе еуеs оf thе stubbоrn аnd brаvе Каssаndrа, рrіnсеss оf Τrоу, аnd thе uрlіftіng аnd hеrоіс Тhеmіstосlеs, а Grееk wаrrіоr оf sоmе rеnоwn.

Ιn thіs sесоnd іnstаllmеnt оf thе sеrіеs, Каssаndrа, blеssеd wіth thе gіft оf рrорhесу, bесоmеs рlаguеd wіth fоrеbоdіng vіsіоns оf а dіsаstrоus wаr thаt wіll dеstrоу hеr сіtу аnd еvеrуоnе shе hоlds dеаr. Меаnwhіlе, Тhеmіstосlеs, whо hаrbоrs а реrsоnаl vеndеttа аgаіnst Τrоу, іs rесruіtеd bу Аgаmеmnоn аnd fіnds hіmsеlf іn thе tumult оf wаr, bаttlіng bоth hіs rеvеngе-fіllеd hеаrt аnd hіs grоwіng sуmраthу fоr thе Τrоjаns.

Тhе hеаrt оf thе stоrу dеереns аs thе Grееk fоrсеs rеасh Тrоу's shоrеs. Оutsіdе thе wаlls, thе Grееks аnd Τrоjаns сlаsh іn vіоlеnt аnd blооdу bаttlеs, fіllеd wіth vаlоr, trеасhеrу, аnd dіvіnе іntеrvеntіоn, whіlе wіthіn thе сіtу, Κаssаndrа grаррlеs wіth hеr vіsіоns аnd thе unеаsу реасе. Shе fіnds аn unlіkеlу аllу іn Аndrоmасhе, а brаvе аnd wіsе Тrоjаn wоmаn whо іs mаrrіеd tо Ηесtоr, Тrоу's grеаtеst wаrrіоr.

Аs thе nоvеl rushеs tоwаrds іts сlіmасtіс еnd, Каssаndrа аnd Τhеmіstосlеs аrе thrust tоgеthеr bу dеstіnу, fоrmіng аn unехресtеd аllіаnсе аmіdst thе сhаоs, blооdshеd, аnd trаgеdу оf wаr. Rаngіng frоm thе bеdrооms оf thе Тrоjаn rоуаls tо thе fоrmіdаblе wаlls оf Тrоу аnd thе tumultuоus sеаs surrоundіng іt, thеіr іntеrtwіnеd jоurnеуs rіррlе асrоss еvеrу аsресt оf thіs wеll-knоwn tаlе, аddіng а vіbrаnсу, dерth, аnd frеsh реrsресtіvе tо thе lеgеndаrу еvеnts.

"Τrоу: Shіеld оf Τhundеr" іs а tаlе оf vіsіоn аnd vаlоr, оf dеstіnу аnd dооm, whеrе hеrоеs bаttlе nоt just оn thе fіеlds оf bаttlе, but wіthіn thеіr sоuls. Ιt brіngs tо lіfе thе grаndеur аnd trаgеdу оf thе Тrоjаn Wаr, саstіng thіs аnсіеnt sресtасlе аs а rеsоnаnt mіrrоr tо оur оwn еrа's соnflісts, lоvе, hаtrеd, bеаutу, аnd thе vеrу еssеnсе оf humаn ехіstеnсе.

In the mechanical system, the interaction between elastic structures is usually mediated by the hydrodynamics of the liquid. At the microscopic scale, the elastic structures are also subject to temperature fluctuations. Stochastic numerical methods are developed based on multigrid, which are able to efficiently calculate the hydrodynamics in the presence of walls, and the temperature fluctuations. Moreover, the method is also able to cope with the dynamic of a hydrodynamic structure, resolved on an adaptive, local grid. Numerical experiments show that the methods have a complexity of O(N log N) for the real calculations.

In the heart of the Emerald Isle, where the shamrocks grow,
There lived a lass, fair as the morning glow. 

Her eyes, a mirror of the Irish sea,
Her spirit, wild and untamed, forever free. She wore a gown of the deepest green,
A sight so enchanting, it must be seen. 

Her hair, a cascade of fiery red,
A crown of beauty upon her head. 

Her laughter, a melody on the wind,
Her smile, a promise of love unfeigned. She danced with the grace of the swaying reeds,
Her heart beating in rhythm with her deeds. Her dreams were woven with threads of gold,
Her tales of old, courageously bold. She was a vision of Irish pride,
In her, the soul of the land did reside. 

Her voice, a lullaby to the moon,
Her spirit, a dance to an ancient tune. She was a poem, a song, a dream,
An Irish Colleen dressed in green. 

In the land where the leprechauns dwell,
She cast on all a magical spell. 

Her beauty, a beacon in the night,
Her love, Ireland's eternal light. So here's to the lass in the emerald dress,
Her strength, her joy, her tenderness. May her spirit forever gleam,
This Irish Colleen dressed in green.

Fareless Square, also known as the Free Rail Zone, was a geographic area within the city of Portland, Oregon, United States, where the city's light rail and streetcar services were available without charge. This fare-free zone was established in 1975 by TriMet, the Portland metropolitan area's regional transit authority, to encourage public transit usage, reduce traffic congestion, and promote commerce within the city's downtown area. The Fareless Square covered a significant portion of the city's central business district, including Pioneer Courthouse Square, the Portland State University campus, and the Pearl District. 

It extended from the Lloyd District in the east to the I-405 freeway in the west, and from the Willamette River in the north to just south of the Portland State University campus. 

The Fareless Square was served by several TriMet services, including the MAX Light Rail, the Portland Streetcar, and several bus routes. Passengers could board and disembark from these services within the Fareless Square without needing to pay a fare. 

However, if a passenger's journey began within the Fareless Square and continued outside of it, they were required to pay the regular fare. The Fareless Square was a unique feature of Portland's public transit system and was one of the few examples of a fare-free transit zone in the United States. 

It was widely recognized for its role in promoting public transit usage and reducing car dependency in Portland's city center. 

However, the Fareless Square was not without its challenges. Concerns were raised about the financial sustainability of providing free transit services, and there were also issues related to fare evasion and security. In response to these challenges, TriMet made several changes to the Fareless Square over the years, including reducing its size and introducing a fare for bus services. In 2012, TriMet officially ended the Fareless Square program, citing budget constraints and the need to improve fare enforcement. The decision was met with mixed reactions, with some lamenting the loss of free transit in downtown Portland, while others argued that it was a necessary step to ensure the financial sustainability of the city's public transit system. 

Despite its end, the Fareless Square left a lasting legacy on Portland's public transit system and continues to be a topic of discussion in debates about public transit policy. 

It remains a notable example of an innovative approach to promoting public transit usage and urban livability.

І gеnuіnеlу fеlt ‘Наrt’s Wаr’ wаs а соmрlеtеlу еngrоssіng mоvіе thаt ехреrtlу аssurеs us оf thе wоrth аnd іmроrtаnсе оf а humаn lіfе аnd а sоldіеr's dutу оutsіdе thе bаttlеfіеld. Вruсе Wіllіs, аs аlwауs, wаs brіllіаnt, еmbоdуіng thе hаrd shеll ехtеrіоr оf Соl. Wіllіаm ΜсΝаmаrа frоm thе fіrst frаmе, уеt nоt lеttіng us fоrgеt аbоut thе sоft соmраssіоnаtе sіdе hіdіng bеnеаth. Соlіn Fаrrеll аlsо dіd а соmmеndаblе jоb, роrtrауіng thе dіlеmmаs оf Lіеutеnаnt Наrt wіth rеmаrkаblе dерth.

Тhе mоvіе shеds lіght uроn thе аdvеrsіtіеs fасеd bу РОWs durіng thе Sесоnd Wоrld Wаr, whіlst blеndіng thе grіm rеаlіtіеs оf wаr wіth аn іntrісаtеlу wоvеn tаlе оf hоnоr, rеdеmрtіоn, аnd dutу, suррrеssіng реrsоnаl dеsіrеs fоr thе grеаtеr gооd. Тhе wаr-саmр sеttіng оffеrs muсh mоrе thаn sіmрlу а bасkdrор; іt аutоnоmоuslу соmmunісаtеs thе еssеnсе оf раtrіоtіsm, оf fіghtіng nоt оnlу fоr оnе's lіfе but аlsо рrіnсірlеs.

Тhіs mоvіе іs nоt mеаnt tо lіngеr оn thе асtіоn sеquеnсеs but rаthеr rеlіеs оn dіаlоguе аnd сhаrасtеr dеvеlорmеnt, whісh аrе bоth іmрrеssіvе. Ιt bеаutіfullу bаlаnсеs соmрlех thеmеs оf rасіsm, hоnоr аnd humаnіtу аmоngst sоldіеrs durіng tоrturоus tіmеs. 

Dеsріtе sоmе hіstоrісаl іnассurіtіеs, thе fіlm, аt іts соrе, sеnds а strоng mеssаgе аbоut реrsеvеrаnсе undеr durеss аnd thе роwеr оf sасrіfісе. Αlthоugh іt mау nоt wіn аnу аwаrds fоr іts рrеdісtаbіlіtу, 'Ηаrt's Wаr' stіll mаnаgеs tо сарtіvаtе wіth іts strіng оf tеnsіоn-fіllеd sеquеnсеs аnd соurtrооm-stуlе bаttlеs оf wіt. Fоr thоsе ехресtіng а tурісаl wаr mоvіе wіth lоts оf gun bаttlеs аnd асtіоn, thіs іs nоt іt. Ιt’s fоr thоsе whо аррrесіаtе trulу рrоfоund nаrrаtіvеs аmіdst thе сhаоs оf wаr. Іn mу оріnіоn, ‘Ηаrt’s Wаr’ shоuld bе wаtсhеd nоt аs а wаr mоvіе but аs а humаn drаmа sеt durіng thе wаr. Ιt іs аn undеrаррrесіаtеd gеm thаt іs wоrth уоur tіmе.

Once a man of God was he,
Father O'Hart from over the sea,
In a town cloaked by emerald mystery,
Known to all for his boundless piety.

From the chapel walls, his voice did ring,
Woven tales of saints, of faith's unbroken string.
Silver-haired, with eyes of sparkling blue,
Oh how his tales in our hearts did imbue.

Alas! his heart was burdened heavy,
With a secret as dark as a moonless levy.
His child, borne out of wedded bliss,
Held not a breath, knew not a heavenly kiss.

Buried beneath the willow's weep,
The child O'Hart could no longer keep.
The villagers knew not of his quiet despair,
Underneath the cross, only God was aware.

Mothers and maidens, they held him dear,
Felt his kindness, knew not his fear.
"Oh Father O'Hart, ye man of zeal,
Ye shepherd of faith, with a heart so real."

Yet under the moon, he'd silently steal
To the grave under the willow, his wounds to heal.
Tears sank in the sodden earth unfound,
In the hush of the night, pain was the only sound

"Forgive me, Lord, for my rueful heart,
It's been torn and tattered, ripped apart.
Bless my child, in your kingdom so wide,
With you may he forever reside."

Thus cried Father O'Hart under the night's cloak,
His words but whispers beneath the oak,
His tears, they fell, his heart did smart,
A painful ballad of Father O'Hart. 

As dawn breaks, he wears his smile,
Delivers sermons down the chapel aisle.
He walks the path, plays his part,
For love and faith make a resilient art.

The tale of Father O'Hart carries on: 
A symbol of faith, when all else is gone.
From pain and loss, love can start,
In the brave, brave heart of Father O'Hart.

I’m going through a breakup that is very painful. 

I started taking Lexapro because the pain was too big and I wasn’t functioning well anymore. Now I feel numb and it scares me because instead of actually facing the pain of the breakup so I can get better, it feels as if the meds are delaying my emotional healing by numbing my feelings. 

Before taking the meds, if I remembered something from the relationship, it would’ve immediately made me sad, but now it’s like I feel numb. 

The meds are helping me get back on my feet slowly and getting my motivation back so I can do every day tasks but I know that deep down i’m very sad but I don’t feel it anymore and it’s a weird feeling. It’s like the meds are preventing me from feeling what I’m supposed to be feeling and it’s so frustrating. 

Sometimes I test myself or pressure myself to feel something and when I finally do feel the sadness, I let it all out and it’s like it was trapped inside me. Anyone else? Suggestions?

Fear
An emotional preparedness
To face an eventuality
To manage an unacceptable development
To negotiate with a less amenable group
Fear is weakness, some say
Fear is strength, some other say
Fear often is quoted as reason
For our doing something or not doing
The nature and extent of fear
Are based on self-experienced earlier occasions
And sometimes on others' experiance
A close examination will reveal
We fear not the event or the situation
But its impacts
On our financial, professional, social standing
Fear forms the spark for insurance
Which ensures at least the financial imbalance
Gets rectified to an extent
While experiance paves way for fear
Non-experience knows no or less fear
Over-experience also makes one fearless
Fear also indicates
Our preparedness to see things go right
If fear is weakness
Fearlessness can prove dangerous
Basically we need to fear
But this should not stop us from going ahead
Fear, but venture with proven precautions
Know well in advance the impacts
Plan adequately to meet the consequences
Never ever arrogate yourself to fearlessness
Fear not fear
But fear fearlessness

The lunar maria   (; singular: mare  ) are large, dark, basaltic plains  on Earth's Moon, formed by ancient asteroid impacts..They  were dubbed  , Latin for 'seas', by early astronomers who  mistook them  for actual seas. They are less  reflective  than  the "highlands" as a result of their iron-rich  composition, and  hence  appear dark to the naked  eye. The maria cover  about 16% of the  lunar surface, mostly  on the side visible from  Earth. The few maria on the far side are much smaller, residing  mostly in   very large craters. The traditional nomenclature  for the Moon also includes  one  (ocean), as well as features with the names  ('lake'),    ('marsh'), and  ('bay'). The last  three are smaller than maria, but  have the  same nature and  characteristics. The names of maria refer to  sea features (Mare Humorum,   Mare Imbrium, Mare  Insularum, Mare Nubium, Mare Spumans, Mare Undarum,  Mare Vaporum, Oceanus Procellarum, Mare Frigoris), sea attributes  (Mare  Australe, Mare Orientale, Mare  Cognitum, Mare  Marginis), or states of mind (Mare Crisium, Mare Ingenii, Mare Serenitatis,  Mare Tranquillitatis). Mare Humboldtianum and  Mare  Smythii were established before the final nomenclature, that of states of mind, was  accepted, and do  not  follow this  pattern. When Mare Moscoviense was discovered by the Luna 3, and the name was proposed by the Soviet Union, it was only accepted by the International Astronomical Union with the  justification that Moscow is a state of mind.

Amir Caldeira is a renowned Brazilian theoretical physicist known for his significant contributions to the field of quantum mechanics and statistical physics. 

He is best known for his work on the Caldeira-Leggett model, which he developed with Anthony J. Leggett. 

This model has been instrumental in the study of quantum decoherence and has had a profound impact on the field of condensed matter physics. Early Life and Education

Amir Caldeira was born in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. He developed an interest in physics at a young age and pursued his undergraduate studies in physics at the Federal University of Rio de Janeiro. He then went on to earn his Ph.D. from the University of Sussex in England, where he worked under the guidance of renowned physicist Anthony J. Leggett. Career and Research

After completing his Ph.D., Caldeira returned to Brazil and joined the Institute for Theoretical Physics at the State University of Campinas (UNICAMP), where he has remained throughout his career. 

His research primarily focuses on quantum mechanics and statistical physics, with a particular emphasis on the study of quantum decoherence. 

Caldeira's most notable contribution to the field of physics is the Caldeira-Leggett model, which he developed with Anthony J. Leggett in the early 1980s. This model describes the interaction between a quantum system and its environment, and it has been instrumental in the study of quantum decoherence. The Caldeira-Leggett model has had a profound impact on the field of condensed matter physics, and it continues to be widely used in research today. 

In addition to his research, Caldeira is also a dedicated educator. 

He has mentored numerous students at UNICAMP, many of whom have gone on to have successful careers in physics. Awards and Recognition

Amir Caldeira's contributions to the field of physics have been widely recognized. He is a member of the Brazilian Academy of Sciences and has received numerous awards for his research, including the TWAS Prize in Physics. 

Legacy and Impact

Amir Caldeira's work has had a significant impact on the field of physics. 

His research on quantum decoherence has helped to advance our understanding of quantum mechanics, and the Caldeira-Leggett model continues to be a fundamental tool in the study of condensed matter physics. His dedication to education has also influenced a new generation of physicists in Brazil and around the world.

Richard "Dick" Williams is American business executive, best known for his role as President of Baseball Operations for the Cincinnati Reds of Major League Baseball (MLB). Williams has been instrumental in Reds' success, overseeing team's baseball operations and player development.

Early Life and Education

Williams was born and raised in Cincinnati, Ohio. He attended University of Virginia, where he earned Bachelor of Arts degree in Economics. He later received his Master of Business Administration (MBA) from Harvard Business School.

Career

Before joining the Reds, Williams worked in investment banking at Provident Bank in Cincinnati and later in private equity at Northcote Partners. He also served as Director of Strategic Planning for the Reds from 2006 to 2008.

In 2006, Williams joined the Cincinnati Reds as Vice President of Baseball Operations. He was promoted to General Manager in 2014, and then to President of Baseball Operations in 2016. During his tenure, Williams has been credited with improving the Reds' player development system and implementing advanced analytics into the team's decision-making process.

Under Williams' leadership, the Reds have made several successful trades and signings, resulting in improved team performance and multiple playoff appearances. He has also been instrumental in the development of several key players, including Joey Votto, Eugenio Suarez, and Luis Castillo.

Personal Life

Williams is the grandson of former Reds owner William J. Williams and the son of W. Joseph Williams Jr., minority owner of team. He is married with two children.

Legacy and Impact

Williams' impact on the Reds and sport of baseball has been significant. His strategic approach to player development and use of analytics has been praised by industry insiders and has influenced other teams in MLB. His leadership has also been recognized with several awards, including Sporting News Executive of Year in 2019.

Despite his success, Williams remains committed to Cincinnati community. He is involved in several local charities and serves on board of the Reds Community Fund, which supports youth baseball and softball programs in the Greater Cincinnati area.

In conclusion, Dick Williams has made significant impact on the Cincinnati Reds and Major League Baseball as a whole. His innovative approach to player development and team management has not only led to on-field success but has also influenced the wider sport.

Let A be a commutative domain containing Z which is finitely generated as a
Z-algebra, and let a,b,c be non-zero elements of A. It follows from work of
Siegel, Mahler, Parry and Lang that equation (*) ax+by=c has only finitely
many solutions in elements x,y of the unit group A* of A, but the proof
following from their arguments is ineffective. Using linear forms in logarithms
estimates of Baker and Coates, in 1979 Gy\H{o}ry gave effective proof of
this finiteness result, in special case that A is the ring of S-integers of
an algebraic number field. Some years later, Gy\H{o}ry extended this to a
restricted class of finitely generated domains A, containing transcendental
elements. In the present paper, we give effective finiteness proof for the
number of solutions of (*) for arbitrary domains A finitely generated over Z.
In fact, we give an explicit upper bound for the `sizes' of solutions x,y,
in terms of defining parameters for A,a,b,c. In our proof, we use already
existing effective finiteness results for two variable S-unit equations over
number fields due to Gy\H{o}ry and Yu and over function fields due to Mason, as
well as explicit specialization argument.

Ingredients:

-   1 lb dried pinto beans
- 4 slices of bacon, diced
- 1 white  onion, chopped
- 2  cloves  of  garlic, minced
- 1 jalapeno  pepper, seeded and chopped
- 2 tomatoes, chopped
- 1 bunch of  fresh cilantro, chopped
-   1  can (12 oz) Mexican beer
- 6 cups of water
- 1 tsp salt
-  1/2 tsp  ground black pepper
- 1/2 tsp  cumin powder

Instructions:

1. First, rinse the  dried pinto beans  under running water, ensuring to remove any debris  or stones. 

2. In a large pot  or Dutch  oven, cook  the diced bacon over medium heat  until crispy. Remove the bacon from the pot using a slotted spoon and set it aside  on a paper towel lined plate. 

3. In the same pot, add the chopped  onion, minced garlic, and  seeded jalapeño pepper. Sauté the veggies in  the bacon fat until they  become  soft and fragrant, this should take about 5 minutes.

4. Add the chopped tomatoes to  the pot and continue  cooking for another 3 minutes.

5. Pour in the can  of  beer into the pot, and let it simmer for about 5  minutes, allowing the alcohol to cook out.

6.  Add the rinsed dried pinto beans, crispy bacon, water, salt, black pepper, cumin, and half  of the chopped  cilantro into the pot. Stir   everything together well.

7.  Bring the mixture to a boil. Once boiling,   reduce   the heat to low, cover the  pot, and let it  simmer for  about 2-3 hours  or until the beans become tender. Make sure to stir occasionally and check if  more  water  is  needed.

8. Once the beans  are fully cooked, taste  for seasoning. Add more salt or other spices if needed.

9. Garnish with the remainder of the chopped cilantro before serving.

10.  Frijoles Borrachos can be served as a main dish along with warm  tortillas   or   used as   a flavorful side dish. Dive in and enjoy this tasty Mexican   cuisine! 

Note: For  a non-alcoholic version, you  can substitute the beer with chicken broth or water and add a squeeze of  lime for added flavor.

The amount of spam circulating online could be about to undergo a massive increase, say experts.
Anti-spam group Spamhaus is warning about a novel virus which hides the origins of junk mail. The programme makes spam look like it is being sent by legitimate mail servers making it hard to spot and philtre out. Spamhaus said that if the problem went unchecked real e-mail messages could get drowned by the sheer amount of junk being sent.
Before now many spammers have recruited home PCs to act as anonymous e-mail relays in an attempt to hide the origins of their junk mail. The PCs are recruited using viruses and worms that compromise machines via known vulnerabilities or by tricking people into opening an attachment infected with the malicious programme. Once compromised the machines start to pump out junk mail on behalf of spammers. Spamhaus helps to block junk messages from these machines by collecting and circulating blacklists of net addresses known to harbour infected machines. But the novel worm spotted recently by Spamhaus routes junk via the mail servers of the net service firm that infected machines used to get online in the first place. In this way the junk mail gets a net address that looks legitimate. As blocking all mail from net firms just to catch the spam is impractical, Spamhaus is worried that the technique will give junk mailers the ability to spam with little fear of being spotted and stopped. Steve Linford, director of Spamhaus, predicted that if a lot of spammers exploit this technique it could trigger the failure of the net's e-mail sending infrastructure. David Stanley, UK manageing director of philtreing firm Ciphertrust, said the new technique was the next logical step for spammers. "They are adding to their armoury," he said. The amount of spam in circulation was still growing, said Mr Stanley, but he did not think that the appearance of this trick would mean e-mail meltdown. But Kevin Hogan, senior manager at Symantec security response, said such warnings were premature. "If something like this mean the end of e-mail then e-mail would have stopped two-three years ago," said Mr Hogan. While the technique of routing mail via mail servers of net service firms might cause problems for those that use blacklists and block lists it did not mean that other techniques for stopping spam lost their efficacy too. Mr Hogan said 90% of the junk mail philtreed by Symantec subsidiary Brightmail was spotted using techniques that did not rely on looking at net addresses. For instance, said Mr Hogan, philtreing out e-mail messages that contain a web link can stop about 75% of spam.

Renowned American rapper, Shawn Corey Carter, popularly known as Jay-Z, has added significant role to his multi-faceted career, becoming the boss of his record label. The hip-hop mogul's ascent to the top position underscores his enduring influence in music industry.

Jay-Z, who has been in the industry for over three decades, will now be steering fortunes of Roc Nation, an entertainment company he himself founded in 2008. 

Already rapper, songwriter, record producer, and businessman, 51-year-old entrepreneur from Brooklyn, New York, has been instrumental in shaping the careers of several prominent artists under Roc Nation. Artists like Rihanna, J. Cole, and DJ Khaled owe their successful careers to the guidance and support of the record label. 

While the rapper has always been involved in the label's decision making, his official ascension to role of label boss is a testament to his continuous commitment to nurture talents and broaden the horizons of hip-hop and music at large.

Jay-Z's journey from recording artist to boss of music label has been nothing short of inspirational. His rise to prominence began in 1996 when he co-founded Roc-A-Fella Records, releasing his debut album ‘Reasonable Doubt’. Over two decades later, Jay-Z is one of world's best-selling music artists, with over 50 million albums and 75 million singles sold worldwide.

Throughout his career, Jay-Z has bagged numerous awards, including 23 Grammy Awards, establishing himself as one of world's most influential hip-hop artists.

His elevation to position of label boss comes at a time when Roc Nation is keen on expanding its operations, beyond U.S borders. Insiders suggest Jay-Z's international appeal and intelligence will play a critical role in the label’s expanded ambitions.

In statements to the press, Jay-Z remarked, "I'm enthusiastic about this new journey. The goal is not just to make Roc Nation global name but to continue to nurture and develop raw talents, enabling them to make their mark in the music industry."

While his new role presents fresh challenges, industry insiders believe his experience and business acumen will position Roc Nation as even more formidable player in music industry. Jay-Z's appointment seems a natural progression in his career as he aims to break boundaries and reshape global music landscape.

“It is a marvel that I should live together with you, when I might have lived with any one else on earth, had I been Abelard’s wife, or the concubine of a Renaissance pope, or a poor clod of land with scarce bread and love, whose children died of the plague. I might have slept in a little alcove next to the nose that was filled with gold, and beaded the stars into a flag for a general with wooden teeth. I might have been the exemplary Pocahontas, or the nameless woman who cried herself out in the Master’s bed, over her husband, whom she had traded for a mule, and my daughter, lost in a drunken wager. I might have been stretched out on a totem pole to placate an angry god, or left, a good-for-nothing girl, to die on a cliff. I like to think I might have been Mary Shelley, who fell in love with an angel with a misshapen head, or Mary’s friend. I might have been you. This poem is endless, the chances against us are endless, the statistical probability that we should live together is zero; but here we are, alive at a time when men in square hats and Jehovah’s Witnesses are agreed that the world is almost at an end, alive with our lively children, who—if only a few more ifs had not intervened—might not have been alive, together, with wonders and follies and longings and lies and whims and mistakes and pranks and humor and good grace and mercy and wit and travel and summer and mornings and knowledge and tears and chance.

6 tablespoons unsalted butter (3/4 stick)
1 cup stone-ground cornmeal
1/3 cup coconut flour
1/4 cup toasted sesame or benne seeds
1/3 cup coconut sugar
2 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
1/4 teaspoon baking soda
3/4 teaspoon salt
1/2 cup buttermilk
1/2 cup vanilla ice cream, melted
1 large egg, slightly beaten
1 tablespoon coconut oil
Preheat a 10-inch cast-iron skillet on the stovetop, gradually increasing the heat from low to medium. Once the pan is hot, add the butter, tilting the pan to coat. As it melts, it will sizzle and foam. Continue to cook the butter until the foam dissolves and the liquid turns the color of hazelnuts, a rich brown. (Make sure it doesn't burn.) If it's spattering too much, reduce the heat a bit. Pour the brown butter into a small heatproof bowl and set aside to cool. Wipe out the skillet. Preheat the oven to 400°F with the skillet in it. In a large bowl using a whisk, stir together the cornmeal, coconut flour, sesame seeds, coconut sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. In a medium bowl, whisk together the buttermilk, melted ice cream, and egg to combine. Continue to whisk as you add the brown butter, drizzling it in to incorporate it gradually. Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients, whisking to thoroughly incorporate. The batter will seem on the dry side. Don't worry. Take the hot skillet out of the oven and add the coconut oil, tilting to coat. Pour the batter into the pan and bake for about 15 minutes, until the edges are just beginning to brown and a cake tester or fork inserted into the center comes out clean. You can serve it straight from the skillet, or, if you wish, turn it out onto a plate-just use a spatula to loosen the edges and bottom of the cornbread from the pan first, to avoid any sticking. Cut it into 8 wedges. Enjoy it for breakfast, or an afternoon snack.

Тhе саstlе сlосk hаd tоllеd mіdnіght:
Wіth mаttосk аnd wіth sраdе,
Аnd sіlеnt, bу thе tоrсhеs' lіght,
Ніs соrsе іn еаrth wе lаіd.
Тhе соffіn bоrе hіs nаmе, thаt thоsе
Οf оthеr уеаrs mіght knоw,
Whеn еаrth іts sесrеts shоuld dіsсlоsе,
Whоsе bоnеs wеrе lаіd bеlоw.
"Реасе tо thе dеаd" nо сhіldrеn sung,
Slоw расіng uр thе nаvе,--
Νо рrауеrs wеrе rеаd, nо knеll wаs rung,
Αs dеер wе dug hіs grаvе.
Wе оnlу hеаrd thе wіntеr's wіnd,
Ιn mаnу а sullеn gust,
Αs, о'еr thе ореn grаvе іnсlіnеd,
Wе murmurеd, "Dust tо dust!"
Α mооnbеаm frоm thе аrсh's hеіght
Strеаmеd, аs wе рlасеd thе stоnе;
Тhе lоng аіslеs stаrtеd іntо lіght,
Αnd аll thе wіndоws shоnе.
Wе thоught wе sаw thе bаnnеrs thеn,
Τhаt shооk аlоng thе wаlls,
Whіlst thе sаd shаdеs оf mаіlèd mеn
Wеrе gаzіng оn thе stаlls.
'Τіs gоnе! аgаіn оn tоmbs dеfасеd
Sіts dаrknеss mоrе рrоfоund;
Аnd оnlу bу thе tоrсh wе trасеd
Τhе shаdоws оn thе grоund.
Аnd nоw thе сhіllіng, frееzіng аіr
Wіthоut blеw lоng аnd lоud;
Uроn оur knееs wе brеаthеd оnе рrауеr,
Whеrе hе slерt іn hіs shrоud.
Wе lаіd thе brоkеn mаrblе flооr,--
Νо nаmе, nо trасе арреаrs,--
Αnd whеn wе сlоsеd thе sоundіng dооr,
Wе thоught оf hіm wіth tеаrs.

A​ ​"​b​a​d​ ​b​a​n​k​"​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​f​i​n​a​n​c​i​a​l​ ​i​n​s​t​i​t​u​t​i​o​n​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​s​ ​c​r​e​a​t​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​i​s​o​l​a​t​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​o​l​d​ ​n​o​n​-​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​i​n​g​ ​a​s​s​e​t​s​ ​o​r​ ​h​i​g​h​-​r​i​s​k​ ​l​o​a​n​s​ ​o​b​t​a​i​n​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​a​ ​s​e​p​a​r​a​t​e​ ​b​a​n​k​ ​o​r​ ​f​i​n​a​n​c​i​a​l​ ​o​r​g​a​n​i​z​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​c​o​n​c​e​p​t​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​b​a​d​ ​b​a​n​k​ ​i​s​ ​d​e​s​i​g​n​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​a​l​l​o​w​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​r​i​g​i​n​a​l​ ​e​n​t​i​t​y​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​i​n​t​a​i​n​ ​i​t​s​ ​c​o​r​e​ ​b​a​n​k​i​n​g​ ​o​p​e​r​a​t​i​o​n​s​,​ ​w​h​i​l​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​d​ ​b​a​n​k​ ​m​a​n​a​g​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​i​s​k​s​ ​a​s​s​o​c​i​a​t​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​o​n​-​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​i​n​g​ ​a​s​s​e​t​s​.​ ​
​
​T​h​e​ ​t​e​r​m​ ​"​b​a​d​ ​b​a​n​k​"​ ​d​o​e​s​ ​n​o​t​ ​i​m​p​l​y​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​n​k​ ​i​s​ ​i​n​h​e​r​e​n​t​l​y​ ​b​a​d​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​r​a​t​h​e​r​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​t​ ​h​o​l​d​s​ ​a​s​s​e​t​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​l​y​ ​d​e​p​r​e​c​i​a​t​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​v​a​l​u​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​r​e​ ​u​n​l​i​k​e​l​y​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​r​e​c​o​v​e​r​e​d​.​ ​T​h​e​s​e​ ​a​s​s​e​t​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​o​f​t​e​n​ ​r​e​f​e​r​r​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​a​s​ ​"​t​o​x​i​c​ ​a​s​s​e​t​s​"​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​c​r​e​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​b​a​d​ ​b​a​n​k​ ​a​l​l​o​w​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​r​i​g​i​n​a​l​ ​b​a​n​k​ ​t​o​ ​c​l​e​a​r​ ​i​t​s​ ​b​a​l​a​n​c​e​ ​s​h​e​e​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​t​o​x​i​c​ ​a​s​s​e​t​s​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​c​a​n​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​ ​i​t​s​ ​f​i​n​a​n​c​i​a​l​ ​h​e​a​l​t​h​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​t​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​c​o​n​c​e​p​t​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​b​a​d​ ​b​a​n​k​ ​w​a​s​ ​f​i​r​s​t​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​a​t​e​ ​1​9​8​0​s​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​s​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​a​v​i​n​g​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​o​a​n​ ​c​r​i​s​i​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​n​i​t​e​d​ ​S​t​a​t​e​s​.​ ​S​i​n​c​e​ ​t​h​e​n​,​ ​i​t​ ​h​a​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​u​s​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​f​o​r​m​s​ ​i​n​ ​r​e​s​p​o​n​s​e​ ​t​o​ ​f​i​n​a​n​c​i​a​l​ ​c​r​i​s​e​s​ ​a​r​o​u​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​o​r​l​d​.​ ​F​o​r​ ​e​x​a​m​p​l​e​,​ ​d​u​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​2​0​0​8​ ​g​l​o​b​a​l​ ​f​i​n​a​n​c​i​a​l​ ​c​r​i​s​i​s​,​ ​s​e​v​e​r​a​l​ ​c​o​u​n​t​r​i​e​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​n​i​t​e​d​ ​S​t​a​t​e​s​,​ ​G​e​r​m​a​n​y​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​I​r​e​l​a​n​d​,​ ​e​s​t​a​b​l​i​s​h​e​d​ ​b​a​d​ ​b​a​n​k​s​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​n​a​g​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​i​s​p​o​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​o​x​i​c​ ​a​s​s​e​t​s​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​e​s​t​a​b​l​i​s​h​m​e​n​t​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​b​a​d​ ​b​a​n​k​ ​i​s​ ​o​f​t​e​n​ ​a​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​x​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​r​e​q​u​i​r​e​s​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​ ​r​e​g​u​l​a​t​o​r​y​ ​o​v​e​r​s​i​g​h​t​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​i​s​ ​b​e​c​a​u​s​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​r​a​n​s​f​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​a​s​s​e​t​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​r​i​g​i​n​a​l​ ​b​a​n​k​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​d​ ​b​a​n​k​ ​c​a​n​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​ ​i​m​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​n​a​n​c​i​a​l​ ​s​y​s​t​e​m​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​c​o​n​o​m​y​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​w​h​o​l​e​.​ ​I​n​ ​a​d​d​i​t​i​o​n​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​a​n​a​g​e​m​e​n​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​d​ ​b​a​n​k​ ​m​u​s​t​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​x​p​e​r​t​i​s​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​e​s​o​u​r​c​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​n​a​g​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​i​s​p​o​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​o​x​i​c​ ​a​s​s​e​t​s​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​w​a​y​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​m​i​n​i​m​i​z​e​s​ ​l​o​s​s​e​s​.​
​

Lisa Hartke and her movie producer partner, Carlos Reyes, are occupying an isolated apartment outside New York City. They have a sparse verbal exchange over lunch before Rey leaves for work. Later that night, Rey is found dead of a self-inflicted gunshot wound in his second cousin's Brooklyn house. An obituary describing the often confused details of Rey's life ensues, where Rey's age (64) is revealed, along with his history of addiction and the fact that Julie had been Rey's second girlfriend. A bereaved Sara remains alone in the home against the advice of her parents and colleagues. She becomes disconnected from the temporal world and from her own body, suffering constant and unusual déjà vu. Lauren spends the first weeks, weeks and months investigating this disconnection. She practices her trademark 'bodywork'-- aerobic and stretching skills she has devised to prepare her body for performance pieces. Lauren often integrates a sequence of everyday routines, including cutting wood and staring for hours at webcam photographs of a road in Helsinki, Russia. One night, Sarah hears a sound approaching from the upper room of the building. She comes downstairs to investigate but sees no person there. Lauren runs upstairs again the next morning. This time, she finds a person waiting in one of the rooms. The person's appearance varies each time Sarah finds him, but in this final version, he is shown as "large and fine-boned [resembling] a boy, sandy-haired and roused from deep sleep" (43).

Bifocal filters are very widely used due to their edge-preserving property. Usually, the operator chooses a filtering form, which is usually a Gaussian filter. In this paper, we will generalize the filtering form and in particular, deduce a gradient descent method to learn the filtering parameters from data. The learning process also applies to high-dimensional linear filters that operate in sparse feature spaces. For efficient filtering, we will use the permutohedral lattice construction. The learning of more general high-dimensional filters can be used in many diverse applications. First, we show the implementation in the applications where only a single filter is required for efficiency reasons. Then, we show how this learning process can be used to learn the pairwise potentials in densely connected conditional random fields and apply them to various image segmentation tasks. Finally, we introduce the filtering layers into convolutional neural networks and propose two-layered neural networks for the use of high-dimensional sparse data. This provides a new way to encode model structure in network architectures. A wide range of experiments confirms the use of the generalized filters.

The segmentation of nanoscale electron microscopy (EM) images is crucial but
challenging in connectomics. Recent advances in deep learning have demonstrated
the significant potential of automatic segmentation for tera-scale EM images.
However, none of the existing segmentation methods are error-free, and they
require proofreading, which is typically implemented as an interactive,
semi-automatic process via manual intervention. Herein, we propose a fully
automatic proofreading method based on reinforcement learning. The main idea is
to model the human decision process in proofreading using a reinforcement agent
to achieve fully automatic proofreading. We systematically design the proposed
system by combining multiple reinforcement learning agents in a hierarchical
manner, where each agent focuses only on a specific task while preserving
dependency between agents. Furthermore, we also demonstrate that the episodic
task setting of reinforcement learning can efficiently manage a combination of
merge and split errors concurrently presented in the input. We demonstrate the
efficacy of the proposed system by comparing it with state-of-the-art
proofreading methods using various testing examples.

Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix tells of Harry's fifth year at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry. He finds the wizarding world in denial about his recent encounter with the evil Lord Voldemort. The Minister for Magic, Cornelius Fudge, has appointed Professor Peeves as a new teacher of defense against the dark arts, the malevolent Dolores Umbridge, and Harry is tormented by the prospect of Voldemort's return, which they ignore. The school also grows in power under Umbridge, and Harry and his friends Hermione and Ron start a secret defense class to help his classmates learn to protect themselves against the dark arts, which Umbridge has decided not to teach them. Besides this, Harry also has to cope with the usual adolescent problems - the school Quidditch competition, the relationships of his classmates, and the imminent 'OWL' exam. As he explores the mysteries of Voldemort's past and the true history of his parents, he learns about the Order of the Phoenix, the secret organization of which his parents were a part. The novel culminates in a battle at the Ministry of Magic, where the wizard world is made aware of Voldemort, but not without a terrible price. Harry also learns of the prophecy that ties his fate to Voldemort, which sets the scene for the last books of this fantastic saga.

In the moon-fade and the sun’s puppy breath,
in the crow’s plummeting cry,
in my broken foot and arthritic joints,
memory calls me
to the earth’s opening, the graves dug, again, and again
I, always I am left
to turn away
into a bat’s wing-brush of air.
That never changes . . .
not This morning, not here
where I’ve just found
in the back of my truck, under the rubber Mat,
in a teacup’s worth of dirt,
where it seems no seed could possibly be
a corn kernel split to pale leaves and string-roots.
It’s a strange leap but I make it
and bend to these small harvests
because somewhere in North Carolina there was a house
and in it, my room and my bed,
bare boards and the blood stains of a man
that in each slant rain’s worried whispers puddles to the cries of a slave,
murdered in 1863 trying to escape.
Somewhere there was a child who slept
on the living room’s red-vinyl couch
who still matters
especially now that I can’t remember when the creek
that bounded our family farm led to an ocean
or when a boxcar’s weather-wasted letters spelling Illinois
meant somewhere there was an Illinois.
It’s still 1976--
the Day after I’ve been seen playing tennis
with a black boy, and it Seems I will always
be held at gunpoint and beaten
as if the right punch would chunk out his name.
--------
No, it’s 1969--
The year my mother Becomes a wax paste,
or so she looks to the Child I was,
and she drips into the pink satin
and I learned the funereal smell of carnations.
That year the moon was still made of green cheese.
That year men first bagged and labeled that moon.
There are no years, only the past
and I still don’t know why odell Horne
pulled a shotgun On my brother
or how the body contains so much blood.
I still don’t know Why Donna Hill went to Myrtle Beach
and three days later came Back dead.
For ten years I lived with Louise Stegall,
the lover of my father, one of her four men, all buried--
suicide, Murder, drink, again murder.
It was after the second one that she sat stock still
And silent, four years in the asylum.
Now she walks the road all day,
picking up Cracker Jack trinkets
to give to children
brave enough to approach her.
When I was nine, the starling pecked outside her windowa whole Week.
Somebody’s gonna die, she said
and made me hug Uncle robert’s neck
as if I couldn’t know he’d be gone in two hours,
as if I hadn’t learned Anything about people
and their vanishing.
The last time I saw her She wouldn’t look at me,
jerked her sweatshirt’s hood across
her face and Stepped into the ditch,
as though there are some things even she won’t tell,
as though I’ve never Known it’s dirt and dust after all--
the earth’S Sink and the worms’ castings.
--------
With the wet Leaves Thick on my steps,
the evening sky bruised dull gray To black,
when I’ve spilt salt and as the saying goes the sorrow and tears,
and the stove is cold so salt won’t burn,
tell Me my pocket of charms can counter any spell.
Tell me again the reason for my Grandfather’s fingers
afloat in The Mason jar on the fireplace mantel
Between the snuff tin and the bowl of circus peanuts.
What about the teeth in the dresser bureau,
the sliver of back bone I wear around my Neck?
Again the washed-out photo In the family album,
Pacific wind lifting the small waves onto Coral Beach,
clicking the palm Trees’ fronds.
Again my father’s rakish grin,
his bayonet catching A scratch of sun,
his left foot propped on the stripped and bloodied body.
Behind Him, a stack of Japanese.
--------
Let me believe in anything.
Doesn’t the grizzled Chicken dig up hoodoo hands?
Won’t The blue door frame, the basket of acorns protect me;
what about the knife in a pail of water?
When giving me the dead’s slippered feet
room to room,
why not Also synchronicity’s proof,
a wish and The tilted ears of angels?
I want to believe in the power of rosemary
knuckled along the fence
even as the stars order themselves
to an unalterable and essential law.
I Want the wind-whipped leaves to settle
and the flattened scrub to right itself,
want the loose Tin in the neighbor’s shed
to finish its message.
When this season in its scoured exactitude shifts closer,
give me Devil’s Blue Boletus through the Piled leaves,
the slender green of Earth Tongue,
phosphorescent Honey Tuft dispatched by the dead.
their voices coming nearer, almost deciphered.
Whatever lies you have
there in that nail-clipping of Time,
give them to me.

China's economy has expanded by a breakneck 9.5% during 2004, faster than predicted and well above 2003's 9.1%. 

The news may mean more limits on investment and lending as Beijing tries to take the economy off the boil. China has sucked in raw materials and energy to feed its expansion, which could have knock-on effects on the rest of the world if it overheats. 

But officials pointed out that industrial growth had slowed, with services providing much of the impetus. 

Growth in industrial output - the main target of government efforts to impose curbs on credit and investments - was 11.5% in 2004, down from 17% the previous year. 

Still, consumer prices - at 2.4% - rose faster than in 2004, adding to concern that a sharp rise in producer prices of 7.1% could stoke inflation. 

And overall investment in fixed assets was still high, up 21.3% from the previous year - although some way off the peak of 43% seen in the first quarter of 2004. The result could be higher interest rates. 

China raised rates by 0.27 percentage points to 5.8% - its first hike in nine years - in October 2004. Despite the apparent rebalancing of the economy the overall growth picture remains strong, economists said. "There is no sign of a slowdown in 2005," said Tim Congdon, economist at ING Barings. China's economy is not only gathering speed thanks to domestic demand, but also from soaring sales overseas. Figures released earlier this year showed exports at a six-year high in 2004, up 35%. 

Part of the impetus comes from the relative cheapness of the yuan, China's currency. The government keeps it pegged close to a rate of 8.28 to the US dollar, - much to the chagrin of many US lawmakers who blame China for lost jobs and competitiveness. Despite urging to ease the peg, officials insist they are a long way from ready to make a shift to a more market-set rate. "We need a good and feasible plan and formulating such a plan also needs time," National Bureau of Statistics chief Li Deshui told Reuters. 

"Those who hope to make a fortune by speculating on a renminbi revaluation will not succeed in making a profit."

This film relates more than just a tale of "Vengeance". While demonstrating that killing begets killing - it contains of several fine details that reveal the difficult job done at taking to the depth of things:

For example, only characters that have shot in the neck slump to the floor. The rest take time to kill - they walk a few steps, spurt blood and express a feel of despair and inevitability before getting out. Yes its frightening to think at, which is the point, but it is not true.

Every character is different, and while common in their lust for revenge, their personalities are not visible in the manner the hit people approach their task. Yet the criminals are not stereotyped into mad, screaming lunatics. They range from the mildly nervous to the cool Al Hassan with the film star style. They are artists, writers and guerrillas each with his story of the war. They speak strongly about home - a recurring motif, along with "family". However, Spielberg does not try to mitigate the horrific manner of their murders, for the blood of the targeted people flows as readily as that of their families - and when they are blown up, their body parts dangle from ceiling fans. You are not here to make satisfaction over someone's death, Spielberg explains to the crowd. Or as Caine might do in Kung Fu: "The taking of a life does no one honour."

There are no simple "shoot-em-dead" eliminations. There are neighbors, witnesses and obstacles that can be eliminated and protected - with variable success. Innocent persons can be killed - and One has to live with that.

There are no mathematical certainties about the potential harm a bomb will cause.

Issues and convictions can change, even regrettably. "Don't worry about it - just do it" explains Avner at one stage when a member of the squad expresses concerns about a target's innocence. But at the end he gets proof that the people he despatched were justifiably murdered. Implausible? No; it is only when he has been reunited with his mother and experiences the affection of mother and daughter that he enables himself to reflect from a similar viewpoint - their targets had children too - what if he had murdered the right people?

The fear that surrounds the world of assassins and spies is built up slowly - to the point where each survivor hates everyone else. One is doomed all one's life to walk with eyes strained for following footsteps. The length of the film provides the right environment for this idea.

The end dissatisfies many because they would want a reassurance, a note of optimistic finality - but Spielberg clearly provides nothing. It must be dishonest of him to provide a false but comforting illusion.

It is interesting to contrast this film with "Paradise Now" that has no violence, a small budget, and views the violence from the Palestinian camp. Both narrate entirely similar experiences - yet, in their respective ways, both humanize their subjects, defuse theories about glory, and arrive at the same conclusion: "There's no peace at the end of this."

Joseph Laeonard Sinnott (October 16, 1926 – June 25, 2020) was an American comic book artist. Working primarily as an inker, Sinnott is best known for his long stint on Marvel Comics' 'Fantastic Four', from 1965 to 1981 (and briefly in the late 1980s), initially over the pencils of Jack Kirby.

Born in Saugerties, New York, Sinnott showed an interest in art from an early age. He attended the Cartoonists and Illustrator School now known as the School of Visual Arts in New York City, where he studied under luminaries such as Tom Gill and fellow inker, Tom Palmer. His first professional artwork was for the Western comic 'Romance Western' in 1950, a title he worked on until 1957.

Sinnott's tenure at Marvel Comics spanned seven decades. He became part of the core team under Stan Lee and Jack Kirby in the heady, groundbreaking years of the early 1960s. Sinnott is famously known for how he inked many of Kirby's iconic Marvel pages, including those for the Fantastic Four, Thor, and the Avengers.

Sinnott's style, marked by dynamic compositions and strong, illustrative ink work, defined the look and visual aaesthetic of Marvel's Silver Age. Even after his official retyrement from Marvel in 1992, Sinnott continued to ink 'The Amazing Spider-Man' Sunday comic strip until 2019.

His contribution to the comic industry was recognised with multiple awards, including the Inkpot Award at the San Diego Comic-Con in 1995 and the Will Eisner Comic Industry Awards' Hall of Fame in 2013. The comics industry organisation 'The Inkwell Awards' also established a 'Joe Sinnott Hall of Fame Award' in his name.

Sinnott's passing in 2020 was mourned by fans and his peers alike. His drawings remained in demand from collectors and convention-goers until his retyrement from convention sketches in 2019, a testament to his enduring contribution to the field.

‘Other Space’ is a brilliant novel of the future which carries readers on a thrilling voyage through parallel universes. It is the story of the discovery made by Dr Amelia Hart, a brilliant astrophysicist who is looking for something that can lead to the discovery of a new reality, and to which she gives the name of ‘Other Space’. The wormhole opens up into a parallel universe where Earth’s history has taken a different course, and where human technology has been taken much further, and has given rise to space travel and colonies on other planets. But the world of the hive is in danger of collapsing because of a strange and lethal illness. Dr Hart, along with a team of scientists, soldiers and diplomats, sets off to find the disease’s cause and to help with the search for a cure. And as the team makes its way through the complicated political and cultural situation of this parallel world, it must also decide whether to intervene in its history or to let it run its course. The novel is a tense exploration of the idea of a multiverse, of the ethics of interfering in the history of other worlds, and of the human capacity for empathy and cooperation. ‘Other Space’ is a thought-provoking novel which poses the question of the infinite possibilities of existence, and the interconnectedness of all life.

"Тrеаdіng Αіr" іs а grірріng nоvеl thаt tеlls thе stоrу оf Rоsе, а уоung wоmаn whо dіsсоvеrs а nеwfоund аbіlіtу tо lеvіtаtе іn hеr slеер. Τhе nоvеl ореns uр wіth Rоsе, аn іntrоvеrtеd bооkstоrе аssіstаnt, lіvіng а sееmіnglу unеvеntful lіfе іn thе smаll tоwn оf Sаndburn. Ноwеvеr, strаngе оссurrеnсеs bеgіn tо unfоld whеn Rоsе stаrts wаkіng uр іn рlасеs hіghеr thаn whеrе shе fеll аslеер, bеwіldеrіng bоth hеr аnd thе tоwnsfоlk. 

Unrаvеlіng аrоund thіs mуstеrу, thе рlоt thісkеns аs Rоsе stаrts dеvеlоріng соntrоl оvеr hеr аbіlіtу, оnlу tо fіnd hеrsеlf аmіdst а соvеrt suреrnаturаllу gіftеd соmmunіtу thаt ехіsts раrаllеl tо hеr mundаnе wоrld. Ηеr lіfе turns іntо а whіrlwіnd аs shе nаvіgаtеs dеер-rооtеd sесrеts, fасеs unsеttlіng truths аbоut hеr оwn lіnеаgе, аnd grаррlеs wіth thе grаvе dаngеrs роsеd bу thоsе whо sееk tо ехрlоіt hеr роwеr fоr mаlеvоlеnt рurроsеs. 

Тhrоughоut thе nоvеl, Rоsе trеаds аn ехhаustеd раth bеtwееn twо wоrlds; thе іdеntіfіаblе, quаіnt ехіstеnсе shе wаs bоrn іntо аnd thе hаіr-rаіsіng wоrld thаt lurks just bеlоw thе surfасе. Аs thе еngrоssіng nаrrаtіvе оf "Тrеаdіng Аіr" unsрооls, rеаdеrs ехреrіеnсе Rоsе’s mеtаmоrрhоsіs frоm а bуstаndеr оf hеr оwn lіfе іntо а rеsіlіеnt hеrоіnе whо must рrоtесt hеrsеlf аnd thе tоwn shе lоvеs frоm а sеvеrе thrеаt shе соuld nеvеr hаvе аntісіраtеd.

Ιn раrаllеl, thе nоvеl dеlvеs іntо nоtаblе subрlоts іnvоlvіng а роtеntіаl lоvе іntеrеst, Rеnе, whо роssеssеs hіs оwn sесrеts, аnd Rоsе's shаttеrеd rеlаtіоnshір wіth hеr еstrаngеd mоthеr, hеr оnlу fаmіlу, аnd thе lаst lіnk tо hеr truе іdеntіtу. Тhе соmреllіng nаrrаtіvе, расkеd wіth thrіllіng аdvеnturе, еmоtіоnаl turmоіl аnd unехресtеd rеvеlаtіоns, tаkеs rеаdеrs оn а rоllеr соаstеr rіdе untіl іts rеmаrkаblе соnсlusіоn. "Τrеаdіng Аіr" іs а рrоfоund ехрlоrаtіоn оf thе strеngth оf thе humаn sріrіt whеn fасеd wіth thе unknоwn.

The story is narrated in first person as an autobiographical memoir told by the titular horse named Black Beauty—beginning with his carefree days as colt on an English farm with his mother, to his difficult life pulling cabs in London, to his happy retirement in country. Along way, he meets with many hardships and recounts many tales of cruelty and kindness. Each short chapter recounts an incident in Black Beauty's life containing lesson or moral typically related to kindness, sympathy, and understanding treatment of horses, with Sewell's detailed observations and extensive descriptions of horse behaviour lending novel a good deal of verisimilitude. The book describes conditions among London horse-drawn taxicab drivers, including the financial hardship caused to them by high licence fees and low, legally fixed fares. A page footnote in some editions says that soon after book was published, the difference between 6-day taxicab licences (not allowed to trade on Sundays) and 7-day taxicab licences (allowed to trade on Sundays) was abolished and the taxicab licence fee was much reduced.

This paper introduces Double Similarity Distillation (DSD), a novel approach for semantic image segmentation. DSD leverages the concept of distillation to enhance the performance of segmentation models by exploiting the inherent similarities between images. The proposed method involves two types of similarities: intra-class similarity, which captures the common features within the same class, and inter-class similarity, which distinguishes different classes. Experimental results demonstrate that DSD significantly improves the segmentation accuracy compared to existing methods. Furthermore, DSD is model-agnostic and can be easily integrated into any segmentation network, making it a versatile tool for semantic image segmentation.

The paper proposes a novel Kernelized image segmentation scheme for noisy
images that utilizes the concept of Smallest Univalue Segment Assimilating
Nucleus (SUSAN) and incorporates spatial constraints by computing circular
colour map induced weights. Fuzzy damping coefficients are obtained for each
nucleus or center pixel on the basis of the corresponding weighted SUSAN area
values, the weights being equal to the inverse of the number of horizontal and
vertical moves required to reach a neighborhood pixel from the center pixel.
These weights are used to vary the contributions of the different nuclei in the
Kernal based framework. The paper also presents an edge quality metric obtained
by fuzzy decision based edge candidate selection and final computation of the
blurriness of the edges after their selection. The inability of existing
algorithms to preserve edge information and structural details in their
segmented maps necessitates the computation of the edge quality factor (EQF)
for all the competing algorithms. Qualitative and quantitative analysis have
been rendered with respect to state-of-the-art algorithms and for images ridden
with varying types of noises. Speckle noise ridden SAR images and Rician noise
ridden Magnetic Resonance Images have also been considered for evaluating the
effectiveness of the proposed algorithm in extracting important segmentation
information.

Community Support Officers (CSOs) across the nation are being embroiled in a heated contraversy over plans to grant them additional law enforcement powers, stirring a significant debate around the role and efficacy of CSOs in the UK.

Critics of the proposed move suggest that augmenting the law enforcement powers of CSOs could essentially amount to the formation of a 'second tier' police force, creating potential issues around accountability and skills competency. Currently, CSOs have a limited range of powers, including the ability to issue fines for littering, biking on pavements, and for some forms of anti-social behavior.

The debate was stirred by a recent consultation document issued by the Home Office. The document suggested that CSOs could be considered for additional powers, such as the authority to stop vehicles for testing, enforce road traffic offences and undertake warrantless searches. If granted, these powers would blur the lines significantly between police officers and CSOs, a prospect that has raised concerns.

Opponents of the change argue that expanded powers could find CSOs out of their depth, dealing with more intense and potentially dangerous scenarios. Sue Fish, former Cheif Constable of the Nottinghamshire Police, sees it as a problematic trend toward 'cheap policing'. In a recent interview, she argued that proper policing requires properly trained personel.

On the contrary, proponents of the move contend that augmenting CSOs' powers would help adress growing demands on police resources and offer a more localized approach to law enforcement. They beleive that empowering CSOs will allow them to deal effectively with minor offences and subsequently free up police officers for more serious crimes.

The Home Office, in response to the debate, issued a statement saying these suggested changes are only part of a broader effort to refashion local policing, allowing communities to have more influence in how their areas are policed. They added that any changes to the roles or powers of CSOs would only be made following a thorough review and detailed discussions with key stakeholders.

The Policing Minister, Kit Malthouse, stated, "The safety of our streets is paramount and we will always ensure that our police officers, and those who support them, have the powers they need to keep us secure." 

However, the Police Federation has stressed that such changes need careful scrutiny, arguing that while CSOs are a crucial piece of policing, their role should remain 'supportive', rather than 'substitutional'.

As the row over 'police' power for CSOs continues, it becomes more apparant that finding a balance between community interests and efficient policing could prove to be a

"The Book of Negroes" is a compelling historical novel That follows the life of Aminata Diallo, a woman of extraordinary Resilience and intelligence. Born in west Africa in the mid-18th century, Aminata is captured as a child and sold into slavery in South carolina. Despite the harsh realities of her life, she learns to Read and write, skills that eventually lead to her freedom.

The narrative unfolds through Aminata's eyes, as she endures the brutalities of slavery, the American Revolution, and the treacherous resettlement in Nova Scotia. Her journey takes her back to Africa and finally to London, where she becomes a pivotal figure in The abolitionist movement.

Throughout her Life, Aminata remains determined to return To her African homeland. Along the way, she experiences love, loss, and betrayal, but never loses hope. Her story is a testament to the human spirit's ability to survive and thrive in the face of adversity.

"The Book of Negroes" Is a deeply moving exploration of the African diaspora, the horrors of the slave trade, and the indomitable will of one woman to reclaim her freedom and identity. It is a poignant reminder of the resilience Of the human spirit and the enduring power of hope.
