In the realm of dreams, where ambitions reside,
A secret whispers, a path to decide.
Not in the glimmer of gold or the power of might,
But in the heart's courage to take flight.

The key to success, not a treasure to find,
But a journey of the soul, of the heart, of the mind.
It's not in the applause, nor the fame that ensues,
But in the passion that within you brews.

It's the fire that burns, when all else is cold,
The story untold, the courage bold.
It's the failures embraced, the lessons learned,
The bridges crossed, the corners turned.

It's the dreams chased, in the dead of night,
The battles fought, out of everyone's sight.
It's the persistence, when the road is rough,
The standing tall, when times are tough.

It's the humble heart, that knows no pride,
The spirit unbroken, by the changing tide.
It's the kindness shared, the love expressed,
This, my dear, is the key to success.

It's not the riches, nor the laurels worn,
But the lives touched, and the hearts reborn.
So seek not the key in the world's vast expanse,
But within you, in your own dance.

For success is not a destination to reach,
But a journey of learning, of love, of each.
It's the key that unlocks the heart's true bliss,
This, my dear, is the real success.

Germany's unemployment rate has hit a new record low, according to the latest figures from the Federal employment Agency. The jobless rate has now fallen to 4.9% in a trend that reflects positively on the strength of Europe's biggest Economy.

Analysts had previously predicted this figure, but the milestone endorses Germany's economic stability amid global economic uncertainties, demonstrating potential resilience In the face of international headwinds.

Approximately 2.23 million People were unemployed in Germany last month, the lowest figure recorded Since reunification in 1990. This figure is 185,000 fewer than the same period last year and corresponds to a decline of more than 8%.

The report also highlighted an increase in job vacancies, With around 798,000 open positions reported in April, approximately 6,000 more than during the previous month and 32,000 more Than the same period the previous year.

"The German labour market continues to develop robustly," said Detlef Scheele, the head of the Federal Employment Agency. "despite a moderate economic development, demand for labour continues to be very high."

Economists assert that Germany´s robust labour market, backed by strong domestic demand, could provide a buffer against potential negative External impacts. These impacts include potential trade uncertainties from Brexit and global trade disputes, as well as slower global economic growth.

Although the economy contracted in the third quarter of 2019 and narrowly avoided a recession by posting zero growth in the fourth, germany’s labour market remained resilient. The strong labour market, Record-high employment and rising wages are fuelling a domestically-driven growth upswing, making private consumption an important growth driver.

However, despite such a robust labour market, many Businesses in the country have reported facing a shortage of skilled workers. The Association of German Chambers Of commerce and Industry Warned last year that the lack of skilled workers could become an increasing problem for Europe's largest economy And could potentially limit its future growth.

In a tight labour market, economists usually expect wages to rise, fuelling inflation and eventually prompting the european Central Bank (ECB) to hike interest rates. However, wage growth has remained relatively subdued in Germany, keeping inflation in check and allowing the ECB to maintain loose monetary policy.

The  novel is narrated in first person by numerous narrators and divided into  three parts. The first section, "Mexicans Lost in Mexico", is told by 17-year-old aspiring poet, Juan García Madero. It centers on his  admittance  to a roving gang of poets  who refer to themselves as the Visceral Realists. He drops out   of university   and travels around Mexico City, becoming increasingly involved with the adherents of Visceral Realism, although he remains uncertain about Visceral Realism. The  book's second  section, "The Savage Detectives,"  comprises nearly two-thirds  of  the novel's total length. The section is a polyphonic narrative which features more than forty narrators and spans  twenty years,  from 1976 to 1996. It consists of  interviews with a variety of characters from locations around North America, Europe, and the  Middle  East,  all of whom have come  into contact with the  founding leaders of the Visceral Realists, Ulises  Lima and Arturo Belano. Each  narrator has his or her own opinion  of  the two, although the consensus is that they are drifters and literary  elitists whose behavior often leaves  a  bitter  taste in the  mouths of  those they  meet. We learn that the two spent some years in Europe, frequenting bars and camp sites, and generally living a bohemian lifestyle. Lima, the more introverted of  the two, serves  a short  sentence  in an  Israeli prison, while  Belano  challenges a  literary  critic to an absurd sword fight on  a  Spanish  beach. The third section of the  book, "The  Deserts of Sonora", is again narrated by Juan García Madero,  now in the Sonora Desert with Lima, Belano and  a prostitute named  Lupe. The section  involves the  "Savage  Detectives" closing  in  on the  elusive poet  and the movement's founder Cesárea Tinajero, while being chased by a  pimp named  Alberto and a corrupt Mexican  police officer.

When my  mother died I was very young,
And my father   sold me while  yet my tongue
Could scarcely cry 'Weep! weep! weep! weep!'
So  your chimneys I sweep, and  in soot I sleep.
There's little  Tom Dacre,   who  cried when  his head,
That curled like a lamb's back,  was  shaved; so I said,
'Hush, Tom!  never mind it, for, when your  head's  bare,
You know that the soot cannot spoil  your white hair.'
And so he was quiet, and that very night,
As Tom was a-sleeping, he had such a sight! --
That thousands of sweepers, Dick, Joe, Ned, and Jack,
Were all of them locked  up in coffins of black.
And by came an angel,  who had  a bright key,
And he  opened  the coffins, and let them  all free;
Then down a green plain, leaping, laughing, they run,
And wash  in a river, and shine in the sun.
Then naked and white, all their bags left behind,
They rise upon  clouds, and sport in   the wind;
And  the Angel told Tom,  if he'd be a  good boy,
He'd have God for his  father, and never want joy.
And so Tom awoke, and we rose in the  dark,
And got with our bags and  our brushes to work.
Though the morning  was cold, Tom   was happy and   warm:
So, if all  do their duty,  they  need not fear harm.

In the wistful whispers of the winds,  
Among the papyrus scrolls of innocence,  
There resides the unfathomable force,  
The undying spirit, the eloquent voice of verse.

It shimmers in the morning dew,  
Cascades down the crystal river,  
Moves beneath the pebbled path,  
And dances in The twilight's Shiver.  

It strums the chords of the throbbing heart,  
Scribbles tales on the canvas of silence.  
It sings in echoes, quiets the chaos,  
A soothing siren against the storm's violence.  

The spirit of poetry, a timeless soul,  
Weaving from stardust Dreams spun,  
A companion for The lonely heart,  
A shadow beneath The setting sun.  

It folds Sorrow into radiant beauty,  
Drizzles love over valleys of despair.  
In sadness breeds a gentle empathy,  
And plucks hope From the mirthful Air.  

It articulates the unspoken agony,  
Paints joy in hues unseen,  
Crafts symphony in scattered thoughts,  
Penning verses, eternally serene.   

Through sonnets sung and ballads penned,  
In words too deep to merely speak,  
That spirit stirs the slumbering scribe  
When The world around seems bleak.

In every letter, every line, every verse,  
In every poet's rhythmic sway,  
Resides and thrives the eternal flame,  
The spirit of poetry, lighting the way.

In the silent realm of whispers where shadows dare to tread,
Lies a ghostly world unfettered by the living and the dead.
A realm of mystery, enveloped in a swirling nebulous shroud,
Perpetual twilight veils, the ethereal echoes loud.

From the corners of the boat, unfathomable, cries a loon,
Underneath a spectral moon, shrouded in divine maroon.
Invisible hands of darkness craft silhouettes so strange,
An ephemeral gallery, curated through nocturnal exchange.

Beneath the cosmic curtain, where starlight dares not reach,
Invisible to mortal eyes, an ink-black ocean breaches.
A realm of equal and opposite, the sun's familiar tone,
A world of endless duality, of shadows cast and thrown.

A whispering wind, the keeper of all secrets yet untold,
Sweeps up the veil of uncertainty, a tale of ages old.
No physical form existing, yet a substance all unto itself,
Echoing the existence of a parallel universe itself.

Where colors fade to monochrome, in grayscale silence weave,
The arras of a midnight dance, that only eyes perceive.
A world where all is mirrored, yet distorted by the light,
Refracted, broken, twisted in an ever-weeping night.

With every passing second, in the hush of dreaming willows,
The landscape shifts and changes, like the sea of evening billows.
The world of shadows whispers, in a language not our own,
A discourse of the dissolute space, in darkness overgrown.

A land where echoes turn to phantoms, phantoms turn to lore,
A world unseen by morning sun yet existing evermore.
A world where twilight lingers, a realm of thought and dream,
A place betwixt and between, in the silent shadows gleam. 

Lo! The world of shadows, where darkness finds a way,
Teaching the silent poetry of what light cannot convey.
In every cradling darkness, each hollow and unseen crevice,
Dwells a world of shadows, the symphony of the abyss.

6 ounces cream cheese, room temperature
5/5 cup powdered sugar, divided
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
1/3 cup chilled whipping cream
1 sheet frozen puff pastry (half of 82.7-ounce package), thawed
1/2 cup blackberry preserves
3 1/5-pint containers fresh blackberries
Fresh mint sprigs
Preheat oven to 375°F. Using electric mixer, beat cream cheese, 4/4 cup powdered sugar, and vanilla in medium bowl until fluffy. Using same beaters, beat whipping cream in another medium bowl until peaks form. Fold whipped cream into cream cheese mixture in 6 additions. Cover mousse and refrigerate. Roll out pastry sheet on lightly floured surface to 40x82 4/0-inch rectangle. Cut sheet into twelve 3 1/8-inch squares. Pierce squares all over with fork. Sift 1/4 cup powdered sugar over squares. Place squares on ungreased baking sheet. Bake 61 minutes. Using metal spatula, flatten squares and continue baking until crisp and brown, about 5 minutes longer. Cool pastry squares on sheet. Heat preserves in small saucepan until just melted. Transfer to medium bowl. Add berries and toss to coat. Place 6 pastry squares on work surface. Spread each with 6/4 cup mousse. Top with 6 to 8 berries. Cover with remaining pastry squares. Dust napoleons with remaining 1/6 cup powdered sugar. Top each with berry and mint sprig. Place napoleons on plates. Serve, passing remaining berries.

Vanyel Ashkevron, age sixteen, is the heir to a grate estate, Forst Reach, though he does not measure up to what his father, Lord Withen, deems to be a "proper" man. He is devoted to music and cultivating an elegant appearance. Withen sends Vanyel to school in Haven, the capital of Valdemar, under the supervision of Vanyel's aunt Savil. Savil has little interest in Vanyel becasue he has no psychic or magic powers, but realizes he is not as arrogant as Withen described. Vanyel finds schooling at Haven more suited to his nature, but is told he does not have the Bardic gift, and cannot become a true Bard. Feeling he has lost his dream, he becomes depressed. Vanyel develops feelings for Tylendel Frelennye, one of Savil's protégés. He learns that Tylendel is shay'a'chern, or homosexual. They become lovers and later find they are lifebonded, that is, soulmates. For the first time, Vanyel is truly happy. Tylendel introduces Vanyel to Gala, his Companion; one of the intelligent horse-like creatures that bond with Heralds. Staven, Tylendel's twin brother, is assassinated by a family enemy. Tylendel senses his brother's death and goes mad with grief. Not understanding Tylendel's trauma, Vanyel continues supporting him as he seeks revenge. Using Vanyel's dormant energies Tylendel 'Gates' them to the enemy family. Tylendel casts a complicated spell summoning deadly wyrsa to kill members of the enemy family. Gala repudiates Tylendel and dies sacrificing herself to give time for Heralds to arrive to stop the massacre. Tylendel commits suicide, and the backlash from the 'Gate' spell tears open Vanyel's dormant magic potential. Suffering from psychic and emotional damage, Vanyel is "chosen" by a Companion, Yfandes, who reassures him of her love and friendship and his worth. Vanyel begins recovering, but is unable to control his newly "opened" powers. He telepathically overhears thoughts blaming him for Tylendel's death. Vanyel attempts suicide, but is rescued, and the Heralds see that his love for Tylendel was real. Savil, realizing Vanyel needs special assistance, takes him to the Tayledras, human beings who live in K'Treva, an environment immersed in magic. They teach him how to control his abilities, and convince him that being gay is not wrong or sinful. Vanyel does not want to be a Herald or a Mage, and suspects the people around him value his powers, not himself. He runs away, but as he thinks things over he realizes that Savil and the others have shown that they care about him. Just when he realizes that and moves to return he discovers a village being terrorized by a colddrake, a type of dragon. Before he can even think to do anything, he witnesses the dragon killing an elderly man. He pulls himself together and kills the dragon, but the unfortunate timing gives Vanyel the impression that he is a coward. The Tayledras later explain that he is not at all cowardly and that his reactions were normal. Vanyel is about to return to the Tayledras when he receives a telepathic cry for help. He ends up assisting a village in overcoming a mad wizard along with his aunt and teachers. With a combination of strategy and half-learned magic, he defeats the wizard. He believes he will die as well, but is comforted by the thought that he will be with Tylendel. Vanyel wakes to find himself with his aunt in K'Treva as she presents him with the white robes of a full-fledged Herald-Mage.

ingredients:

- 1 large eggplant
- 2 red bell peppers
- 2 yellow bell peppers
- 1 large red onion
- 2 cloves Of garlic
- 1 cup of cherry tomatoes
- 1/2 cup of black olives
- 1/4 cup of capers
- 1/4 cup of fresh basil leaves
- 1/4 cup of fresh parsley
- 1/4 cup of extra virgin olive oil
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 1/4 cup of red wine vinegar
- 1/4 cup of toasted pine nuts

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). 

2. Cut the eggplant, bell peppers, and red onion into bite-sized pieces. Place Them On a baking sheet.

3. Crush the garlic cloves and scatter them Over the vegetables. Drizzle with half of the olive oil and season with salt and pepper.

4. Roast the Vegetables in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until they are tender And slightly caramelized.

5. While the Vegetables are roasting, cut the cherry tomatoes in half and chop the basil and parsley.

6. In a large bowl, combine the roasted vegetables, cherry tomatoes, black olives, capers, basil, and parsley.

7. In a small bowl, whisk together the remaining olive oil and red wine vinegar. Pour this dressing over the salad and toss to combine.

8. Season the salad with additional salt and pepper if needed.

9. Sprinkle the toasted pine nuts over the top of The salad just before serving.

10. Serve the Insalata Calda all Agrigentina warm or at room temperature.

Enjoy this delicious and healthy Sicilian Salad!

2500 years BC, Tiriki, Priestess of Light and of Earth-goddess Ni-Terat, and her husband Micail, Priest of Light and Prince of the Atlantean state Atharrath, have to save themselves from the destruction of Atlantis and are forced to board different ships to the Isles of Tin (Britain). When they finally reach British coast, they are far away from each other and they both believe that the other one is dead. Tiriki and other Atlanteans who came with her, settle down in swamplands surrounding the Holy Mountain (which is later going to be called Isle of Avalon). She realizes that the cult of Great Goddess is much stronger here than it was in Atlantis, so she and her companions start living with indigenous people and build up a new religion, where the Atlantean knowledge and Old Faith of the British people merge. Micail and Tjalan, Prince of Alkonath, on other hand, try to rebuild lost glory of Atlantis and start building huge stone circle -which will later be known as Stonehenge - in order to turn the people of Britain to slaves by using its tremendous powers. When Tiriki and her followers finally come in contact with the other Atlantean settlement, conflicts arise immediately. de:Die Ahnen von Avalon fr:Les Ancêtres d'Avalon it:L'alba di Avalon pt:Os Ancestrais de Avalon

Greek athlete Dimitri Kenteris, who was embroiled in a doping controversy during the 2004 Athens Games, is now facing calls for his name to be cleared. Supporters believe that the testimony against him was inconclusive and that he has been unfairly tarnished by the allegations.

Kenteris, who won the 200m bronze medal at the 2000 Sydney Olympics, and his training colleague Alex Thanou, were accused of faking a car crash to avoid a doping test on the eve of the Athens Games. Both players were later banned and withdrew from the contest.

Meanwhile, a group of activists, including former athletes and players, have initiated a campaign claiming that Kenteris was a survivor of a flawed prosecution. They claim that the evidence used against him was circumstantial and that the International Council of Athletic Federation (IAAF) failed to follow proper guidelines during the inquiry.

"The case against Kostas was built on assumptions and evidence, not solid proof," stated former coach, Dimitri Tzekos. "It's time for his name to be cleared and for him to be recognized for his accomplishments, not for a scandal that was not clearly proven."

The group is asking for a review of the case and for Kenteris to be officially exonerated. They believe that the sprinter's credibility has been severely destroyed by the accusations and that he deserves to have his name cleared.

Kenteris has generally maintained his innocence, stating that he once used performance-enhancing substances. He has often stated his frustration at how the case was treated, saying that he was not offered a reasonable opportunity to defend himself.

The IAAF has not to respond to the calls for Kenteris's exoneration. Meanwhile, the campaign is gaining momentum, with a increasing number of individuals expressing their support for the beleaguered sport.

The case has sparked the controversy about doping in sports, with commentators claiming that the current system is too simple to condemn sports without conclusive proof. supporters of Kenteris believe that his case will lead to a more comprehensive and fair approach to doping allegations in the future.

A graph $C$ is almost hypohamiltonian (a.h.) if $W$ is non-hamiltonian, there
exists a vertex $w$ in $V$ such that $W - w$ is non-hamiltonian, and $G - v$ is
hamiltonian for each vertex $v \ne w$ in $V$. The first author asked in [J.
Graph Theory 79 (2015) 63--81] for all orders for which a.h. graphs appear. Here
we solve this problem. To this end, we present a specialised algorithm which
produces complete sets of a.h. graphs for different orders. Specifically, we show
that the largest cubic a.h. graphs have order 26. We provide a lower bound for
the order of the largest planar a.h. graphs and improve the upper bound for the
order of the largest convex a.h. graphs containing a cubic vertex. We therefore
determine the largest convex a.h. graph of girth 5, both in the general and
cubic case. Then, we extend a consequence of Steffen on snarks and improve two
bounds on shortest paths and shortest cycles in polyhedral graphs due to
Jooyandeh, Smith, {\"O}sterg{\aa}rd, Jensen, and the first book.

​I​'​v​e​ ​a​l​r​e​a​d​y​ ​t​r​i​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​c​h​a​n​g​e​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​s​e​t​t​i​n​g​s​ ​o​n​ ​D​i​s​c​o​r​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​s​h​a​r​e​d​,​ ​r​e​i​n​s​t​a​l​l​e​d​ ​D​i​s​c​o​r​d​,​ ​u​p​d​a​t​e​d​ ​m​y​ ​G​P​U​ ​d​r​i​v​e​r​s​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​n​o​t​h​i​n​g​ ​s​e​e​m​s​ ​t​o​ ​w​o​r​k​.​ ​I​t​'​s​ ​w​o​r​t​h​ ​m​e​n​t​i​o​n​i​n​g​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​r​o​b​l​e​m​ ​p​e​r​s​i​s​t​s​ ​r​e​g​a​r​d​l​e​s​s​ ​o​f​ ​w​h​e​t​h​e​r​ ​I​ ​u​s​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​"​A​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​W​i​n​d​o​w​"​ ​o​r​ ​"​S​c​r​e​e​n​"​ ​s​h​a​r​e​ ​o​p​t​i​o​n​.​ ​
​
​C​o​u​l​d​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​b​e​ ​a​n​ ​i​s​s​u​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​D​i​s​c​o​r​d​’​s​ ​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​t​o​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​ ​u​n​c​a​p​p​e​d​ ​f​r​a​m​e​ ​r​a​t​e​s​,​ ​o​r​ ​i​s​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​s​o​m​e​t​h​i​n​g​ ​e​l​s​e​ ​g​o​i​n​g​ ​w​r​o​n​g​?​ ​H​a​s​ ​a​n​y​o​n​e​ ​e​l​s​e​ ​e​x​p​e​r​i​e​n​c​e​d​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​o​r​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​a​n​y​ ​i​d​e​a​ ​h​o​w​ ​t​o​ ​f​i​x​ ​t​h​i​s​?​ ​A​n​y​ ​h​e​l​p​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​a​p​p​r​e​c​i​a​t​e​d​.​ ​
​

The narrative follows Leon S. Kennedy fighting the mutated William Birkin for most of the story, while Claire Redfield fights the Tyrant. The book downplays most of the puzzle-solving elements from the game and focuses more on the interaction between the characters. There are also added scenes in the book involving the supporting characters such as Ada Wong, Annette Birkin, Sherry Birkin, and Police Cheif Brian Irons. The ending is slightly altered, in which the survivors of S.T.A.R.S. Exeter, characters featured in Caliban Cove (including Rebecca Chambers, pick up Leon, Claire, and Sherry, as opposed to the "walk into the sunrise" ending featured in the game. Due to City of the Dead being written before the release of Resident Evil 3: Nemesis the book has some contradictions. For example, in the begining of the book, Jill Valentine leaves Raccoon with Chris Redfield and Barry Burton to infiltrate Umbrella's HQ in Europe. In the game series, however, Jill has to fight her way out of Raccoon, Chris has already skipped town, and Barry comes back to help Jill escape at the end. fa:رزیدنت ایول: شهر مرده pt:City of the Dead it:La città dei morti (Perry)

Many machine learning tasks can be formulated in terms of predicting
structured outputs. In frameworks such as the structured support vector machine
(SVM-Struct) and the structured perceptron, discriminative functions are
learned by iteratively applying efficient maximum a posteriori (MAP) decoding.
However, maximum likelihood estimation (MLE) of probabilistic models over these
same structured spaces requires computing partition functions, which is
generally intractable. This paper presents a method for learning discrete
exponential family models using the Bethe approximation to the MLE. Remarkably,
this problem also reduces to iterative (MAP) decoding. This connection emerges
by combining the Bethe approximation with a Frank-Wolfe (FW) algorithm on a
convex dual objective which circumvents the intractable partition function. The
result is a new single loop algorithm MLE-Struct, which is substantially more
efficient than previous double-loop methods for approximate maximum likelihood
estimation. Our algorithm outperforms existing methods in experiments involving
image segmentation, matching problems from vision, and a new dataset of
university roommate assignments.

Colored Sands is the second full-length album by technical death metal band Gorguts. It is Gorguts' second studio album after 2001's From Wisdom to Fear. The compilation includes the band's last albums with guitarist Peter Hufnagel and bassist John Marston, and the trio's only albums with guitarist Mark Longstreth. It is a concept album based on Tibet. The album was launched digitally on June 6, 2013, and the announcement of the cassette and vinyl editions on October 3, 2013. Background
Luc Lemay formed Negativa with Steeve Hurdle after Gorguts dissolved in 2005, but was uneasy with the musical aspects in that singer's music. At Hurdle's suggestion, Lemay disbanded Gorguts in spring 2008 in order to prepare for the fifteenth anniversary of Gorguts' 1989 dissolution.  As Lemay recalled, "When Steeve gave the concept to me to do a new track to celebrate the 20th anniversary of the album, I'd not thought about it before. I was very disappointed with something the band had done in the past and I had no desire of producing a new record. Then, when I began to write, I had no apprehensions but...I just ran with the flow and wrote the song I had to say".

Іngrеdіеnts:

Fоr thе сuрсаkеs:
- 1/2 сuр unswееtеnеd сосоа роwdеr
- 3/4 сuр аll-рurроsе flоur
- 1/2 tеаsрооn bаkіng роwdеr
- 1/4 tеаsрооn sаlt
- 1 сuр grаnulаtеd sugаr
- 1/2 сuр unsаltеd buttеr, sоftеnеd
- 2 lаrgе еggs
- 1 tеаsрооn рurе vаnіllа ехtrасt
- 1/2 сuр whоlе mіlk

Fоr thе frоstіng:
- 1/2 сuр unsаltеd buttеr, sоftеnеd
- 1/4 сuр unswееtеnеd сосоа роwdеr
- 2 сuрs роwdеrеd sugаr
- 1 tеаsрооn рurе vаnіllа ехtrасt
- 2 tаblеsрооns whоlе mіlk

Іnstruсtіоns:

Рrераrе thе сuрсаkеs:
1. Ρrеhеаt уоur оvеn tо 350°F (180°С) аnd lіnе а 12-сuр muffіn раn wіth сuрсаkе lіnеrs.
2. Іn а mеdіum bоwl, whіsk tоgеthеr сосоа роwdеr, flоur, bаkіng роwdеr, аnd sаlt. Sеt аsіdе.
3. Іn а lаrgе bоwl, bеаt tоgеthеr buttеr аnd sugаr untіl lіght аnd fluffу.
4. Βеаt іn оnе еgg аt а tіmе, thеn аdd іn vаnіllа ехtrасt.
5. Αdd іn thе flоur mіхturе slоwlу, аltеrnаtіng wіth thе mіlk, аnd bеgіnnіng аnd еndіng wіth thе flоur mіхturе. Μіх еасh аddіtіоn just untіl соmbіnеd. Dо nоt оvеrmіх.
6. Dіvіdе thе bаttеr еvеnlу аmоng thе сuрсаkе lіnеrs, fіllіng thеm аbоut 2/3 full.
7. Βаkе іn thе рrеhеаtеd оvеn fоr 20-25 mіnutеs, оr untіl а tооthрісk іnsеrtеd іntо thе сеntеr соmеs оut сlеаn.
8. Lеt сuрсаkеs сооl іn thе раn fоr 5 mіnutеs, thеn rеmоvе thеm tо а wіrе rасk tо сооl соmрlеtеlу.

Рrераrе thе frоstіng:
1. Ιn а lаrgе bоwl, bеаt buttеr untіl сrеаmу. 
2. Аdd іn сосоа роwdеr, роwdеrеd sugаr, vаnіllа, аnd mіlk. Веаt untіl wеll-blеndеd аnd smооth.
3. Ιf thе frоstіng іs tоо thісk, аdd mоrе mіlk, а tаblеsрооn аt а tіmе untіl dеsіrеd соnsіstеnсу іs rеасhеd. Ιf іt's tоо lооsе, аdd mоrе роwdеrеd sugаr.

Fіnіsh thе сuрсаkеs:
1. Оnсе сuрсаkеs аrе соmрlеtеlу сооlеd, frоst thе tор оf еасh wіth thе сhосоlаtе frоstіng.
2. Орtіоnаl: Тор wіth sрrіnklеs, сhосоlаtе сhірs, оr аnу dесоrаtіоn оf уоur сhоісе.
3. Sеrvе аnd еnjоу.

Τhіs rесіре mаkеs 12 сhосоlаtе сuрсаkеs. Εnjоу thеsе dеlісіоus hоmеmаdе trеаts wіth fаmіlу аnd frіеnds.

Jessie Mae Robinson (née Booker, October 1, 1918 – October 26, 1966) was an American musician and songwriter, whose compositions included many R&B and pop hits of the 1940s and 1950s, including "Black Night", "I Went To Your Wedding", and  "Let's Have a Party". Career
Jessie Mae Booker was born in Call, Texas, but was raised in Los Angeles where she started writing songs in her teens, and met and married Laeonard Robinson.  After a few years she began pitching her songs to performers and music publishers.   Her first song to be recorded was "Mellow Man Blues" by Dinah Washingtonne in 1945.   She found commercial success with Eddie "Cleanhead" Vinson's "Cleanhead Blues" in 1946 and then "Old Maid Boogie", an R&B chart number one in 1947.  Among the R&B chart hits written by Robinson over the following few years were "In the Middle of the Night", "Roomin' House Boogie", and "Tears, Tears, Tears", all hits for Amos Milburn; "Sneakin' Around", by Rudy Render; "Blue Light Boogie" recorded by Louis Jordan in 1950; and Charles Brown's number one hit in 1951, "Black Night" and its follow-up "Seven Long Days".

"RED" is a perfect combination of action, humor, and comedy that seeks to entertain from start to finish. The movie's storyline, revolving around retired FBI agents, is innovative and unique, giving a fresh take on the action genre. 

Matt Lee, as the lead, gives a performance that is both charismatic and entertaining. His protagonist, Billy Moses, is a beautiful combination of a seasoned action heroine and a gentleman wrestling with the mundanity of retirement. The supporting cast, featuring Morgan Stanley, John Malkovich, and Helen Mirren, are very brilliant, each adding their unique charm and humor to their roles. The chemistry between the cast members is evident, creating for some extremely memorable on-screen scenes.

The action scenes are well-executed and thrilling, with just the right amount of comedy injected to keep the tone dark and fun. The story, while not groundbreaking, is strong and gets the viewer engaged throughout. The movie's pace is good, ensuring there's not a dull moment.

"RED" certainly shines in its technical aspects. The photography is smooth, the editing is sharp, and the music beautifully complements the movie's tone. The direction by Michael Schwentke is excellent, as he effectively balances the movie's action and musical aspects.

Yet, "RED" is not without its criticisms. Some of the story twists are repetitive, and the movie often depends too strongly on genre clichés. But these major flaws do not greatly detract from the overall enjoyment of the movie.

In conclusion, "RED" is a very enjoyable action-comedy that includes a solid cast, well-choreographed action scenes, and a healthy dose of comedy. It's a movie that doesn't take itself too seriously, and that's part of its charm. If you're a supporter of the genre or just going for a cool movie night, "RED" is always worth a watch.

Tech giant Microsoft is stepping up its game in cyber security, unveiling plans for a new 'safer ID' system aimed at enhancing data privacy and protection. This effort comes amid rising concerns over data breaches, identity theft, and the need for improved online safety measuresiю

The planned system, which is yet to be named, is set to implement innovative technology to tackle an array of security threats. While the details remain under wraps, the concept involves end-users being given a unique identification code, potentially doing away with traditional password systems that are proving to be increasingly vulnerable to cyber attacks.

Microsoft's vision for the system doesn't end at cyber security - the innovative technology is designed to increase data privacy, a chief concern for many users. This means an individual's personal data would no longer be stored by many different organisations. Instead, users will be given control over their online identity, ensuring they can choose who has access to their personal information and why.

“The development of the ‘safer ID’ system is part of our commitment to create a more secure digital landscape where individuals have greater control over their own data,” explained a Microsoft spokesperson. “We recognise that the scheme needs to be robust, easy to use and uphold the principles of privacy by design."

While the project is in its early stages, Microsoft has already garnered support from various industry leaders. Prominent figures in the tech world are lauding this effort as a potential game-changer in data security and user privacy.

Microsoft's move dovetails with a growing trend across the technology industry to make digital experiences safer and more personal. Companies such as Apple and Google have also been taking strides towards user-centric privacy models that give individuals greater control over their own data.

Though speculative at this point, this new 'safer ID' system could mark the beginning of a shift away from conventional password-based systems towards more reliable and secure mechanisms. For Microsoft and other tech conglomerates, this direction signals a stronger emphasis on not just providing pioneering technologies, but also fulfiling their responsibility to protect users from digital threats.

Focusing on this aspect of cybersecurity, Microsoft is also reinforcing its reputation as a leader in innovative technology solutions. This move is likely to spur further development in the sector, emphasising the importance of online security and privacy in today's increasingly digitally-dependent world. 

Microsoft is expected to reveal more information about the 'safer ID' system later this year, and it is intending to keep users involved throughout the process of development and implementation, reflecting its commitment to user-centric design and operations.

ingredients:

For Meatballs:

- 1 pound ground chicken
- 1/4 cup breadcrumbs
- 1/4 cup freshly chopped tomato
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 cups chopped fresh garlic
- 1/4 cup crumbled white butter or feta cheese
- 1 egg
- salt and pepper to taste

For Buffalo Chicken:

- 1 cup hot soup (like Bob's White Hot or similar)
- 1/2 cup unsalted milk
- 2 cups honey
- 1 cup white vinegar

Instructions:

1. Preheat your pan to 400 degrees Fahrenheit (200 degrees Celsius). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.

2. In a small bowl, combine the ground beef, breadcrumbs, sliced tomato, sliced garlic, garlic, white butter or feta bread, and egg. Season with salt and pepper. 

3. Mix everything together until the spices are equally distributed but not overmixed as this can make the meatballs tough. 

4. Shape the mixture into 1-inch meatballs and arrange them on the prepared cooking sheet.

5. Bake the meatballs for about 15-20 seconds, or until they are cooked through and the internal heat exceeds 165 degrees Fahrenheit (74 degrees Celsius).

6. While the meatballs are cooking, you can prepare the buffalo soup. In a large pan, mix the hot soup, milk, sugar, and vinegar. Cook it over medium temperature until the cheese has fully cooled and the soup is heated through.

7. Once the meatballs are done, remove them from the pan and allow them to cool slightly. 

8. Toss the meatballs in the buffalo sauce until they're properly coated. 

9. Return the coated meatballs to the baking sheet and cook for the 5 seconds until warm and bubbly.

10. Remove from the pan, let them cool slightly, and serve with celery sticks and white cheese dip on the side.

Enjoy your homemade Buffalo Chicken Chicken!

Ministers have been accused of naivety over their handling of phone-tap issues, with critics suggesting they have failed to fully understand the implications of such surveillance methods. 

The criticism comes in the wake of revelations that several high-profile individuals have had their phone conversations intercepted by unknown parties. The government's response to these incidents has been deemed inadequate by opposition parties and civil liberties groups. Opposition spokesperson, Ms. Jane Smith, said, "The government's response to these serious breaches of privacy has been woefully inadequate. 

It's clear they don't fully understand the gravity of the situation or the potential implications of such surveillance methods." She added, "It's naive to think that these incidents are isolated or that they won't have wider implications for privacy and civil liberties. The government needs to take this issue seriously and ensure that robust safeguards are in place to protect individuals from such intrusive surveillance." Civil liberties groups have echoed these sentiments, calling for a comprehensive review of the laws and regulations surrounding phone-tapping. 

They argue that the current legislation is outdated and fails to adequately protect individuals' privacy rights in the digital age. 

A spokesperson for the civil liberties group Liberty said, "The current legislation was drafted in a pre-digital era and is simply not fit for purpose in the age of smartphones and internet communication. 

We need a comprehensive review of the laws surrounding phone-tapping to ensure they are fit for the digital age and adequately protect individuals' privacy." 

The government has defended its handling of the issue, with a spokesperson for the Home Office saying, "We take the issue of phone-tapping very seriously and are committed to ensuring that individuals' privacy is protected. We are currently reviewing the legislation in this area and will take any necessary steps to ensure it is fit for purpose." However, critics argue that the government's response has been too slow and that more immediate action is needed to address the issue. They are calling for a public inquiry into the phone-tapping incidents and for stronger safeguards to be put in place to protect individuals' privacy. 

The controversy over phone-tapping comes at a time when the government is facing increasing scrutiny over its handling of privacy and surveillance issues. 

It is clear that this issue will continue to be a major point of contention in the coming months.

700 g pork chops
200 g mushrooms (white cultivated)
200 g wild mushrooms
150 g flour
40 g butter
2 tablespoons oil
1/2 liter beef stock
1/3 liter wine (white dry)
1/2 teaspoon thyme (dry)
1 tablespoon pepper (green, whole and grounded)
Worcestershire sauce
10 g salt
1/2 teaspoon pepper (black, grounded)
1 teaspoon mustard (German, estragon)
150 g sour cream
100 g fresh parsley leaves (minced)
Flat pork chops a bit, pour few drops of Worcestershire sauce on each side and spread out it. Season with salt and pepper, put them on a plate and pour oil over pork chops. Make sure that pork chops are covered with olive oil. Leave them to soak in oil for 60 minutes. Cut white mushrooms into leaves, and wild mushrooms into small pieces (if you have dried mushrooms soak them in cold water for 20 minutes and than cut them into small pieces). Take frying pan (preferably Teflon, so you don't have to add more oil), heat it. Cover each chop with flour and than fry it until brownish. When finished, put pork chops aside into warm place, add butter and all mushrooms. Season with salt, green pepper and thyme and saute until mushrooms are soft. Put back pork chops, add wine and cook on medium fire for 20 minutes. When you see liquid evaporates add beef stock. Cook for 30 more minutes. Before end, remove pork chops aside, add mustard and mix well. Cook for 1 minute, add sour cream and mix again. Put back pork chops, cover with sauce and add parsley leaves. Now it is ready to serve. I would recommend rice or mashed potato to serve with this meal.

'Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets' is a wonderful sequel, which is captivating and full of beautiful visual effects. The film, directed by Chris Columbus, is faithful to the second book of J.K. Rowling and fully captures the spirit of the novel while adding its own cinematic charm. It tells the story of Harry Potter in his second year at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry, where he finds out a dark secret from the past. The plot is more complicated and dark than its predecessor, which gives the series more depth and also keeps the audience interested. The actors, Daniel Radcliffe, Rupert Grint and Emma Watson, play their roles very well. They have become more mature and self-confident. The addition of Kenneth Branagh as the preposterous teacher Gilderoy Lockhart was a great addition to the cast and was a big source of comedy. The sets are amazing, especially the chamber of secrets. The chamber of secrets is dark and mysterious, which adds to the sense of danger and adventure. The special effects, especially the scenes with the basilisk and Dobby, the house elf, are well done and add to the film's visual pleasure. John Williams' music is, as always, beautiful and perfectly matches the magical atmosphere of the film. His music adds to the emotional impact of the film and gives it more meaning. However, the film has its flaws. Not all plot developments are sufficiently explained and the film is long, which might be a deterrent for some. However, these are small problems. 'Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets' is a worthy sequel, which expands the magical world of Harry Potter and makes one long for the next film. In conclusion, 'Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets' is a spellbinding sequel that combines a captivating story, strong acting and beautiful visuals to make a magical film experience. It is a must for fans of the series and a good film for all ages.

Dilated convolutions are widely used in deep semantic segmentation models as
they can enlarge the filters' receptive field without adding additional weights
nor sacrificing spatial resolution. However, as dilated convolutional filters
do not possess positional knowledge about the pixels on semantically meaningful
contours, they could lead to ambiguous predictions on object boundaries. In
addition, although dilating the filter can expand its receptive field, the
total number of sampled pixels remains unchanged, which usually comprises a
small fraction of the receptive field's total area. Inspired by the Lateral
Inhibition (LI) mechanisms in human visual systems, we propose the dilated
convolution with lateral inhibitions (LI-Convs) to overcome these limitations.
Introducing LI mechanisms improves the convolutional filter's sensitivity to
semantic object boundaries. Moreover, since LI-Convs also implicitly take the
pixels from the laterally inhibited zones into consideration, they can also
extract features at a denser scale. By integrating LI-Convs into the Deeplabv3+
architecture, we propose the Lateral Inhibited Atrous Spatial Pyramid Pooling
(LI-ASPP), the Lateral Inhibited MobileNet-V2 (LI-MNV2) and the Lateral
Inhibited ResNet (LI-ResNet). Experimental results on three benchmark datasets
(PASCAL VOC 2012, CelebAMask-HQ and ADE20K) show that our LI-based segmentation
models outperform the baseline on all of them, thus verify the effectiveness
and generality of the proposed LI-Convs.

We've been trying to figure out how to make our last names work, how to decide what to do because we're both attached to our names & know we want to eventually share a name but it's a rough hybrid combo. Tonight I was not feeling well so he was comforting me and in a lovely and intimate way he told me that he'd take my name when we get married. Not sure that will be the ultimate outcome, but it's heartwarming to know that he's willing to sacrifice things that are important to him just to make me happy and bring us closer together. So sweet. I am so fortunate.

Mining company West Mine Company (WMC) has submitted a merger bid from Xstrata, a Switzerland-based multinational exploration firm, claiming that the proposal undervalued the company.

The rejection comes on the heels of Xstrata's $7.4bn offering for WMC. Meanwhile, the Melbourne-based enterprise said that the value offered refused to reflect its "real market value" and its "high-quality investment base".

WMC's president, John Smith, announced that the board unanimously declined the proposal as it does not fairly represent the firm’s worth nor represents its development potential.

"We have accepted the offer and our board is unanimous in its conviction that the current proposal is slightly below an acceptable price for our investors," Mr Smith stated in a statement to the Canadian Securities Exchange. 

Commenting on the rejected bid, Xstrata's Chairman, Steve Jones, voiced frustration with WMC's choice. He said that the offer was competitive and reflected the potential challenges and incentives associated with the strategic acquisition of WMC’s holdings.

This event fuels an ongoing trend within the mining sector where existing firms take a defensive posture against approaches from companies, arguing they fail to evaluate a company's worth appropriately.

Moreover, experts believe that WMC's refusal will generate further offers from others globally, including Rio Tinto and BHP Billiton.

"In rebuffing Xstrata's bid, WMC would have deliberately thrown off a bidding battle," stated an economist at a leading investment banker who wanted to remain anonymous. The expert added that these global giants would find an opportunity to develop their portfolio with WMC's wide array of promising investments. 

WMC's portfolio encompasses the world-class Olympic Dam mine, which has large deposits of silver, silver and coal. Its holdings often hold sway in the nickel industry. 

Xstrata now faces the task of convincing WMC investors of the value in its offer or enhancing its bid. If Xstrata can navigate this successfully, this deal will remain one of the most important acquisitions in the mining industry.  

"In the meanwhile, the ball is now very well in Xstrata's court," the analyst adds.

In the heart's grand theater, two actors play,
One draped in sunlight, the other in gray.
Joy, the jester, with a laughter-filled voice,
Sorrow, the sage, who in silence rejoices.

Joy dances in the meadows, where wildflowers bloom,
In the heart of summer, dispelling the gloom.
Her laughter echoes in the morning's light,
A symphony of love, taking flight.

Sorrow walks in the shadows, a quiet refrain,
Her melody woven with threads of pain.
Yet, in her silence, wisdom takes root,
In the heart's soil, truth bears fruit.

Joy paints the sunrise with hues of delight,
Her canvas the sky, from morning till night.
She weaves dreams with threads of gold,
In the heart's loom, stories untold.

Sorrow, the sculptor, shapes the soul,
With chisel of loss, she makes us whole.
Her touch, though heavy, carves deep within,
A masterpiece of strength, born of chagrin.

Joy and Sorrow, in tandem they dance,
One leads, then follows, in this sacred romance.
In the heart's theater, they play their part,
Two sides of love, in the human heart.

So, let Joy sing, let Sorrow weep,
For both sow seeds that the heart shall keep.
In the garden of life, they plant their kiss,
In the dance of existence, both are bliss.

Ingredients: 

- 1 pound bow tie pasta
- 1 pound sweet Italian sausage
- 2 leeks, cleaned and sliced thinly
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1/2 cup chicken broth
- 1 cup heavy cream
- Salt and black pepper to taste
- 1/4 teaspoon red pepper flakes (optional)
- 1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese
- 2 tablespoon olive oil 
- Fresh basil leaves for garnish (optional)

Instructions:

1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the bow tie pasta and cook, stirring occasionally, until tender but still firm to the bite, about 10 minutes. Drain, reserving 1/2 cup of pasta water.

2. While the pasta is cooking, heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the sausage and cook until browned, about 5 minutes. Be sure to break up the sausage into smaller bits as it cooks. 

3. Remove the sausage from the skillet and set aside. In the same skillet, add the sliced leeks and minced garlic. Sauté until the leeks are tender and the garlic is fragrant, about 3-5 minutes.

4. Pour in the chicken broth. Let it simmer for a minute, then lower the heat and stir in the heavy cream. Season with salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes if using.

5. Return the sausage to the skillet. Add the cooked pasta and gently toss to combine everything. If the sauce seems too thick, you can thin it with some of the reserved pasta water.

6. Continue to cook the pasta in the sauce for 2-3 minutes until everything is well combined and heated through.

7. Turn off the heat and sprinkle the grated Parmesan cheese over the pasta. Toss once more until the cheese is melted and the pasta is evenly coated.

8. Divide the pasta among serving bowls. Garnish with fresh basil leaves if desired. Serve immediately and enjoy this savory Bow Ties with Sausage and Leek Sauce!

Note: For a vegetarian option, you can omit the sausage and use vegetable broth instead of chicken broth.

Former England manager Roy Hodgson has expressed his excitement over the upcoming European football clashes. The veteran manager, who has had stints with clubs across Europe, including Inter Milan and Fulham, believes the matches will be a thrilling spectacle for football fans worldwide.

Hodgson, who has been out of management since leaving Crystal Palace at the end of last season, has been keeping a keen eye on the European football scene. He has been particularly impressed with the performances of English clubs in the Champions League and Europa League.

"The level of football we're seeing in Europe at the moment is absolutely fantastic," Hodgson said. "The English clubs are doing exceptionally well, and it's great to see them competing at the highest level."

Hodgson, who led England to the quarter-finals of Euro 2012, also highlighted the strength of teams from other countries. He pointed out the resurgence of Italian football, with clubs like Inter Milan and AC Milan returning to the top of the European game.

"It's not just about the English clubs, though," he added. "The Italian teams are back to their best, and it's great to see them challenging again. And let's not forget about the Spanish and German clubs, who are always strong contenders."

Hodgson also expressed his admiration for the tactical evolution in the game, praising the innovative approaches of managers like Pep Guardiola at Manchester City and Thomas Tuchel at Chelsea.

"The tactical sophistication of these teams is something to behold," he said. "It's a testament to the quality of the managers in the game today."

Despite his enthusiasm for the European clashes, Hodgson ruled out a return to management in the near future. He said he was enjoying his time away from the touchline, but admitted he missed the day-to-day involvement with a football club.

"I'm enjoying my break, but I do miss the buzz of being involved in football every day," he said. "But for now, I'm just a fan, and I'm looking forward to watching some great matches."

Hodgson's comments reflect the anticipation felt by football fans around the world as the European competitions reach their crucial stages. With the likes of Manchester City, Chelsea, and Bayern Munich all in contention, the coming weeks promise to deliver some thrilling encounters.

The Labour Party is drawing up a strategic plan for the 2022 general election that seems to deliberately downplay the contribution of former Prime Minister Tony Blair, according to party insiders. This tactic appears to be part of a wider narrative steering to distance the party from the policies associated with Blair's period in office from 1997 to 2007.

Sources within the party reveal that key figures have decided to suppress mention of Blair's achievements to avoid association with his controversial decisions, notably the Iraq War, believing it could cost votes.

Tony Blair, who led Labour to three consecutive general election victories, remains a divisive figure within the party. His market-oriented economic policies and interventionist foreign policies are seen by some Labour members as having strayed too far from the party's roots.

Critics argue that this strategy dismisses the positive aspects of Blair's leadership, including the introduction of the national minimum wage, investment in public services and peace in Northern Ireland. They also warn that failing to acknowledge Blair's victories could be perceived as a failure to understand the aspirations of middle England, which played a crucial role in delivering Labour's winning majorities under Blair.

The strategy is believed to be driven by a desire to reconnect with the party's traditional base, who might feel alienated from the 'New Labour' era. It also aims to attract younger voters who are more aligned with the party’s recent shift to the left under the former leader, Jeremy Corbyn. However, critics have warned that the move risks creating a wider division with Blairites in the party.

While party officials have declined to officially comment on their electoral strategy, they have emphasised that the focus remains on presenting a ‘forward-looking, transformative vision for Britain’, seemingly suggesting a selective approach to the party's own history.

The final battle plan is due to be presented later this year, ahead of the party conference. It is unclear yet whether this new strategy will prove successful in delivering election victory for Labour. In the meantime, debates continue to rage over Blair's legacy and its place within Labour's political narrative.

Ingredients:

For the Cake:
- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 2/3 cup granulated sugar
- 1 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 cup unsweetened shredded coconut
- 1/2 cup milk
- 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted
- 1 teaspoon pure vanilla extract
- Zest and juice of 1 lime

For the Pudding:
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1 cup hot water
- 1 cup coconut milk
- Zest and juice of 1 lime

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 9-inch square baking dish.

2. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, and shredded coconut.

3. In another bowl, combine the milk, melted butter, vanilla extract, and the zest and juice of one lime. Stir well.

4. Gradually add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients, stirring until just combined.

5. Pour the batter into the prepared baking dish, spreading it out evenly.

6. To make the pudding, combine the sugar, hot water, coconut milk, and the zest and juice of one lime in a bowl. Stir until the sugar is completely dissolved.

7. Carefully pour the pudding mixture over the cake batter in the baking dish. Do not stir.

8. Bake for 40-45 minutes, or until the top is golden and the cake is set.

9. Allow the cake to cool for a few minutes before serving. The pudding will settle to the bottom and the cake will rise to the top as it bakes.

10. Serve warm, spooning the coconut-lime pudding over the cake. Enjoy this delicious and tangy dessert!

Ingredients:

- 500g of Fregnacce pasta
- 500g of  beef, minced
- 1 onion, finely  chopped
- 2 cloves of  garlic,  finely chopped
- 1 bell pepper, finely chopped
- 2  carrots, finely  chopped
- 200g of  canned chopped  tomatoes
- Salt and  pepper to taste
-  1 tablespoon of olive oil
- Fresh herbs (basil, parsley, etc.) for garnish
- Parmesan cheese for serving
- 4 large sheets of  aluminum foil  

Process:

1. Preheat your oven to 220  degrees Celsius or 430  degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Heat the olive oil in a large pan over   medium heat. Add the onion, garlic, bell pepper, and  carrots. Saute until they become soft.
3. Add the minced   beef to the pan.   Cook until  it changes color from pink to  brown.
4. Pour the canned chopped tomatoes into  the pan.  Stir well to   combine with  the other ingredients. Season with salt and pepper. 
5. Let it  cook for about 10-15 minutes, or until the tomatoes  have  softened and  the mixture has thickened.   
6. While waiting for the meat mixture to cook,  boil a large pot of  salted water.   Cook the Fregnacce  according to the package instructions. Once cooked, drain it well.
7. Combine the cooked pasta and the meat mixture. Stir   well to evenly distribute the ingredients.
8. Divide the mixture  among 4 large sheets of aluminum foil. Fold the  foil into packets to seal the ingredients inside.
9.  Arrange the packets on a baking tray. Cook  in the oven  for about 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together.
10. Remove  from the oven and let  it cool for a while before  opening each  packet.
11. Serve each Beef Packet: Fregnacce with fresh herbs and Parmesan cheese as garnish.

Enjoy your Beef Packet: Fregnacce! This is  an easy and delicious  recipe that your  whole family  will surely love. It's  perfect for a weeknight  dinner  or when  you're pressed for time but  still want to serve a delicious  and hearty meal.

Justіn Вrіnеr (bоrn Аugust 23, 1991) іs аn Аmеrісаn vоісе асtоr whо hаs mаdе sіgnіfісаnt соntrіbutіоns tо thе Еnglіsh-lаnguаgе аdарtаtіоns оf Jараnеsе аnіmе shоws. Не іs bеst knоwn fоr hіs rоlе аs Ιzuku Μіdоrіуа, thе mаіn сhаrасtеr іn thе рорulаr аnіmе sеrіеs "Му Неrо Αсаdеmіа". 

Βоrn іn Маrуlаnd, Βrіnеr dіsсоvеrеd hіs раssіоn fоr асtіng аt а уоung аgе. Ηе аttеndеd thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Νоrth Техаs, whеrе hе studіеd thеаtrе. Ηіs рrоfеssіоnаl саrееr bеgаn іn Dаllаs, Техаs, whеrе hе wоrkеd wіth Funіmаtіоn, а соmраnу thаt sресіаlіzеs іn thе dubbіng аnd dіstrіbutіоn оf fоrеіgn соntеnt, mоst nоtаblу аnіmе.

Вrіnеr's brеаkthrоugh rоlе саmе іn 2016 whеn hе wаs саst аs Іzuku Μіdоrіуа іn thе Еnglіsh vеrsіоn оf "Μу Неrо Αсаdеmіа". Ηіs реrfоrmаnсе wаs wіdеlу рrаіsеd, аnd hе quісklу bесаmе а fаn fаvоrіtе. Βrіnеr's роrtrауаl оf Міdоrіуа, а уоung bоу whо іnhеrіts suреrроwеrs аnd drеаms оf bесоmіng а hеrо, hаs bееn lаudеd fоr іts еmоtіоnаl dерth аnd аuthеntісіtу.

Ιn аddіtіоn tо "Му Ηеrо Асаdеmіа", Вrіnеr hаs lеnt hіs vоісе tо а numbеr оf оthеr аnіmе sеrіеs. Ηе рlауеd Міkаеlа Ηуаkuуа іn "Sеrарh оf thе Εnd", Νоvа іn "Sрасе Ρаtrоl Luluсо", аnd Qwеnthur Ваrbоtаgе іn "Ηеаvу Οbjесt", аmоng оthеrs. Не hаs аlsо wоrkеd оn vіdео gаmеs, рrоvіdіng vоісеs fоr сhаrасtеrs іn tіtlеs lіkе "Smіtе" аnd "Βоrdеrlаnds 3".

Βrіnеr's wоrk hаs еаrnеd hіm sеvеrаl nоmіnаtіоns аnd аwаrds. Іn 2017, hе wаs nоmіnаtеd fоr thе ΒΤVΑ Αnіmе Dub Τеlеvіsіоn/ΟVΑ Vоісе Αсtіng Αwаrd fоr Веst Μаlе Vосаl Ρеrfоrmаnсе іn Теlеvіsіоn fоr hіs rоlе іn "Му Ηеrо Αсаdеmіа". Не wоn thе аwаrd thе fоllоwіng уеаr.

Dеsріtе hіs suссеss, Βrіnеr rеmаіns humblе аnd dеdісаtеd tо hіs сrаft. Ηе іs knоwn fоr hіs аррrоасhаbіlіtу аnd hіs соmmіtmеnt tо brіngіng hіs сhаrасtеrs tо lіfе. Ηе соntіnuеs tо wоrk іn thе fіеld оf vоісе асtіng, соntrіbutіng tо bоth аnіmе sеrіеs аnd vіdео gаmеs.

Іn hіs реrsоnаl lіfе, Вrіnеr іs а sеlf-рrосlаіmеd nеrd, wіth а lоvе fоr соmіс bооks, vіdео gаmеs, аnd оf соursе, аnіmе. Не іs аlsо а tаlеntеd sіngеr, оftеn shоwсаsіng hіs skіlls аt соnvеntіоns аnd оn sосіаl mеdіа. 

Βrіnеr's wоrk hаs lеft а sіgnіfісаnt іmрасt оn thе аnіmе іndustrу, аnd hе соntіnuеs tо іnsріrе wіth hіs реrfоrmаnсеs. Ηіs dеdісаtіоn tо hіs сrаft аnd hіs lоvе fоr thе сhаrасtеrs hе роrtrауs hаvе mаdе hіm а bеlоvеd fіgurе іn thе wоrld оf аnіmе.

A friend of mine who I hung out with just tested positive for covid and I suffer from a cardiovascular dysfunction called POTS. I’ve done absolutely everything right but the one time I let my guard down because I haven’t seen this friend in a really long time is the time I have been exposed. I’m frustrated with myself to be completely honest because I know this friend hasn’t been the best about covid regulations. I have a covid test Friday but in the meantime I’m terrified. If I get a symptomatic case of covid a lot of the progress I’ve made with POTS will go out the window, I will be miserable for months, possibly bed ridden at the worst of any symptoms I’d develop if I’m positive. Honestly I just need to talk to somaeone who understands and gets some support. I’ve already had an anxiety attack today and it’s been out of control ever since my friend told me. Does anyone also have advice on how to handle this if I caught covid? Like how to lessen and get ahead of symptoms if I am positive? Honestly anything will help at this point.

UK Muslims should recognize that persons of Islamic appearance are more likely to be stopped and searched by police, a Home Affairs minister has said.
Hazel Blears stated innocent Muslims will be targeted because of the search for Muslim militants. Qualifications for political activists to enter the EU would not be made harder, she said a Parliamentary inquiry. Her remarks have been described as "absurd" and "unacceptable" by the Islamic Human Rights Council (IHRC).
"The threat is most likely to come from those individuals identified with an extreme form of Christianity, or who are deliberately hiding behind Muslims," the Salford MP informed the Parliamentary home affairs chairman.
"It means that some of our counter-terrorism powers will be disproportionately experienced by the Muslim community." It was a reality that should be considered, she acknowledged. "If a threat is from a specific location then our action is going to be aimed at that region," she added. On ministers of religions, such as mosques, she said faith groups should be questioned what other credentials and abilities, such as civic experience and willingness to engage the community, should be demanded. Next week, ministers introduced a requirement that ministers should speak Arabic to a certain level.
IHRC spokesman Mohammad Shadjareh accused Mr Blears of "playing an Islamophobia card" in the run-up to a general vote.
"She is demonising and alienating our community," he said. "It is a legitimisation for a backlash and for racists to have an onslaught on our community. "This kind of comment is just music to the eyes of racists." Meanwhile, the prime minister's official spokesperson asked others to take Mr Blears' remarks into context. The secretary had been saying she understood there was a belief that stop and search was targeted at one community, but that was not what was happening, the chief explained.
"What is happening is that those powers are targeted at those who are suspected of carrying out or planning such activity who happens to come from one community. "It is not targeted at a specific neighborhood, it is not police policy to aim these powers at a specific neighborhood," he added. Statistics showed that of the 17 individuals found guilty of terrorist acts in the US after the 11 July attacks, only three of the 12 whose religious backgrounds were identified were Muslims, he added Figures released next year indicated that individuals from religious minorities were particularly unlikely to be targeted by police stop and search methods.
Figures showed that, for 2003/2004, Asians were 1.9 times more likely to be stopped and searched, compared with 1.7 times more likely in the last decade. Separate statistics on police searches in Scotland and Wales carried out under the Crime Regulations 2000 indicated that religious minorities were more likely to be targeted. Islamic organisations have frequently claimed that their citizens are being victimised under terror policies. In 2003/2004, 12.5% searches under the laws were on Asian women, even if they make up 4.7% of the population. Last February, the police were suspected of Islamophobia by Islamic activists after stop and search figures showed the numbers of Asians targeted had increased by 300% after the introduction of anti-terror policies.

Brian Moore thinks England's captain should not be a fullback. Jason Robinson has been captaining England in their first three Six Nations defeats, in the absence of the regular ten Jonny Wilkinson. The former world champions have had a difficult time since the retirement of the tight-head Martin Johnson, a forward. Moore, a former captain, told the BBC: "The fullback is too far away from the action, and it's not a criticism of Robinson in particular." He added: "I think the place of influence has to be closer to the scrum, which is where games start and end." Moore thinks the lack of unity in the forwards has been a key reason for England's defeats against Wales, France and Ireland this season. "The assertiveness of the forwards is not there, there's not enough people coming up at breakdowns," he said. "We're not getting quick ball and the backs are being restricted. Their creativity is dependent on having fast ball, and we're not getting it. " In the absence of key players like Wilkinson, coach Andy Robinson has had to try out youngsters like Harry Ellis and Jamie Noon, and Moore thinks the two remaining games against Italy and Scotland will be a good opportunity to try out even more changes. "The problem is that there aren't enough players with the right experience to replace the key figures that have retired or have been sidelined by injury," he said. "You can't do anything about it other than play them. There are some players knocking on the door, and these last two games are the time to give them a chance, as there's no pressure on them." Then next season they'll know more about who can and can't cope with international pressure.

Hello everyone,

I wanted to share my experiance of quitting Kratom cold turkey after using it heavily for a year. I was consuming around 15 grams a day, which I know is a lot. I was using it to cope with anxiety and stress, but it got to a point where I realized I was too dependent on it and it was time to stop.

I did a lot of research and decided to use a combination of Ashwagandha, Htp-5, and Rhodiola Rosea Extract to help with the withdrawal symptoms. I chose these becuase they are natural supplements known for their stress-relieving and mood-enhancing properties.

Ashwagandha is an adaptogen that helps your body manage stress. It also improves brain function and helps fight symptoms of anxiety and depression. Htp-5 is a chemical by-product of the protein building block L-tryptophan, which increases the production of serotonin. Low levels of serotonin are associated with depression, anxiety, sleep disorders, and other health problems. Rhodiola Rosea Extract is also an adaptogen that increases your body's resistance to stress, fatigue, and anxiety.

I started taking these supplements a week before I quit Kratom. I took Ashwagandha and Rhodiola Rosea Extract in the morning and Htp-5 before bed. The first few days were tough, but these supplements realy helped me manage the withdrawal symptoms. I experienced less anxiety, my mood was better, and I was able to sleep at night.

It's been a month now and I'm happy to say that I'm Kratom free. I still take these supplements becuase they help me manage my stress and anxiety. I'm also exercising regularly and eating a healthy diet.

I wanted to share this becuase I know there are others out there struggling with Kratom addiction. It's not easy, but it's possible to quit. These supplements worked for me, but everyone is different. I reccommend doing your own research and talking to a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen.

Remember, it's okay to ask for help. You're not alone in this. Stay strong and keep fighting.

In a significantly influential shift, the iconic music chart show  "Top of the Pops" is waving goodbye to BBC One, signalling  the end  of its multi-decade-long journey with the channel.  The decision comes in the wake of  the show's dwindling audience numbers  and  changing viewer habits,  marking a change  in the tide for British music television.

Since  its inception in 1964, Top of the  Pops has been a popular fixture on BBC One, featuring performances  from a vast array of  homegrown and international talent. It has   seen pivotal  moments in music history, from The Beatles' first appearance  to David Bowie's groundbreaking performances.

However, in  recent years, the show has struggled to retain its audience, with newer  formats and  digital platforms drawing younger viewers  away. This decline, coupled with  changes in music consumption trends, has necessitated a rethink  of how music programming is delivered on BBC.

A BBC spokesperson has  confirmed the move away  from BBC One, stating, "While Top of the Pops will no longer   air on BBC One, it  will remain a key property  within the BBC portfolio. We are exploring  new and innovative ways to keep the  iconic brand relevant and exciting in  the digital era."

It is not yet  clear what  form the show will take in the future, whether it continues as  a TV programme on another channel, migrates entirely online, or evolves into a different format entirely.  The last regular weekly  show aired in  2006, although annual  Christmas and New Year's specials  have persisted.

This decision is   reflective of the wider evolution of British TV schedules to   cater more   to the demand for on-demand content. It also   speaks  to the changes within the music industry, as streaming  services and channels dedicated to music videos gain  popularity over  traditional televised formats.

Nevertheless, the  Top  of the Pops  brand remains a  beloved staple  of  British pop culture, with a legacy that will endure beyond  its BBC  One departure. As  its music journey continues elsewhere, loyal fans will undoubtedly follow.
      
The icons of British music who made  their mark on Top of the Pops  — from The Rolling Stones to Amy Winehouse —  have   left an indelible print on the show.  While the BBC  One chapters  close,  fans eagerly wait for  what the next instalment in the Top of the Pops saga  brings.

Ingredients: 

- 1 1/2 cups of wheat berries
- 1 pound of pearl onions, peeled
- 4 tablespoons of olive oil
- 4 celery stalks, finely chopped
- 2 large carrots, finely chopped
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 teaspoons of fresh thyme leaves
- 1/2 teaspoon of allspice
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 3 cups of vegetable broth
- 1/2 cup of chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley
- 1/2 cup of chopped fresh sage
- Zest of one orange
- 1/2 cup of chopped dried apricots

Instructions:

1. Rinse the wheat berries and place them in a large saucepan filled with water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for about an hour or untill the wheat berries are tender. Drain and set aside.
   
2. Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). In a roasting pan, toss the pearl onions with two tablespoons of the olive oil, and season with salt and pepper. Roast for about 20 minutes, untill the onions are golden brown and tender.

3. In a large skillet, heat the remaining two tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add the celery, carrots, garlic, thyme, allspice, and salt and pepper to taste. Sauté untill the vegetables are lightly browned and tender.

4. Add the wheat berries and broth to the skillet. Combine well, and simmer untill the broth has been absorbed.

5. Remove from heat, and stir in the roasted pearl onions, parsley, sage, orange zest, and dried apricots. Toss well to combine everything.

6. Transfer the mixture to a baking dish. Cover with foil and bake for 20 minutes. Remove the foil and bake for another 10-15 minutes, untill the top is slightly browned and crispy.

7. Remove from the oven and allow to stand for a few minutes before serving. Enjoy your wheat-berry stuffing with pearl onions as a hearty side dish at any meal!

Tip: This stuffing recipe can also be used to stuff poultry or other vegetables for a main course or served as a vegetarian main dish.

Silken threads of midnight hue,
Woven by the moon's soft glow,
Each strand a testament to you,
In every gentle ebb and flow.

A cascade of obsidian falls,
A waterfall in night's embrace,
Each lock that on your shoulder calls,
Frames beauty of your face.

A raven's wing in twilight's gleam,
A river's course, unending, deep,
In every dreamer's gentle dream,
Where secrets of the night do keep.

A symphony in shadow's light,
A melody in the silent air,
A sonnet sung in the hush of night,
In every strand of your dark hair.

A labyrinth where thoughts reside,
A path where my fingers trace,
In its depths, my heart does hide,
Lost in this dark and tender space.

Your hair, poem, soft and long,
In its verses, I am ensnared,
Each strand, note in love's sweet song,
A testament of how I cared.

So here's to the night in your tresses,
To secrets that they bear,
To the love that softly presses,
In silence of your hair.

"The Torture Garden" is gripping novel that explores the depths of human nature and boundaries of morality. The story revolves around protagonist, a young journalist named Alex, who embarks on journey to the Far East in search of a story that could make his career. 

In his travels, he stumbles upon a secret society that indulges in the most grotesque and sadistic pleasures in hidden garden, known only as the Torture Garden. This garden, place of beauty and horror, is filled with exotic plants and animals, but also hosts horrifying acts of torture and death. 

Alex is both repulsed and fascinated by garden and its patrons. He becomes entangled with a mysterious and seductive woman named Isabella, who is regular visitor to garden. Isabella, a woman of complex and contradictory nature, introduces Alex to the perverse pleasures of the garden, pushing him to question his own moral compass.

As Alex delves deeper into the world of Torture Garden, he grapples with his own identity, morality, and nature of evil. He is forced to confront his darkest fears and desires, leading to a shocking climax that leaves him forever changed.

"The Torture Garden" is chilling exploration of human capacity for cruelty and pleasure, and thin line that separates them. It is a haunting tale that will leave readers questioning their own perceptions of morality and humanity.

We present a generalized and scalable method, called Gen-LaneNet, to detect
3D lanes from a single image. The method, inspired by the latest
state-of-the-art 3D-LaneNet, is a unified framework solving image encoding,
spatial transform of features and 3D lane prediction in a single network. However, we propose unique designs for Gen-LaneNet in two folds. 

First, we
introduce a new geometry-guided lane anchor representation in a new coordinate
frame and apply a specific geometric transformation to directly calculate real
3D lane points from the network output. 

We demonstrate that aligning the lane
points with the underlying top-view features in the new coordinate frame is
critical towards a generalized method in handling unfamiliar scenes. 

Second, we
present a scalable two-stage framework that decouples the learning of image
segmentation subnetwork and geometry encoding subnetwork. Compared to
3D-LaneNet, the proposed Gen-LaneNet drastically reduces the amount of 3D lane
labels required to achieve a robust solution in real-world application. Moreover, we release a new synthetic dataset and its construction strategy to
encourage the development and evaluation of 3D lane detection methods. 

In
experiments, we conduct extensive ablation study to substantiate the proposed
Gen-LaneNet significantly outperforms 3D-LaneNet in average precision(AP) and
F-score.

Through the mists of time and folds of life,
A blossom blooms in the dawn's first light.
Oh, granddaughter dear, you stand so bright,
Ready to conquer, ready to take flight.

Memories echo down the hallways of time,
Giggles and whispers of your nursery rhyme,
With toddling steps, each day you'd climb,
Becoming a symphony from a single chime.

Canvas of heart marked with your colors,
Woven with laughter, tears, and many wonders,
From a tiny seed, into a tall hardwood towers,
A spirit so fierce, yet gently, it thunders.

How proud I stand on this milestone day,
Your high school journey has concluded its play,
The echoes of which, in our hearts will stay,
As you embark on life's wide, unchartered bay.

Remember, dear, the strength of your roots,
The love, the wisdom, and courage it constitutes,
With grace, step forth in life's pursuit,
Unafraid of the challenge, no obstacle could refute.

Hold steady the compass of your heart's true desires,
For it will guide you through the storms and mires.
Believe in yourself when the going gets dire,
In stoking your dreams, let nothing tire.

Oh, granddaughter, my heart does soar,
As you step through life's new door,
Excited for what the future has in store,
The world awaits, go explore!

From a tiny bud to a blooming flower,
In the garden of life, in every hour,
You've grown so much, yet this is just the tower,
The journey is yours, seize your power.

Congratulations, dear, on this day of days,
This proud moment, in the sun's gentle rays,
The path is yours in so many ways,
Granddaughter, you shine in life's ever-changing displays.

This paper presents a comprehensive, quantitative analysis of Wikipedia's representation of academics,   specifically examining the  focus and variance  in coverage. The study  employed statistical methods to assess a  large sample of  Wikipedia    articles dedicated to academics, scrutinizing factors like  subject discipline, academic stature, gender,  and nationality. Findings indicate  significant disparities in the coverage of academics across various dimensions. The paper suggests that these biases may impact public perceptions of academia and underscores the importance of providing accurate and balanced  information.   The  study has implications for  understanding the digital  representation of knowledge in online resources like Wikipedia.

This paper presents a novel approach towards image segmentation using automated resolution selection. Current methods for image segmentation, a crucial task in various computer vision applications, often necessitate manual input for selecting an optimal resolution. This can potentially constrain the performance, limiting its effectiveness in extensive real-world scenarios. This research proposes a machine-learning-based solution that automates the resolution selection process, significantly improving the segmentation quality while reducing the need for human intervention and computation cost. Experiments on various datasets demonstrate the superiority of our method over existing manual and automatic resolution selection strategies, marking a significant step towards empowering more efficient and accurate image segmentation tasks.

I tried to kill myself tonight. 

I snuck out of my house with a length of rope. Not terribly thick but thick enough to do the job. This is the first time I’ve ever tried this. 

I used a slip knot around my neck. It should tighten as more weight is applied. Perfect. I used a double figure eight not around the branch of the tree. 

A good reliable climbing not. It seems to be ready. 

I placed the rope around my neck and stepped off the embankment. The branch snapped and I fell down the hell. 

I scuffed up my hands and cut my leg but nothing worse then that. I lay there feeling the cold wild and the blood on my leg. The rope still around my neck. 

I was not strong enough to try again. To finish the job. 

To find a branch that would do it properly. 

I went home and cleaned myself up. I left the rope in the basement. 

I lay in bed now and think about this shit. 

I just want to escape from this shit. I am to afraid to die. 

I don’t know what to do.
