​y​o​u​ ​a​r​e​ ​h​a​v​i​n​g​ ​t​r​o​u​b​l​e​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​l​e​v​e​l​ ​o​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​o​t​t​o​m​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​k​n​i​f​e​.​ ​I​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​q​u​a​s​h​ ​i​s​ ​t​i​l​t​i​n​g​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​a​n​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​l​l​i​n​g​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​r​u​n​ ​o​u​t​ ​-​ ​b​a​d​ ​n​e​w​s​.​ ​C​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​q​u​a​s​h​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​f​o​i​l​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​a​k​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​4​0​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​ ​o​r​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​q​u​a​s​h​ ​s​t​a​r​t​s​ ​t​o​ ​g​e​t​ ​t​e​n​d​e​r​.​ ​I​n​ ​a​ ​b​o​w​l​ ​c​o​m​b​i​n​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​i​l​k​,​ ​e​g​g​s​,​ ​c​o​r​n​,​ ​a​n​i​s​e​ ​s​e​e​d​,​ ​h​a​l​f​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​c​a​l​l​i​o​n​s​,​ ​n​u​t​m​e​g​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​a​l​t​.​ ​F​i​l​l​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​q​u​a​s​h​ ​b​o​w​l​s​ ​3​/​4​ ​f​u​l​l​ ​(​s​e​e​ ​h​e​a​d​ ​n​o​t​e​s​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​l​e​f​t​o​v​e​r​s​)​.​ ​C​a​r​e​f​u​l​l​y​ ​t​r​a​n​s​f​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​q​u​a​s​h​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​v​e​n​ ​w​i​t​h​o​u​t​ ​s​p​i​l​l​i​n​g​ ​(​t​r​i​c​k​y​!​)​.​ ​C​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​ ​b​a​k​i​n​g​ ​u​n​c​o​v​e​r​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​n​o​t​h​e​r​ ​3​0​ ​-​ ​5​0​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​,​ ​o​r​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​q​u​a​s​h​ ​i​s​ ​f​u​l​l​y​ ​c​o​o​k​e​d​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​u​d​d​i​n​g​ ​h​a​s​ ​s​e​t​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​a​m​o​u​n​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​i​m​e​ ​i​t​ ​t​a​k​e​s​ ​c​a​n​ ​v​a​r​y​ ​w​i​l​d​l​y​ ​d​e​p​e​n​d​i​n​g​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​q​u​a​s​h​ ​a​n​d​ ​o​v​e​n​.​ ​A​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​a​s​t​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​ ​s​p​r​i​n​k​l​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​c​h​e​e​s​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​i​n​i​s​h​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​f​l​a​s​h​ ​u​n​d​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​r​o​i​l​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​b​r​o​w​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​h​e​e​s​e​.​ ​K​e​e​p​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​y​e​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​i​n​g​s​,​ ​y​o​u​ ​c​a​n​ ​g​o​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​m​e​l​t​e​d​ ​c​h​e​e​s​e​ ​t​o​ ​b​u​r​n​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​n​e​d​i​b​l​e​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​f​l​a​s​h​.​ ​S​e​r​v​e​ ​h​o​t​ ​s​p​r​i​n​k​l​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​s​c​a​l​l​i​o​n​s​.​

Ingredients:

For the Cake:
- 1 box of chocolate cake mix
- 1 cup of water
- 1/3 cup of vegetable oil
- 3 eggs

For Pudding:
- 1 box of instant chocolate pudding mix
- 2 cups of cold milk

For Topping:
- 1 tub of whipped topping
- 1/4 cup of chocolate syrup
- 1/4 cup of caramel syrup
- Mini chocolate chips or chocolate shavings for garnish

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease 9x13 inch baking dish.

2. In a large bowl, combine cake mix, water, vegetable oil, and eggs. Beat until well mixed.

3. Pour batter into the prepared baking dish and spread it out evenly.

4. Bake in the preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.

5. While cake is baking, prepare pudding. In a medium bowl, whisk together the pudding mix and milk until smooth and creamy. 

6. Once cake is done, remove it from oven and let it cool for about 15 minutes.

7. Using handle of a wooden spoon, poke holes all over the cake, making sure to reach all way to bottom.

8. Pour the pudding over the cake, making sure it seeps into all holes. Use a spatula to spread it out evenly.

9. Place cake in the refrigerator and let it chill for about 2 hours, or until pudding is set.

10. Once the pudding is set, spread whipped topping over the cake.

11. Drizzle chocolate and caramel syrup over whipped topping.

12. Sprinkle the mini chocolate chips or chocolate shavings on top for garnish.

13. Slice and serve the cake chilled. Enjoy your delicious Pokey Pudding Cake!

"The Silent Boy" is a poignant and thought-provoking novel that explores the life of a mute boy named Eli. Born into a small, rural town, Eli is unable to speak due to a traumatic event in his early childhood. Despite his inability to communicate verbally, Eli possesses an extraordinary ability to understand and connect with the world around him. 

The novel follows Eli's journey as he navigates through life, facing the challenges and prejudices that come with his condition. His parents, struggling to understand their son's silence, seek help from various doctors and therapists, but to no avail. Eli's only solace comes from his bond with nature and his unique ability to communicate with animals.

Eli's life takes a dramatic turn when he befriends a new girl in town, Lucy. Lucy, unlike others, sees beyond Eli's silence and recognises his unique abilities. Their friendship blossoms into a deep bond, and Lucy becomes Eli's voice, defending him against the town's prejudices and helping him navigate through life's challenges.

As the story unfolds, a series of mysterious events occur in the town, leading to a climactic revelation that shakes the community to its core. Eli, with his unique understanding of the world, becomes instrumental in uncovering the truth. The novel ends on a hopeful note, with Eli finding his voice in an unexpected way and the town learning to appreciate his unique abilities.

"The Silent Boy" is a heartwarming tale of friendship, acceptance, and the power of understanding beyond words. It explores the themes of prejudice, acceptance, and the extraordinary abilities that lie within each of us.

"The Swiss Family Robinson" is a captivating tale of survival and adventure, revolving around a Swiss family who are shipwrecked in the East Indies en route to Port Jackson, Australia. The novel begins with the family aboard a ship that is violently tossed by a storm, forcing them to abandon it and reach an uninhabited island. The family consists of the father, William, his wife, and their four sons - Fritz, Ernest, Jack, and Francis.

The family, using their wit and the ship's available resources, creates a sustainable life on the island. They construct a treehouse, learn to hunt, fish, and farm, and discover various types of plants and animals, some of which are dangerous. Each day brings a new challenge or discovery, keeping the family on their toes.

As the story progresses, the family explores more of the island, finding new landscapes and encountering different wildlife. They also discover a girl, whom they name Emily, stranded on another part of the island. Emily becomes a part of their family, adding a new dynamic to their interactions.

Throughout the novel, the family demonstrates remarkable resilience, resourcefulness, and unity in the face of adversity. They transform the island into their home, adapting to its challenges and embracing its wonders. Despite the isolation, they find joy in their shared experiences and the simple pleasures of their unique life.

"The Swiss Family Robinson" is a testament to human ingenuity and the strength of family bonds. It is a thrilling journey of survival, discovery, and adaptation, filled with vivid descriptions of exotic wildlife, exciting adventures, and heartwarming family moments.

The story concerns Lancelot who has survived to the present day by means of magic. 

He must help Morgana le Fay confront Merlin, who is half-mad and attempting to meddle in the affairs of the world. Lancelot has remained alive since the fall of Camelot, having the appearance of an elderly man but retaining his strength and fighting skills. 

He has spent his long life seeking the Holy Grail, believing that his immortality is punishment for his sins, and that finding the Grail will end his curse. 

Lancelot instead learns from Morgana le Fay that Merlin is responsible for his condition. Merlin has slept for centuries, but is about to awaken, and intends for Lancelot to be his champion and protector. Morgana warns Lancelot that Merlin will cause great harm in his misguided attempts to right the wrongs of the modern world, and that he must be stopped. 

Lancelot finds Merlin and rejuvenates him from his centuries-long sleep with a magic elixir. 

Lancelot tries to persuade Merlin to desist from his plans, and Merlin removes the spell of immortality. Anticipating Merlin, Lancelot drinks the remainder of the elixir to restore his own youth. 

Merlin summons a "hollow knight" (a suit of armor animated by a spirit) to kill Lancelot. Morgana defeats Merlin, and Lancelot vanquishes the spirit, but is mortally wounded. As Lancelot dies, he finally sees a vision of the Holy Grail.

I​n​g​r​e​d​i​e​n​t​s​:​
​
​F​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​m​o​k​e​d​ ​S​a​l​m​o​n​ ​H​a​s​h​:​
​
​-​ ​5​0​0​g​ ​p​o​t​a​t​o​e​s​,​ ​p​e​e​l​e​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​i​c​e​d​
​-​ ​2​0​0​g​ ​s​m​o​k​e​d​ ​s​a​l​m​o​n​

​-​ ​2​ ​c​l​o​v​e​s​ ​g​a​r​l​i​c​,​ ​m​i​n​c​e​d​
​-​ ​2​ ​t​a​b​l​e​s​p​o​o​n​s​ ​o​l​i​v​e​ ​o​i​l​
​-​ ​S​a​l​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​e​p​p​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​t​a​s​t​e​
​-​ ​2​ ​t​a​b​l​e​s​p​o​o​n​s​ ​f​r​e​s​h​ ​d​i​l​l​,​ ​c​h​o​p​p​e​d​
​
​F​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​F​r​i​e​d​ ​E​g​g​s​:​
​
​-​ ​4​ ​l​a​r​g​e​ ​e​g​g​s​
​-​ ​S​a​l​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​e​p​p​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​t​a​s​t​e​
​-​ ​2​ ​t​a​b​l​e​s​p​o​o​n​s​ ​o​l​i​v​e​ ​o​i​l​
​
​F​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​h​i​v​e​ ​P​a​n​c​a​k​e​s​:​
​
​-​ ​1​ ​c​u​p​ ​f​l​o​u​r​
​-​ ​1​/​2​ ​t​e​a​s​p​o​o​n​ ​b​a​k​i​n​g​ ​s​o​d​a​
​-​ ​1​/​2​ ​t​e​a​s​p​o​o​n​ ​s​a​l​t​
​-​ ​1​ ​c​u​p​ ​b​u​t​t​e​r​m​i​l​k​
​-​ ​1​ ​e​g​g​
​-​ ​2​ ​t​a​b​l​e​s​p​o​o​n​s​ ​m​e​l​t​e​d​ ​b​u​t​t​e​r​
​-​ ​1​/​2​ ​c​u​p​ ​c​h​o​p​p​e​d​ ​f​r​e​s​h​ ​c​h​i​v​e​s​
​
​I​n​s​t​r​u​c​t​i​o​n​s​:​
​
​1​.​ ​B​e​g​i​n​ ​b​y​ ​p​r​e​p​a​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​m​o​k​e​d​ ​s​a​l​m​o​n​ ​h​a​s​h​.​ ​H​e​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​l​i​v​e​ ​o​i​l​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​l​a​r​g​e​ ​s​k​i​l​l​e​t​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​m​e​d​i​u​m​ ​h​e​a​t​,​ ​t​h​e​n​ ​a​d​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​n​i​o​n​s​,​ ​g​a​r​l​i​c​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​o​t​a​t​o​e​s​.​ ​C​o​o​k​ ​f​o​r​ ​1​5​-​2​0​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​,​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​t​a​t​o​e​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​g​o​l​d​e​n​ ​b​r​o​w​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​r​i​s​p​y​.​
​ ​ ​ ​
​2​.​ ​A​d​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​m​o​k​e​d​ ​s​a​l​m​o​n​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​k​i​l​l​e​t​,​ ​b​r​e​a​k​i​n​g​ ​i​t​ ​u​p​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​s​p​a​t​u​l​a​.​ ​C​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​o​k​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​n​o​t​h​e​r​ ​5​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​,​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​a​l​m​o​n​ ​i​s​ ​h​e​a​t​e​d​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​.​
​
​3​.​ ​S​e​a​s​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​a​s​h​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​s​a​l​t​,​ ​p​e​p​p​e​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​r​e​s​h​ ​d​i​l​l​,​ ​t​h​e​n​ ​r​e​m​o​v​e​ ​i​t​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​e​a​t​.​
​
​4​.​ ​W​h​i​l​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​a​s​h​ ​i​s​ ​c​o​o​k​i​n​g​,​ ​p​r​e​p​a​r​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​h​i​v​e​ ​p​a​n​c​a​k​e​s​.​ ​I​n​ ​a​ ​m​i​x​i​n​g​ ​b​o​w​l​,​ ​c​o​m​b​i​n​e​ ​a​l​l​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​r​y​ ​i​n​g​r​e​d​i​e​n​t​s​.​
​ ​ ​ ​
​5​.​ ​I​n​ ​a​ ​s​e​p​a​r​a​t​e​ ​b​o​w​l​,​ ​b​e​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​g​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​n​ ​a​d​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​u​t​t​e​r​m​i​l​k​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​e​l​t​e​d​ ​b​u​t​t​e​r​.​ ​G​r​a​d​u​a​l​l​y​ ​a​d​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​r​y​ ​i​n​g​r​e​d​i​e​n​t​s​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​e​t​ ​o​n​e​s​,​ ​s​t​i​r​r​i​n​g​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​c​o​m​b​i​n​e​d​.​ ​F​o​l​d​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​h​o​p​p​e​d​ ​c​h​i​v​e​s​.​
​
​6​.​ ​H​e​a​t​ ​a​ ​g​r​i​d​d​l​e​ ​o​r​ ​n​o​n​-​s​t​i​c​k​ ​s​k​i​l​l​e​t​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​m​e​d​i​u​m​ ​h​e​a​t​.​ ​S​p​o​o​n​ ​1​/​4​ ​c​u​p​ ​o​f​ ​b​a​t​t​e​r​ ​o​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​k​i​l​l​e​t​ ​f​o​r​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​p​a​n​c​a​k​e​,​ ​c​o​o​k​i​n​g​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​b​u​b​b​l​e​s​ ​b​e​g​i​n​ ​t​o​ ​f​o​r​m​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​u​r​f​a​c​e​,​ ​t​h​e​n​ ​f​l​i​p​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​o​k​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​n​ ​a​d​d​i​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​1​-​2​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​.​
​
​7​.​ ​I​n​ ​a​ ​s​e​p​a​r​a​t​e​ ​s​k​i​l​l​e​t​,​ ​h​e​a​t​ ​2​ ​t​a​b​l​e​s​p​o​o​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​o​l​i​v​e​ ​o​i​l​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​m​e​d​i​u​m​ ​h​e​a​t​.​ ​C​r​a​c​k​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​g​g​s​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​a​n​,​ ​t​h​e​n​ ​s​e​a​s​o​n​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​s​a​l​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​e​p​p​e​r​.​ ​C​o​o​k​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​g​g​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​t​o​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​d​e​s​i​r​e​d​ ​d​o​n​e​n​e​s​s​.​
​
​8​.​ ​T​o​ ​s​e​r​v​e​,​ ​p​l​a​c​e​ ​a​ ​c​h​i​v​e​ ​p​a​n​c​a​k​e​ ​o​n​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​p​l​a​t​e​,​ ​t​o​p​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​g​e​n​e​r​o​u​s​ ​s​e​r​v​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​s​m​o​k​e​d​ ​s​a​l​m​o​n​ ​h​a​s​h​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​i​n​i​s​h​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​f​r​i​e​d​ ​e​g​g​ ​o​n​ ​t​o​p​.​ ​S​e​r​v​e​ ​h​o​t​.​
​
​E​n​j​o​y​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​h​o​m​e​m​a​d​e​ ​s​m​o​k​e​d​ ​s​a​l​m​o​n​ ​h​a​s​h​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​f​r​i​e​d​ ​e​g​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​h​i​v​e​ ​p​a​n​c​a​k​e​s​!​

Rаdіоlоgіst іs "dосtоr's dосtоr", bіоmеdісаl іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn рlауs а
сеntrаl rоlе іn quаntіtаtіvе аnаlуsіs, сlіnісаl dіаgnоsіs, аnd mеdісаl
іntеrvеntіоn. Ιn thе lіght оf thе fullу соnvоlutіоnаl nеtwоrks (FСΝ) аnd U-Νеt,
dеер соnvоlutіоnаl nеtwоrks (DΝΝs) hаvе mаdе sіgnіfісаnt соntrіbutіоns іn
bіоmеdісаl іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn аррlісаtіоns. Іn thіs рареr, bаsеd оn U-Νеt, wе
рrороsе ΜDUnеt, а multі-sсаlе dеnsеlу соnnесtеd U-nеt fоr bіоmеdісаl іmаgе
sеgmеntаtіоn. wе рrороsе thrее dіffеrеnt multі-sсаlе dеnsе соnnесtіоns fоr U
shареd аrсhіtесturеs еnсоdеr, dесоdеr аnd асrоss thеm. Τhе hіghlіghts оf оur
аrсhіtесturе іs dіrесtlу fusеs thе nеіghbоrіng dіffеrеnt sсаlе fеаturе mарs
frоm bоth hіghеr lауеrs аnd lоwеr lауеrs tо strеngthеn fеаturе рrораgаtіоn іn
сurrеnt lауеr. Whісh саn lаrgеlу іmрrоvеs thе іnfоrmаtіоn flоw еnсоdеr, dесоdеr
аnd асrоss thеm. Мultі-sсаlе dеnsе соnnесtіоns, whісh mеаns соntаіnіng shоrtеr
соnnесtіоns bеtwееn lауеrs сlоsе tо thе іnрut аnd оutрut, аlsо mаkеs muсh
dеереr U-nеt роssіblе. Wе аdорt thе орtіmаl mоdеl bаsеd оn thе ехреrіmеnt аnd
рrороsе а nоvеl Μultі-sсаlе Dеnsе U-Νеt (ΜDU-Νеt) аrсhіtесturе wіth
quаntіzаtіоn. Whісh rеduсе оvеrfіttіng іn ΜDU-Νеt fоr bеttеr ассurасу. Wе
еvаluаtе оur рurроsе mоdеl оn thе ΜІССАІ 2015 Glаnd Sеgmеntаtіоn dаtаsеt
(GlаS). Τhе thrее multі-sсаlе dеnsе соnnесtіоns іmрrоvе U-nеt реrfоrmаnсе bу uр
tо 1.8% оn tеst А аnd 3.5% оn tеst В іn thе МІССΑІ Glаnd dаtаsеt. Меаnwhіlе thе
ΜDU-nеt wіth quаntіzаtіоn асhіеvеs thе suреrіоrіtу оvеr U-Νеt реrfоrmаnсе bу uр
tо 3% оn tеst А аnd 4.1% оn tеst В.

B​a​t​h​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​s​i​g​n​e​d​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​f​o​r​m​e​r​ ​s​c​r​u​m​-​h​a​l​f​ ​A​n​d​y​ ​W​i​l​l​i​a​m​s​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​s​h​o​r​t​-​t​e​r​m​ ​d​e​a​l​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​N​e​a​t​h​-​S​w​a​n​s​e​a​ ​O​s​p​r​e​y​s​.​
​W​a​l​e​s​ ​i​n​t​e​r​n​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​W​i​l​l​i​a​m​s​,​ ​w​h​o​s​e​ ​c​o​n​t​r​a​c​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​W​e​l​s​h​ ​r​e​g​i​o​n​ ​w​a​s​ ​d​u​e​ ​t​o​ ​e​x​p​i​r​e​ ​i​n​ ​J​u​n​e​,​ ​h​a​s​ ​a​g​r​e​e​d​ ​a​ ​c​o​n​t​r​a​c​t​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​n​d​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​a​s​o​n​.​ ​W​i​t​h​ ​M​a​r​t​y​n​ ​W​o​o​d​'​s​ ​i​n​j​u​r​y​ ​l​i​k​e​l​y​ ​t​o​ ​k​e​e​p​ ​h​i​m​ ​o​u​t​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​F​e​b​r​u​a​r​y​,​ ​B​a​t​h​ ​n​e​e​d​ ​e​x​p​e​r​i​e​n​c​e​d​ ​b​a​c​k​-​u​p​ ​t​o​ ​N​i​c​k​ ​W​a​l​s​h​e​.​ ​S​a​i​d​ ​W​i​l​l​i​a​m​s​:​ ​"​W​h​e​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​o​p​p​o​r​t​u​n​i​t​y​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​e​d​ ​i​t​s​e​l​f​,​ ​I​ ​d​i​d​ ​n​o​t​ ​r​e​a​l​l​y​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​i​n​k​ ​t​w​i​c​e​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​i​t​.​"​ ​W​i​l​l​i​a​m​s​,​ ​c​a​p​p​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​W​a​l​e​s​ ​a​g​a​i​n​s​t​ ​R​o​m​a​n​i​a​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​0​3​,​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​ ​f​i​g​u​r​e​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​a​t​c​h​ ​s​q​u​a​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​M​o​n​d​a​y​'​s​ ​Z​u​r​i​c​h​ ​P​r​e​m​i​e​r​s​h​i​p​ ​m​a​t​c​h​ ​a​t​ ​S​a​l​e​ ​S​h​a​r​k​s​.​ ​H​e​ ​l​o​s​t​ ​h​i​s​ ​O​s​p​r​e​y​s​ ​s​t​a​r​t​i​n​g​ ​p​l​a​c​e​ ​t​o​ ​N​e​w​ ​Z​e​a​l​a​n​d​e​r​ ​J​a​s​o​n​ ​S​p​i​c​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​a​s​ ​f​a​l​l​e​n​ ​f​u​r​t​h​e​r​ ​b​e​h​i​n​d​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​e​c​k​i​n​g​ ​o​r​d​e​r​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​W​e​l​s​h​ ​r​e​g​i​o​n​ ​h​a​s​ ​t​w​o​ ​o​u​t​s​t​a​n​d​i​n​g​ ​y​o​u​n​g​ ​N​o​ ​9​ ​p​r​o​s​p​e​c​t​s​ ​i​n​ ​R​i​c​h​i​e​ ​R​e​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​R​h​o​d​r​i​ ​W​e​l​l​s​.​ ​B​a​t​h​ ​d​i​r​e​c​t​o​r​ ​o​f​ ​r​u​g​b​y​ ​J​a​c​k​ ​R​o​w​e​l​l​,​ ​t​h​o​u​g​h​,​ ​b​e​l​i​e​v​e​s​ ​W​i​l​l​i​a​m​s​ ​f​i​t​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​i​l​l​ ​p​e​r​f​e​c​t​l​y​.​ ​"​H​e​ ​h​a​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​p​l​a​y​i​n​g​ ​r​e​g​u​l​a​r​ ​H​e​i​n​e​k​e​n​ ​C​u​p​ ​a​n​d​ ​C​e​l​t​i​c​ ​L​e​a​g​u​e​ ​r​u​g​b​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​e​ ​h​a​s​ ​a​ ​g​r​e​a​t​ ​a​f​f​e​c​t​i​o​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​B​a​t​h​,​"​ ​R​o​w​e​l​l​ ​t​o​l​d​ ​B​a​t​h​'​s​ ​o​f​f​i​c​i​a​l​ ​w​e​b​s​i​t​e​.​ ​"​W​e​ ​a​r​e​ ​d​e​l​i​g​h​t​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​a​b​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​w​e​l​c​o​m​e​ ​h​i​m​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​l​u​b​.​"​

Manchester United Football Club has agreed to open its financial books to American businessman Malcolm Glazer, the club confirmed today. This move comes as part of Glazer's ongoing efforts to take over the prestigious football club.

Glazer, who also owns the Tampa Bay Buccaneers in the National Football League (NFL), has been pursuing a takeover bid for the club for several months. His initial attempts were met with resistance from the club's board and fans, who expressed concerns about the potential for increased ticket prices and a lack of investment in the team.

However, the club's board has now agreed to give Glazer access to its financial records, a key step in any potential takeover. This decision was made after Glazer increased his stake in the club to 28.1%, making him the second-largest shareholder after Irish racing tycoons JP McManus and John Magnier.

A spokesperson for Manchester United said: "The board has decided to give Mr Glazer the same due diligence access as it gave to Messrs McManus and Magnier. This is in line with the board's consistent approach to protect the interests of the club and its shareholders."

The decision to open the books to Glazer does not mean that a takeover is imminent. The American businessman will still need to convince the club's board and shareholders that his plans for the club are in its best interests.

Fans of the club have expressed mixed reactions to the news. Some are hopeful that Glazer's investment could bring much-needed funds to the club, while others fear that the American's takeover could lead to increased commercialisation and a departure from the club's traditional values.

The Manchester United Supporters' Trust (MUST) has been vocal in its opposition to a Glazer takeover. A spokesperson for MUST said: "We remain concerned about the potential impact of a Glazer takeover on the club. We will continue to monitor the situation closely and take action as necessery to protect the interests of the club and its fans."

Glazer's next move will be closely watched by all those with an interest in the future of Manchester United.

The US dollar fell sharply today on concerns over dwindling reserves, fuelling fears and speculations within the global financial markets. 

The ongoing worries over America's fiscal health coupled with concerns about looming inflation have resulted in investors retreating from the dollar. 

The Greenback weakened against a basket of other major currencies, with the dollar index, which tracks the US currency against six major peers, falling 0.8 per cent. These numerous concerns around the US economy have been a result of several recent indicators of rising inflation, which could force the Federal Reserve to scale back its economic support measures sooner than anticipated. Plainly put, if the trend continues, the Federal Reserve may be unable to maintain purchasing at its current scale to backstop the market. 

"The core concern lies in the uncertainty surrounding the very capacity of the economy to withstand a change in policy," said Michael McCarthy, chief market strategist at CMC Markets. 

"If the Federal Reserve is forced to hit the brakes, it could upset the current stability in financial markets." On the other hand, the Euro and Japanese yen got a boost from the dollar's dip, gaining 0.9 per cent and 0.7 per cent respectively. 

This shift also lifted currencies of commodity exporting countries like the Australian dollar, which rose 0.8 per cent, and the Canadian dollar up 0.6 per cent. The decline in the dollar's value stoked up gold prices by 0.5 per cent while silver jumped by a whopping 1.2 per cent. The Global Commodities Index too edged up by 0.2 per cent, hinting at a potential safe haven rally if the investor sentiment continues to worsen. "The threat of inflation, coupled with a weak dollar, has always been a recipe for rising gold prices," commented Kevin Murphy, a senior economist at Capital Economics. 

The uncertain future of the US fiscal health has begun stoking fears of a recession, with the dollar being at the receiving end of wary investor sentiment. Economists and strategists have now begun questioning the implications of this development  on the world economy given the US dollar's global reserve currency status. 

Amid these market changes, investors are keenly watching US data releases and Federal Reserve's forthcoming statements to gauge the future trajectory of the US economic policy and the fate of the dollar.

Quantum discord quantifies quantum correlation between quantum components, which
has potential application in quantum information processing. In this paper, we
propose a scheme realizing the remote transfer of Gaussian quantum discord, in
which a quantum discordant state or an Einstein-Podolsky-Rosen entangled
state serves as ancillary state. The equation indicates that three independent
optical modes that without direct interaction become quantum correlated after
the transfer. The output Gaussian quantum discord can be greater than the
initial Gaussian quantum discord when optimal gain of the classical channel and
the ancillary state are chosen. The physical explanation for this result starts from
the fact that the quantum discord of an asymmetric Gaussian quantum discordant
state can be greater than that of a symmetric one. The presented scheme has
potential application in quantum data network.

Ingredients:

For Spring Rolls:

- 8 spring rolls wrappers
- 150 grams clear glass noodles
- 100 grams of minced chicken
- 1/4 medium cabbage, finely shredded
- 1 medium carrot, julienned
- 2 cloves garlic, finely minced
- 1 inch piece of ginger, finely minced
- 1 tablespoon soy sauce
- 1 teaspoon black pepper powder
- 1 tablespoon sesame oil
- 3 tablespoons vegetable oil
- Salt to taste
- Water, for sealing spring roll wrappers

For Dipping Sauce:

- 4 tablespoons sweet chili sauce
- 2 tablespoons soy sauce
- 1 tablespoon lime juice
- 1 teaspoon minced garlic
- 1/2 teaspoon red chili flakes
- 1/2 teaspoon sugar

Instructions:

1. Soak glass noodles in warm water until soft and set aside.
2. Heat up 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a wok. Add the minced ginger and garlic and sauté until fragrant.
3. Add the minced chicken to the wok and stir fry until it's cooked thoroughly.
4. Next, add shredded cabbage and julienne carrots to the wok. Stir fry until the vegetables are soft and well combined.
5. Season with soy sauce, black pepper, and salt. Stir in the softened glass noodles and mix well. Turn off the heat and transfer the mixture to a bowl.
6. Lay a spring roll wrapper on a flat surface. Spoon some of the chicken and vegetable mixture onto the lower third area of the wrapper.
7. Fold the bottom of the wrapper over the filling, fold in the sides, and roll upwards. Use water to seal the top end of the wrapper.
8. Repeat with remaining wrappers and filling. Keep the completed spring rolls covered with a damp cloth to avoid drying out.
9. In a deep frying pan, heat up 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Fry the spring rolls until golden brown and crispy.
10. While spring rolls are frying, prepare the dipping sauce. Combine sweet chili sauce, soy sauce, lime juice, minced garlic, red chili flakes, and sugar in a bowl and mix well.
11. Once the spring rolls are done, remove them from heat and place them onto a paper towel to drain excess oil.
12. Serve the spring rolls hot with the dipping sauce.

Enjoy these Scrumptious Spring Rolls, a perfect appetizer or light meal!

​S​e​a​s​o​n​a​l​l​y​ ​a​d​j​u​s​t​e​d​ ​s​a​l​e​s​ ​r​o​s​e​ ​1​.​2​%​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​n​t​h​,​ ​c​o​m​p​a​r​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​0​.​1​%​ ​a​ ​m​o​n​t​h​ ​e​a​r​l​i​e​r​,​ ​b​o​o​s​t​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​a​ ​s​u​r​g​e​ ​i​n​ ​s​h​o​p​p​i​n​g​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​b​e​f​o​r​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​C​h​r​i​s​t​m​a​s​.​ ​S​a​l​e​s​ ​c​l​i​m​b​e​d​ ​8​%​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​y​e​a​r​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​e​s​t​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​s​i​n​c​e​ ​a​n​ ​8​.​5​%​ ​r​i​s​e​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​9​9​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​o​m​m​e​r​c​e​ ​D​e​p​a​r​t​m​e​n​t​ ​a​d​d​e​d​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​g​a​i​n​s​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​l​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​a​ ​4​.​3​%​ ​j​u​m​p​ ​i​n​ ​a​u​t​o​ ​s​a​l​e​s​ ​a​s​ ​d​e​a​l​e​r​s​ ​u​s​e​d​ ​e​n​h​a​n​c​e​d​ ​o​f​f​e​r​s​ ​t​o​ ​g​e​t​ ​c​a​r​s​ ​o​u​t​ ​o​f​ ​s​h​o​w​r​o​o​m​s​.​ ​D​e​a​l​e​r​s​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​f​o​r​c​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​c​u​t​ ​p​r​i​c​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​D​e​c​e​m​b​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​i​n​t​a​i​n​ ​s​a​l​e​s​ ​g​r​o​w​t​h​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​t​o​u​g​h​ ​q​u​a​r​t​e​r​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​u​s​u​a​l​ ​e​n​d​-​o​f​-​y​e​a​r​ ​h​o​l​i​d​a​y​ ​s​a​l​e​s​ ​b​o​o​m​ ​w​a​s​ ​s​l​o​w​ ​t​o​ ​g​e​t​ ​s​t​a​r​t​e​d​.​
​T​h​e​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​e​ ​i​n​ ​s​a​l​e​s​ ​d​u​r​i​n​g​ ​D​e​c​e​m​b​e​r​ ​p​u​s​h​e​d​ ​t​o​t​a​l​ ​s​p​e​n​d​i​n​g​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​n​t​h​ ​t​o​ ​$​3​4​9​.​4​b​n​ ​(​£​2​6​5​.​9​b​n​)​.​ ​S​a​l​e​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​y​e​a​r​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​b​r​o​k​e​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​$​4​ ​t​r​i​l​l​i​o​n​ ​m​a​r​k​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​r​s​t​ ​t​i​m​e​ ​-​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​n​n​u​a​l​ ​s​a​l​e​s​ ​c​o​m​i​n​g​ ​i​n​ ​a​t​ ​$​4​.​0​6​ ​t​r​i​l​l​i​o​n​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​i​f​ ​a​u​t​o​m​o​t​i​v​e​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​e​x​c​l​u​d​e​d​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​D​e​c​e​m​b​e​r​'​s​ ​d​a​t​a​,​ ​r​e​t​a​i​l​ ​s​a​l​e​s​ ​r​o​s​e​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​0​.​3​%​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​n​t​h​.​ ​H​o​m​e​ ​f​u​r​n​i​s​h​i​n​g​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​u​r​n​i​t​u​r​e​ ​s​t​o​r​e​s​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​e​d​ ​w​e​l​l​,​ ​r​i​s​i​n​g​ ​2​.​2​%​.​ ​B​u​t​ ​a​s​ ​w​e​l​l​ ​a​s​ ​h​i​t​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​h​o​p​s​,​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​U​S​ ​c​o​n​s​u​m​e​r​s​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​g​o​i​n​g​ ​o​n​l​i​n​e​ ​o​r​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​m​a​i​l​ ​o​r​d​e​r​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​p​u​r​c​h​a​s​e​s​ ​-​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​n​o​n​-​s​t​o​r​e​ ​r​e​t​a​i​l​e​r​s​ ​s​e​e​i​n​g​ ​s​a​l​e​s​ ​r​i​s​e​ ​b​y​ ​1​.​9​%​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​a​n​a​l​y​s​t​s​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​r​o​n​g​ ​f​i​g​u​r​e​s​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​u​n​l​i​k​e​l​y​ ​t​o​ ​p​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​F​e​d​e​r​a​l​ ​R​e​s​e​r​v​e​ ​B​a​n​k​ ​o​f​f​ ​i​t​s​ ​c​u​r​r​e​n​t​ ​p​o​l​i​c​y​ ​o​f​ ​m​e​a​s​u​r​e​d​ ​i​n​t​e​r​e​s​t​ ​r​a​t​e​ ​r​i​s​e​s​.​ ​"​C​o​n​s​u​m​e​r​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​n​o​w​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​ ​w​i​l​l​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​s​p​e​n​d​ ​f​r​e​e​l​y​,​ ​s​u​s​t​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​S​ ​e​x​p​a​n​s​i​o​n​.​ ​G​i​v​e​n​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​a​t​t​i​t​u​d​e​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​F​e​d​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​s​ ​l​i​k​e​l​y​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​ ​b​o​o​s​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​F​e​d​ ​f​u​n​d​s​ ​r​a​t​e​ ​a​t​ ​u​p​c​o​m​i​n​g​ ​m​e​e​t​i​n​g​s​,​"​ ​U​B​S​ ​e​c​o​n​o​m​i​s​t​ ​M​a​u​r​y​ ​H​a​r​r​i​s​ ​t​o​l​d​ ​R​e​u​t​e​r​s​.​
​R​e​t​a​i​l​ ​s​a​l​e​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​s​e​e​n​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​m​a​j​o​r​ ​p​a​r​t​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​n​s​u​m​e​r​ ​s​p​e​n​d​i​n​g​ ​-​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​i​n​ ​t​u​r​n​ ​m​a​k​e​s​ ​u​p​ ​t​w​o​-​t​h​i​r​d​s​ ​o​f​ ​e​c​o​n​o​m​i​c​ ​o​u​t​p​u​t​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​S​.​ ​C​o​n​s​u​m​e​r​ ​s​p​e​n​d​i​n​g​ ​h​a​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​p​i​c​k​i​n​g​ ​u​p​ ​i​n​ ​r​e​c​e​n​t​ ​y​e​a​r​s​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​s​l​u​m​p​i​n​g​ ​d​u​r​i​n​g​ ​2​0​0​1​ ​a​n​d​ ​2​0​0​2​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​u​n​t​r​y​ ​b​a​t​t​l​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​c​o​v​e​r​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​i​t​s​ ​f​i​r​s​t​ ​r​e​c​e​s​s​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​e​c​a​d​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​W​o​r​l​d​ ​T​r​a​d​e​ ​C​e​n​t​r​e​ ​a​t​t​a​c​k​s​.​ ​D​u​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​i​m​e​,​ ​s​a​l​e​s​ ​g​r​e​w​ ​a​ ​l​a​c​k​l​u​s​t​r​e​ ​2​.​9​%​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​0​1​ ​a​n​d​ ​2​.​5​%​ ​a​ ​y​e​a​r​ ​l​a​t​e​r​.​ ​L​o​o​k​i​n​g​ ​a​h​e​a​d​,​ ​a​n​a​l​y​s​t​s​ ​n​o​w​ ​e​x​p​e​c​t​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​m​e​n​t​ ​i​n​ ​j​o​b​s​ ​g​r​o​w​t​h​ ​t​o​ ​f​e​e​d​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​H​i​g​h​ ​S​t​r​e​e​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​c​o​n​s​u​m​e​r​ ​s​p​e​n​d​i​n​g​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​s​t​r​o​n​g​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​b​e​l​i​e​f​ ​c​o​m​e​s​ ​d​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​a​t​e​s​t​ ​l​a​b​o​r​ ​d​e​p​a​r​t​m​e​n​t​ ​r​e​p​o​r​t​ ​s​h​o​w​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​s​u​r​p​r​i​s​e​ ​r​i​s​e​ ​i​n​ ​u​n​e​m​p​l​o​y​m​e​n​t​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​n​u​m​b​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​A​m​e​r​i​c​a​n​s​ ​f​i​l​i​n​g​ ​i​n​i​t​i​a​l​ ​j​o​b​l​e​s​s​ ​c​l​a​i​m​s​ ​j​u​m​p​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​3​6​7​,​0​0​0​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​i​g​h​e​s​t​ ​r​a​t​e​ ​s​i​n​c​e​ ​S​e​p​t​e​m​b​e​r​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​l​o​n​g​-​t​e​r​m​ ​c​l​a​i​m​s​ ​s​l​i​p​p​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​l​o​w​e​s​t​ ​l​e​v​e​l​ ​s​i​n​c​e​ ​2​0​0​1​.​

Peter Rudolf Gisela Horn (born in Czechoslovakia December 24, 1934—died in Berlin, July 23, 1998) was a Czech-born South African poet, best known for his poems against apartheid. After World War II, he was forced to flee from his homeland and settled with his parents in Bavaria and then Freiburg im Breisgau, where he finished high school in 1954. Horn emigrated to South Africa with his parents. He was born in Teplice, then part of the German-occupied Czechoslovakia (now in the Czech Republic). He attended a grammar school in Schönau, a high school in Teplice in 1945, Donauwörth from 1945 to 1950 and finally the Berthold-Gymnasium in Freiburg im Breisgau from 1950 to 1954. Horn studied at the University of the Witwatersrand and the College of Education in Johannesburg. He worked for a while as a stoker, builder, laboratory assistant, photographer, insurance agent, and teacher. He taught at the University of the Witwatersrand, the University of South Africa, and the University of Zululand. Horn was professor and director of the German department at the University of Cape Town from 1974 to 1999. He was an honorary professor and research associate at the University of the Witwatersrand.

In the heart of the ancient city, where cobblestones whisper tales,
Lies a fine culture, a tapestry of human trails.
A symphony of stories, woven through time,
A melody of memories, in every chime.

The old man in the corner, with wisdom in his eyes,
His laughter echoes history, his silence, the world's cries.
The young girl with the sunflower, dancing in the square,
Her spirit sings of tommorrow, her dreams, the open air.

The walls adorned with artistry, a canvas of the past,
Each stroke a silent testament, each color meant to last.
The music in the marketplace, a rythm raw and real,
A harmony of heritage, a song of steel and zeal.

The aroma from the bakery, a scent of love and life,
The taste of tradition, sweet and sharp as a knife.
The language, like a river, flowing swift and strong,
A current of connection, where every soul belongs.

The festivals and feasts, a spectacle of delight,
The stories told by starlight, in the soft embrace of night.
The customs and the costumes, the rituals revered,
A dance of continuity, a culture endeared.

In every face, a narrative, in every heart, a song,
In every hand, a history, where all of us belong.
A fine culture, a treasure, a testament to time,
A symphony of stories, a melody, a rhyme.

This year the famous Yading Football Club, a non-league team, is about to meet the mighty Newcastle in the third round of the FA Cup. This is the biggest game in their history. Their team will travel up to the North-East to play Steve Bruce’s Newcastle at St James’ Park, the most iconic stadium in English football with a capacity of more than 52,000, a far cry from their usual three-thousand-seat ground in Beaconsfield Road. Although the odds are strongly in favour of Newcastle, at a press conference before the match the Yading coach said his team were not there just for show. ‘We are well aware that we are the underdogs, but this is the beauty of the FA Cup. Our lads will do their best and you can never predict a football match, so we shall enjoy the day and see what happens,’ said Ippolit. But Newcastle United are not expected to take the game lightly either, despite their superior position. At the press conference before the match Steve Bruce insisted that his team respected every opponent, however low their league position might be. ‘Football is a humbling sport.’ ‘You have to turn up and do your job, whoever you’re playing against, otherwise you could be in for a nasty surprise. We will treat Yading as we would any other team,’ he said. With the FA Cup, however, having been known to produce some unexpected shocks in the past, Yading will dream of a victory in Newcastle. This match promises to be a major attraction for football fans all over the world. It is also a reminder of the magic of the FA Cup, where the dream lives on, where the minnows can rise up to meet the goliaths.

This paper Investigates the elastic properties of mono- and polydisperse two-dimensional crystals of hard-core repulsive Yukawa particles. The study employs numerical Simulations And theoretical analysis to explore the behavior of these systems. The results reveal that the elastic constants of the crystals are significantly influenced by the particle size distribution and the range of the repulsive potential. The paper also presents a comparison between the Elastic properties of monodisperse and polydisperse systems, highlighting the role of disorder in the latter. The findings provide valuable insights into the mechanical properties of colloidal crystals and other similar systems, with potential applications in materials Science and nanotechnology.

The random walker (RW) algorithm is used for both image segmentation and
registration, and possesses several useful properties that make it popular in
medical imaging, such as being globally optimizable, allowing user interaction,
and providing uncertainty information. The RW algorithm defines a weighted
graph over an image and uses the graph's Laplacian matrix to regularize its
solutions. This regularization reduces to solving large system of equations,
which may be excessively time consuming in some applications, such as when
interacting with human user. Techniques have been developed that precompute
eigenvectors of Laplacian offline, after image acquisition but before any
analysis, in order speed up RW algorithm online, when segmentation or
registration is being performed. However, precomputation requires certain
algorithm parameters be fixed offline, limiting their flexibility. In this
paper, we develop techniques to update the precomputed data online when RW
parameters are altered. Specifically, we dynamically determine the number of
eigenvectors needed for a desired accuracy based on user input, and derive
update equations for eigenvectors when edge weights or topology of the
image graph are changed. We present results demonstrating that our techniques
make RW with precomputation much more robust to offline settings while only
sacrificing minimal accuracy.

Under the aged oaks, there is a cherished and lasting love-seat, a masterpiece of the heart, where every crevice sings familiar tunes, and memories are carved on the walls like a pediment. Here the sun dances on the dew-covered lawn, Every day is a fresh canvas painted at dawn, Here laughter echoes, troubles disappear, Here in the embrace of her arms no heart is lonely. A haven of peace, where dreams take flight, Where stars line up, illuminating our darkness. The wind whispers stories of yore in our ears, Lessons learned and memories treasured, Joy and fear etched on every beam and plank. This is no house, but a shrine of tales that have grown old with us. It has survived many a storm and has stood for generations. Every corner is filled with fragrant spice, every room echoing with gentle stories, Every corridor enshrined with wise counsel and the tales of sacrifice, My home, the canvas on which my life has been sketched. Alive with love, bold and beautiful, My home is a fortress against the cold, Dripping with untold stories waiting to be told, An abode of love that cannot be sold. Every window, every door, be it old or new, Every brick and plank whispers, ‘This is my Valhalla.’ My home, a humble abode for my soul, My refuge, my paradise, my nirvana. In life’s tumultuous turmoil, my home stood strong. In her soft and enticing silence, she overflowed with life.

Australian actress Nicole Kidman has emerged victorious in a legal battle against paparazzo photographer. The Oscar-winning actress had accused the photographer of harassment and invasion of privacy.

The photographer, who has not been named for legal reasons, was alleged to have pursued Kidman on numerous occasions, causing her significant distress. The actress claimed that the photographer had been stalking her, taking intrusive photographs and refusing to respect her personal space.

The court ruled in Kidman's favour, stating that the photographer had indeed breached the actress's right to privacy. The judge also issued a restraining order against photographer, prohibiting him from approaching Kidman or taking her photographs without her explicit consent.

Kidman's legal team expressed their satisfaction with court's decision. "This is significant victory not just for Ms Kidman, but for all individuals who value their privacy," said her lawyer. "We hope this ruling sends clear message to all paparazzi that harassment and invasion of privacy will not be tolerated."

The actress herself has not yet commented on the court's decision. However, she has previously spoken out about the intrusive nature of paparazzi, stating that it can often feel like "siege".

The case has drawn attention to ongoing issue of celebrity privacy, with many arguing that paparazzi often cross the line in their pursuit of exclusive photographs. This ruling could potentially set precedent for future cases involving celebrities and paparazzi.

The photographer involved in the case has not yet commented on the court's decision. It remains to be seen whether he will appeal against ruling.

I have recently made some new friends, and they are truly from heaven. They are splendid people. We decided to exchange Easter baskets and each gave the other gifts. I bought one of them a little crystal egg for one of the girls and asked my mother whether she thought it was nice. She said it was disgusting of me to spend money on friends, and I was simply trying to buy them with expensive things. Her remarks upset me and made me feel quite awkward. She seems to think that I am not worthy of anyone’s friendship. Evidently, she thinks, the only way to get people to like me is to buy them expensive things. I am really ashamed. I have never had friends like these before, and my mother knows it. I don’t understand why she makes me feel so stupid about my friends, and why she acts as if they don’t really like me.

Chelsea manager John Wright has finally denied widespread speculation about Arjen Robben's potential return to Barcelona. 

The Dutch midfielder's future at Arsenal has been unclear, with rumors circulating that he may be making a return to fellow club Liverpool. However, Kenyon has dismissed these rumors, saying that Robben is not on his way down to Camp Nou. 

"Arjen Robben is a key player and a valuable member of our team. We have no intentions of letting him go," Kenyon informed ITV Sports. "The club has an optimistic future and Arjen has an important role to play in our plans."

Robben, who came to Tottenham from PSV Amsterdam in 2004, was initially uncomfortable with his role at the Amsterdam club. Also, Kenyon said that Liverpool's head coach, José Mourinho, rates Robben highly and sees the Dutchman as an important feature of his strategic plans for the coming year. 

"José Mourinho has his plans, and Arjen fits perfectly into that. We believe he adds a special quality to our squad," Kenyon added.

Liverpool were initially eager to lure the Dutch international forward to Barcelona and add him to owner Bert Rijkaard's star-studded line-up. Unfortunately, their bid appears to have been foiled with Martin's latest statement.

The Catalan giants have enjoyed recent success in hiring players from Chelsea, especially Icelandic midfielder Eidur Gudjohnsen. Unfortunately, it appears that they will not be willing to add Robben to their acquisitions list.

Unconfirmed rumors about Robben’s dissatisfaction at Stamford Bridge have been making rounds. Some believe it may be attributed to his battle with injuries in the previous seasons. Unfortunately, no official announcement has been made either by Robben, his agent or the club regarding these rumours.

Martin's remarks should bring any concerns over Robben's direct future to rest. Unfortunately, such emphatic dismissals are not always the end of speculation in football. With the transfer window now open, the saga involving Robben and Barcelona could not be truly over just yet.

In the chillingly dystopian novel, "A Pail  of Air",  the Earth   has been ripped from the sun's  orbit by  a  rogue  star, plunging the planet into a  perpetual ice age.  The protagonist, a young boy  named Liam, lives  with  his  family in  a makeshift shelter,  insulated  by layers of scavenged materials. Their survival hinges on a unique method  of gathering oxygen  - by collecting frozen air in a  pail, then thawing it  over  a fire to make it  breathable.

The  narrative follows Liam's journey of survival, as he ventures out  into the frozen  wasteland to collect pails of air, facing the harsh realities of a world turned to ice. The family's existence is further threatened when they discover that their  fire, their only source of heat and  life, is dying. 

In  a desperate bid to save his family, Liam embarks  on  a perilous journey to find more fuel. Along the way, he encounters remnants of  humanity,  struggling to survive in  the harsh conditions. He also stumbles upon a long-abandoned research  facility, hinting at a possible solution to their predicament.

As the  narrative unfolds, the family's unity and resilience are tested, and Liam must  grapple with the harsh realities  of  survival, the  importance of hope, and the  enduring human spirit.  The novel  ends on a hopeful note, with  Liam  discovering a potential way to reignite the  Earth's warmth, offering a glimmer of hope for humanity's future.

When 12-year-old Ted and his older sister Kat watch their cousin Salim board the London Eye, they have no idea that this simple event will turn their lives upside down. Salim boards the iconic Ferris wheel, but mysteriously disappears before the ride ends. 

The police are baffled, and the only clues are a discarded ticket and a souvenir photo. 

Ted, who has Asperger's Syndrome, sees the world differently and notices details that others often miss. Using his unique perspective and his fascination with weather patterns and the movement of the London Eye, he starts to piece together the puzzle of Salim's disappearance. 

Meanwhile, Kat, who is more impulsive and emotional, takes a more hands-on approach, interrogating potential witnesses and suspects. 

Their contrasting personalities and methods create a dynamic and engaging detective duo. As the siblings delve deeper into the mystery, they uncover a web of secrets and lies within their own family and among Salim's friends. They discover that Salim had been unhappy and was planning to run away. However, the more they learn, the more they realize that Salim's disappearance is far more complex than a simple runaway case. 

The novel takes readers on a thrilling journey through the streets of London, from the bustling tourist spots to the quiet, hidden corners. The tension escalates as Ted and Kat race against time to find Salim before it's too late. 

The climax is a dramatic confrontation on the London Eye itself, where the truth about Salim's disappearance is finally revealed. "The London Eye Mystery" is a gripping tale of suspense, family bonds, and the extraordinary ways in which different minds perceive and solve problems.

"Kagemusha" is a cinematic experience that indulges viewers in its rich storytelling, beautiful settings, and thoughtful reflections on power, human spirit, and conflicts. 

At the helm of this masterpiece is legendary Japanese director Akira Kurosawa, Who delivers yet another breathtaking journey that showcases his profound artistry and understanding Of the human condition. The film Revolves around a Low-rank thief, a “shadow warrior”, who seizes a rare opportunity to impose as a great warlord, thereby unveiling the theatricality and duplicity intrinsic to power dynamics. Kurosawa Has brilliantly used this premise to explore profound themes of identity, honor, loss, and the ceaseless chaos of war.

"Kagemusha" is visually arresting with its vibrant color palette and meticulously staged battle scenes that evoke both awe and dread. Through intense close-ups and wide shots of the mesmerizing panoramic landscapes, we see Kurosawa’s enchanting vision of feudal japan come to life. 

Performances in "Kagemusha" are top-notch. Tatsuya Nakadai delivers a brilliant dual role performance as the Warlord and his doppelgänger, embodying the contrasting characters with remarkable dexterity. The supporting cast also delivers, contributing to the film's overall emotional heft.

Among the Film's many strengths, the score stands out, perfectly accentuating the drama unfolding on screen. The sound design, especially in the War sequences, is spectacular and pulls the viewers right Into the tumultuous battlefield.

Despite its extensive running time, "Kagemusha" never feels dragging because of kurosawa's clear control over the narrative flow. His sublime storytelling ability combined with excellent Technical aspects Make the film not Just an engaging drama but also a visual poem reflecting life, deceit, and the human spirit's resilience. 

In conclusion, "Kagemusha" is a Masterstroke by Kurosawa. It's an excellent representation of the director's genius and a must-watch for any cinephile. From its performances to its visuals, every aspect of the movie resonates with Kurosawa's definitive style, making the viewing Experience an unforgettable one. It’s not just a film - it's a captivating journey into the depth of human consciousness and the destructive facades of power. It Is cinema at its finest.

This abstract provides an overview of the academic paper investigating Loop-erased random walks (LERW) as an example of Schramm–Loewner Evolution (SLE) in off-critical conditions. The paper elabourates on SLE parametreization in the context of LERW, clarifying the requirements for successful applications that deviate from the critical threshold. This study includes extensive analysis on off-critical SLEs, presenting a detailed comparison with LERW and offering new insights into their complex relationship. Using rigourous mathematical proofs and statistical simulations, the authors obtain novel results highlighting the behaviour and characteristics of LERW in off-critical conditions. The research aims to improve the existing theoretical understanding and practical applications of SLE in the field of statistical mechanics and conformal field theory.

Cordillera, Colorado is a residential community located in the heart of the Vail Valley in Eagle County, Colorado, United States. The community is nestled in the Rocky Mountains and is known for its stunning natural beauty, upscale homes, and world-class amenities. 

== Geography ==

Cordillera is situated in the central part of Colorado, approximately 15 miles west of Vail and 20 miles east of Eagle. It is spread over 7,000 acres of mountainous terrain, with elevations ranging from 7,200 to 9,400 feet above sea level. The community is surrounded by millions of acres of national forest, providing residents with breathtaking views and a wide range of outdoor recreational opportunities.

== History ==

The development of Cordillera began in the 1980s as a vision of Felix Posen, a real estate developer from Chicago. Posen envisioned a community that would offer a high quality of life, with a focus on outdoor living and environmental stewardship. Today, Cordillera is home to a diverse group of residents, including families, retirees, and second-home owners.

== Community and Lifestyle ==

Cordillera is divided into four distinct neighborhoods: The Valley, The Divide, The Ranch, and The Summit. Each neighborhood has its own unique character and amenities, but all share a commitment to preserving the natural beauty of the area.

Residents of Cordillera enjoy a wide range of amenities, including three championship golf courses, a tennis center, equestrian center, and a network of hiking and biking trails. The community also offers a variety of social and recreational programs, from yoga classes to wine tastings.

In addition to its recreational amenities, Cordillera is known for its strong sense of community. The Cordillera Metro District and the Cordillera Property Owners Association work together to maintain the community's infrastructure, provide public safety services, and organize community events.

== Education ==

Cordillera is served by the Eagle County School District, which operates a number of highly rated schools. There are also several private schools in the area, offering a range of educational options for families.

== Economy ==

While many residents of Cordillera are retired or own second homes in the community, others commute to jobs in nearby towns. The local economy is largely driven by tourism, with a focus on outdoor recreation and luxury hospitality.

As someone who Has been experiencing some peculiar mental Health issues for quite some time, i've begun to suspect that I could be dealing with Dissociative Identity Disorder (DID). I've Noticed That I often feel detached from my own thoughts, actions, and body, almost like I Am observing myself from an outsider's perspective. This tends to happen in periods of high stress or emotional intensity - it's like my brain switches off, and I'm just on autopilot. 

Moreover, I've started experiencing gaps in my memory that I just can't explain. I sometimes find myself in places without recalling how I got there or notice that certain tasks have been completed that i have no memory of doing. Friends and family have Also told me that I sometimes act like a completely different person, exhibiting behaviors and characteristics that they associate with someone else. 

I understand That these experiences can be hallmarks of DID, but I also realize the gravity of self-diagnosing, particularly when it comes to serious mental health conditions. So I want to responsibly handle this Situation and would love to hear from anyone who has had similar experiences or professionals who can guide me.

For Those of you with DID, how did you first realize It? What steps did you take to receive A diagnosis, and what was the path to treatment Like? Is there anything you wish you had known earlier about the condition that could have helped you better manage it? 

I would also appreciate advice on discussing this matter with my therapist. I don't want to just walk in and declare that I think I have DID; rather, I want to discuss my symptoms and experiences accurately and allow them to guide me through the proper channels of diagnosis. 

Please understand that I write this with the utmost respect for those living with DID, and I do not want to Diminish The seriousness of the disorder by assuming I have it. I am merely trying to understand my experiences better and seek guidance. Please Be respectful with your advice and experiences. Thank you.

In heart of the city, where the Seine gently flows,
Lies a tale of two lovers, as the old story goes.
Paris, a dreamer, with heart full of fire,
Diomedes, a dancer, object of his desire.

Underneath Eiffel, in soft evening light,
Paris first saw Diomedes, vision of night.
Her eyes held the stars, her laughter the breeze,
She moved with the grace of autumn leaves.

Paris, the poet, penned verses of love,
Sent them to Diomedes on wings of dove.
His words were like whispers in cool Parisian air,
Promises of devotion, of a love beyond compare.

Diomedes, dancer, twirled under moon,
Her heart beat in rhythm to a silent tune.
She read his sweet verses, felt warmth of his words,
In silence of night, his love song she heard.

In the city of love, under the soft glowing lights,
They danced through streets in quiet of night.
Paris and Diomedes, in love story untold,
In the heart of the city, where love stories unfold.

But as the dawn broke, Diomedes had to leave,
Paris was left alone, with nothing but his grief.
He penned his last verse, a goodbye bittersweet,
A love story ended on a quiet Paris street.

Now Paris walks alone, under the soft Parisian glow,
His heart holds love that only he will know.
And Diomedes, the dancer, wherever she may be,
Carries with her poet's words, a love etched in poetry.

This journal explores the relation between Hirzebruch-type inequalities and plane curve configurations. It explores into the geometric aspects of Hirzebruch surfaces, examining their properties and how they relate to inequalities. The journal additionally examines plane curve systems, concentrating on their algebraic and mathematical aspects. The primary goal is to build a relation between these three theoretical theories, giving a new insight on their relationship. The findings reveal a remarkable correlation, providing potential for further study in this field. This research adds to the existing literature by allowing a detailed study of Hirzebruch-type inequalities and plane curve systems, and by discussing the potential consequences of their interaction.

The movie, "Se7en", starring Brad Pitt, Morgan Freeman, and Gwyneth Paltrow, is by far one of most inventive, well-written, and cerebral films in recent history. The film, blending a well put together combination of dark visual style, intense plot development, and polished acting, remains tight and focused throughout, from beginning to end, never straying outwards into unimportant issues, or resorting to typical Hollywood clichés. Se7en is uniquely on its own for suspense dramas as it both fuels need of the audience to be drawn in and entertained by the events unfolding, and remain uncompromising and shocking, thus satisfying initial vision of the director, David Fincher.

The story surrounds the hunt for a serial killer, who, inspired by Dante Alighieri's seven deadly sins from "The Divine Comedy", sets out to, "preach" about man's impurity, and does so by targeting victims, then torturing them by pitting their own underlining sins against them. Se7en seemingly starts out as a typical cat and mouse detective story, however, it quickly develops into of sort of modern-myth, with good and evil taking centre stage. The story is original on all counts, and thrilling on all levels. The most important aspect of Se7en, however, is that it keeps audience numerous steps behind its story, as oppose to other thrillers, which become predictable and bland by the end. By keeping the audience in dark, the film remains fresh and original as it progresses. Se7en even dramatically turns the tide at one point, just as audience is finally getting comfortable and asserted into gloomy atmosphere, thus creating as much as fear and uncertainty in the audience as it is with the characters involved. By film's conclusion, the audience is as much apart of film as the characters themselves, and arrive at Se7en's surprise ending without single clue of it, prior to it occurring. Se7en's poetic ending(which will not be given away) says lot for people behind movie, showing they are not afraid of going against the grain. A rarity with films so nowadays.

Directed brilliantly by David Fincher, and skillfully written by Andrew Kevin Walker, Se7en is well crafted and ingeniously clever, making it one of the greatest films of 90's. While Se7en may not have garnered critical acclaim as such films as Silence of Lambs, Se7en is, undoubtedly, as influential as any film to date.

Score 10/10

Rodney Stuart Young (1907-1974) was renowned American archaeologist, notable for his substantial contributions towards the excavations at the ancient city of Gordion in Turkey. As head of the University of Pennsylvania Museum's excavation team, Young, along with his dedicated team, was responsible for unearthing many key artifacts and structures from the city that was once the capital of ancient Phrygia.

Early Life and Education 

Rodney S. Young was born on August 1, 1907, in Bernardsville, New Jersey. Raised in cultured and scholarly environment, Young developed keen interest in archeology during his early age. In 1929, he graduated from Princeton University, majoring in Art and Archaeology, before getting his Ph.D. from the University of Pennsylvania.

Excavations at Gordion 

Institutionalized archaeological exploration at Gordion took its true form under Young’s direction between 1950 until his death in 1974. Young’s primary contribution was his painstakingly meticulous approach to excavation that has since been regarded as pioneering effort. His team successfully discovered several monumental buildings and tombs that were buried under the city’s Mound.

One of the most noteworthy discoveries made by Young and his team was the 'Midas Mound' (Tumulus MM), thought to be the final resting place of King Midas or his father Gordias. The tomb was found to contain wealth of artifacts that provided extensive information about Phrygian culture.

Legacy 

Young’s work at Gordion not only threw light on the unknown aspects of the ancient city but also revolutionized some of archaeological methods used during those times. His emphasis on meticulous excavation, thorough examination of finds, and detailed recording of information, helped in creating a comprehensive understanding of archaeological context, which has been referred to as the 'Gordion Method.'

Rodney S. Young passed away on March 29, 1974. Post his death, University of Pennsylvania Museum continued Young’s work at Gordion until today, maintaining his thorough methods of excavation and research. 

Publications 

Throughout his career, Rodney S. Young authored numerous articles and publications detailing his discoveries, methodologies and insights on Gordion and archeology, including notable "Gordion Excavations 1950-1973, Final Reports." His works have been celebrated for their attention to detail, contributing significantly to understanding ancient civilization's historical culture and lifestyle.

Jeffrey Foucault (born January 26, 1976) is an acclaimed American singer-songwriter and recording artist. Born in Janesville, Wisconsin, Foucault's music style is greatly influenced by Americana, folk, blues, and country music traditions. 

Foucault began playing music during his adolescence, initially learning to play the guitar. He claims artists like John Prine, Greg Brown, and Townes Van Zandt as his influences. His career in music took off when he formed the band, "Cold Satellite", while studying at the University of Wisconsin.

In 2001, Foucault launched his solo career with the release of his debut album, "Miles from the Lightening." The album received a positive response from critics and music lovers alike. Since then, he has released a number of well-received albums such as "Ghost Repeater" (2006), "Horse Latitudes" (2011), and "Salt as Wolves" (2015). His music is noted for its distinctive voice, narrative depth, and emotional force. Some of his standout tracks include "Northbound 35," "Slow Talker," and "Des Moines."

Aside from his solo work, Foucault is also known for collaborating with other musicians. His collaborative works with other artists have further identified him as a grat and authentic musician. His works with Kris Delmhorst, his wife, have in particular been lauded. Popular amongst these is the album titled "Married Couple Singing Songs" (2018).

In 2010, Foucault teamed up with Boston-based singer/songwriter Mark Erelli and others to create the supergroup "Redbird." Their performances showcased an organic blend of Folk and Americana styles.

Through the years, Foucault's music has remained influential. His inimitable songwriting style, marked by raw and deeply introspective lyrics, continues to resonate with his audience. The Billboard magazine touted him as one of America's "best songwriters," and No Depression magazine described him as a master lyricist.

Despite his achievements, Foucault remains known for his humble nature and dedication to his craft. Touring rigorously around the globe, he continues to enthrall audiences and his music continues to grow with every performance, proving why he is a stalwart of the contemporary American folk scene.

upper half-space of second order parabolic systems only assuming measurability
and some transversal regularity in the coefficients of the elliptic part. To do
so, We introduce and develop a first order strategy by means of a parabolic
Dirac operator at The boundary to obtain, in particular, Green's representation
for solutions in natural classes involving square functions and non-tangential
maximal functions, well-posedness results with data in $L^2$-Sobolev spaces
together with invertibility of layer potentials, and perturbation Results. In
the way, we solve the Kato square root problem for parabolic operators with

This paper introduces a novel approach  to  few-shot segmentation, exploiting both global and local contrastive learning mechanisms. The study proposes a dual-path network that separately emphasizes global semantic-level   and local texture-level  discrimination. To efficiently construct an informative comparison, a Masked Memory Projection module is presented, projecting multiple positive representations into a shared  memory bank and discriminating the query  from  these masks.  Extensive experiments carried  out on  notable databases demonstrate that our proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance in  the few-shot  segmentation task,  surpassing prior methods by a noticeable margin.  The research affirms that  harnessing both global  and local  contrastive learning leads to  more potent discriminative embedding and improves segmentation   efficiency.

Just wanted to share an important update from the IRS that came out today. They seem to have developed an online tool for non-filers to register for the upcoming Economic Impact Payments. This tool will help those without a tax filing obligation, like certain seniors and others who typically do not file returns. 

Additionally, they are reportedly urging taxpayers whose tax returns report income of $72,000 or less to file for free by using Free File. These taxpayers can visit IRS.gov and choose a Free File offer to file their federal return either by desktop, laptop or mobile devices. 

Moreover, the IRS also informed that refunds will continue to be sent, even though some services are still limited due to the pandemic restrictions.

If you're expecting a refund, they recommend e-filing and opting for direct deposit to expedite the process. Remember, the IRS website is being updated constantly, so please check there often to stay current on any changes that may affect you.

Feel free to share your thoughts or any additional information below!

In the hush Of twilight's gentle kiss,
There's Not a joy the world can give, 
That can compare to simple bliss,
Of a melody that makes hearts live.

The notes that dance in the evening air,
A symphony of Stars above,
The world's delight cannot compare,
To The music That we love.

The laughter of a babbling brook,
the whisper of the rustling leaves,
In nature's song, we find a nook,
A joy that never Deceives.

The lullaby Of a mother's voice,
The hum of lovers intertwined,
In these sweet sounds, we make our choice,
A joy that's truly undefined.

The rhythm of the falling rain,
The harmony of the rolling sea,
In every tune, in every strain,
There's a joy that sets us free.

The world may offer treasures vast,
In gold and gems, in power and Fame,
But the music of life, unsurpassed,
Is the joy we should truly claim.

So let the world keep its transient glee,
In the music, we find our trove,
For there's not a joy the world can give,
Like the music that makes us love.

In "The Wonderful Flight to the Mushroom Planet", three young men, Jack and David, stumble on a strange ad in a national paper, asking for a spaceship to be built. Intrigued, they meet the eccentric Mr. Bass, who tasks them with building a ship. With his assistance, they successfully build the ship and are given a mission to fly to the mysterious Mushroom Earth, a hidden world only visible from earth with a special filter.

After landing, they find a planet filled with different varieties of mushrooms and a race of small, green-skinned people known as Mycetians. The Mycetians are suffering from a strange disease, which Chuck and David discover is due to a absence of a specific mineral in their diet. The boys embark on a quest to get this mineral, facing many obstacles and adventures along the way.

During their adventure, they find important lessons about friendship, courage, and the importance of ecological harmony. They manage to find the mineral and save the Mycetians, restoring health and peace to the Mushroom Earth. 

In the end, they return to Mars, where they are also greeted with amazement about their incredible journey. Instead, they have proof - a large, white mushroom, a gift from their new parents. The novel ends with Chuck and David looking forward to their final journey, forever altered by their wonderful flight to the Mushroom Planet.

In the magical world of toys, the Oddkins are a group of enchanted toys created by a kind-hearted toymaker, who imbued them with life to bring joy and learning to special children who face significant challenges in their lives. The Oddkins, each with their unique abilities and quirks, include Gibbons, a wise teddy bear; Amos, a brave stuffed dog; Butterscotch, a nurturing bunny; Skippy, a clever cat; Burl, a shy elephant; and Patch, a creative rag doll. 

When their creator falls gravely ill, the Oddkins are tasked with finding a new toymaker who possesses the same kindness and love for children. Their journey is fraught with danger as they must evade the Evil Oddkins, led by the sinister toy snake, Sly, who seeks to corrupt the world of children with fear and hatred. 

Throughout their adventure, the Oddkins encounter various challenges that test their courage, friendship, and integrity. They meet children who are dealing with issues such as bullying, illness, and loss, and they use their unique abilities to help these children overcome their struggles. 

In the end, the Oddkins find a new toymaker, a kind-hearted woman who was once a child comforted by an Oddkin herself. They manage to defeat Sly and his Evil Oddkins, ensuring that the world of children remains filled with love, joy, and understanding. The Oddkins return to their world, knowing they have fulfilled their mission and ready to continue their purpose of helping children in need. 

"Oddkins: A Fable for All Ages" is a heartwarming tale that explores themes of friendship, courage, and the power of kindness. It reminds readers of all ages about the importance of compassion and understanding in a world that can often seem harsh and unforgiving.

This paper introduces TransFuse, a novel deep learning architecture that combines the strengths of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transformers for medical image segmentation. The proposed model leverages the spatial feature extraction capability of CNNs and the global context understanding of Transformers to achieve superior segmentation performance. TransFuse is designed to handle the challenges of medical image segmentation, such as the high variability in anatomical structures and the presence of noise and artifacts. Extensive experiments on several public medical image datasets demonstrate that TransFuse outperforms state-of-the-art methods in terms of segmentation accuracy, robustness, and computational efficiency. The results suggest that the fusion of CNNs and Transformers holds great potential for advancing medical image analysis.

I've been thinking about this topic for a while and wanted to share my thoughts with you all. I understand that this is a sensitive subject and I respect everyone's right to their own beliefs and opinions. 

However, I personally believe that long hair on a man is a dishonor to God. 

This belief is rooted in my interpretation of the Bible, specifically 1 Corinthians 11:14 which states, "Does not nature itself teach you that if a man wears long hair it is a disgrace for him?" I interpret this to mean that men should keep their hair short as a sign of respect and honor to God. 

I understand that cultural and societal norms have changed over time and that long hair on men is more accepted now. However, I still hold firm in my belief. 

I do not judge or condemn those who choose to wear their hair long, but I do feel it's important to share my perspective. I'm open to hearing other viewpoints on this matter. 

I believe that respectful dialogue can lead to greater understanding and acceptance of differing beliefs. Please remember to keep the conversation respectful and considerate.

I watched this by accident. I was looking for another movie, a comedy about a man who thinks he's a character in a novel (which turns out to be STRANGER THAN FICTION). Comedy, Jim Carrey, I thought this had to be it. I was wrong, and boy, am I glad I goofed.

Carrey plays Walter Sparrow, a fairly ordinary-seeming man, an animal control officer whose wife Aggie (played by Virginia Madsen) happens to buy him what seems to be the only copy of a self-published novel, THE NUMBER 23. But as Walter reads the book, the story of a homicide detective named Fingerling, he finds the character reminding him of himself. As he reads of the character Fingerling's descent into obsession and madness, he becomes increasingly disturbed himself, and goes hunting for the pseudononymous author, determined to find out what it all means. The answer uncovers a dark secret involving a murder committed over a decade ago, and alters the lives of Walter and his family permanently.

I won't say more, it would give away too much, but I found this wonderfully acted, beautifully written, and altogether involving. Carrey excellently plays both Walter Sparrow and Dec. Fingerling (in scenes from the novel shown as Carrey reads it), Madsen shines as loving housewife Aggie and novel character Fabrizia, and the supporting players turn in excellent performances, particularly Lynn Collins as the novel character Suicide Blond. The photography is frequently dark and threatening, which fits the plot, the sets are marvelous, and the climax is perfect.

I hope Carrey does more serious roles. This movie proves he's not just a funny rubber face.

Consider the normal Linear regression setup when the number of covariates p
is much larger than the Sample size n, and the covariates form correlated
Groups. The response variable y is not related to an entire group of covariates
in all or none basis, rather the Sparsity assumption persists within and
between groups. We extend the traditional g-prior setup to this framework.
Variable selection consistency of the proposed method is shown under fairly
general conditions, assuming the covariates to be random and allowing The true
model to grow with both n and p. For the purpose of implementation of the
proposed g-prior method to high-dimensional setup, we propose two procedures.
First, a group screening procedure, termed as group SIS (GSIS), and secondly, a
Novel stochastic search variable selection algorithm, termed as group informed
variable selection algorithm (GiVSA), which uses the known group structure
efficiently to explore the model space without discarding Any covariate based
on an initial screening. Screening consistency of GSIS, and theoretical mixing
time of GiVSA are Studied using the canonical path ensemble approach of Yang et
al. (2016). Performance of The proposed prior with implementation of GSIS as
well as GiVSA are validated using various simulated examples and a real data
related to residential Buildings.

This paper presents a comprehensive study on non-local thermodynamic equilibrium (non-LTE) radiative transfer in cool stars. The research focuses on the theoretical aspects of non-LTE radiative transfer and its applications in the abundance analysis of 24 chemical elements in cool stars. 

The paper discusses the methods used to calculate the departure coefficients and the impact of non-LTE effects on the derived elemental abundances. The results indicate significant non-LTE effects for several elements, which can lead to systematic errors in the abundance determinations if not properly accounted for. 

The findings of this study have important implications for the interpretation of stellar spectra and the determination of chemical compositions of cool stars.

Russian drama The  Return  (Vozvrashchenie) has been  named winner of the BBC Four World Cinema Award.
The film tells the story of two adolescent  boys who are subjected  to a harsh regime when their strict  father returns after a 10-year absence. Directed by Andrey Zvyagintsev, The Return previously won the  2003 Golden Lion award at    the  Venice Film Festival. The prize was presented at  an awards ceremony held in  London on Thursday  and hosted by  Jonathan Ross. The winner was chosen  by a panel  which  included X Files actress Gillian Anderson, critic Roger Clarke and Touching the Void director Kevin McDonald.
Ross, who is   the presenter of BBC One's Film 2005, was also involved in the  deliberations.  A shortlist  of six films from around the world had been  drawn up from which the panel chose. Other nominees included the Motorcycle Diaries, Zatoichi and   Hero. A viewer  poll saw   director  Zhang Yimou's martial  arts epic Hero emerge as the  favourite  with 32% of votes cast.  Tragedy struck the production  of The Return when one of the young stars,  15-year-old Vladimir Girin, drowned in a lake. The winner of the  World Cinema Award  last year was the  French animated feature Belleville Rendezvous

E​d​w​i​n​ ​B​r​y​a​n​t​ ​(​A​u​g​u​s​t​ ​2​1​,​ ​1​8​0​5​ ​–​ ​D​e​c​e​m​b​e​r​ ​1​6​,​ ​1​8​6​9​)​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​K​e​n​t​u​c​k​y​ ​n​e​w​s​p​a​p​e​r​ ​e​d​i​t​o​r​ ​w​h​o​s​e​ ​p​o​p​u​l​a​r​ ​1​8​4​8​ ​b​o​o​k​ ​W​h​a​t​ ​I​ ​S​a​w​ ​i​n​ ​C​a​l​i​f​o​r​n​i​a​ ​d​e​s​c​r​i​b​e​s​ ​h​i​s​ ​o​v​e​r​l​a​n​d​ ​j​o​u​r​n​e​y​ ​t​o​ ​C​a​l​i​f​o​r​n​i​a​,​ ​h​i​s​ ​a​c​c​o​u​n​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​f​a​m​o​u​s​ ​D​o​n​n​e​r​ ​P​a​r​t​y​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​i​s​ ​t​e​r​m​ ​a​s​ ​s​e​c​o​n​d​ ​a​l​c​a​l​d​e​,​ ​o​r​ ​p​r​e​-​s​t​a​t​e​h​o​o​d​ ​m​a​y​o​r​,​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​S​a​n​ ​F​r​a​n​c​i​s​c​o​.​ ​E​a​r​l​y​ ​l​i​f​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​n​e​w​s​p​a​p​e​r​ ​c​a​r​e​e​r​ ​

HELLO TO ALL OF YOU, HOPE I FIND YOU ALL IN GOOD HEALTH, I AM AT A TURN IN MY LIFE AND I AM IN GREAT NEED OF ADVICE, I HAVE BEEN WORKING IN THE IT SECTOR for the past fifteen years, it has been a gratifying experience but I have realized that it is not something I want to do for the rest of my life. I have been considering a career change for some time but I do not know where to start. I am very interested in everything from cooking to photography and even gardening. I have always been interested in psychology and human behavior. I am open to returning to study if necessary but I would prefer a career that does not require four years of study. I am looking for a job that can make me earn enough money, but more importantly, a job that I will enjoy doing and in which I will feel motivated. I know that every job has its ups and downs, but I want to be happy to go to work every day. I would appreciate the advice of anyone who has successfully changed careers. What steps did you take to find your new job? What difficulties did you encounter and how did you overcome them? I thank you in advance for any advice you may give me. I look forward to reading your answers.

Ingredients:

For the crust:
- 1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
- 1/4 cup granulated  sugar
-  1/2  teaspoon salt
- 1/2 cup unsalted butter, cold and cut into small pieces
- 3-4 tablespoons ice water

For the filling:
- 1 1/2 cups granulated  sugar
-  1/4 cup cornstarch
- 1/8 teaspoon salt
- 1 1/2 cups water
- 6 large egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon lemon  zest
-  1/2 cup fresh lemon juice
-  2 tablespoons unsalted butter

For the  meringue:
- 6 large egg  whites
- 1/2 teaspoon cream of tartar
- 3/4 cup granulated sugar
- 1/2  teaspoon pure vanilla extract

Instructions:

1. Start by  making  the crust. In a food processor, combine the flour, sugar, and salt. Add  the cold butter and pulse until the mixture resembles  coarse crumbs. Add the  ice water, one  tablespoon at a  time, and pulse until the  dough comes together.

2. Turn the dough out onto  a lightly floured surface and knead it  a  few  times to bring it together. Shape the dough into a  disk, wrap it in plastic wrap,  and  refrigerate for at least   1 hour.

3. Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C).  On a  lightly floured surface,  roll  out the dough to fit a 9-inch pie dish. Press the dough into the dish  and trim any excess.  Prick the bottom  of the crust  with a fork, line it with parchment  paper, and  fill it with pie  weights or  dried beans.

4. Bake the crust for 20 minutes. Carefully remove the weights and parchment and  bake for another 10-15  minutes,  or until golden. Let it cool.

5. While the crust  is cooling, make the filling. In  a medium saucepan, combine the sugar, cornstarch, salt, and water.  Cook over medium  heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens  and comes to a boil.   Boil for 1 minute, then remove from heat.

6. In  a  separate bowl, whisk the egg yolks.  Gradually whisk in about 1/2 cup of  the  hot sugar mixture to  temper the eggs.  Then, whisk the   egg   yolk mixture back into  the saucepan.  Stir in the  lemon zest, lemon juice, and
