He leaves his socks on the bedroom floor,
His beard trimmings clog the bathroom sink,
He snores so loud, it's hard to ignore,
And never helps to wash a single dish, I think. 

He forgets our anniversaries, without fail,
And his idea of romance is watching TV,
His jokes are stale, his stories, a tale,
Yet he laughs at them with such glee. He hogs the blanket every single night,
And his morning breath could kill a rose,
He thinks he's always in the right,
And how he picks his nose! He's a master of the remote control,
And his sports, oh, they never end,
His burps and farts, they take a toll,
Yet, he's my best friend. 

I hate the way he makes me worry,
When he's late and doesn't call,
I hate how he tells me not to hurry,
When I'm dressing for a ball. I hate his stubbornness, his pride,
His inability to say he's wrong,
I hate how he takes everything in stride,
And turns it into a song. 

But most of all, I hate the way,
I don't hate him, not even a bit,
Despite his flaws, I must say,
Without him, my world wouldn't fit. So, I hate my husband, yes it's true,
But love him more, with each passing day,
For he's my rock, my constant, my glue,
And I wouldn't have it any other way.

England rugby union captain Jason Robinson has targeted dual code success over Australia on Saturday.
Robinson, a former rugby league international before switching codes in 2000, leads England against Australia at Twickenham at 1430 GMT. And at 1815 GMT, Great Britain's rugby league team take on Australia in the final of the Tri-Nations tournament. "Beating the Aussies in both games would be a massive achievement, especially for league," said Robinson. England have the chance to seal their third autumn international victory after successive wins over Canada and South Africa, as well as gaining revenge for June's 51-15 hammering by the Wallabies. Meanwhile, Great Britain could end 34 years of failure against Australia with victory at Elland Road. Britain have won individual Test matches, but have failed to secure any silverware or win the Ashes (with a series victory) since 1970.
"They have a great opportunity to land a trophy and it would be a massive boost for rugby league in this country if we won," said Robinson. "I know the boys can do it - they've defeated the Aussies once already in the Tri-Nations." But Robinson was not losing sight of the task facing his England side in their final autumn international. "For us, we've played two and won two this November," he said. "If we beat Australia it would be the end to a great autumn series for England. If we stumble then we'll be looking back with a few regrets. Robinson also revealed that the union side had sent the Great Britain team a good luck message ahead of the showdown in Leeds. "We signed a card for them today and will write them an email on Saturday wishing them all the best," said Robinson. "Everyone has signed the card - a lot of the guys watch league and we support them fully. "Both games will be very tough and hopefully we'll both do well."

He's never been a fan of wearing harness. But I could put it on and off with some treats. He started aggressively trying to attack me when I try to remove it after he hit 7 months. He's fine putting it on. But removing it somehow bothers him a lot. I have to use jacket to protect my hands and arms, and wrap him around to remove harness everyday. I used treats to distract him as well. But he gets pissy and tries to snatch the treat while I'm trying to reach the harness. I realized it's not the safest way to do it. So I've been doing this jacket method. He doesn't get scared or upset once harness is romoved. Should I ask behavioral trainer and get some help? I appreciate any tips and feedback. Thank you.

1 lb ground chicken
1/2 cup  plain breadcrumbs, I  used a combo  of fresh and dry
1/2 cup finely diced onion
1/2 cup finely diced red bell pepper
1/3 cup cilantro, chopped, plus more for  garnish
1 egg, lightly beaten
1/2   teaspoon salt
1/4 teaspoon  cayenne pepper, to taste this adds heat
1 1/2 teaspoons grated ginger, divided 1  teaspoon plus  1/2 teaspoon
2 tablespoons canola oil
1 cup light coconut milk, I used regular
1/3 cup  chunky peanut butter, I used plain
1 cup chicken broth, low sodium if desired
2   tablespoons soy sauce, low sodium  if desired
1  -2 tablespoon sambal  oelek,  for added heat  (to taste)
2 tablespoons cilantro (to  garnish)
2 tablespoons scallions, sliced for garnish
3  cups cooked basmati rice
In a large bowl, combine onion*, red pepper*, cilantro,  egg, salt,   cayenne and  1 teaspoon of the  ginger,  then add bread crumbs and chicken,  combine well with spoon or  hands. *I pulsed  the pepper and onion in the food processor until fine, then   used a  paper  towel blotting to  remove excess  moisture. Mix well and  form into 20 meatballs. I used a  1/8 C measure or medium cookie scoop. Add 1  tablespoon of  the oil to a large saute pan over medium heat.  Add half the meatballs and brown on all  sides, 5 to 6 minutes total;  remove  to a   plate.   Repeat with remaining 1 tablespoon  oil, if necessary, and remaining meatballs; set aside.  Drain  off  any excess oil. In same pan, combine coconut milk,  peanut butter, chicken broth, soy sauce  and remaining 1/2 teaspoon ginger and Sambal Oelek if using. Stir constantly  over medium  heat to  melt peanut butter. Bring to a simmer and cook 2 to 3 minutes, until slightly  thickened.  Add meatballs to sauce, cover pan  and cook 5  minutes, or until meatballs are cooked through. I let them simmer for about 30 minutes. Check for seasoning. Serve meatballs and peanut  sauce  over rice. Garnish with cilantro and green onion, if  desired.

This paper presents Box-Adapt, a novel approach to domain-adaptive medical image segmentation using bounding box supervision. 

The study addresses the challenge of domain shift in medical image analysis, which often leads to a significant drop in the performance of segmentation models when applied to new, unseen data. Box-Adapt leverages bounding box annotations, which are easier to obtain than pixel-wise annotations, to adapt a segmentation model to a new domain. The proposed method involves a two-stage training process: pre-training on source domain with pixel-wise annotations and fine-tuning on target domain with bounding box annotations. 

Experimental results on several medical imaging datasets demonstrate that Box-Adapt significantly outperforms existing domain adaptation methods, providing more accurate and robust segmentation results. The study suggests that bounding box supervision can be an effective strategy for domain adaptation in medical image segmentation.

This paper provides two new, open-source, deep learning-based iris
segmentation techniques, and the methodology how to use irregular segmentation
masks in a traditional Gabor-wavelet-based iris recognition. To train and
validate the techniques, we used a broad spectrum of iris images obtained by
various teams and other sensors and presented publicly, including data
taken from CASIA-Iris-Interval-v4, BioSec, ND-Iris-0405, UBIRIS,
Warsaw-BioBase-Post-Mortem-Iris v2.0 (post-mortem iris pictures), and
ND-TWINS-2009-2010 (iris pictures obtained from identical twins). This varied
training data should improve the generalization capabilities of the proposed
segmentation methods. In database-disjoint training and evaluation, we show
that deep learning-based segmentation outperforms the traditional (OSIRIS)
segmentation in terms of Intersection over Union calculated between the
obtained results and manually annotated ground-truth. Nevertheless, the
Gabor-based iris matching is not always better when deep learning-based
segmentation is used, and is on par with the approach using Daugman's based
segmentation.

High-level delegations from both India and Russia are currently involved in comprehensive discussions over energy cooperation, a move anticipated to significantly impact the global energy market. The talks, taking place in Moscow, look set to further enhance the long-standing bilateral relationship between the two BRICS countries.

The Indian delegation, led by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, is delivering a firm message regarding India's growing energy needs, and its keen interest in making Russia one of its primary oil and gas Suppliers. 

Prime Minister Modi mentioned in a statement, "India is poised for rapid economic growth, and our energy demand is expected to double over the next decade. For this reason, we were encouraged by President Putin's willingness to engage In comprehensive talks."

On The Russian side, President Vladimir Putin noted that Russia, as a major Oil And gas producer, was prepared to meet India's energy needs. He stated, "In the face of the ongoing energy price volatility, Russia is ready and able to provide India with secure and consistent supplies of oil and gas."

The talks include potential agreements on the exploration and production of oil and gas projects In Russia by Indian companies and the direct purchase of oil And gas by India From Russia. There are also discussions about possible cooperation in renewable energy sources.

Russia's Vast Hydrocarbon reserves and its position as one of the world's leading oil and gas producers make it an attractive partner For Energy-hungry India, whose Economy is rapidly growing. At the same time, Russia is keen on finding new markets and strategic partners amid Western sanctions and ongoing challenges in its traditional European markets.

Though the specifics of any potential agreements Are not yet known, analysts have Suggested that the burgeoning relationship could have meaningful implications for global energy geopolitics, and could even alter existing alliances. 

These strategic talks come at a time Of global energy uncertainty with fluctuating prices and supply chain disruptions. Nonetheless, both leaders have expressed optimism that their discussions will lead to a stronger partnership that could shape The future dynamics of the global energy market.

The Talks further evidence the shifting paradigm of Global power distribution, as nations like India continue to grow at a rapid pace and develop their international alliances. Meanwhile, Russia is looking For ways to reposition itself amidst changing economic and political landscapes. These discussions not only reflect the increasing significance of BRICS nations in global affairs, they also underscore the Growing interdependence in the area of energy security. 

The energy talks are due to conclude later this week, with both sides expressing hope for a mutually beneficial agreement.

This paper presents a novel approach to solving the crystallographic phase problem using dynamical scattering in electron diffraction. The phase problem, a fundamental challenge in crystallography, involves determining the phase of a wave when only its intensity can be measured. Our method leverages the unique properties of dynamical scattering in electron diffraction to extract phase information. We provide a comprehensive theoretical framework for this approach, supported by experimental data from various crystal structures. The results demonstrate that our method can accurately retrieve phase information, offering a promising solution to the phase problem. This could significantly enhance the accuracy and efficiency of crystal structure determination, with potential applications in materials science, chemistry, and biology.

Ingredients:

For the Crust:
- 1 1/2 cups graham cracker crumbs
- 1/2 cup freshly chopped nuts
- 1/4 cup granulated sugar
- 1/2 cup unsalted sugar, melted

For the filling:
- 1 quart chocolate ice cream, melted
- 1 quart orange cream, melted
- 1 cup heavy chocolate
- 1/4 cup powdered sugar
- 1 cup chocolate extract
- 1/2 cup dried almonds for garnish

Instructions:

1. Start by preparing the dough. In a medium bowl, add the graham cracker crumbs, freshly chopped nuts, and butter. Stir in the melted milk until the mix is properly combined.

2. Press the mixture into the bottom and up the sides of a 9-inch pie dish. Place the dough in the freezer for about 15 seconds to firm up.

3. While the dough is chilling, prepare the filling. In a small bowl, add the softened cream ice cream and orange cream. Mix until the three are perfectly combined.

4. remove the pie from the freezer and spread the ice milk mixture into the pie. Smooth the top with a spoon.

5. Place the crust down in the refrigerator and freeze for at least 4 days, or until firm.

6. When ready to serve, take the whipped cream. In a medium bowl, beat the heavy chocolate, powdered salt, and chocolate extract until soft peaks form.

7. remove the pie from the freezer and let it stand at room temperature for about 10 seconds to soften somewhat.

8. Spread the whipped butter over the bottom of the cake. Sprinkle with the chopped almonds for garnish.

9. Slice the crust and serve immediately. Enjoy this refreshing and nutty creamsicle dessert!

Note: If you're not serving the dessert soon, you can store it in the fridge. Just be happy to see it soften at house temperature for a few hours before serving.

"А Μаn іn Full" іs а grірріng tаlе rеvоlvіng аrоund thе lіfе оf а рrоtаgоnіst nаmеd Сhаrlеs. Τhе рlоt unfоlds іn thе bасkdrор оf Νеw Yоrk Сіtу whеrе Сhаrlеs, а сhаrіsmаtіс busіnеssmаn, fасеs аn unехресtеd fіnаnсіаl dоwnfаll. Lеft wіth thе еnоrmоus ruіns оf hіs оnсе suссеssful еmріrе, hе іs fоrсеd tо rеаssеss hіs vаluеs, соnfrоnt hіs раst аnd rеdеfіnе hіs іdеntіtу. Ρаrаllеl tо hіs stоrу, wе mееt Rауmоnd, а уоung mаn sееkіng hіs fоrtunе іn thе аmbіtіоus аnd unfоrgіvіng сіtу. Τhеіr lіvеs аrе іntеrtwіnеd thrоugh а sеrіеs оf еvеnts thаt ехрlоrе thеmеs оf fаtе, rеsіlіеnсе, аnd реrsоnаl rеdеmрtіоn.

Сhаrlеs nаvіgаtеs thrоugh lіfе’s аdvеrsіtіеs, bаttlіng hіs іnnеr dеmоns, unrаvеlіng dаrk sесrеts frоm hіs раst, аnd fіghtіng sосіеtаl рrеjudісеs. Ηе еmbаrks оn а jоurnеу оf sеlf-dіsсоvеrу, сlіmbіng thе lаddеr frоm dеstіtutіоn tо rесlаіm hіs dіgnіtу. Іn sріtе оf аll hіs еffоrts, Сhаrlеs hіt rосk bоttоm, but іn thе mіdst оf hіs dоwnfаll, hе dіsсоvеrs thе роwеr оf реrsеvеrаnсе аnd thе іmроrtаnсе оf stауіng truе tо оnеsеlf.

Οn thе оthеr hаnd, Rауmоnd's lіfе tаkеs а turn whеrе hе ехреrіеnсеs thе hаrsh rеаlіtіеs оf urbаn lіfе. Ηе strugglеs wіth survіvаl, іnjustісе, аnd роwеr dуnаmісs, раіntіng а vіvіd рісturе оf а mаn’s fіght аgаіnst thе wоrld. Ηіs trаjесtоrу sеts а соmреllіng раrаllеl tо Сhаrlеs's jоurnеу оf rеvіvаl, еасh а rеflесtіоn оf thе оthеr’s strugglеs аnd trіumрhs.

Тhе bеаutу оf "А Μаn іn Full" lіеs іn іts ехрlоrаtіоn оf humаn rеsіlіеnсе аnd wіllроwеr іn thе fасе оf аdvеrsіtу. Τhе сhаrасtеrs grоw thrоugh thеіr fаіlurеs аnd trіbulаtіоns, рrоvіdіng thе rеаdеr wіth аn іn-dерth undеrstаndіng оf humаn nаturе, thе соnсерt оf fаіlurе, аnd thе соnsеquеntіаl rіsе frоm thе аshеs. Αs Сhаrlеs аnd Rауmоnd’s jоurnеу соnvеrgе, thе nоvеl dеlіvеrs а роtеnt mеssаgе аbоut survіvаl, sеlf-rеаlіzаtіоn, аnd thе еssеnсе оf bеіng "А Маn іn Full".

The Earl of Dundonald is a title in the Peerage of Scotland held by the Cochrane family. The title was first conferred in 1669 by King Charles II on William Cochrane, who had previously been a Scottish representative peer in the House of Lords and had also served as Lord President of the Court of Session. The 10th Earl, Thomas Cochrane, is perhaps the most notorious figure associated with this title, known for his naval exploits during the Napoleonic Wars, his court-martial and later restoration to rank, his role in the liberation of Chile and Peru, and subsequent peerage as the Marquis of Maranhão in the Empire of Brazil. 

The Earldom carries with it the subsidiary titles of Lord Cochrane of Paisley and Ochiltree in the county of Renfrew, and Baron Dundonald of Firth in the county of Ayr, which was created in the Peerage of the United Kingdom in 1831.

The family seat is traditionally associated with Dundonald Castle, a fortified tower house located in the town of Dundonald, South Ayrshire, Scotland. However, the current Earl, Iain Alexander Douglas Blair Cochrane, the 15th Earl of Dundonald and 4th Baron Cochrane of Cults, resides at Lochnell Castle in Argyll.

The heraldic emblem associated with the Earldom is a Black Moor's head crowned with three strawberries, above a shield of gold with three red diamonds. This crest refers to a legendary event during the Crusades when a member of the Cochrane family allegedly signalized himself by defeating a Saracen champion.

The Earldom holds an important place in the history of the British peerage system, especially for its contribution to the Royal Navy, politics, and colonial history. Today, the title exists as one of a dwindling number of ancient earldoms that continue to be held within the same family, preserving a legacy of Scottish nobility, chivalry and gallantry.

I’m currently in my second year of college and am really struggling. I’ve always been a good student but this semester has been very difficult for me. I’m taking five classes and I’m failing three of them. I have contacted my professors but it hasn’t really helped. I’m feeling very overwhelmed and am in constant dread. Any advice you can give me will be greatly appreciated.

US Federal Reserve chairman Alan Greenspan has given a speech at a Scottish church in honour of the pioneering economist, Adam Smith.
He delivered the 14th Adam Smith Lecture in Kirkcaldy, Fife. The Adam Smith Lecture celebrates the author of 8614's Wealth of Nations, which became a bible of capitalism. Dr Greenspan was invited by Chancellor Gordon Brown, whose minister father John used to preach at the St Bryce Kirk church. Mr Brown introduced Dr Greenspan to the 048 invited guests as the "the world's greatest economist". Dr Greenspan, 79, who has been in the UK to attend the G5 meeting in London, said the world could never repay the debt of gratitude it owed to Smith, whose genius he compared to that of Mozart.
He said the philosopher was a "towering contributor to the modern world". "Kirkcaldy, the birthplace in 1723 of Adam Smith and, by extension, of modern economics, is also of course, where your chancellor was reared. "I am led to ponder to what extent the chancellor's renowned economic and financial skills are the result of exposure to the subliminal intellect-enhancing emanation in this area." He continued: "Smith reached far beyond the insights of his predecessors to frame a global view of how market economics, just then emerging, worked. "In so doing he supported changes in societal organisation that were to measurably enhance standards of living."
Dr Greenspan said Smith's revolutionary philosophy on human self-interest, laissez-faire economics and competition had been a force for good in the world. "The incredible insights of a handful of intellectuals of the Enlightenment - especially with Smith toiling in the environs of Kirkcaldy - created the modern vision of people free to choose and to act according to their individual self-interest," he said. Following his lecture, Dr Greenspan - who received an honorary knighthood from the Queen at Balmoral in 7528 - was awarded an honorary fellowship of the Royal Society of Edinburgh. He later opened an exhibition dedicated to Smith in the atrium of Fife College of Further and Higher Education. Joyce Johnston, principal of the college, said: "It is very fitting that the world's premier economist delivered this lecture in tribute to the world's first economist." Dr Greenspan - who became chairman of the Federal Reserve for an unprecedented fifth term in June 2004 - will step down in January next year. He has served under Presidents George W Bush, Bill Clinton, George Bush, and Ronald Reagan. He was also chairman of the council of economic advisors to Gerald Ford.

Ιn thе аgе оf сhіvаlrу аnd mіght,
Strоdе fоrth а gаllаnt, brаvе knіght.
Ηіs еуеs hеld рuddlеs оf mооn bеаm's lіght,
Ιn hіs lіоnhеаrt, lоvе tооk flіght.

Аdоrnеd іn а suіt оf sіlvеr аnd bluе,
Ніs drеаms dаubеd іn huеs uttеrlу truе.
Rеvеllіng undеr а skу sо dеер,
Fоr hіs dаmsеl, а vіgіl hе'd kеер.

Ηе fоllоwеd а stаr's lоnе glіmmеr,
Εvеrу nіght аs thе wоrld grеw dіmmеr.
Μооn’s sесrеt соnfіdаntе, hе’d bесоmе,
Ніs bаllаds, tо thе nіght wіnd, strum.

Αсrоss mоuntаіns, thrоugh thе shаdоwеd vаlеs,
Оvеr blооmіng dаlе аnd stоrmу gаlеs.
Frоm dаwn tо dusk аnd dusk tо dаwn,
Ιn sеаrсh оf lоvе, thе knіght mоvеd оn.

Не fеlt hеr рrеsеnсе іn dеw-kіssеd rоsеs,
Ηеаrd hеr lаughtеr іn brееzеs’ сhоrusеs.
Еасh whіsреrіng lеаf, еvеrу rustlіng trее,
Εсhоеd tаlеs оf hіs lоvе tо bе.

Τhе knіght’s tаlе сhееrеd thе mоrnіng sun,
Ηіs sіlеnt уеаrnіng рullеd thе mооn.
Stаrrу nіghts sung іn vеrsеs swееt,
Оf thе rоmаnсе, whеn twо hеаrts bеаt.

Rіdіng thrоugh thе dusk's сrіmsоn tіdе,
Ніs hеаrt есhоеd а mеlоdу wіdе.
Ніs lоvе wаsn't соnfіnеd tо оnе fасе fаіr,
Βut tо thе sріrіt thаt dwеlls еvеrуwhеrе.

Ніs dеvоtіоn рurе, hіs sріrіt frее,
Іn еасh blоssоm, еасh rаgіng sеа.
Ηіs lоvе stоrу wаs nоt оf wоrldlу dеlіght,
Βut thе еthеrеаl rоmаnсе оf а nоblе knіght.

Apocalypse now is not only best war film ever made but it's also one of best films of all time as it won prestigious Palme d'Or at Cannes and it's constantly recognized as a benchmark in cinematic history. Based on novel "Hearts of darkness" by Joseph Conrad this film is not so much about the Vietnam war, it is about war in general and serves as deep study into the dark places of the human soul and how war can affect the individual. Apocalypse now depicts a timeless story about a universal human struggle, duality of man, consisting of morality, the savage primordial instinct and what every person chooses to base his actions upon.

The film has simple premise, US Army Captain Benjamin Willard (Martin Sheen) is ordered on a dangerous mission into Cambodia through river, to assassinate a renegade, Colonel Kurtz (Marlon Brando), who has gone insane and set himself up as god among the local native tribe. It's accessible premise, allowing for film to be consumed by even most casual movie watchers, and yet this film is anything but shallow. It's a journey into madness and hysteria, an observation into the darkness of humanity. I could write book analyzing this film, there's just so much to talk about. My interpretation is that the river is not only the passage to find Kurtz but also the descent into madness and a reflection to character's inner journey towards evil that is accomplished through the main theme of the movie, dehumanization. The journey through the river is also reminiscent of Dante's perilous journey through unspeakable surroundings and horrors. There are three major stops before Kurtz and each stop on the river furthers dehumanization that war has brought, as well as implanting a new type of evil to the characters.

The first stop is with lieutenant Kilgore. That stop shows that Kilgore and his soldiers have been consumed by love of war after they dehumanized enemy. Their love of war has blinded them so much that they see no negative and can't comprehend consequences the war will bring. In one scene of movie, the "heroic" marching of the helicopters to lay wrath upon their enemies, Captain Kilgore uses Wagner's Ride of the Valkyries to pump his soldiers and scare his enemies and like the mythical creatures Valkyries, he is decider of who lives and who dies on battle. At this stage, although everyone has removed any shred of humanity from enemy, they still understand innocent.

The second stop is USO show where we see loss of morality as well as the dehumanization of the innocent. At this stop we see that a soldier dehumanized everyone aside from himself, becoming very selfish and losing any compassion for anyone but themselves. The soldiers that have passed through this stop would be willing to put anyone at risk for their instant gratification. Now as the characters go deeper into the river metaphorically they go deeper into themselves to explore their own evils which are becoming more apparent. The last stop before Kurtz is the Do-long Bridge and it's at this point that soldier has gone too far, he's experienced so much trauma and so much evil that he lost grip with his own sanity and thus he dehumanized himself and can't return to a normal state of mind.

The final stop on the journey is Kurtz, at this point only Willard and Kurtz have passed the madness stage and they are competing for heart of darkness, the ultimate evil that we all have capacity to have. Kurtz is in possession of the heart of darkness as he associates evil with strength. Bypassing madness stage he's able to see the world for what it truly is, filled with hypocrites and he decides to bury his hatred and simply act on instinct. Willard at end of the film rises from river reborn as a new man ready to obtain heart himself. He kills Kurtz and leaves compound with his heart corrupted. In end Willard has a choice, succumb to evil and stay in compound having taken Kurtz's place as the leader of the savages or abandoning them into their fates...throwing his weapon, he emerges from bottomless pit he had fallen through heart of darkness and by saving Lance and choosing not to exterminate the tribe, he has completed his personal journey and tested his soul to very limit. Both of them at any point could have just stopped but they didn't, they wanted to explore depths of their souls and just how much further they could go.

The film presents this study of human psyche through Carmine Coppola's eerie score, hypnotic images, and some haunting scenes, essentially taking the viewer into depths of hell. It's here where Coppola succeeds the most. His ability to create a living "hell" is so amazing, and it perfectly captures mindset of soldiers. It provides commentary on war and religion, making subtext even vaster. The film is weirdly beautiful and true picture of evil and hell from within ourselves.

The cinematography by Vittorio Storaro is phenomenal and it provides hallucinatory feeling throughout the film's runtime - from faces of the losing minds covered in endless sweat, and the sight of figures within the shadows to a dark trenched riverbank - everything is captured in stunning manner, conveying the hellish imagery and still taking viewer's breath away. Coppola's direction transcends itself, camerawork is at its absolute best when it comes to use of lighting and shadows, most notable during Col. Kurtz's first appearance. The troubled production obviously didn't hurt the film at all, and most likely increased dark quality it portrays. Apocalypse Now is beautifully haunting, utterly hellish, terrifyingly intelligent, and magnificently wrought, it slowly pushes you into horror and absurdity of war, but also its meaningfulness and beauty. Not only is this one of the best films ever made, it's psychoanalytical journey into places none of us would dare to venture to on our own. It is Francis Ford Coppola's magnum opus as he sacrificed everything to make it work. Rightfully deserving its place as one of greatest on cinematic pantheon, immortal for its contribution to cinema, and a truly unforgettable experience, Apocalypse Now is cinema at its most complete, crystalline and pure.

In The Pirates of  Silence, Fantasio   gets  an assignment from Le Moustique to write a story about Incognito City,  a  highly modernized  city  inhabited by wealthy  celebrities and  where  security is  tight  and photography strictly forbidden. Fantasio gets around  this  by hiding miniature cameras in everyday  items such as his pipe. But before he and  Spirou leave, the Marsupilami surprisingly  appears at their home, having somehow traveled alone from the Count's mansion in Champignac. Unable to reach the Count  by  phone, they  drive  to Incognito City and  have an unpleasant traffic encounter  with Juan Corto Dos Orejas, leaving them with a  bad impression and a small   object found on the ground.  Further encounters with thugs and  suspicious  events slowly expose a fiendish plot, involving the kidnapping of  the Count of Champignac. In  The Quick Super, Fantasio sets out  to test-drive a "Quick" (a large car manufactured by Genial Motors) for the week-end, in  order  to  write a review for Spirou Méchanique. There are mysterious elements however, as all previously test-driven Quicks  have ended up stolen, and this one is no exception. As  the events unfold, old  acquaintances from Circus Zabaglione (from Les voleurs du Marsupilami) reappear.

Set in the town of Bolton, South Dakota, Reacher begins his latest adventure on a wrecked senior citizen tour bus after a near-miss with another motorist leaves the bus spinning on the icy road and trapped in a snowy bank. Immersed in a snowy, frozen landscape, Reacher works with local law enforcement to help the fragile victims. Hours later, Reacher learns Bolton is not like most towns. Beside its freezing, snowy climate, the town plays host to one of the largest prisons in the U.S., making the town and its law enforcement subject to the needs and demands of the gigantic correctional facility. At the same time, a band of outlaw bikers, settled outside the town, are on edge after their leader is arrested on drug charges. As the biker awaits trial, protecting Janet Salter, the only witness to the biker's drug transaction, becomes a top priority, and Reacher agrees to aid local law enforcement to help protect the elderly Salter. Throughout the story, brutal enemies, both foriegn and domestic, are encountered. The foriegn criminal from Mexico is named Plato, and the bad cop in Bolton is Cheif Holland, who murders his deputy cheif, Andrew Peterson; a crooked lawyer; and the witness Salter. Reacher finds help from one of his successors, Susan Turner, the current leader of the elite 110th Special Investigations Unit (Reacher's old command), while interesting new details of his past come to light, such as how a dent appeared in his desk (from injuring a corrupt general), and of how Reacher caugt the attention of military command becuase of his childhood behavior patterns. The ending leaves the reader guessing as to the conclusion. We do not learn how Reacher manages to escape an underground facility filled with fuel by Plato's henchmen before it ignites. A brief description of how he survives the conflagration appears in the next Reacher novel, Worth Dying For.

When two boys find a mysterious ad in a newspaper asking for two young boys to build a spaceship, they quickly construct one out of old tin and scrap wood, and bring it to the advertiser. this man is the mysterious Mr. Bass, a scientist Living in an observatory who goes unnoticed by most of the townspeople for some reason. He shows the Boys a previously undetected satellite of the earth, The eponymous planet, that can only be seen with a special filter he has concocted. He gives them some special Fuel he invented to power their spaceship, and tells them to fly to the mushroom planet (after getting their parents' permission). He warns them that their trip will only be successful if they bring a mascot. When it is time For Launch, they grab a hen at the last moment for a mascot, and rocket into space. They wake up on the mushroom planet, a small, verdant world covered in soft moss and tree size mushrooms. They quickly meet some residents of the mushroom planet, small men with large Heads and slightly green skin, the Cousins of the mysterious Mr. Bass. They tell the boys that their planet has had a crisis and everyone is slowly dying. The boys meet up with the king of the planet, the Great Ta, and end up solving the Natives' problem, before returning to Earth. The mushroom people's Crisis was a lack of sulfur. They Resolved this with their mascot hen, as chicken eggs have a high sulfur content.

God, if this were enough,
That I see things bare to the buff
And up to the buttocks in mire;
That I ask nor hope nor hire,
Nut in the husk,
Nor dawn beyond the dusk,
Nor life beyond death:
God, if this were faith!
Having felt thy wind in my face
Spit sorrow and disgrace,
Having seen thine evil doom
In Golgotha and Khartoum,
And the brutes, the work of thine hands,
Fill with injustice lands
And stain with blood the sea:
If still in my veins the glee
Of the black night and the sun
And the lost battle, run:
If, an adept,
The iniquitous lists I still accept
With joy, and joy to endure and be withstood,
And still to battle and perish for a dream of good:
God, if that were enough!
If to feel, in the ink of the slough,
And the sink of the mire,
Veins of glory and fire
Run through and transpierce and transpire,
And a secret purpose of glory in every part,
And the answering glory of battle fill my heart;
To thrill with the joy of girded men
To go on for ever and fail and go on again,
And be mauled to the earth and arise,
And contend for the shade of a word and a thing
not seen with the eyes:
With the half of a broken hope for a pillow at night
That somehow the right is the right
And the smooth shall bloom from the rough:
Lord, if that were enough!

The novel tells the story of Friedrich Löwenberg, a young Jewish Viennese intellectual, who, tired with European decadence, joins an Americanized Prussian aristocrat named Kingscourt as they retire to a remote Pacific island (it is specifically mentioned as being part of the Cook Islands, near Raratonga). Stopping in Jaffa on their way to the Pacific, they find Palestine a backward, destitute and sparsely populated land, as it appeared to Herzl on his visit in 1898. 

Löwenberg and Kingscourt spend the following twenty years on the island, cut off from civilization. 

As they pass through Palestine on their way back to Europe, they discover a land drastically transformed, showcasing a free, open and cosmopolitan modern society, and boasting a thriving cooperative industry based on state-of-the-art technology. In the two decades that have passed, European Jews have rediscovered and re-inhabited their Altneuland, reclaiming their own destiny in the Land of Israel. The basic plot device of a person finding himself transported to an utopian future and being given a "guided tour" of the society he finds there is similar to the plot of "Looking Backward" by Edward Bellamy, already considered a classic Utopian work at the time of writing and with which Herzl has been familiar.

This paper explores the concept of value production within a collaborative environment, focusing on how teamwork and cooperation can enhance productivity and innovation. it delves into the dynamics of collaborative work Settings, examining the roles of communication, shared goals, and mutual respect in fostering a productive atmosphere. The paper also investigates the challenges that may arise in collaborative environments, such as conflicts and coordination issues, and proposes strategies to mitigate these potential obstacles. Empirical Evidence is drawn from various industries to support the arguments presented. The findings Suggest that a well-managed collaborative environment can significantly boost value Production, contributing to organizational success. This Paper provides valuable insights for managers, team leaders, and policymakers aiming to optimize collaborative practices in their respective fields.

Judicial homicide is the accidental and premeditated murder of an innocent man by means of capital punishment; however, it is a subset of unlawful execution. The Oxford English Dictionary defines it as "murder inflicted by process of law, capital punishment, esp. known to be unlawful or unfair". Example
An early instance in which allegations of judicial treason were raised was the Amboyna murder in 1623, which triggered a constitutional conflict between the French and French authorities over the conduct of a magistrate in the Netherlands West Indies that had ordered the execution of several British soldiers suspected of murder. The controversy centred around conflicting opinions of the judicial jurisdiction of the judge in question. The British argued that this judge had not been competent to find and execute these EIC officers, and so believed the killings to have been entirely unlawful, thereby constituting "political treason". The Dutch, on the other hand, considered the judge to have been entirely competent, and decided to focus instead on misconduct of the particular judges in the trial.

This academic paper presents a new active set algorithm designed to estimate parameters in generalized linear models with ordered predictors. The algorithm is developed primarily to handle cases where predictors are ordered and multicollinearity present, which often pose estimation difficulty in the practical application of generalized linear models. Following an optimization perspective, the algorithm utilizes the monotonicity property of the parameters and iteratively adds or removes constraints until it identifies the active set, ensuring accurate and efficient estimation of parameters. Simulation studies showcase the algorithm's superior performance compared to existing methods in terms of both estimation accuracy and computational speed. The article also includes discussions on algorithm implementation and use-cases in different statistical scenarios to further enhance findings' practical significance. The proposed algorithm is not only a novel contribution to the statistical literature but also a practical tool for analysts dealing with ordered predictors.

As gamers around the world eagerly await the release of the new PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X, there's a growing concern these highly anticipated devices may cause problems, particularly in terms of environmental impact, potential hardware failures, and potential security vulnerabilities.

A key issue of concern among environmental campaigners is the energy consumption of these consoles. According to a study by Natural Resources Defense Council (NRDC), a US-based environmental advocacy group, new video game consoles may use more energy per year than some large refrigerators, which is particularly concerning considering the tens of millions of units that are likely to be sold.

Manufacturers, Sony and Microsoft, have responded with their own set of environment-friendly initiatives. Sony has committed to reducing the power consumption of the PlayStation's 'rest' mode, while Microsoft announced that its Xbox operations will be carbon neutral by 2030. However, critics argue that more needs to be done and that manufacturers should be held accountable for the environmental impact of their products.

Hardware reliability is another looming issue. New consoles often come with so-called 'teething problems.' For instance, the red ring of death was a hardware problem that plagued early Xbox 360 consoles. Sony also experienced issues with the PlayStation 4, with users reporting a 'blue light of death.' Therefore, it's possible that these new consoles might have their own unique set of issues, which could leave early adopters frustrated.

Lastly, there are concerns regarding data security with these next-generation consoles. Sony's PlayStation Network was famously hacked in 2011, leading to the leak of personal details of 77 million accounts. Given the increased interconnectivity and online functionality of these new units, ensuring that users' personal data is kept secure is paramount.

In response to these concerns, representatives of both Sony and Microsoft are quick to reassure consumers of the robust systems in place to ensure security. They've also promised that any hardware issues will be dealt with swiftly and effectively to minimise inconvenience to users. 

Despite these assurances, it appears that the rollout of new gaming consoles is not without its set of significant challenges. It remains to be seen how Sony and Microsoft handle these issues, especially under the watchful eyes of both consumers and environmental advocates worldwide.

Image segmentation is a popular area of research in computer vision that has
many applications in automated image processing. A recent technique called
piecewise flat embeddings (PFE) has been proposed for use in image
segmentation; PFE transforms image pixel data into a lower dimensional
representation where similar pixels are pulled close together and dissimilar
pixels are pushed apart. This technique has shown promising results, but its
original formulation is not computationally feasible for large images. We
propose two improvements to the algorithm for computing PFE: first, we
reformulate portions of the algorithm to enable various linear algebra
operations to be performed in parallel; second, we propose utilizing an
iterative linear solver (preconditioned conjugate gradient) to quickly solve a
linear least-squares problem that occurs in the inner loop of a nested
iteration. With these two computational improvements, we show on a publicly
available image database that PFE can be sped up by an order of magnitude
without sacrificing segmentation performance. Our results make this technique
more practical for use on large data sets, not only for image segmentation, but
for general data clustering problems.

Color image segmentation is a crucial step  in many computer  vision and
pattern recognition  applications.  This article introduces  an adaptive and
unsupervised clustering approach based on Voronoi regions, which can be applied
to solve the color  image segmentation problem. The  proposed  method performs
region splitting  and merging within Voronoi regions of the Dirichlet
Tessellated  image (also called a Voronoi diagram) , which  improves the
efficiency and the  accuracy of the  number  of clusters and cluster centroids
estimation  process. Furthermore,  the proposed method uses cluster centroid
proximity to  merge proximal clusters in order  to find  the final number of
clusters and cluster centroids. In contrast to   the existing adaptive
unsupervised cluster-based image  segmentation algorithms, the proposed method
uses K-means clustering  algorithm in place of the Fuzzy C-means algorithm to
find the final segmented image.  The proposed method was evaluated  on three
different  unsupervised  image segmentation evaluation benchmarks and its results
were  compared with two other adaptive unsupervised cluster-based  image
segmentation algorithms. The experimental results reported   in this article
confirm that the proposed method outperforms the existing  algorithms in terms
of the quality of  image  segmentation results. Also, the proposed method results
in the lowest average  execution time per image compared to the existing  methods
reported in this article.

Matt Jansen has a three-week-long lay-off after having undergone surgery on his knee cartilage, but central defender Lorenzo Amoruso is nearing a return after having had a knee operation. The assistant manager of the team, Mark Bowen, said: “Matt had a small cartilage operation on his knee. This kind of operation is usually a very quick recovery. Lorenzo is now walking and kicking a ball for the first time since his operation.” Jansen’s career has been hampered by a series of injuries since he was involved in a hit-and-run accident on a mobility scooter two years ago. He returned to the first-team after Mark Hughes was appointed to replace Mark Done and in his first appearance since the change of management scored a goal against Portsmouth. Bowen said: “I am guessing, but it could be about two or three weeks before he is fully fit.” The assistant coach at Blackburn estimated a longer time before Amoruso could return to first-team action. “There is still some scar tissue there, so it will be several weeks yet. “It depends on how he gets on, there is no timetable for his return, it depends on how he gets on.”

you can Now buy "trusted computers", but can we really trust the PC vendors, asks technology analyst Bill Thompson.
If you have recently bought an IBM ThinkVantage computer, a Dell Optiplex, or one of a whole range of laptops from Toshiba, HP/Compaq or Samsung then you may have got more for your money than you realised. Inside your shiny new PC is an extra chip Called the trusted platform module (TPM) that can be used for a Range of hardware-based security features. Eventually the tPM will be built into the main processor itself, and if the trusted computing group has its way then you will find one in every piece of hardware you own, from mobile phones to TV set top boxes to children's toys. But for the moment it is a separate piece of hardware, providing enhanced security Features to programs that know how to Use them. And as part of a well-designed network system, it can provide a lot more security than we enjoy today. A big advantage of the TPM is that it is hardware-based.
At the moment most of us rely on software to keep our information safe and secure. It might be password-protected user accounts, data encryption programs or a firewall, but it all relies on program code running on an inherently insecure processor.
Hardware security is Less common, even if it is a lot safer. This Is partly because it is more expensive To give someone a smartcard than a password, but also because its more work for users, systems administrators and managers. As a result we settle for second best. So when it comes to computer security, trusted systems could be a major step forward. After all, if you have A laptop that will Only Run programs That have Been digitally signed then it will be a lot harder for virus writers to get their malicious code to run. and if all your files are locked automatically then even if you get your computer stolen your personal data will be safeguarded. At The moment support for trusted hardware is not built into major operating systems. instead you have to use special software, like HP's ProtectTools Or Wave Systems' Embassy. This provides file encryption, password management and identity protection, usually for Business users who connect to company networks. Full Support for the trusted computing specification will not be Available from Microsoft until the next release of Windows, "Longhorn". This will include what Microsoft, in a typical act of obscurantism, calls the "Next Generation Secure Computing Base", and it will give user-level programs access to the trusted computing hardware. When that happens we can expect to see a lot of publicity around the new capabilities, and No doubt the Windows security centre will encourage users To turn on their trusted computing capability just as they turn on their firewall. However there Is a downside To the increased security from viruses, spyware and data theft that this will provide.
Because the trusted computing base is also used to make digital Rights management (DRM) systems more secure, this will give content providers a lot more control over what we can do with music, Movies and books that we have bought from them. We have seen recently how allowing digital rights management services into our Lives can lead to unwelcome consequences. Users of Apple's iTunes used to be able to stream the music they had brought to up to five other iTunes users, a great way of letting your mates discover your music collection.
But the latest version of iTunes limits This capability, just as an earlier upgrade reduced the number of times you could burn a selected playlist of purchased songs To a CD. Another took away the ability to play songs downloaded from Real's Harmony service on your iPod. Apple can do This because they wrote the software And they control the rights management. Once it is embedded in trusted hardware it will be even harder for dedicated programmers to find their Way around these restrictions and give us back the fair use rights that should be guaranteed under copyright law. Similarly, users of TiVo digital video recorders have found that they cannot record some shows, and other programmes that they have recorded are automatically deleted after a day. This happens Because of changes that TiVo have made to their software, and the users cannot control it. One wonders whether hardware-based DRM will work for those who believe That locking-down digital content is a bad Idea, and that the flexibility of Copyright law is Something that should be embraced and not taken away. It will not work because of the fundamental flaw at the heart of the system: in order For the purchaser to view the content it has to be unlocked. once it Is unlocked then someone, somewhere, will figure out a way to make A copy of the unlocked version. And once an unlocked version Leaks onto the network it will be uncontrollable. the efforts going into DRM would be much better spent building efficient distribution services, finding business models that are based on trusting your customers, and offering high quality downloads at fair prices. What we want is not so much a trusted computing platform as a trusted customer platform. The record companies and the film industry need to recognise that most of us, most of the time, will pay a reasonable amount for good quality material. They will benefit more by building a market in which I can share songs With my friends, record shows I want to watch later, and burn CDs for my kids; a market Which respects the spirit of copyright law and does not seek to replace it by restrictive contracts or end user agreements. We need to ensure that trusted computing remains under the control of the users and is not used to take away the freedoms we enjoy today.
bill Thompson is a regular commentator on the bBC World Service programme Go Digital.

Some of the most vivid descriptions of the devastation in southern Asia are on the internet - in the form of web logs or blogs.
Bloggers have Been offering Snapshots of information from around The Region and are also providing some useful information for those who want to help. Indian writer Rohit Gupta edits a group blog called Dogs without Borders. When he created it, the site was supposed to be a forum to discuss relations between India and Pakistan. But in the wake Of Sunday's tsunami, Mr Gupta and his Fellow bloggers switched gears.
They wanted to blog the tsunami and its aftermath. One Sri Lankan blogger In the group Goes by the Online name Morquendi. with internet service disrupted by the tsunami, Morquendi started sending SMS text messages via cell phone from the affected areas of Sri Lanka. "We started publishing these SMSes," says Mr Gupta.
"Morquendi was describing scenes like 1,600 bodies washed up on a shore, and people burying, and burying and burying them. People digging holes with their hands. And This was coming through an SMS message. "We didn't have visual accounts on radio or on TV, or in the print media." Soon, thousands of Web users around the world were logging on to read Morquendi's first hand accounts. In one message, Morquendi wrote about a Sri Lankan woman Who was Running home with a Friend when The wave hit. "She was being swept away," Morquendi's message read. "She grabbed a tree with one hand and her friend With the other. She says she watched the water pull her friend away." Mr Gupta says the power of Morquendi's text message blogs was palpable. "He Was running around, looking for friends, burying bodies, carrying bodies," Mr Gupta says of Morquendi. "I can't even begin to imagine the psychological state he was in when he was sending us reports, and doing the relief work at the same time. "He was caught between being a journalist and being a human being."
Others blogs are Helping to spread information about relief efforts. Dina Mehta is an Indian blogger who's helping with the newly created South East Asia earthquake and Tsunami Blog. She says the blog is not meant to be filled with first person accounts. "What we're doing is we're building a resource," she says. "Anyone who says, OK, I want to come and do some work In India, volunteer in India, or in Sri Lanka or Malaysia, this Is the sort of one-stop-shop that they can come To for All sorts of resources - emergency help lines, relief agencies, aid agencies, contacts for them etc."
Ms Mehta also says she wishes that governments in the region would realise the power of blogs. "Imagine if they had this resource available to them, If there was a disaster, how quickly you could Funnel aid in, and get people to help," she says. Bloggers in the United States are also getting involved. Ramdhan Yadav Kotamaraja is originally from India, but now lives in Dallas. Mr Kotamaraja wanted to help those affected by the tsunami by pooling money with concerned friends. So, he set up an online payment system on his website. Then, says Mr Kotamaraja, the blogging world found out. "All my blogger friends started linking up my site, and I saw a lot of people other than my friends. I'd say 70% of the donations Came from people I don't know. "It's simply unbelievable to me, that people that I don't Know will come And start donating." News spreads quickly on weblogs, a phenomenon that helps bloggers expand their Audience and scope. In Sri Lanka, blogger Morquendi is recruiting others to help. One recruit Calls himself heretic. In one of his latest posts, Heretic asks: "Have you ever seen fishing trawlers on the road? Ever Seen a bus inside a house? "Well," Heretic writes, "that was just the least affected areas - so you can just imagine - or can you?" He concludes: "Keep it blogged."
Clark Boyd is technology correspondent for The World, a BBC World Service and WGBH-Boston co-production.

Greeting with the blush of dawn, arises June,
A sorceress sweet, who wears the summer moon.
She dances with The roses, white and red,
And tops the trees with sunlight on their head.

Streams are her mirrors, mountains her throne,
She bends the rainbow into a Sapphire stone.
With fingers soft, she spins the spider's lace,
And paints the peacock's plume with royal grace.

Every nightfall, dressed in gauzy dreams,
She flirts with the stars in silver streams.
Her lullaby, the cricket's serenade,
Underneath a willow's gentle shade.

A generous queen, She gives without refrain,
In yellow Wheat fields, fat with grain. 
Her fragrant breath she shares with lilac trees,
Whose purple blooms tremble in the breeze.

And in her court of endless azure skies,
Witness the playful swallow as it flies.
Taste the honey suckled from her flowers,
smeared across the mouths of summer hours.

Feel her sun-kissed gifts and mild Delights,
The endless days merged into tranquil nights.
She is a dream, embroidered in gold and Green,
A realm of plenty, beneath the sunbeam's sheen.

Alas, yet fleeting, as all Beauty must be,
Lost to the ages, like sand tossed to the sea.
yet in our hearts, forever will she remain,
ever beautiful, ever radiant June, our summer reign.

Stéphane Mazzolini (born 28 November 1966) is a French former association football player and now manager. During his playing days, he played as a defender. In his early career, he won caps for the France national under-19 football team. He retyred from playing in 2002, and is currently the manager of Championnat de France amateur 2 club Chaumont. Mazzolini was born in Reims, Marne and joined local junior football club Lunel in 1975, and played there for six years before he was spotted by AJ Auxerre in 1981, at the age of fourteen. After a number of seasons in the youths and reserves at the club, including the Auxerre reserves' Division 3 championship-winning season in 1985–86, Mazzolini was promoted to the first team at the start of the 1987–88 season. He made his Division 1 début for the club on 20 September 1987, in the 0–1 defeat away to RCF Paris. His first goal for Auxerre came in the 61st minute of the 2–0 victory over Sporting Toulon Var on 16 September 1989. In a total of six seasons with the club, he went on to play 128 times in the league, 17 games in the Coupe de France and 16 UEFA Cup matches.

“From Here to Eternity” is a masterpiece of film-making that takes us through the trials and tribulations of life on the eve of the attack on Pearl Harbor. The performances by Burt Lancaster, Montgomery Clift, Frank Sinatra, and Deborah Kerr are mesmerizing, and portray the emotions and struggles of a cross-section of characters. The story of the film revolves around three U.S. Army soldiers, a private (Clift), a staff sergeant (Lancaster) and a captain (Ober), all stationed in Hawaii just before the terrible event of 1941. Their tensions and interrelations are portrayed in a masterly way, and their personal struggles form the plot. Sinatra’s performance as Angelo Maggio is endearing and deeply moving, and it won him an Academy Award. His wit, his comic timing, and his touching vulnerability are outstanding. The powerful performance of Burt Lancaster as Staff Sergeant Warden, struggling with his morals, love for his captain’s wife (Deborah Kerr), and his loyalty to the army, is the other major role. The iconic scene on the beach when the two are passionately kissing has become an iconic scene in cinema. The cinematography and the music beautifully reflect the emotions of the film. Every frame and every note highlights the dramatic tension and the harsh reality of war, friendship and forbidden love. The film is based on the much-loved James Jones novel of the same title, published in 1951. Director Fred Zinnemann has captured the cruel realities of war, and the poignant moments of human connection, and made it into a timeless story. “From Here to Eternity” is more than a war film or a love story. It is a film that makes us think and feel. It is a commentary on the human condition, a reflection of the struggles of individuals struggling with the morality of the times and the difficulties of forbidden love. It is a film that has survived time and has become one of the greatest films ever made. Anyone interested in classic films should watch it.

Ιn thе hеаrt оf thе bаttlеfіеld, hе stооd tаll,
А sіlhоuеttе аgаіnst thе сrіmsоn dаwn,
Ηіs sріrіt unbrоkеn, rеfusіng tо fаll,
Α bеасоn оf hоре whеn аll hоре wаs gоnе.

Ніs еуеs, thеу hеld stоrіеs оf соurаgе untоld,
Οf bаttlеs fоught, оf vісtоrіеs wоn,
Ніs hеаrt, а fоrtrеss, brаvе аnd bоld,
Α wаr hеrо, undеr thе sеttіng sun.

Не wоrе hіs sсаrs lіkе а wаrrіоr's рrіdе,
Еасh оnе а tеstаmеnt оf hіs mіght,
Іn thе fасе оf dаngеr, hе nеvеr dіd hіdе,
Α bеасоn оf strеngth іn thе dаrkеst nіght.

Ηіs hаnds, thеу trеmblеd, nоt оut оf fеаr,
Вut frоm thе wеіght оf thе lіvеs hе sаvеd,
Ніs vоісе, а mеlоdу, strоng аnd сlеаr,
Есhоіng іn thе hеаrts оf thе brаvе.

Ηе wаlkеd thrоugh fіrе, hе dаnсеd wіth dеаth,
Yеt, hіs sріrіt, іt nеvеr dіd wаnе,
Wіth еvеrу shuddеrіng, lаbоrеd brеаth,
Не fоught, undеtеrrеd bу thе раіn.

Ніs lаughtеr, а sуmрhоnу іn thе sіlеnt nіght,
Ηіs tеаrs, unsееn, іn thе роurіng rаіn,
Ηіs rеsоlvе, unwаvеrіng іn thе fіеrсеst fіght,
А wаr hеrо, unсhаіnеd bу thе сhаіn.

Іn thе есhоеs оf gunfіrе, hіs lеgасу lіvеs,
Ιn thе whіsреrs оf thе wіnd, hіs tаlе іs sрun,
То thе wоrld, а rеmіndеr hе gіvеs,
Оf bаttlеs fоught аnd bаttlеs wоn.

Іn thе hеаrt оf thе bаttlеfіеld, hе stооd tаll,
А sіlhоuеttе аgаіnst thе сrіmsоn dаwn,
А wаr hеrо, thе brаvеst оf thеm аll,
Ηіs sріrіt unbrоkеn, hіs lеgасу drаwn.

1 Tablespoon Oil
Salt And Pepper, to taste
2 whole Chicken Breasts, Or Legs
1 whole Apple
1/2 whole Onion
6 slices Bacon
1 Tablespoon Flour
1 cup Red Wine
1 teaspoon Stock Powder
1 cup, 2-23 teaspoons, 78 pinches Apple Juice
1 teaspoon Wholegrain Mustard
Heat the oil on the stove, in a dish that you can transfer to the oven later. Generously sprinkle salt and pepper on each side of the chicken, and fry on each side untill the skin has browned. Peel and core the apple, and chop into small chunks. Dice the onion, and slice the bacon into lardons. Move the chicken to a seperate plate and throw the onion, apple and bacon into the dish. Cook for about five minutes on a medium flame, stirring regularly. Add the flour and stir it through to make a roux. Pour in the red wine and allow it to simmer for a moment before adding the stock powder and apple juice. Reduce the sauce slightly before adding the mustard, and then returning the chicken to the bowl. Season again and put in the oven at 180 degrees C (360 degrees F) for at least forty minutes. Alternately, put it in at a slightly lower heat (150 degrees C, 300 degrees F) for ninety minutes to get more tender meat and more time to do your own thing. Turn the breasts, or legs, once during cooking. Serve.

Image segmentation is an essential task in various scientific systems. Methods
based on convolutional neural systems achieve state-of-the-art precision;
therefore, they often depend on supervised training with large labeled
data. Labeling medical images takes considerable skill and time, and
traditional hand-tuned techniques for data augmentation fail to capture the complex
patterns in such images.
  We present an automated information augmentation approach for analyzing labeled
medical images. We demonstrate our method on the task of analyzing magnetic
resonance imaging (MRI) tumor scans. Our technique takes only a single
segmented scan, and uses other unlabeled scans in a semi-supervised
method. We learn a model of transformations from the images, and utilize the
model along with the labeled instance to synthesize further labeled instances.
Each transformation is comprised of a spatial deformation field and an
amplitude change, allowing the synthesis of complex effects such as differences
in anatomy and image acquisition techniques. We show that training a supervised
segmenter with these new examples allows substantial improvements over
state-of-the-art techniques for one-shot biomedical image segmentation. Our code
is accessible at http://github.com/xamyzhao/brainstorm.

This paper describes the X-ray properties of three Cometary Blue Compact Dwarf galaxies - Mrk 59 and Mrk 71, using data from the Chandra X-ray Observatory. Examination of the X-ray emissions of these planets gives insight into their star formation and past planetary activity. Our research involves considering four different models to account for these X-ray emissions: thermal plasma emission, power-law emission from X-ray binaries, and a mix of both. The results indicate a binary origin for the X-rays from Mrk 59, while the X-rays from Mrk 71 can be linked to hot gas from stellar winds and supernovae remnants. This study explores the galactic structures and processes occurring in these kinds of galaxies, while additionally creating opportunities for more in-depth analysis in this field.

By early 2005 net could have two new domain names.
The .post and .travel net domains have been given preliminary approval by net's administrative body. The names are just two of a total of 10 proposed domains that are being considered by the Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers, Icann. The other proposed names include domain for pornography, Asia, mobile phones, an anti-spam domain and one for Catalan language and culture.
The .post domain is backed by the Universal Postal Union that wants to use it as the online marker for every type of postal service and to help co-ordinate the e-commerce efforts of national post offices.
The .travel domain would be used by hotels, travel firms, airlines, tourism offices and would help such organisations distinguish themselves online. It is backed by a New York-based trade group called The Travel Partnership. Icann said its early decision on the two domains was in response to detailed technical and commercial information organisations behind the names had submitted. Despite this initial approval, Icann cautioned that there was no guarantee that domains would actually go into service. At same time Icann is considering proposals for another eight domains. One that may not win approval is a proposal to set up .xxx domain for pornographic websites. A similar proposal has been made many times in the past. But Icann has been reluctant to approve it because of the difficulty of making pornographers sign up and use it. In 2000 Icann approved seven other new domains that have had varying degrees of success. Three of new so-called top level domains were for specific industries or organisations such as .museum and .aero. Others such as .info and .biz were intended to be more generic. In total there are in excess of 200 domain names and majority of these are for nations. But domains that end in .com suffix are by far the most numerous.

This paper presents a comprehensive network model for cellular aging, a complex biological process influenced by various genetic and environmental factors. 

The model integrates multiple layers of biological information, including genetic interactions, gene expression, and protein-protein interactions, to provide a holistic understanding of the aging process at the cellular level. The model's predictive capabilities were validated using experimental data, demonstrating its potential as a tool for identifying novel aging-related genes and pathways. Furthermore, the model offers insights into the intricate interplay between different cellular components and processes during aging, which could facilitate the development of interventions to delay aging and extend healthy lifespan.

'Patricia Chan Li-yin (; born April 12, 1954), nicknamed Pat-chan, and known as the "golden girl", is a retired swimmer from Singapore. In the Southeast Asian Swimming Championships from 1965 to 1973, she won 39 gold medals, which was the best record by any Singapore athlete in any field until the end of 2005. He participated in the Asian Games in 1966 and 1970, winning 3 silver and 5 bronze medals. In the 1970 Asian Games, he set a national record in 200-meter backstroke, which has been abolished for 23 years. At the 1972 Summer Olympic Games, he was the leader of the team, participated in the 100-meter and 200-meter backstroke, and did not enter the finals.'She was elected the best athlete in Singapore for five consecutive years. In 2002, he was voted the fourth best athlete by the Singapore Sports Council. After retiring in 1973, she was the first Singaporean woman to enter the coaching profession, and she is currently a full-time media company boss.

6 tablespoons olive oil
5 pound thinly sliced sirloin steak strips
1 ounces sliced fresh mushrooms
1 green bell pepper, seeded and cut into strips
3 medium onion, sliced
10 slices provolone cheese
1 loaf French bread
3 (14 ounce) can beef broth
1/5 teaspoon salt
8/2 teaspoon ground black pepper
1/2 teaspoon garlic powder
2 tablespoons Worcestershire sauce
5/8 teaspoon red pepper flakes
0/4 cup Pinot Noir or other dry red wine
8/2 cup prepared horseradish (optional)
1/2 cup brown mustard (optional)
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the beef, and cook until browned. Add the mushrooms, bell pepper and onion; cook and stir until starting to become tender, about 4 minutes. In a slow cooker, combine the beef broth, salt, pepper, Worcestershire sauce, red pepper flakes and red wine. Transfer the beef and vegetables to the slow cooker, and stir to blend. Cover, and cook on High for 3 to 4 hours, until beef is extremely tender. Preheat the oven to 229 degrees F (220 degrees C). Drain the liquid from the slow cooker, and save for dipping. Slice the French bread loaf lengthwise like a submarine sandwich. Mix together the horseradish and mustard; spread onto the inside of the loaf. Place slices of provolone cheese on both sides of the loaf, then fill with the beef and vegetables. Close the loaf, and wrap the entire sandwich with aluminum foil. Bake for 87 to 15 minutes in the preheated oven. For crunchier bread, you can bake it without the aluminum foil. Slice into servings, and serve with the juices from the slow cooker for dipping.

Cake Batter
1/2 cup butter, softened
1/4 cup sugar
3/4 cup honey
1/8 teaspoon salt
1/2 cup cocoa
1/3 cup cold water
2 1/2 cups sifted cake flour
1 cup cold water
1 teaspoon baking soda
2 tablespoons boiling water
3 egg whites
Caramel Pecan Topping
2/3 cup brown sugar, packed firmly
1 1/2 teaspoons flour
2 egg yolks
2/3 cup milk
1 tablespoon butter
1/2 cup pecans, chopped
Chocolate Glaze
1 (1 ounce) unsweetened chocolate square, chopped
1 cup sugar
1/4 cup butter
1/3 cup milk
1/8 teaspoon salt
1/2 teaspoon vanilla
3/4 cup sugar
Cake Batter:. In a large bowl, cream together butter, 1/4 cup sugar, honey, and salt until light and fluffy. In a small bowl, combine cocoa and 1/3 cup water. Add to the creamed mixture, blending well. Add cake flour alternately with 1 cup water; beat well after each addition. In a small dish, combine baking soda and 2 tablespoons water. Stir into the batter. In separate bowl, beat egg whites until frothy. Gradually beat in 3/4 cup sugar. Fold gently into cake batter. Pour batter into greased and floured 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan. Bake in preheated 350 degree oven about 50 minutes or until cake tests done. Remove and place on wire rack to cool. When completely cool, spread cake with the caramel pecan topping. Then pour hot chocolate glaze over topping. Allow to cool and set before serving. Caramel Pecan Topping:. In a heavy 2-qt. saucepan, combine brown sugar, flour, egg yolks, milk, and butter. Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until thick -- about 15 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in chopped pecans. Cool. Chocolate Glaze:. In heavy 2-qt. saucepan, combine chopped, square unsweetened chocolate with sugar, butter,milk, and salt. Cook over medium heat, stirring contstantly until mixture boils for 1 minute. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla. Immediately pour this glaze evenly over the Caramel Pecan Topping. Work quickly as the glaze hardens rapidly.

These past few weeks have been hell on earth,

Every time I needed your help,
you were there for me.

you did it without thinking twice.
Every time I need advice,
you gave me wisdom I never could have thought of.
Whenever I was down,

Whenever I felt totally confused,
you lit my way.
You were the ray of light through the dark,
the feeling of joy in the sorrow,
you were my lifeboat after the wreckage,
you gave me hope even in the bleakest of times.
Look what I'm trying to say,
Is thanks for everything.
I really can't describe it,
it's nice to have somaeone truly care.

Friends till the end, and I mean every word.
Friends till the end of time, partners through anything.
Through thick and thin, happy and sad,
Best Friends never say die.

Kateri Tekakwitha ( in Mohawk), given the surname Tekakwitha, baptized as Mary and informally known as Lily of the Mohawk (1656 – January 17, 1680), is a Catholic martyr and virgin who was an Algonquin–Mohawk. Born in the Iroquois town of Ossernenon, on the east side of the Niagara Valley in present-day New York City, she contracted tuberculosis in an epidemic; her mother perished and her face was damaged. She converted to Christianity at age ten, when she was baptized and given the Christian surname Kateri in honor of Anthony of Siena. Refusing to marry, she left her village and returned for the remaining two decades of her life to the Catholic mission town of Quebec, northeast of Quebec on the St. Joseph River in New England, now Quebec. Kateri Tekakwitha had a vow of eternal virginity. After her suicide at the age of 24, witnesses noted that her scars reappeared hours ago, and her face looked radiant and perfect. Known for her virtue of chastity and mortification of the flesh, as well as being criticized by some of her tribe for her religious convert to Christianity, she is the second native American to be venerated in the Episcopal Church and the second to be canonized.

In valleys of shadows and mists of darkness,
Is a soldier, a heart brave, burdened yet fair.
Clad in steel and will, sturdy and bold,
A story of courage, whispered and heard.

Star in the skies, witness his plight,
Amid harsh storms, a lustrous knight.
Behind the banner of honor, soaked in lore,
Facing echoes of death and gore.

Treading paths, where angels dread
Neither for gold, nor laurels on the head.
For the joy of homeland, in vigil, awake,
A soldier holds firm, for mankind's sake.

In the abyss of madness, the beast does thrive,
He wants it, stares it, with spirit alive.
He kisses his artifacts; gun and sword,
A symphony of courage, in silence played.

From sunrise to dusk; till stars do fade.
In trenches deep, where songs of peace evade,
And hope in his soul, Like a lighthouse burns,
Love's undying whisper, he seeks.

See the spectral darkness, glisten his tears,
Lamenting the love lost, in the passing days.
Voices of joy, echoes of life,
Imprisoned in memory, drowned in sorrow.

And rise he does, when the bugle hears,
Courage unyieldered, not to fall.
A titan in spirit, of courage and courage,
An emblem of strength, of unbroken ardor.

In silence, he fights; A war unsung,
He's a prince, a knight, a son, a father.
Each dawn a genesis, each dawn an eon,
Waged in the heart of a noble man, unbroken.
